spanish_meaning_en: The body is the set of all the parts of a person.
spanish_example_en: My body is tired.

spanish_meaning: La axila está debajo de la unión del brazo y el torso.
spanish_example: Me puse desodorante en la axila.
spanish_meaning_en: The armpit is below the junction of the arm and torso.
spanish_example_en: I put deodorant in my armpit.

spanish_meaning: La espalda es la parte posterior del cuerpo.
spanish_example: Me duele la espalda.
spanish_meaning_en: The back is the back part of the body.
spanish_example_en: My back hurts.

spanish_meaning: La cadera une la pelvis con la pierna.
spanish_example: Mi cadera cruje al caminar.
spanish_meaning_en: The hip connects the pelvis to the leg.
spanish_example_en: My hip creaks when I walk.

spanish_meaning: La parte inferior es la zona más baja o trasera.
spanish_example: Se sentó en el fondo de la silla.
notes: Sense: lower part or underside.
spanish_meaning_en: The bottom is the lowest or rear area.
spanish_example_en: He sat at the bottom of the chair.

spanish_meaning: El talón es la parte trasera del pie.
spanish_example: Me golpeé el talón en la mesa.
spanish_meaning_en: The heel is the back part of the foot.
spanish_example_en: I hit my heel on the table.

spanish_meaning: El tobillo une pie y pierna.
spanish_example: Me torcí el tobillo.
spanish_meaning_en: The ankle joins the foot and leg.
spanish_example_en: I sprained my ankle.

spanish_meaning: La pantorrilla es la parte trasera de la pierna.
spanish_example: Me dolió la pantorrilla.
spanish_meaning_en: The calf is the back part of the leg.
spanish_example_en: My calf hurt.

spanish_meaning: La uña de la mano cubre y protege la punta de los dedos.
spanish_example: Tengo la uña de la mano rota.
spanish_meaning_en: The fingernail covers and protects the tips of the fingers.
spanish_example_en: I have a broken fingernail.

spanish_meaning: El nudillo es la articulación visible de los dedos.
spanish_example: Me dolía el nudillo.
spanish_meaning_en: The knuckle is the visible joint of the fingers.
spanish_example_en: My knuckle hurt.

spanish_meaning: La cintura es la parte media entre pecho y caderas.
spanish_example: La cintura se me ensanchó.
spanish_meaning_en: The waist is the middle part between the chest and hips.
spanish_example_en: My waist widened.

spanish_meaning: El antebrazo está entre muñeca y codo.
spanish_example: Me apreté el antebrazo del perro.
spanish_meaning_en: The forearm is between the wrist and the elbow.
spanish_example_en: I squeezed the dog's forearm.

spanish_meaning: El codo es la articulación que une brazo y antebrazo.
spanish_example: Me dolía el codo al moverlo.
spanish_meaning_en: The elbow is the joint that joins the arm and forearm.
spanish_example_en: My elbow hurt when I moved it.

spanish_meaning: El brazo superior es la parte alta del brazo.
spanish_example: Entreno el brazo superior.
spanish_meaning_en: The upper arm is the upper part of the arm.
spanish_example_en: I train the upper arm.

spanish_meaning: El hombro une el brazo con el tórax.
spanish_example: Me pesa la mochila sobre el hombro.
spanish_meaning_en: The shoulder joins the arm to the thorax.
spanish_example_en: My backpack weighs on my shoulder.

spanish_meaning: Hermano significa varón de la familia o mismo padre/madre.
spanish_example: Mi hermano estudia música.
spanish_meaning_en: Brother means male of the family or same father/mother.
spanish_example_en: My brother studies music.

spanish_meaning: Hermana es la persona femenina con la misma familia.
spanish_example: Mi hermana cocina bien.
spanish_meaning_en: Sister is the female person with the same family.
spanish_example_en: My sister cooks well.

spanish_meaning: Padre es quien engendra o cría a un hijo.
spanish_example: Mi padre llega tarde.
spanish_meaning_en: Father is one who begets or raises a child.
spanish_example_en: My father is late.

spanish_meaning: Madre es la persona que da vida y cuida a su hijo.
spanish_example: Mi madre prepara café.
spanish_meaning_en: Mother is the person who gives life and cares for her child.
spanish_example_en: My mother makes coffee.

spanish_meaning: El padrastro es el marido de tu madre sin parentesco biológico.
spanish_example: Mi padrastro me llevó al cine.
notes: Ambigüedad: depende de si tu madre se volvió a casar.
spanish_meaning_en: The stepfather is your mother's husband without biological relationship.
spanish_example_en: My stepfather took me to the movies.

spanish_meaning: Madrastra es la esposa de tu padre sin ser tu madre biológica.
spanish_example: La madrastra me llevó al médico.
spanish_meaning_en: Stepmother is your father's wife without being your biological mother.
spanish_example_en: The stepmother took me to the doctor.

spanish_meaning: Padres son la mamá y el papá de una persona.
spanish_example: Mis padres viven en Madrid.
spanish_meaning_en: Parents are a person's mom and dad.
spanish_example_en: My parents live in Madrid.

spanish_meaning: Abuelo es el padre del padre o de la madre.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo cuenta buenas historias.
spanish_meaning_en: Grandfather is the father of the father or mother.
spanish_example_en: My grandfather tells good stories.

spanish_meaning: Abuela es la madre del padre o de la madre.
spanish_example: La abuela vive cerca.
spanish_meaning_en: Grandmother is the mother of the father or mother.
spanish_example_en: Grandma lives nearby.

spanish_meaning: Abuelos son el abuelo y la abuela de alguien.
spanish_example: Mis abuelos nos visitan los domingos.
spanish_meaning_en: Grandparents are someone's grandfather and grandmother.
spanish_example_en: My grandparents visit us on Sundays.

spanish_meaning: Tío es el hermano de tu padre o madre.
spanish_example: Mi tío vive en otra ciudad.
spanish_meaning_en: Uncle is your father or mother's brother.
spanish_example_en: My uncle lives in another city.

spanish_meaning: La tía es la hermana de tu padre o de tu madre.
spanish_example: Mi tía vive lejos.
spanish_meaning_en: The aunt is the sister of your father or mother.
spanish_example_en: My aunt lives far away.

spanish_meaning: Primo o prima es el hijo de los hermanos de tus padres.
spanish_example: Mi primo vino ayer.
spanish_meaning_en: Cousin or cousin is the child of your parents' siblings.
spanish_example_en: My cousin came yesterday.

spanish_meaning: La sobrina de los padres es un primo o prima para ti.
spanish_example: La sobrina de mis padres vive conmigo.
notes: La fórmula es más literal y ambigua.
spanish_meaning_en: The parents' niece is a cousin for you.
spanish_example_en: My parents' niece lives with me.

spanish_meaning: Primo o prima es el hijo de los hermanos de tus padres.
spanish_example: Mi primo vino ayer.
spanish_meaning_en: Cousin or cousin is the child of your parents' siblings.
spanish_example_en: My cousin came yesterday.

spanish_meaning: El sobrino de los padres también se considera primo o prima.
spanish_example: Su sobrino de mis padres estudia ingeniería.
notes: La fórmula es más literal y ambigua.
spanish_meaning_en: The parents' nephew is also considered a cousin.
spanish_example_en: My parents' nephew studies engineering.

spanish_meaning: Rojo es un color de la rueda cromática.
spanish_example: La manzana es roja.
spanish_meaning_en: Red is a color of the color wheel.
spanish_example_en: The apple is red.

spanish_meaning: Rosa es un color claro entre rojo y blanco.
spanish_example: Me gusta el vestido rosa.
spanish_meaning_en: Pink is a light color between red and white.
spanish_example_en: I like the pink dress.

spanish_meaning: Verde es el color de la naturaleza.
spanish_example: La hierba es verde.
spanish_meaning_en: Green is the color of nature.
spanish_example_en: The grass is green.

spanish_meaning: Verde lima es un tono de verde brillante.
spanish_example: Quiero una camisa verde lima.
spanish_meaning_en: Lime green is a bright shade of green.
spanish_example_en: I want a lime green shirt.

spanish_meaning: Verde azulado es un color entre verde y azul.
spanish_example: El sofá es verde azulado.
spanish_meaning_en: Teal is a color between green and blue.
spanish_example_en: The sofa is teal.

spanish_meaning: Azul es un color del cielo y del mar.
spanish_example: El cielo está azul.
spanish_meaning_en: Blue is a color of the sky and the sea.
spanish_example_en: The sky is blue.

spanish_meaning: Azul marino es un azul muy oscuro.
spanish_example: Llevaba un abrigo azul marino.
notes: Sense: color, not military navy.
spanish_meaning_en: Navy blue is a very dark blue.
spanish_example_en: He was wearing a navy blue coat.

spanish_meaning: Amarillo es el color del sol.
spanish_example: El plátano está amarillo.
spanish_meaning_en: Yellow is the color of the sun.
spanish_example_en: The banana is yellow.

spanish_meaning: Morado es un color entre azul y rojo.
spanish_example: La flor es morada.
spanish_meaning_en: Purple is a color between blue and red.
spanish_example_en: The flower is purple.

spanish_meaning: Marrón es un color parecido al de la tierra.
spanish_example: Sus botas son marrones.
spanish_meaning_en: Brown is a color similar to that of the earth.
spanish_example_en: His boots are brown.

spanish_meaning: Beige es un color crema-grisáceo neutro.
spanish_example: El sofá es color beige.
spanish_meaning_en: Beige is a neutral grayish-cream color.
spanish_example_en: The sofa is beige.

spanish_meaning: Naranja es el color entre rojo y amarillo.
spanish_example: La pelota es naranja.
spanish_meaning_en: Orange is the color between red and yellow.
spanish_example_en: The ball is orange.

spanish_meaning: Blanco es el color de la luz sin tinte.
spanish_example: La nieve es blanca.
spanish_meaning_en: White is the color of light without tint.
spanish_example_en: The snow is white.

spanish_meaning: Negro es la ausencia de color visible.
spanish_example: El auto es negro.
spanish_meaning_en: Black is the absence of visible color.
spanish_example_en: The car is black.

spanish_meaning: Gris es un color entre negro y blanco.
spanish_example: El cielo está gris.
spanish_meaning_en: Gray is a color between black and white.
spanish_example_en: The sky is gray.

english_meaning: To agree is to have the same opinion or belief as another person.
english_example: The students agree they have too much homework.
spanish_meaning: Estar de acuerdo significa tener la misma opinión o creencia que otra persona.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes están de acuerdo en que tienen demasiados deberes.
spanish_meaning_en: To agree is to have the same opinion or belief as another person.
spanish_example_en: The students agree they have too much homework.

english_meaning: Alcohol is a type of drink that can make people drunk.
english_example: A person should not drive a car after he or she has been drinking alcohol.
spanish_meaning: El alcohol es un tipo de bebida que puede embriagar.
spanish_example: Una persona no debe conducir un coche después de haber bebido alcohol.
spanish_meaning_en: Alcohol is a type of drink that can make people drunk.
spanish_example_en: A person should not drive a car after he or she has been drinking alcohol.

english_meaning: To arrive is to get somewhere.
english_example: They arrived at school at 7 a.m.
spanish_meaning: Llegar significa alcanzar un lugar.
spanish_example: Llegaron a la escuela a las 7 a.m.
spanish_meaning_en: To arrive is to get somewhere.
spanish_example_en: They arrived at school at 7 a.m.

english_meaning: August is the eighth month of the year.
english_example: Is your birthday in August?
spanish_meaning: Agosto es el octavo mes del año.
spanish_example: ¿Tu cumpleaños es en agosto?
spanish_meaning_en: August is the eighth month of the year.
spanish_example_en: Is your birthday in August?

english_meaning: A boat is a vehicle that moves across water.
english_example: There is a small boat on the lake.
spanish_meaning: Un barco es un vehículo que se mueve por el agua.
spanish_example: Hay un barco pequeño en el lago.
spanish_meaning_en: A boat is a vehicle that moves across water.
spanish_example_en: There is a small boat on the lake.

english_meaning: Breakfast is the morning meal.
english_example: I ate eggs for breakfast.
spanish_meaning: El desayuno es la comida de la mañana.
spanish_example: Comí huevos para desayunar.
spanish_meaning_en: Breakfast is the morning meal.
spanish_example_en: I ate eggs for breakfast.

english_meaning: A camera is a piece of equipment that takes pictures.
english_example: I brought my camera on my vacation.
spanish_meaning: La cámara es un equipo que toma fotografías.
spanish_example: Llevé mi cámara de vacaciones.
spanish_meaning_en: A camera is a piece of equipment that takes pictures.
spanish_example_en: I brought my camera on my vacation.

english_meaning: A capital is a city where a country’s government is based.
english_example: The capital of the United States is Washington, D.C.
spanish_meaning: La capital es la ciudad donde se encuentra el gobierno de un país.
spanish_example: La capital de Estados Unidos es Washington, D.C.
spanish_meaning_en: A capital is a city where a country’s government is based.
spanish_example_en: The capital of the United States is Washington, D.C.

english_meaning: To catch is to grab or get something.
english_example: Did you catch the ball during the baseball game?
spanish_meaning: Atrapar es agarrar o tomar algo.
spanish_example: ¿Atrapaste la pelota durante el partido de béisbol?
spanish_meaning_en: To catch is to grab or get something.
spanish_example_en: Did you catch the ball during the baseball game?

english_meaning: A duck is a small water bird.
english_example: People feed ducks at the lake.
spanish_meaning: Un pato es un ave pequeña que vive en el agua.
spanish_example: La gente alimenta patos en el lago.
spanish_meaning_en: A duck is a small water bird.
spanish_example_en: People feed ducks at the lake.

english_meaning: To enjoy is to like something.
english_example: The woman enjoys riding her bicycle.
spanish_meaning: Disfrutar es gustar algo o encontrar placer en ello.
spanish_example: A la mujer le gusta montar en bicicleta.
spanish_meaning_en: To enjoy is to like something.
spanish_example_en: The woman enjoys riding her bicycle.

english_meaning: To invite is to ask someone to come to a place or event.
english_example: I will invite my friends to my birthday party.
spanish_meaning: Invitar significa pedir a alguien que asista a un lugar o evento.
spanish_example: Invitaré a mis amigos a mi fiesta de cumpleaños.
spanish_meaning_en: To invite is to ask someone to come to a place or event.
spanish_example_en: I will invite my friends to my birthday party.

english_meaning: To love is to like something or someone a lot.
english_example: I love my family very much.
spanish_meaning: Amar es querer mucho a alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Quiero mucho a mi familia.
spanish_meaning_en: To love is to like something or someone a lot.
spanish_example_en: I love my family very much.

english_meaning: A month is one of 12 periods of time in one year.
english_example: January is the first month of the year.
spanish_meaning: Un mes es uno de los 12 períodos de tiempo de un año.
spanish_example: Enero es el primer mes del año.
spanish_meaning_en: A month is one of 12 periods of time in one year.
spanish_example_en: January is the first month of the year.

english_meaning: To travel is to go to a faraway place on vacation or business.
english_example: They will travel to Argentina this summer.
spanish_meaning: Viajar es ir a un lugar lejano, por vacaciones o por trabajo.
spanish_example: Viajaremos a Argentina este verano.
spanish_meaning_en: To travel is to go to a faraway place on vacation or business.
spanish_example_en: They will travel to Argentina this summer.

english_meaning: If something is typical, it is normal, or something that usually happens.
english_example: My typical breakfast is toast and eggs.
spanish_meaning: Típico significa normal, habitual o lo que sucede con frecuencia.
spanish_example: Mi desayuno típico es tostadas y huevos.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is typical, it is normal, or something that usually happens.
spanish_example_en: My typical breakfast is toast and eggs.

english_meaning: To visit is to go and spend time in another place or see another person.
english_example: She wants to visit her grandmother.
spanish_meaning: Visitar es ir a pasar tiempo en otro lugar o ver a otra persona.
spanish_example: Quiere visitar a su abuela.
spanish_meaning_en: To visit is to go and spend time in another place or see another person.
spanish_example_en: She wants to visit her grandmother.

english_meaning: Weather is the temperature and the state of the outdoors.
english_example: Today’s weather is rainy and cloudy.
spanish_meaning: El clima es la temperatura y el estado del tiempo exterior.
spanish_example: El clima de hoy es lluvioso y nublado.
spanish_meaning_en: Weather is the temperature and the state of the outdoors.
spanish_example_en: Today’s weather is rainy and cloudy.

english_meaning: A week is a period of time that is seven days long.
english_example: What are you doing next week?
spanish_meaning: Una semana es un período de tiempo de siete días.
spanish_example: ¿Qué harás la próxima semana?
spanish_meaning_en: A week is a period of time that is seven days long.
spanish_example_en: What are you doing next week?

english_meaning: Wine is an alcoholic drink made from grapes.
english_example: The store carried both red and white wine.
spanish_meaning: El vino es una bebida alcohólica hecha de uvas.
spanish_example: La tienda vendía vino tinto y blanco.
spanish_meaning_en: Wine is an alcoholic drink made from grapes.
spanish_example_en: The store carried both red and white wine.

english_meaning: An adventure is a fun or exciting thing that you do.
english_example: Riding in the rough water was an adventure.
spanish_meaning: Una aventura es una experiencia divertida o emocionante.
spanish_example: Montar por agua brava fue una aventura.
spanish_meaning_en: An adventure is a fun or exciting thing that you do.
spanish_example_en: Riding in the rough water was an adventure.

english_meaning: To approach something means to move close to it.
english_example: The boy approached his school.
spanish_meaning: Acercarse significa moverse hacia una persona, lugar o cosa.
spanish_example: El niño se acercó a su escuela.
spanish_meaning_en: To approach something means to move close to it.
spanish_example_en: The boy approached his school.

english_meaning: Carefully means with great attention, especially to detail or safety.
english_example: The baby carefully climbed down the stairs.
spanish_meaning: Cuidadosamente significa hacerlo con mucha atención.
spanish_example: El bebé bajó las escaleras cuidadosamente.
spanish_meaning_en: Carefully means with great attention, especially to detail or safety.
spanish_example_en: The baby carefully climbed down the stairs.

english_meaning: A chemical is something that scientists use in chemistry.
english_example: The scientist mixed the chemicals.
spanish_meaning: Un químico es una sustancia que se usa en la química.
spanish_example: El científico mezcló los químicos.
spanish_meaning_en: A chemical is something that scientists use in chemistry.
spanish_example_en: The scientist mixed the chemicals.

english_meaning: To create means to make something new.
english_example: She created an igloo from blocks of snow.
spanish_meaning: Crear significa hacer algo nuevo.
spanish_example: Ella creó un iglú con bloques de nieve.
spanish_meaning_en: To create means to make something new.
spanish_example_en: She created an igloo from blocks of snow.

english_meaning: Evil describes something or someone bad or cruel, not good.
english_example: They felt a strange, evil presence as they got closer to the house.
spanish_meaning: Malo o malvado describe a algo o alguien cruel o dañino.
spanish_example: Sintieron una presencia extraña y malvada al acercarse a la casa.
spanish_meaning_en: Evil describes something or someone bad or cruel, not good.
spanish_example_en: They felt a strange, evil presence as they got closer to the house.

english_meaning: An experiment is a test that you do to see what will happen.
english_example: The student did an experiment in science class.
spanish_meaning: Un experimento es una prueba para ver qué ocurre.
spanish_example: La estudiante hizo un experimento en la clase de ciencias.
spanish_meaning_en: An experiment is a test that you do to see what will happen.
spanish_example_en: The student did an experiment in science class.

english_meaning: To kill someone or something is to make them die.
english_example: I killed the fly.
spanish_meaning: Matar es hacer que alguien o algo muera.
spanish_example: Maté la mosca.
spanish_meaning_en: To kill someone or something is to make them die.
spanish_example_en: I killed the fly.

english_meaning: A laboratory is a room where a scientist works.
english_example: My mother works in a laboratory.
spanish_meaning: Un laboratorio es una sala donde trabaja un científico.
spanish_example: Mi madre trabaja en un laboratorio.
spanish_meaning_en: A laboratory is a room where a scientist works.
spanish_example_en: My mother works in a laboratory.

english_meaning: Laugh is the sound made when someone is happy or a funny thing occurs.
english_example: The sound of her laugh filled the room.
spanish_meaning: La risa es el sonido que se hace al estar feliz o cuando pasa algo divertido.
spanish_example: El sonido de su risa llenó la sala.
spanish_meaning_en: Laugh is the sound made when someone is happy or a funny thing occurs.
spanish_example_en: The sound of her laugh filled the room.

english_meaning: If a sound is loud, it is strong and very easy to hear.
english_example: The man’s voice was so loud that we all could hear him.
spanish_meaning: Algo fuerte se escucha con mucha intensidad.
spanish_example: La voz del hombre fue tan fuerte que todos pudimos oírle.
spanish_meaning_en: If a sound is loud, it is strong and very easy to hear.
spanish_example_en: The man’s voice was so loud that we all could hear him.

english_meaning: When a person is nervous, they think something bad will happen.
english_example: The boy became nervous when he heard the news.
spanish_meaning: Una persona nerviosa piensa que puede pasar algo malo.
spanish_example: El niño se puso nervioso al oír la noticia.
spanish_meaning_en: When a person is nervous, they think something bad will happen.
spanish_example_en: The boy became nervous when he heard the news.

english_meaning: A noise is an unpleasant sound.
english_example: The crying baby made a loud noise.
spanish_meaning: Ruido es un sonido desagradable o molesto.
spanish_example: El llanto del bebé produjo un ruido fuerte.
spanish_meaning_en: A noise is an unpleasant sound.
spanish_example_en: The crying baby made a loud noise.

english_meaning: A project is a type of work that you do for school or a job.
english_example: His afternoon work project was to paint the room green.
spanish_meaning: Un proyecto es un trabajo que se realiza para escuela o trabajo.
spanish_example: Su trabajo de la tarde fue pintar la habitación de verde.
spanish_meaning_en: A project is a type of work that you do for school or a job.
spanish_example_en: His afternoon work project was to paint the room green.

english_meaning: To scare someone is to make them feel afraid.
english_example: My uncle was scared by what he saw in the room.
spanish_meaning: Asustar significa hacer que alguien sienta miedo.
spanish_example: Mi tío se asustó por lo que vio en la habitación.
spanish_meaning_en: To scare someone is to make them feel afraid.
spanish_example_en: My uncle was scared by what he saw in the room.

english_meaning: A secret is something that you do not tell other people.
english_example: The two boys were sharing a secret.
spanish_meaning: Un secreto es algo que no se le dice a otras personas.
spanish_example: Los dos niños compartían un secreto.
spanish_meaning_en: A secret is something that you do not tell other people.
spanish_example_en: The two boys were sharing a secret.

english_meaning: To shout is to say something loudly.
english_example: My boss shouted at me because I was late for work.
spanish_meaning: Gritar es decir algo muy en voz alta.
spanish_example: Mi jefe me gritó porque llegué tarde al trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: To shout is to say something loudly.
spanish_example_en: My boss shouted at me because I was late for work.

english_meaning: To smell something means to use your nose to sense it.
english_example: The two friends smelled the flower.
spanish_meaning: Oler significa detectar un olor con la nariz.
spanish_example: Los dos amigos olieron la flor.
spanish_meaning_en: To smell something means to use your nose to sense it.
spanish_example_en: The two friends smelled the flower.

english_meaning: If something is terrible, it is very bad.
english_example: The way he treated his classmate was terrible.
spanish_meaning: Algo terrible es muy malo o muy grave.
spanish_example: La forma en que trató a su compañero fue terrible.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is terrible, it is very bad.
spanish_example_en: The way he treated his classmate was terrible.

english_meaning: If something is worse, it is of poorer quality than another thing.
english_example: Business was worse this month than last month.
spanish_meaning: Peor es cuando algo es de menor calidad o más negativo que otra cosa.
spanish_example: Los negocios fueron peores este mes que el mes pasado.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is worse, it is of poorer quality than another thing.
spanish_example_en: Business was worse this month than last month.

english_meaning: An alien is a creature from a different world.
english_example: The alien came in peace.
spanish_meaning: Un extraterrestre es una criatura de otro mundo.
spanish_example: El extraterrestre llegó en paz.
spanish_meaning_en: An alien is a creature from a different world.
spanish_example_en: The alien came in peace.

english_meaning: If you are among certain things, they are all around you.
english_example: There was a red apple among the green ones.
spanish_meaning: Entre significa estar rodeado por o dentro de un grupo.
spanish_example: Había una manzana roja entre las verdes.
spanish_meaning_en: If you are among certain things, they are all around you.
spanish_example_en: There was a red apple among the green ones.

english_meaning: A chart is a list of information.
english_example: We used a chart to see how we had improved.
spanish_meaning: Un gráfico es una forma de mostrar información para comparar.
spanish_example: Usamos un gráfico para ver cómo habíamos mejorado.
spanish_meaning_en: A chart is a list of information.
spanish_example_en: We used a chart to see how we had improved.

english_meaning: A cloud is a group of water drops in the sky.
english_example: The sky was filled with white clouds.
spanish_meaning: Una nube es un conjunto de partículas de agua en el cielo.
spanish_example: El cielo estaba lleno de nubes blancas.
spanish_meaning_en: A cloud is a group of water drops in the sky.
spanish_example_en: The sky was filled with white clouds.

english_meaning: To describe is to say or write what someone or something is like.
english_example: They described their tree as colorful, with gold ribbon and a star.
spanish_meaning: Describir significa decir o escribir cómo es una persona o cosa.
spanish_example: Describieron su árbol como colorido, con una cinta dorada y una estrella.
spanish_meaning_en: To describe is to say or write what someone or something is like.
spanish_example_en: They described their tree as colorful, with gold ribbon and a star.

english_meaning: Ever means at any time.
english_example: Going skiing last winter was the most fun I’ve ever had.
spanish_meaning: Alguna vez significa en cualquier momento.
spanish_example: Ir a esquiar el invierno pasado fue lo más divertido que he hecho.
spanish_meaning_en: Ever means at any time.
spanish_example_en: Going skiing last winter was the most fun I’ve ever had.

english_meaning: To fail means you do not succeed in what you try to do.
english_example: Since he failed to get the job, he was sad.
spanish_meaning: Fallar significa no tener éxito en lo que intentas.
spanish_example: Como no consiguió el trabajo, se puso triste.
spanish_meaning_en: To fail means you do not succeed in what you try to do.
spanish_example_en: Since he failed to get the job, he was sad.

english_meaning: A grade is a score or mark given to someone’s work.
english_example: I managed to get good grades on my report card.
spanish_meaning: Una calificación es una nota o puntuación de un trabajo.
spanish_example: Logré sacar buenas calificaciones en mi boletín.
spanish_meaning_en: A grade is a score or mark given to someone’s work.
spanish_example_en: I managed to get good grades on my report card.

english_meaning: Instead means in place of.
english_example: He ate the carrot instead of the ice cream.
spanish_meaning: En lugar de significa usar o elegir una cosa en vez de otra.
spanish_example: Él comió la zanahoria en lugar del helado.
spanish_meaning_en: Instead means in place of.
spanish_example_en: He ate the carrot instead of the ice cream.

english_meaning: A library is a place where you go to read books.
english_example: The library at school is full of books.
spanish_meaning: Una biblioteca es un lugar al que vas para leer libros.
spanish_example: La biblioteca de la escuela está llena de libros.
spanish_meaning_en: A library is a place where you go to read books.
spanish_example_en: The library at school is full of books.

english_meaning: I like taking photographs.
english_example: I took this photograph with my cell phone.
spanish_meaning: Una fotografía es una imagen tomada con cámara.
spanish_example: Tomé esta fotografía con mi teléfono celular.
spanish_meaning_en: I like taking photographs.
spanish_example_en: I took this photograph with my cell phone.

english_meaning: A planet is a large round thing in space.
english_example: Saturn is the planet with the ring around it.
spanish_meaning: Un planeta es un cuerpo celeste grande y redondo en el espacio.
spanish_example: Saturno es el planeta que tiene el anillo alrededor.
spanish_meaning_en: A planet is a large round thing in space.
spanish_example_en: Saturn is the planet with the ring around it.

english_meaning: A report is something someone writes for school or work.
english_example: Karen had trouble writing her report.
spanish_meaning: Un informe es un texto escrito para exponer información en la escuela o el trabajo.
spanish_example: Karen tuvo dificultades para escribir su informe.
spanish_meaning_en: A report is something someone writes for school or work.
spanish_example_en: Karen had trouble writing her report.

english_meaning: Several is more than two but not many.
english_example: He had to read several books for class.
spanish_meaning: Varios significa más que dos, pero no muchos.
spanish_example: Tuvo que leer varios libros para la clase.
spanish_meaning_en: Several is more than two but not many.
spanish_example_en: He had to read several books for class.

english_meaning: An object’s shape is the arrangement of its sides and surfaces.
english_example: Even with your eyes closed you can feel the shape of it.
spanish_meaning: La forma de un objeto es la disposición de sus lados y superficies.
spanish_example: Incluso con los ojos cerrados se puede sentir su forma.
spanish_meaning_en: An object’s shape is the arrangement of its sides and surfaces.
spanish_example_en: Even with your eyes closed you can feel the shape of it.

english_meaning: To solve something is to find an answer to it.
english_example: All the students could easily solve the math problem.
spanish_meaning: Resolver es encontrar una respuesta.
spanish_example: Todos los estudiantes pudieron resolver fácilmente el problema de matemáticas.
spanish_meaning_en: To solve something is to find an answer to it.
spanish_example_en: All the students could easily solve the math problem.

english_meaning: If something happens suddenly, it happens quickly and unexpectedly.
english_example: I was surprised when my friends suddenly shouted, “Happy birthday!”
spanish_meaning: De repente significa que algo sucede de forma rápida e inesperada.
spanish_example: Me sorprendí cuando mis amigos gritaron de repente: “¡Feliz cumpleaños!”
spanish_meaning_en: If something happens suddenly, it happens quickly and unexpectedly.
spanish_example_en: I was surprised when my friends suddenly shouted, “Happy birthday!”

english_meaning: To suppose is to guess.
english_example: I suppose I should go home now.
spanish_meaning: Suponer es pensar o imaginar algo como posible.
spanish_example: Supongo que debería ir a casa ahora.
spanish_meaning_en: To suppose is to guess.
spanish_example_en: I suppose I should go home now.

english_meaning: To understand something you need to know what it means.
english_example: Henry could not understand the message.
spanish_meaning: Comprender es saber qué significa algo.
spanish_example: Henry no pudo comprender el mensaje.
spanish_meaning_en: To understand something you need to know what it means.
spanish_example_en: Henry could not understand the message.

english_meaning: To view is to look at something.
english_example: Michael likes to view himself in the mirror.
spanish_meaning: Ver es mirar algo.
spanish_example: A Michael le gusta verse en el espejo.
spanish_meaning_en: To view is to look at something.
spanish_example_en: Michael likes to view himself in the mirror.

english_meaning: When a thing is appropriate, it is right or normal.
english_example: It’s appropriate to wear a suit when you go to the office.
spanish_meaning: Algo apropiado es lo correcto o adecuado para una situación.
spanish_example: Es apropiado llevar traje cuando vas a la oficina.
spanish_meaning_en: When a thing is appropriate, it is right or normal.
spanish_example_en: It’s appropriate to wear a suit when you go to the office.

english_meaning: To avoid something is to stay away from it.
english_example: Avoid the broken bottle on the floor.
spanish_meaning: Evitar significa mantenerse alejado de algo.
spanish_example: Evita la botella rota del suelo.
spanish_meaning_en: To avoid something is to stay away from it.
spanish_example_en: Avoid the broken bottle on the floor.

english_meaning: To behave is to act in a particular way, especially to be good.
english_example: She always behaves well when her father is around.
spanish_meaning: Comportarse es actuar de cierta manera, sobre todo en forma correcta.
spanish_example: Ella siempre se comporta bien cuando su padre está cerca.
spanish_meaning_en: To behave is to act in a particular way, especially to be good.
spanish_example_en: She always behaves well when her father is around.

english_meaning: When someone is calm, they do not get excited or upset.
english_example: A nice warm bath makes me feel so calm.
spanish_meaning: Estar tranquilo significa no excitarse ni alterarse.
spanish_example: Un baño tibio me hace sentir tan tranquilo.
spanish_meaning_en: When someone is calm, they do not get excited or upset.
spanish_example_en: A nice warm bath makes me feel so calm.

english_meaning: Concern is a feeling of worry.
english_example: I was filled with concern after reading the newspaper.
spanish_meaning: Preocupación es un sentimiento de inquietud o inquietud por algo.
spanish_example: Me quedé lleno de preocupación tras leer el periódico.
spanish_meaning_en: Concern is a feeling of worry.
spanish_example_en: I was filled with concern after reading the newspaper.

english_meaning: To be content is to be happy and not want more.
english_example: The baby looked very content sitting on the floor.
spanish_meaning: Estar contento es sentirse feliz y sin querer más.
spanish_example: El bebé se veía muy satisfecho sentado en el suelo.
spanish_meaning_en: To be content is to be happy and not want more.
spanish_example_en: The baby looked very content sitting on the floor.

english_meaning: If you expect something to happen, you believe it will happen.
english_example: I expect the bus to be here very soon.
spanish_meaning: Esperar es creer que algo va a suceder.
spanish_example: Espero que el autobús llegue pronto.
spanish_meaning_en: If you expect something to happen, you believe it will happen.
spanish_example_en: I expect the bus to be here very soon.

english_meaning: When something happens frequently, it happens often.
english_example: We meet frequently, either at the beginning or end of the week.
spanish_meaning: Con frecuencia significa que algo pasa con mucha regularidad.
spanish_example: Nos reunimos frecuentemente, al principio o al final de la semana.
spanish_meaning_en: When something happens frequently, it happens often.
spanish_example_en: We meet frequently, either at the beginning or end of the week.

english_meaning: A habit is a thing that you do often.
english_example: Smoking is a bad habit that can kill you.
spanish_meaning: Un hábito es una conducta que se repite con frecuencia.
spanish_example: Fumar es un mal hábito que puede matarte.
spanish_meaning_en: A habit is a thing that you do often.
spanish_example_en: Smoking is a bad habit that can kill you.

english_meaning: To instruct is to teach.
english_example: My teacher instructs us in several subjects.
spanish_meaning: Instruir significa enseñar o guiar en algo.
spanish_example: Mi profesor nos instruye en varias materias.
spanish_meaning_en: To instruct is to teach.
spanish_example_en: My teacher instructs us in several subjects.

english_meaning: An issue is an important topic.
english_example: The men spoke about issues that were important to the people.
spanish_meaning: Un tema importante es un asunto que merece atención.
spanish_example: Los hombres hablaron de temas importantes para la gente.
spanish_meaning_en: An issue is an important topic.
spanish_example_en: The men spoke about issues that were important to the people.

english_meaning: None means not any of something.
english_example: He spent all his money. There is none left.
spanish_meaning: Ninguno significa que no hay ni una cantidad de eso.
spanish_example: No le quedó ningún dinero; gastó todo.
spanish_meaning_en: None means not any of something.
spanish_example_en: He spent all his money. There is none left.

english_meaning: If people are patient, they don’t become angry or upset easily.
english_example: I had to be patient and wait until 5 o’clock to leave.
spanish_meaning: Paciente significa tener paciencia y tolerar sin ponerse nervioso.
spanish_example: Tuve que ser paciente y esperar hasta las cinco para irme.
spanish_meaning_en: When people are patient, they don’t become angry or upset easily.
spanish_example_en: I had to be patient and wait until 5 o’clock to leave.

english_meaning: If something is positive, it is good.
english_example: She has a positive future ahead of her after finishing college.
spanish_meaning: Positivo significa bueno o favorable.
spanish_example: Ella tiene un futuro prometedor después de terminar la universidad.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is positive, it is good.
spanish_example_en: She has a positive future ahead of her after finishing college.

english_meaning: To punish means to make someone suffer for breaking the rules or laws.
english_example: To punish me, my teacher had me stand in the corner.
spanish_meaning: Castigar significa hacer sufrir a alguien por romper una regla o ley.
spanish_example: Para castigarme, mi maestra me hizo pararme en la esquina.
spanish_meaning_en: To punish means to make someone suffer for breaking the rules or laws.
spanish_example_en: To punish me, my teacher had me stand in the corner.

english_meaning: To represent is to speak or act for a person or group.
english_example: My lawyer will represent me in court.
spanish_meaning: Representar significa hablar o actuar en nombre de una persona o grupo.
spanish_example: Mi abogado me representará en el tribunal.
spanish_meaning_en: To represent is to speak or act for a person or group.
spanish_example_en: My lawyer will represent me in court.

english_meaning: To shake is to move back and forth or up and down quickly.
english_example: When people shake hands, it usually means they agree.
spanish_meaning: Agitar (o estrecharse la mano) significa mover la mano y suele indicar acuerdo.
spanish_example: Cuando la gente se da la mano, suele significar que está de acuerdo.
spanish_meaning_en: To shake is to move back and forth or up and down quickly.
spanish_example_en: When people shake hands, it usually means they agree.

english_meaning: To spread is to move out to cover a larger area.
english_example: I like to spread butter on my toast.
spanish_meaning: Extender es cubrir una superficie con algo.
spanish_example: Me gusta untar mantequilla en mi tostada.
spanish_meaning_en: To spread is to move out to cover a larger area.
spanish_example_en: I like to spread butter on my toast.

english_meaning: To stroll means to walk slowly and calmly.
english_example: My dog and I strolled through the park today.
spanish_meaning: Pasear significa caminar de manera relajada.
spanish_example: Mi perro y yo paseamos hoy por el parque.
spanish_meaning_en: To stroll means to walk slowly and calmly.
spanish_example_en: My dog and I strolled through the park today.

english_meaning: A village is a very small town.
english_example: There are only a few houses in my village.
spanish_meaning: Un pueblo es una población pequeña.
spanish_example: En mi pueblo hay pocas casas.
spanish_meaning_en: A village is a very small town.
spanish_example_en: There are only a few houses in my village.

english_meaning: When someone is active, they move a lot or have a lot of things to do.
english_example: It is important to be active and not sit around all the time.
spanish_meaning: Activo significa moverse y participar en lugar de estar inactivo.
spanish_example: Es importante estar activo y no pasar todo el tiempo sentado.
spanish_meaning_en: When someone is active, they move a lot or have a lot of things to do.
spanish_example_en: It is important to be active and not sit around all the time.

english_meaning: An adult is a person who is more than 18 years old.
english_example: My mom and dad are adults.
spanish_meaning: Adulto es alguien con más de 18 años.
spanish_example: Mi mamá y mi papá son adultos.
spanish_meaning_en: An adult is a person who is more than 18 years old.
spanish_example_en: My mom and dad are adults.

english_meaning: Age is how many years someone has lived.
english_example: She died at the age of 80.
spanish_meaning: La edad es cuántos años de vida tiene alguien.
spanish_example: Murió con 80 años.
spanish_meaning_en: Age is how many years someone has lived.
spanish_example_en: She died at the age of 80.

english_meaning: When something is bad, it is not good.
english_example: She is unhappy because she had a bad day.
spanish_meaning: Malo significa que no es bueno.
spanish_example: Ella está triste porque tuvo un mal día.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is bad, it is not good.
spanish_example_en: She is unhappy because she had a bad day.

english_meaning: Balance is when two or more things are equal.
english_example: A good balance between work and fun helps keep you healthy.
spanish_meaning: El equilibrio es la proporción o estabilidad entre partes.
spanish_example: Un buen equilibrio entre trabajo y diversión ayuda a mantenerte saludable.
spanish_meaning_en: Balance is when two or more things are equal.
spanish_example_en: A good balance between work and fun helps keep you healthy.

english_meaning: A bike is a vehicle with two wheels powered by a human.
english_example: He rides his bike to school every day.
spanish_meaning: Una bicicleta es un vehículo de dos ruedas impulsado por personas.
spanish_example: Él va en bicicleta a la escuela todos los días.
spanish_meaning_en: A bike is a vehicle with two wheels powered by a human.
spanish_example_en: He rides his bike to school every day.

english_meaning: To choose is to pick something or make a decision.
english_example: I have to choose between taking art classes and sports lessons.
spanish_meaning: Elegir es decidir entre opciones.
spanish_example: Tengo que elegir entre tomar clases de arte o clases deportivas.
spanish_meaning_en: To choose is to pick something or make a decision.
spanish_example_en: I have to choose between taking art classes and sports lessons.

english_meaning: A doctor is a person who studies medicine and helps sick people.
english_example: You should go to the doctor when you are sick.
spanish_meaning: Un médico es alguien que estudia medicina y ayuda a personas enfermas.
spanish_example: Deberías ir al médico cuando estés enfermo.
spanish_meaning_en: A doctor is a person who studies medicine and helps sick people.
spanish_example_en: You should go to the doctor when you are sick.

english_meaning: During an event means while the event was happening.
english_example: Did you sleep during the movie?
spanish_meaning: Durante significa mientras ocurre algo.
spanish_example: ¿Dormiste durante la película?
spanish_meaning_en: During an event means while the event was happening.
spanish_example_en: Did you sleep during the movie?

english_meaning: Football is a sport with eleven members and an oval-shaped ball.
english_example: Football is a popular sport in the United States.
spanish_meaning: El fútbol americano es un deporte con once jugadores y un balón con forma ovalada.
spanish_example: El fútbol americano es un deporte popular en Estados Unidos.
spanish_meaning_en: Football is a sport with eleven members and an oval-shaped ball.
spanish_example_en: Football is a popular sport in the United States.

english_meaning: When something is fun, it is enjoyable.
english_example: We had a fun time at the birthday party.
spanish_meaning: Divertido significa que algo resulta agradable.
spanish_example: Nos lo pasamos muy bien en la fiesta de cumpleaños.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is fun, it is enjoyable.
spanish_example_en: We had a fun time at the birthday party.

english_meaning: A game is an activity where people compete against each other.
english_example: Let’s play a board game tonight.
spanish_meaning: Un juego es una actividad en la que compiten personas.
spanish_example: Juguemos a un juego de mesa esta noche.
spanish_meaning_en: A game is an activity where people compete against each other.
spanish_example_en: Let’s play a board game tonight.

english_meaning: A heart is an organ that keeps the body alive.
english_example: My heart beats fast when I am nervous.
spanish_meaning: El corazón es un órgano que mantiene vivo el cuerpo.
spanish_example: Mi corazón late rápido cuando estoy nervioso.
spanish_meaning_en: A heart is an organ that keeps the body alive.
spanish_example_en: My heart beats fast when I am nervous.

english_meaning: Golf is a sport with clubs and a small white ball.
english_example: People play golf in nice weather.
spanish_meaning: El golf es un deporte con palos y una pelota pequeña.
spanish_example: La gente juega golf cuando hace buen tiempo.
spanish_meaning_en: Golf is a sport with clubs and a small white ball.
spanish_example_en: People play golf in nice weather.

english_meaning: To increase is to make something larger.
english_example: I will increase my score if I study for the test.
spanish_meaning: Aumentar significa hacer que algo sea más grande.
spanish_example: Aumentaré mi puntuación si estudio para el examen.
spanish_meaning_en: To increase is to make something larger.
spanish_example_en: I will increase my score if I study for the test.

english_meaning: Life is the time when a person is alive.
english_example: My grandfather had a long life.
spanish_meaning: La vida es el tiempo en que una persona está viva.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo tuvo una vida larga.
spanish_meaning_en: Life is the time when a person is alive.
spanish_example_en: My grandfather had a long life.

english_meaning: A kilometer is a unit of measurement that is 1,000 meters.
english_example: A marathon is 42.2 kilometers.
spanish_meaning: El kilómetro es una unidad de distancia de 1.000 metros.
spanish_example: Una maratón tiene 42,2 kilómetros.
spanish_meaning_en: A kilometer is a unit of measurement that is 1,000 meters.
spanish_example_en: A marathon is 42.2 kilometers.

english_meaning: Often is when something happens many times.
english_example: He often goes to bed early during the week.
spanish_meaning: A menudo significa que algo sucede muchas veces.
spanish_example: Él suele acostarse temprano durante la semana.
spanish_meaning_en: Often is when something happens many times.
spanish_example_en: He often goes to bed early during the week.

english_meaning: If you have plenty of something, there is a lot of it.
english_example: We have plenty of fruit, so help yourself.
spanish_meaning: Mucho indica que hay gran cantidad de algo.
spanish_example: Tenemos mucha fruta, así que sírvete.
spanish_meaning_en: If you have plenty of something, there is a lot of it.
spanish_example_en: We have plenty of fruit, so help yourself.

english_meaning: Weight is how heavy something or someone is.
english_example: I gained weight because I ate a lot of pizza.
spanish_meaning: Peso es cuánto pesa algo o alguien.
spanish_example: Subí de peso porque comí mucha pizza.
spanish_meaning_en: Weight is how heavy something or someone is.
spanish_example_en: I gained weight because I ate a lot of pizza.

english_meaning: When people or things are apart, they are not next to each other.
english_example: They moved apart and then came back together.
spanish_meaning: Cuando las personas o las cosas están separadas, no están una al lado de la otra.
spanish_example: Se separaron y luego volvieron a juntarse.
spanish_meaning_en: When people or things are apart, they are not next to each other.
spanish_example_en: They moved apart and then came back together.

english_meaning: An attribute is a characteristic of a person or thing.
english_example: He isn’t very clever, but he does have some other positive attributes.
spanish_meaning: Un atributo es una característica de una persona o cosa.
spanish_example: Él no es muy inteligente, pero sí tiene otros atributos positivos.
spanish_meaning_en: An attribute is a characteristic of a person or thing.
spanish_example_en: He isn’t very clever, but he does have some other positive attributes.

english_meaning: A bilingual person can speak two languages.
english_example: Since you already know English, after learning French you’ll be bilingual.
spanish_meaning: Una persona bilingüe puede hablar dos idiomas.
spanish_example: Como ya sabes inglés, después de aprender francés serás bilingüe.
spanish_meaning_en: A bilingual person can speak two languages.
spanish_example_en: Since you already know English, after learning French you’ll be bilingual.

english_meaning: Something completely different is very, very different from before.
english_example: I was completely wrong.
spanish_meaning: Algo completamente diferente es muy, muy diferente de antes.
spanish_example: Estaba completamente equivocado.
spanish_meaning_en: Something completely different is very, very different from before.
spanish_example_en: I was completely wrong.

english_meaning: To dash means to run or move quickly.
english_example: Helen dashed up the stairs so she wouldn’t be late for her appointment.
spanish_meaning: Correr o moverse con rapidez significa desplazarse con velocidad.
spanish_example: Helen corrió por las escaleras para que no llegara tarde a su cita.
spanish_meaning_en: To dash means to run or move quickly.
spanish_example_en: Helen dashed up the stairs so she wouldn’t be late for her appointment.

english_meaning: Disgust is a feeling of distaste and anger caused by something rude or unpleasant.
english_example: He felt disgust toward his date because she had such terrible eating habits.
spanish_meaning: El asco es una sensación de disgusto y enojo causada por algo grosero o desagradable.
spanish_example: Sintió asco hacia su cita porque tenía hábitos alimenticios terribles.
spanish_meaning_en: Disgust is a feeling of distaste and anger caused by something rude or unpleasant.
spanish_example_en: He felt disgust toward his date because she had such terrible eating habits.

english_meaning: Something fashionable is what people like to wear and do now.
english_example: It is very fashionable to wear a hat.
spanish_meaning: Algo moderno o de moda es aquello que la gente usa y hace en este momento.
spanish_example: Es muy de moda llevar sombrero.
spanish_meaning_en: Something fashionable is what people like to wear and do now.
spanish_example_en: It is very fashionable to wear a hat.

english_meaning: Something foreign is something we are not used to.
english_example: He has travelled widely in foreign countries.
spanish_meaning: Algo extranjero es algo al que no estamos acostumbrados.
spanish_example: Ha viajado mucho por países extranjeros.
spanish_meaning_en: Something foreign is something we are not used to.
spanish_example_en: He has travelled widely in foreign countries.

english_meaning: A gulf is a gap between people who do not understand each other.
english_example: There has been a gulf between James and Tony since their parents died.
spanish_meaning: Un abismo es una gran separación entre personas que no se entienden entre sí.
spanish_example: Ha habido una brecha entre James y Tony desde que murieron sus padres.
spanish_meaning_en: A gulf is a gap between people who do not understand each other.
spanish_example_en: There has been a gulf between James and Tony since their parents died.

english_meaning: A mirror is usually made of glass, and you can see yourself in it.
english_example: He used the mirror to shine light in their eyes.
spanish_meaning: Un espejo suele ser de vidrio, y en él puedes verte a ti mismo.
spanish_example: Usó el espejo para deslumbrarlos con la luz en los ojos.
spanish_meaning_en: A mirror is usually made of glass, and you can see yourself in it.
spanish_example_en: He used the mirror to shine light in their eyes.

english_meaning: Things that are natural are not made by people.
english_example: We need to take care of the natural world.
spanish_meaning: Las cosas naturales no están hechas por personas.
spanish_example: Necesitamos cuidar el mundo natural.
spanish_meaning_en: Things that are natural are not made by people.
spanish_example_en: We need to take care of the natural world.

english_meaning: Nowadays means at the present time.
english_example: Nowadays, not so many people smoke.
spanish_meaning: Hoy en día significa en el momento actual.
spanish_example: Hoy en día, no fuma tanta gente.
spanish_meaning_en: Nowadays means at the present time.
spanish_example_en: Nowadays, not so many people smoke.

english_meaning: A participant is someone who joins in a social event or competition.
english_example: There were thousands of participants in this year’s marathon.
spanish_meaning: Un participante es alguien que se une a un evento social o una competición.
spanish_example: Hubo miles de participantes en la maratón de este año.
spanish_meaning_en: A participant is someone who joins in a social event or competition.
spanish_example_en: There were thousands of participants in this year’s marathon.

english_meaning: A ritual is a formal custom that people do regularly.
english_example: Ken was very interested to learn about the religious rituals of the natives.
spanish_meaning: Un ritual es una costumbre formal que las personas hacen regularmente.
spanish_example: Ken estaba muy interesado en aprender sobre los rituales religiosos de los nativos.
spanish_meaning_en: A ritual is a formal custom that people do regularly.
spanish_example_en: Ken was very interested to learn about the religious rituals of the natives.

english_meaning: Spoken language is what we say.
english_example: We cannot take back what has been spoken.
spanish_meaning: El lenguaje hablado es lo que decimos.
spanish_example: No podemos echar atrás lo que ya se ha dicho.
spanish_meaning_en: Spoken language is what we say.
spanish_example_en: We cannot take back what has been spoken.

english_meaning: Sport involves games like running, jumping, football, and tennis.
english_example: She loves watching sport on TV.
spanish_meaning: El deporte implica juegos como correr, saltar, fútbol y tenis.
spanish_example: Ella ama ver deporte en la televisión.
spanish_meaning_en: Sport involves games like running, jumping, football, and tennis.
spanish_example_en: She loves watching sport on TV.

english_meaning: You are surprised when something unexpected happens.
english_example: He didn’t appear at all surprised when he read the article.
spanish_meaning: Te sorprendes cuando sucede algo inesperado.
spanish_example: No se mostró para nada sorprendido cuando leyó el artículo.
spanish_meaning_en: You are surprised when something unexpected happens.
spanish_example_en: He didn’t appear at all surprised when he read the article.

english_meaning: When you are tense, you are not comfortable and feel unhappy.
english_example: I felt very tense waiting to hear the result of the vote.
spanish_meaning: Cuando estás tenso, no te sientes cómodo y te sientes infeliz.
spanish_example: Me sentí muy tenso esperando escuchar el resultado de la votación.
spanish_meaning_en: When you are tense, you are not comfortable and feel unhappy.
spanish_example_en: I felt very tense waiting to hear the result of the vote.

english_meaning: If you totally agree, you agree with everything just said.
english_example: I am totally against that.
spanish_meaning: Si tú estás totalmente de acuerdo, aceptas todo lo que se acaba de decir.
spanish_example: Estoy totalmente en contra de eso.
spanish_meaning_en: If you totally agree, you agree with everything just said.
spanish_example_en: I am totally against that.

english_meaning: If something is vague, it is not clear and gives very few details.
english_example: I asked him about his mother’s health, but he was very vague about it.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es vago, no está claro y ofrece muy pocos detalles.
spanish_example: Le pregunté sobre la salud de su madre, pero él fue muy vago al respecto.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is vague, it is not clear and gives very few details.
spanish_example_en: I asked him about his mother’s health, but he was very vague about it.

english_meaning: To allow something to happen means to let it happen.
english_example: Having a ticket will allow you to enter the show.
spanish_meaning: Permitir que algo ocurra significa dejar que suceda.
spanish_example: Tener una entrada te permitirá entrar al espectáculo.
spanish_meaning_en: To allow something to happen means to let it happen.
spanish_example_en: Having a ticket will allow you to enter the show.

english_meaning: To announce something is to make it known.
english_example: He announced to everyone his new idea for the company.
spanish_meaning: Anunciar algo es darlo a conocer.
spanish_example: Les anunció a todos su nueva idea para la empresa.
spanish_meaning_en: To announce something is to make it known.
spanish_example_en: He announced to everyone his new idea for the company.

english_meaning: Someone or something beside you is next to you.
english_example: The two brothers stood beside each other.
spanish_meaning: Alguien o algo al lado de ti está junto a ti.
spanish_example: Los dos hermanos se colocaron uno junto al otro.
spanish_meaning_en: Someone or something beside you is next to you.
spanish_example_en: The two brothers stood beside each other.

english_meaning: A challenge is something difficult to complete.
english_example: It was a challenge to climb to the top of the mountain.
spanish_meaning: Un desafío es algo difícil de completar.
spanish_example: Fue un desafío subir hasta la cima de la montaña.
spanish_meaning_en: A challenge is something difficult to complete.
spanish_example_en: It was a challenge to climb to the top of the mountain.

english_meaning: To claim means to say that something is true.
english_example: He claimed to know why the country’s laws were weak.
spanish_meaning: Afirmar significa decir que algo es cierto.
spanish_example: Afirmó saber por qué las leyes del país eran débiles.
spanish_meaning_en: To claim means to say that something is true.
spanish_example_en: He claimed to know why the country’s laws were weak.

english_meaning: The condition of someone or something is the state that they are in.
english_example: The patient’s condition was very good.
spanish_meaning: La condición de una persona o algo es el estado en que se encuentra.
spanish_example: La condición del paciente era muy buena.
spanish_meaning_en: The condition of someone or something is the state that they are in.
spanish_example_en: The patient’s condition was very good.

english_meaning: To contribute to something means to do something to make it successful.
english_example: We decided to contribute money to the new hospital.
spanish_meaning: Contribuir a algo significa hacer algo para hacerlo exitoso.
spanish_example: Decidimos contribuir con dinero para el nuevo hospital.
spanish_meaning_en: To contribute to something means to do something to make it successful.
spanish_example_en: We decided to contribute money to the new hospital.

english_meaning: A difference is a way that something is not like other things.
english_example: The biggest difference between the birds is the color of their feathers.
spanish_meaning: Una diferencia es una forma en que algo no es como otras cosas.
spanish_example: La mayor diferencia entre las aves es el color de sus plumas.
spanish_meaning_en: A difference is a way that something is not like other things.
spanish_example_en: The biggest difference between the birds is the color of their feathers.

english_meaning: To divide something is to split it into smaller parts.
english_example: We divided the pizza.
spanish_meaning: Dividir algo es separarlo en partes más pequeñas.
spanish_example: Dividimos la pizza.
spanish_meaning_en: To divide something is to split it into smaller parts.
spanish_example_en: We divided the pizza.

english_meaning: An expert is someone who is very good at doing something.
english_example: The wizard was an expert at magic.
spanish_meaning: Un experto es alguien que es muy bueno haciendo algo.
spanish_example: El mago era un experto en magia.
spanish_meaning_en: An expert is someone who is very good at doing something.
spanish_example_en: The wizard was an expert at magic.

english_meaning: A famous person or thing is well known.
english_example: The Eiffel Tower in Paris is very famous.
spanish_meaning: Una persona o cosa famosa es muy conocida.
spanish_example: La Torre Eiffel en París es muy famosa.
spanish_meaning_en: A famous person or thing is well known.
spanish_example_en: The Eiffel Tower in Paris is very famous.

english_meaning: Force is a person’s strength or power.
english_example: He used all his force to try and open the door.
spanish_meaning: La fuerza es la energía o potencia física de una persona.
spanish_example: Usó toda su fuerza para intentar abrir la puerta.
spanish_meaning_en: Force is a person’s strength or power.
spanish_example_en: He used all his force to try and open the door.

english_meaning: To cause harm is to hurt someone or damage something.
english_example: A hot iron can cause great harm if you are not careful.
spanish_meaning: Causar daño es herir a alguien o dañar algo.
spanish_example: Una plancha caliente puede causar mucho daño si no tienes cuidado.
spanish_meaning_en: To cause harm is to hurt someone or damage something.
spanish_example_en: A hot iron can cause great harm if you are not careful.

english_meaning: To lay means to put or place in a horizontal or flat position.
english_example: Don’t lay your socks on the floor.
spanish_meaning: Poner o colocar algo significa ponerlo en posición horizontal o plana.
spanish_example: No pongas tus calcetines en el suelo.
notes: En este contexto equivale a «colocar»; no al verbo «acostarse».
spanish_meaning_en: To lay means to put or place in a horizontal or flat position.
spanish_example_en: Don’t lay your socks on the floor.

english_meaning: Peace is a time without war.
english_example: A white dove is a symbol of peace.
spanish_meaning: La paz es un tiempo sin guerra.
spanish_example: Una paloma blanca es un símbolo de la paz.
spanish_meaning_en: Peace is a time without war.
spanish_example_en: A white dove is a symbol of peace.

english_meaning: A prince is the son of a king.
english_example: The prince and the princess were married.
spanish_meaning: Un príncipe es el hijo de un rey.
spanish_example: El príncipe y la princesa se casaron.
spanish_meaning_en: A prince is the son of a king.
spanish_example_en: The prince and the princess were married.

english_meaning: To protect someone is to stop them from getting hurt.
english_example: Firemen protect us from fires.
spanish_meaning: Proteger a alguien significa impedir que se lastime.
spanish_example: Los bomberos nos protegen de los incendios.
spanish_meaning_en: To protect someone is to stop them from getting hurt.
spanish_example_en: Firemen protect us from fires.

english_meaning: To sense something is to know about it without being told.
english_example: I could sense that he was watching me.
spanish_meaning: Percibir algo es saber de ello sin que te lo digan.
spanish_example: Pude notar que él estaba mirándome.
spanish_meaning_en: To sense something is to know about it without being told.
spanish_example_en: I could sense that he was watching me.

english_meaning: When something is sudden, it happens very quickly.
english_example: He felt a sudden pain in his chest.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es repentino, sucede muy rápido.
spanish_example: Sintió un dolor repentino en el pecho.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is sudden, it happens very quickly.
spanish_example_en: He felt a sudden pain in his chest.

english_meaning: Therefore means for this reason.
english_example: He is fat. Therefore, he should go on a diet.
spanish_meaning: Por lo tanto significa por esta razón.
spanish_example: Es obeso. Por lo tanto, debería hacer dieta.
spanish_meaning_en: Therefore means for this reason.
spanish_example_en: He is fat. Therefore, he should go on a diet.

english_meaning: To accept something that is offered is to take it.
english_example: I accepted the girl’s very nice gift.
spanish_meaning: Aceptar algo que se te ofrece significa tomarlo.
spanish_example: Acepté el bonito regalo de la muchacha.
spanish_meaning_en: To accept something that is offered is to take it.
spanish_example_en: I accepted the girl’s very nice gift.

english_meaning: To arrange things is to put them in the right place.
english_example: Please arrange the words in order from A to Z.
spanish_meaning: Organizar cosas es ponerlas en el lugar adecuado.
spanish_example: Por favor, ordena las palabras en orden de la A a la Z.
spanish_meaning_en: To arrange things is to put them in the right place.
spanish_example_en: Please arrange the words in order from A to Z.

english_meaning: To attend something is to go to it.
english_example: My sister and I attend the same school.
spanish_meaning: Asistir a algo es ir a ello.
spanish_example: Mi hermana y yo asistimos al mismo colegio.
spanish_meaning_en: To attend something is to go to it.
spanish_example_en: My sister and I attend the same school.

english_meaning: You chase people when you run after them and try to catch them.
english_example: I was chased by a dog.
spanish_meaning: Perseguir a personas es correr detrás de ellas e intentar atraparlas.
spanish_example: Un perro me persiguió.
spanish_meaning_en: You chase people when you run after them and try to catch them.
spanish_example_en: I was chased by a dog.

english_meaning: A contrast is a sharp difference between two things.
english_example: The contrast between my parents is very noticeable.
spanish_meaning: Un contraste es una gran diferencia entre dos cosas.
spanish_example: El contraste entre mis padres es muy evidente.
spanish_meaning_en: A contrast is a sharp difference between two things.
spanish_example_en: The contrast between my parents is very noticeable.

english_meaning: To encourage people is to make them want to do something.
english_example: My football coach will encourage us when we are losing.
spanish_meaning: Animar a la gente es hacer que quiera hacer algo.
spanish_example: Nuestro entrenador de fútbol americano nos anima cuando vamos perdiendo.
spanish_meaning_en: To encourage people is to make them want to do something.
spanish_example_en: My football coach will encourage us when we are losing.

english_meaning: People or things familiar to you are those you know well.
english_example: The two friends were very familiar with each other.
spanish_meaning: Algo o alguien familiar para ti es alguien que conoces bien.
spanish_example: Los dos amigos se conocían muy bien entre sí.
spanish_meaning_en: People or things familiar to you are those you know well.
spanish_example_en: The two friends were very familiar with each other.

english_meaning: To grab is to take a hold of someone or something suddenly.
english_example: I grabbed a pear from the tree.
spanish_meaning: Agarrar es tomar la posesión de alguien o algo de forma repentina.
spanish_example: Agarré una pera del árbol.
spanish_meaning_en: To grab is to take a hold of someone or something suddenly.
spanish_example_en: I grabbed a pear from the tree.

english_meaning: To hang something is to keep it above the ground.
english_example: I drew a picture of my family, and my mother hung it on the wall.
spanish_meaning: Colgar algo es mantenerlo por encima del suelo.
spanish_example: Dibujé un retrato de mi familia y mi madre lo colgó en la pared.
spanish_meaning_en: To hang something is to keep it above the ground.
spanish_example_en: I drew a picture of my family, and my mother hung it on the wall.

english_meaning: If something is huge, it is very big.
english_example: At work, my father drives a huge truck.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es enorme, es muy grande.
spanish_example: En el trabajo, mi padre conduce un camión enorme.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is huge, it is very big.
spanish_example_en: At work, my father drives a huge truck.

english_meaning: If something is necessary, you must do it.
english_example: A passport is necessary if you travel to other countries.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es necesario, debes hacerla.
spanish_example: Un pasaporte es necesario si viajas a otros países.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is necessary, you must do it.
spanish_example_en: A passport is necessary if you travel to other countries.

english_meaning: A pattern is a way in which something is done or organized.
english_example: My pattern of brushing my teeth is the same as most people’s.
spanish_meaning: Un patrón es una manera en que se hace o se organiza algo.
spanish_example: Mi patrón de cepillarme los dientes es el mismo que el de la mayoría de la gente.
spanish_meaning_en: A pattern is a way in which something is done or organized.
spanish_example_en: My pattern of brushing my teeth is the same as most people’s.

english_meaning: To propose something is to say that it should be done.
english_example: My grandmother proposed that we have our picture taken with Santa.
spanish_meaning: Proponer algo significa decir que eso debería hacerse.
spanish_example: Mi abuela propuso que nos tomáramos una foto con Santa.
spanish_meaning_en: To propose something is to say that it should be done.
spanish_example_en: My grandmother proposed that we have our picture taken with Santa.

english_meaning: A purpose is the reason that you do something.
english_example: The purpose of exercising is to get into shape.
spanish_meaning: El propósito de algo es la razón por la que lo haces.
spanish_example: El propósito de hacer ejercicio es ponerse en forma.
spanish_meaning_en: A purpose is the reason that you do something.
spanish_example_en: The purpose of exercising is to get into shape.

english_meaning: To release something is to stop holding it.
english_example: She released the bird from her hands.
spanish_meaning: Liberar algo significa dejar de sostenerlo.
spanish_example: Ella soltó al pájaro de sus manos.
spanish_meaning_en: To release something is to stop holding it.
spanish_example_en: She released the bird from her hands.

english_meaning: To require something is to say that it is necessary.
english_example: We require teachers to have a university degree.
spanish_meaning: Requerir algo significa decir que algo es necesario.
spanish_example: Requerimos que los profesores tengan un título universitario.
spanish_meaning_en: To require something is to say that it is necessary.
spanish_example_en: We require teachers to have a university degree.

english_meaning: You are satisfied when you have what you wanted.
english_example: I am very satisfied with your work.
spanish_meaning: Estás satisfecho cuando tienes lo que quieres.
spanish_example: Estoy muy satisfecho con tu trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: You are satisfied when you have what you wanted.
spanish_example_en: I am very satisfied with your work.

english_meaning: If something is single, then there is only one.
english_example: I have a single key in my hand.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es único, entonces hay solo uno.
spanish_example: Tengo una sola llave en la mano.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is single, then there is only one.
spanish_example_en: I have a single key in my hand.

english_meaning: To tear something means to pull it apart.
english_example: It is easy to tear paper.
spanish_meaning: Rasgar algo significa separarlo tirándolo.
spanish_example: Es fácil rasgar papel.
spanish_meaning_en: To tear something means to pull it apart.
spanish_example_en: It is easy to tear paper.

english_meaning: A theory is an idea about how something works.
english_example: We talked about Einstein’s theory of relativity in class.
spanish_meaning: Una teoría es una idea sobre cómo funciona algo.
spanish_example: En clase hablamos sobre la teoría de la relatividad de Einstein.
spanish_meaning_en: A theory is an idea about how something works.
spanish_example_en: We talked about Einstein’s theory of relativity in class.

english_meaning: An animal is a living thing that can move.
english_example: My favorite animal is the panda.
spanish_meaning: Un animal es un ser vivo que puede moverse.
spanish_example: Mi animal favorito es el panda.
spanish_meaning_en: An animal is a living thing that can move.
spanish_example_en: My favorite animal is the panda.

english_meaning: A bus is a large vehicle that people travel on.
english_example: My father takes the bus to work.
spanish_meaning: Un autobús es un vehículo grande en el que viajan personas.
spanish_example: Mi padre toma el autobús para ir al trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: A bus is a large vehicle that people travel on.
spanish_example_en: My father takes the bus to work.

english_meaning: A cat is a small animal related to lions and tigers that is kept as a pet.
english_example: This cat is playing with a ball.
spanish_meaning: Un gato es un animal pequeño relacionado con leones y tigres que se tiene como mascota.
spanish_example: Este gato está jugando con una pelota.
spanish_meaning_en: A cat is a small animal related to lions and tigers that is kept as a pet.
spanish_example_en: This cat is playing with a ball.

english_meaning: A command is an order given to a person or animal to do something.
english_example: Joe gave the students a command to stand up.
spanish_meaning: Una orden es una instrucción dada a una persona o animal para hacer algo.
spanish_example: Joe dio a los estudiantes una orden de ponerse de pie.
spanish_meaning_en: A command is an order given to a person or animal to do something.
spanish_example_en: Joe gave the students a command to stand up.

english_meaning: To depend on something is to need it for support or help.
english_example: My grandfather depends on a cane when he walks.
spanish_meaning: Depender de algo es necesitarlo para apoyo o ayuda.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo depende de un bastón cuando camina.
spanish_meaning_en: To depend on something is to need it for support or help.
spanish_example_en: My grandfather depends on a cane when he walks.

english_meaning: A dog is an animal with four legs and a tail that is kept as a pet or trained to work.
english_example: This woman’s best friend is her dog.
spanish_meaning: Un perro es un animal de cuatro patas y cola que se tiene como mascota o se entrena para trabajar.
spanish_example: El mejor amigo de esta mujer es su perro.
spanish_meaning_en: A dog is an animal with four legs and a tail that is kept as a pet or trained to work.
spanish_example_en: This woman’s best friend is her dog.

english_meaning: A door is an object that swings or slides open and shut.
english_example: Can you close the door, please?
spanish_meaning: Una puerta es un objeto que se abre o cierra al ir y venir.
spanish_example: ¿Puedes cerrar la puerta, por favor?
spanish_meaning_en: A door is an object that swings or slides open and shut.
spanish_example_en: Can you close the door, please?

english_meaning: A friend is someone a person knows and likes spending time with.
english_example: Brian’s friend is very sad.
spanish_meaning: Un amigo es alguien que una persona conoce y le gusta pasar el tiempo con él.
spanish_example: El amigo de Brian está muy triste.
spanish_meaning_en: A friend is someone a person knows and likes spending time with.
spanish_example_en: Brian’s friend is very sad.

english_meaning: To hear is to be aware of sound through your ears.
english_example: Michelle cannot hear what you are saying.
spanish_meaning: Oír es ser consciente de sonidos a través de tus oídos.
spanish_example: Michelle no puede oír lo que estás diciendo.
spanish_meaning_en: To hear is to be aware of sound through your ears.
spanish_example_en: Michelle cannot hear what you are saying.

english_meaning: To help is to do something that makes something easier for someone else.
english_example: Jane can help Nathan climb up the rock.
spanish_meaning: Ayudar es hacer algo que facilite algo para otra persona.
spanish_example: Jane puede ayudar a Nathan a trepar la roca.
spanish_meaning_en: To help is to do something that makes something easier for someone else.
spanish_example_en: Jane can help Nathan climb up the rock.

english_meaning: A horse is a big, strong animal that people ride and use for pulling heavy things.
english_example: I went to a farm and saw a horse.
spanish_meaning: Un caballo es un animal grande y fuerte que la gente monta y usa para tirar de cosas pesadas.
spanish_example: Fui a una granja y vi un caballo.
spanish_meaning_en: A horse is a big, strong animal that people ride and use for pulling heavy things.
spanish_example_en: I went to a farm and saw a horse.

english_meaning: A hospital is where sick or hurt people receive care or treatment.
english_example: The doctor talks to a patient at the hospital.
spanish_meaning: Un hospital es un lugar donde las personas enfermas o heridas reciben atención o tratamiento.
spanish_example: El doctor habla con un paciente en el hospital.
spanish_meaning_en: A hospital is where sick or hurt people receive care or treatment.
spanish_example_en: The doctor talks to a patient at the hospital.

english_meaning: A leg is a body part used for standing and walking.
english_example: She hurt her leg.
spanish_meaning: Una pierna es una parte del cuerpo usada para mantenerse de pie y caminar.
spanish_example: Ella se lesionó la pierna.
