spanish_meaning_en: A leg is a body part used for standing and walking.
spanish_example_en: She hurt her leg.

english_meaning: Medical means related to the treatment of an injury or disease.
english_example: Tyler is a medical student studying to become a doctor.
spanish_meaning: Médico significa relacionado con el tratamiento de una lesión o enfermedad.
spanish_example: Tyler es un estudiante de medicina que estudia para ser médico.
notes: Como adjetivo, también puede usarse «médica/médico». No confundir con «médico» como sustantivo «doctor».
spanish_meaning_en: Medical means related to the treatment of an injury or disease.
spanish_example_en: Tyler is a medical student studying to become a doctor.

english_meaning: To open is to move something so that an opening is not covered.
english_example: Amy likes to open the window to let fresh air in.
spanish_meaning: Abrir es mover algo para que una abertura no esté cubierta.
spanish_example: A Amy le gusta abrir la ventana para dejar entrar aire fresco.
spanish_meaning_en: To open is to move something so that an opening is not covered.
spanish_example_en: Amy likes to open the window to let fresh air in.

english_meaning: To pull is to hold onto something to move it toward you.
english_example: The children pull the rope.
spanish_meaning: Tirar es sujetar algo para moverlo hacia ti.
spanish_example: Los niños tiran de la cuerda.
spanish_meaning_en: To pull is to hold onto something to move it toward you.
spanish_example_en: The children pull the rope.

english_meaning: A rabbit is a small animal with long ears that lives in a hole in the ground.
english_example: Look at the rabbit in the park.
spanish_meaning: Un conejo es un animal pequeño con orejas largas que vive en un agujero en el suelo.
spanish_example: Mira el conejo en el parque.
spanish_meaning_en: A rabbit is a small animal with long ears that lives in a hole in the ground.
spanish_example_en: Look at the rabbit in the park.

english_meaning: A school is a place where children go to learn.
english_example: The students study at school.
spanish_meaning: Una escuela es un lugar donde los niños van a aprender.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes estudian en la escuela.
spanish_meaning_en: A school is a place where children go to learn.
spanish_example_en: The students study at school.

english_meaning: To see is to use your eyes to look at something or someone.
english_example: Steven does not want to see the scary picture.
spanish_meaning: Ver es usar los ojos para mirar algo o alguien.
spanish_example: Steven no quiere ver la imagen aterradora.
spanish_meaning_en: To see is to use your eyes to look at something or someone.
spanish_example_en: Steven does not want to see the scary picture.

english_meaning: Service is the act of helping or serving someone.
english_example: This coffee shop has excellent service.
spanish_meaning: El servicio es el acto de ayudar o servir a alguien.
spanish_example: Esta cafetería tiene un excelente servicio.
spanish_meaning_en: Service is the act of helping or serving someone.
spanish_example_en: This coffee shop has excellent service.

english_meaning: A benefit is a good thing.
english_example: Being able to fly is a benefit to birds.
spanish_meaning: Un beneficio es algo bueno.
spanish_example: Poder volar es un gran beneficio para las aves.
spanish_meaning_en: A benefit is a good thing.
spanish_example_en: Being able to fly is a benefit to birds.

english_meaning: If you are certain about something, you know it is true.
english_example: I am certain that zebras have stripes.
spanish_meaning: Si estás seguro de algo, sabes que es verdadero.
spanish_example: Estoy seguro de que las cebras tienen rayas.
spanish_meaning_en: If you are certain about something, you know it is true.
spanish_example_en: I am certain that zebras have stripes.

english_meaning: A chance is an opportunity to do something.
english_example: I had a chance to see the Coliseum in Rome last summer.
spanish_meaning: Una oportunidad es una ocasión para hacer algo.
spanish_example: Tuve una oportunidad de ver el Coliseo de Roma el verano pasado.
spanish_meaning_en: A chance is an opportunity to do something.
spanish_example_en: I had a chance to see the Coliseum in Rome last summer.

english_meaning: An effect is a change made by something else.
english_example: The medicine had a good effect on the boy.
spanish_meaning: Un efecto es un cambio provocado por algo más.
spanish_example: La medicina tuvo un buen efecto en el niño.
spanish_meaning_en: An effect is a change made by something else.
spanish_example_en: The medicine had a good effect on the boy.

english_meaning: If something is essential, it is very important and necessary.
english_example: It is essential to have oxygen when you scuba dive.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es esencial, es muy importante y necesario.
spanish_example: Es esencial tener oxígeno cuando buceas con escafandra.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is essential, it is very important and necessary.
spanish_example_en: It is essential to have oxygen when you scuba dive.

english_meaning: If something is far, it is not close.
english_example: It’s far from the east coast to the west coast of America.
spanish_meaning: Si algo está lejos, no está cerca.
spanish_example: Es muy lejos de la costa este a la costa oeste de Estados Unidos.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is far, it is not close.
spanish_example_en: It’s far from the east coast to the west coast of America.

english_meaning: To focus on something is to think about it and pay attention to it.
english_example: My mom always helps me to focus on my school work.
spanish_meaning: Centrarse en algo es pensar en ello y prestarle atención.
spanish_example: Mi madre siempre me ayuda a concentrarme en mis deberes escolares.
spanish_meaning_en: To focus on something is to think about it and pay attention to it.
spanish_example_en: My mom always helps me to focus on my school work.

english_meaning: The function of something is what it does.
english_example: The function of a flashlight is to help you see in the dark.
spanish_meaning: La función de algo es lo que hace.
spanish_example: La función de una linterna es ayudarte a ver en la oscuridad.
spanish_meaning_en: The function of something is what it does.
spanish_example_en: The function of a flashlight is to help you see in the dark.

english_meaning: Grass is the green leaves that cover the ground.
english_example: The grass looked so soft and green.
spanish_meaning: La hierba son las hojas verdes que cubren el suelo.
spanish_example: La hierba se veía muy suave y verde.
spanish_meaning_en: Grass is the green leaves that cover the ground.
spanish_example_en: The grass looked so soft and green.

english_meaning: To guard something is to take care of it.
english_example: The police officer will guard us from any harm.
spanish_meaning: Cuidar algo significa encargarse de él.
spanish_example: El policía nos cuidará de cualquier daño.
spanish_meaning_en: To guard something is to take care of it.
spanish_example_en: The police officer will guard us from any harm.

english_meaning: The image of something is a picture of it.
english_example: The image of her eye was very clear.
spanish_meaning: La imagen de algo es una representación o imagen de ello.
spanish_example: La imagen de su ojo estaba muy clara.
spanish_meaning_en: The image of something is a picture of it.
spanish_example_en: The image of her eye was very clear.

english_meaning: If something is immediate, it happens quickly.
english_example: An immediate response came from the pizza place.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es inmediato, sucede rápidamente.
spanish_example: Una respuesta inmediata llegó de la pizzería.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is immediate, it happens quickly.
spanish_example_en: An immediate response came from the pizza place.

english_meaning: If something is primary, it is the most important thing.
english_example: His primary thoughts are about money.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es primordial, es la cosa más importante.
spanish_example: Sus pensamientos principales son sobre el dinero.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is primary, it is the most important thing.
spanish_example_en: His primary thoughts are about money.

english_meaning: People who feel proud are happy about what they have done.
english_example: She is proud of the picture she drew of her house.
spanish_meaning: La gente que se siente orgullosa se alegra por lo que ha hecho.
spanish_example: Ella está orgullosa del dibujo que hizo de su casa.
spanish_meaning_en: People who feel proud are happy about what they have done.
spanish_example_en: She is proud of the picture she drew of her house.

english_meaning: To remain somewhere is to stay there.
english_example: My sister had to remain home since she was sick.
spanish_meaning: Permanecer en algún lugar es quedarse allí.
spanish_example: Mi hermana tuvo que quedarse en casa desde que estaba enferma.
spanish_meaning_en: To remain somewhere is to stay there.
spanish_example_en: My sister had to remain home since she was sick.

english_meaning: To rest is to stop being active while the body gets back its strength.
english_example: I rested on the couch after work.
spanish_meaning: Descansar es dejar de estar activo mientras el cuerpo recupera fuerzas.
spanish_example: Descansé en el sofá después del trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: To rest is to stop being active while the body gets back its strength.
spanish_example_en: I rested on the couch after work.

english_meaning: If two things are separate, they are not together.
english_example: New York and Los Angeles are in two separate parts of America.
spanish_meaning: Si dos cosas están separadas, no están juntas.
spanish_example: Nueva York y Los Ángeles están en dos partes separadas de Estados Unidos.
spanish_meaning_en: If two things are separate, they are not together.
spanish_example_en: New York and Los Angeles are in two separate parts of America.

english_meaning: A site is a place.
english_example: We found the perfect site for our picnic.
spanish_meaning: Un sitio es un lugar.
spanish_example: Encontramos el lugar perfecto para nuestro picnic.
spanish_meaning_en: A site is a place.
spanish_example_en: We found the perfect site for our picnic.

english_meaning: A tail is a part of an animal’s body that sticks out from its rear or back.
english_example: Our dog wags its tail when it’s happy.
spanish_meaning: La cola es una parte del cuerpo de un animal que sobresale por detrás o en la espalda.
spanish_example: Nuestro perro mueve la cola cuando está feliz.
spanish_meaning_en: A tail is a part of an animal’s body that sticks out from its rear or back.
spanish_example_en: Our dog wags its tail when it’s happy.

english_meaning: Trouble is a problem or a difficulty.
english_example: I have trouble working with my boss.
spanish_meaning: Un problema es una dificultad o situación que causa inconvenientes.
spanish_example: Tengo problemas para trabajar con mi jefe.
spanish_meaning_en: Trouble is a problem or a difficulty.
spanish_example_en: I have trouble working with my boss.

english_meaning: To advertise is to describe or draw attention to a product or event by using a public medium.
english_example: The woman started a new company and needed to advertise her products.
spanish_meaning: Dar a conocer algo para llamar la atención pública.
spanish_example: La mujer creó una empresa y necesitaba anunciar sus productos.
spanish_meaning_en: To advertise is to describe or draw attention to a product or event by using a public medium.
spanish_example_en: The woman started a new company and needed to advertise her products.

english_meaning: If someone is aware, they know that something or a situation exists.
english_example: The student became aware that the teacher was watching him.
spanish_meaning: Saber que algo existe o que pasa.
spanish_example: El estudiante se dio cuenta de que el profesor lo estaba mirando.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is aware, they know that something or a situation exists.
spanish_example_en: The student became aware that the teacher was watching him.

english_meaning: A battery is an object placed inside something to supply it with electricity.
english_example: My brother needs a battery for his clock.
spanish_meaning: Objeto que proporciona electricidad.
spanish_example: Mi hermano necesita una batería para su reloj.
spanish_meaning_en: A battery is an object placed inside something to supply it with electricity.
spanish_example_en: My brother needs a battery for his clock.

english_meaning: Black is the darkest color.
english_example: Our street is black at night because there are no street lights.
spanish_meaning: Color más oscuro.
spanish_example: Nuestra calle está oscura de noche porque no hay farolas.
spanish_meaning_en: Black is the darkest color.
spanish_example_en: Our street is black at night because there are no street lights.

english_meaning: A city is a place where a lot of people live.
english_example: Tokyo is a very big city in Japan.
spanish_meaning: Lugar con mucha población.
spanish_example: Tokio es una ciudad muy grande de Japón.
spanish_meaning_en: A city is a place where a lot of people live.
spanish_example_en: Tokyo is a very big city in Japan.

english_meaning: To clean is to make something neat and tidy.
english_example: I clean our kitchen every Saturday.
spanish_meaning: Quitar suciedad para dejar algo ordenado.
spanish_example: Limpio nuestra cocina todos los sábados.
spanish_meaning_en: To clean is to make something neat and tidy.
spanish_example_en: I clean our kitchen every Saturday.

english_meaning: A country is an area of land with the same government and laws.
english_example: France is a country in Europe.
spanish_meaning: Zona de tierra con gobierno y leyes propias.
spanish_example: Francia es un país de Europa.
spanish_meaning_en: A country is an area of land with the same government and laws.
spanish_example_en: France is a country in Europe.

english_meaning: To develop is to make something larger or more advanced.
english_example: This practice will help you develop your math skills.
spanish_meaning: Hacer algo más grande o avanzado.
spanish_example: Esta práctica te ayudará a desarrollar tus habilidades matemáticas.
spanish_meaning_en: To develop is to make something larger or more advanced.
spanish_example_en: This practice will help you develop your math skills.

english_meaning: If something is electric, it uses electricity.
english_example: Some car companies make electric cars.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con la electricidad.
spanish_example: Algunas marcas hacen autos eléctricos.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is electric, it uses electricity.
spanish_example_en: Some car companies make electric cars.

english_meaning: When something happens at a later time or in the end, it happens eventually.
english_example: He will eventually move to London to find a new job.
spanish_meaning: Al final o en un momento posterior.
spanish_example: Finalmente, se mudará a Londres para buscar un nuevo trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: When something happens at a later time or in the end, it happens eventually.
spanish_example_en: He will eventually move to London to find a new job.

english_meaning: A fact is a piece of information that is true.
english_example: A fact about South America is that it is a continent.
spanish_meaning: Información o dato verdadero.
spanish_example: Un hecho sobre Sudamérica es que es un continente.
spanish_meaning_en: A fact is a piece of information that is true.
spanish_example_en: A fact about South America is that it is a continent.

english_meaning: Glass is a transparent, breakable material.
english_example: Windows are made of glass.
spanish_meaning: Material transparente y frágil.
spanish_example: Las ventanas están hechas de vidrio.
spanish_meaning_en: Glass is a transparent, breakable material.
spanish_example_en: Windows are made of glass.

english_meaning: History is the study of the past.
english_example: History was my favorite subject in school.
spanish_meaning: Estudio o relato del pasado.
spanish_example: La historia fue mi asignatura favorita en la escuela.
spanish_meaning_en: History is the study of the past.
spanish_example_en: History was my favorite subject in school.

english_meaning: Nature is everything in the physical world that is not made by people.
english_example: On the weekends, I love to walk in nature among the trees.
spanish_meaning: Todo lo físico del mundo no hecho por humanos.
spanish_example: Los fines de semana me encanta caminar por la naturaleza.
spanish_meaning_en: Nature is everything in the physical world that is not made by people.
spanish_example_en: On the weekends, I love to walk in nature among the trees.

english_meaning: Never is when something is impossible or will not happen.
english_example: They never eat meat because they are vegetarians.
spanish_meaning: No ha ocurrido y no ocurre hasta ahora.
spanish_example: Nunca comen carne porque son vegetarianos.
spanish_meaning_en: Never is when something is impossible or will not happen.
spanish_example_en: They never eat meat because they are vegetarians.

english_meaning: People are humans.
english_example: How many people live in China?
spanish_meaning: Seres humanos.
spanish_example: ¿Cuántas personas viven en China?
spanish_meaning_en: People are humans.
spanish_example_en: How many people live in China?

english_meaning: Plastic is a material made by people.
english_example: Many drinks are sold in plastic bottles.
spanish_meaning: Material sintético fabricado por personas.
spanish_example: Muchas bebidas se venden en botellas de plástico.
spanish_meaning_en: Plastic is a material made by people.
spanish_example_en: Many drinks are sold in plastic bottles.

english_meaning: A problem is a situation when something goes wrong.
english_example: My problem is that I lost my dog.
spanish_meaning: Situación en la que algo sale mal.
spanish_example: Mi problema es que perdí a mi perro.
spanish_meaning_en: A problem is a situation when something goes wrong.
spanish_example_en: My problem is that I lost my dog.

english_meaning: A street is a road in a city or village.
english_example: What is the name of the street you live on?
spanish_meaning: Camino dentro de una ciudad o pueblo.
spanish_example: ¿Cómo se llama la calle donde vives?
spanish_meaning_en: A street is a road in a city or village.
spanish_example_en: What is the name of the street you live on?

english_meaning: To think is to have an opinion or thought about something.
english_example: I think summer is the best season.
spanish_meaning: Tener una opinión o idea sobre algo.
spanish_example: Pienso que el verano es la mejor estación.
spanish_meaning_en: To think is to have an opinion or thought about something.
spanish_example_en: I think summer is the best season.

english_meaning: A person who is alone is not with another person.
english_example: The boy wanted to be alone to think.
spanish_meaning: Sin compañía.
spanish_example: El niño quería estar solo para pensar.
spanish_meaning_en: A person who is alone is not with another person.
spanish_example_en: The boy wanted to be alone to think.

english_meaning: An apartment is a set of rooms in a building where people live.
english_example: She has a nice apartment in the city.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de habitaciones en un edificio donde vive gente.
spanish_example: Ella tiene un buen apartamento en la ciudad.
spanish_meaning_en: An apartment is a set of rooms in a building where people live.
spanish_example_en: She has a nice apartment in the city.

english_meaning: An article is a story in a newspaper or magazine.
english_example: Did you read the article in the newspaper about the soccer game?
spanish_meaning: Texto informativo en un periódico o revista.
spanish_example: ¿Leíste el artículo del periódico sobre el partido de fútbol?
spanish_meaning_en: An article is a story in a newspaper or magazine.
spanish_example_en: Did you read the article in the newspaper about the soccer game?

english_meaning: An artist is a person who paints, draws, or makes sculptures.
english_example: He went to Paris to become an artist.
spanish_meaning: Persona que pinta, dibuja o esculpe.
spanish_example: Se fue a París para convertirse en artista.
spanish_meaning_en: An artist is a person who paints, draws, or makes sculptures.
spanish_example_en: He went to Paris to become an artist.

english_meaning: Someone’s attitude is the way they feel and think.
english_example: John has a bad attitude. He’s always angry.
spanish_meaning: Forma de pensar o de sentirse de alguien.
spanish_example: John tiene una mala actitud. Siempre está enojado.
spanish_meaning_en: Someone’s attitude is the way they feel and think.
spanish_example_en: John has a bad attitude. He’s always angry.

english_meaning: Beauty is the state or quality of being beautiful.
english_example: I heard my mother was a great beauty when she was young.
spanish_meaning: Cualidad de ser o parecer bello.
spanish_example: Escuché que mi madre era muy bella cuando era joven.
spanish_meaning_en: Beauty is the state or quality of being beautiful.
spanish_example_en: I heard my mother was a great beauty when she was young.

english_meaning: To compare means to say how two things are the same and different.
english_example: If you compare cats and dogs, you’ll see that they’re both good pets.
spanish_meaning: Decir en qué se parecen y se diferencian dos cosas.
spanish_example: Si comparas gatos y perros, verás que ambos son buenas mascotas.
spanish_meaning_en: To compare means to say how two things are the same and different.
spanish_example_en: If you compare cats and dogs, you’ll see that they’re both good pets.

english_meaning: To judge something is to say if it is good or bad.
english_example: The boy was going to judge how his mother’s turkey tasted.
spanish_meaning: Decidir si algo es bueno o malo.
spanish_example: El niño iba a juzgar cómo sabía el pavo de su madre.
spanish_meaning_en: To judge something is to say if it is good or bad.
spanish_example_en: The boy was going to judge how his mother’s turkey tasted.

english_meaning: A magazine is a regular publication with news, stories, and articles.
english_example: She likes to read fashion magazines.
spanish_meaning: Publicación periódica con noticias y artículos.
spanish_example: Le gusta leer revistas de moda.
spanish_meaning_en: A magazine is a regular publication with news, stories, and articles.
spanish_example_en: She likes to read fashion magazines.

english_meaning: A material is what is used to make something.
english_example: Brick is a good material for building houses.
spanish_meaning: Lo que se usa para fabricar algo.
spanish_example: El ladrillo es un buen material para construir casas.
spanish_meaning_en: A material is what is used to make something.
spanish_example_en: Brick is a good material for building houses.

english_meaning: A meal is a time when food is eaten, such as breakfast, lunch, or dinner.
english_example: Breakfast is my favorite meal because I enjoy cereal.
spanish_meaning: Momento en que se come (desayuno, almuerzo o cena).
spanish_example: El desayuno es mi comida favorita porque me gusta el cereal.
spanish_meaning_en: A meal is a time when food is eaten, such as breakfast, lunch, or dinner.
spanish_example_en: Breakfast is my favorite meal because I enjoy cereal.

english_meaning: A method is the way to do something.
english_example: One method to remember things is to tie a string around your finger.
spanish_meaning: Manera de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Un método para recordar cosas es atar un hilo en el dedo.
spanish_meaning_en: A method is the way to do something.
spanish_example_en: One method to remember things is to tie a string around your finger.

english_meaning: A neighbor is a person who lives near you.
english_example: I like my neighbor because he’s very friendly.
spanish_meaning: Persona que vive cerca de ti.
spanish_example: Me gusta mi vecino porque es muy amable.
spanish_meaning_en: A neighbor is a person who lives near you.
spanish_example_en: I like my neighbor because he’s very friendly.

english_meaning: If something is professional, it deals with work that uses special skills.
english_example: If you want to be a pilot, you must have professional training.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con un trabajo que requiere habilidades especiales.
spanish_example: Si quieres ser piloto, necesitas formación profesional.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is professional, it deals with work that uses special skills.
spanish_example_en: If you want to be a pilot, you must have professional training.

english_meaning: A profit is the extra money you make when you sell something.
english_example: I made a small profit from selling my old clothes.
spanish_meaning: Dinero extra que se obtiene al vender algo.
spanish_example: Saqué una pequeña ganancia vendiendo mi ropa vieja.
spanish_meaning_en: A profit is the extra money you make when you sell something.
spanish_example_en: I made a small profit from selling my old clothes.

english_meaning: The quality of something is how good it is.
english_example: The quality of his car is very good.
spanish_meaning: Qué tan bueno es algo.
spanish_example: La calidad de su coche es muy buena.
spanish_meaning_en: The quality of something is how good it is.
spanish_example_en: The quality of his car is very good.

english_meaning: A space is an empty area.
english_example: I don’t have much space for things in my small house.
spanish_meaning: Área vacía o lugar libre.
spanish_example: No tengo mucho espacio para las cosas en mi casa pequeña.
spanish_meaning_en: A space is an empty area.
spanish_example_en: I don’t have much space for things in my small house.

english_meaning: Stairs are the things that are used to walk up or down in a building.
english_example: You can take the stairs to the second floor.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de peldaños para subir o bajar en un edificio.
spanish_example: Puedes tomar las escaleras hasta el segundo piso.
spanish_meaning_en: Stairs are the things that are used to walk up or down in a building.
spanish_example_en: You can take the stairs to the second floor.

english_meaning: A symbol is a thing that stands for something else.

spanish_meaning: Objeto o signo que representa otra cosa.
spanish_example: Este símbolo nos indica que aquí no se puede fumar.
spanish_meaning_en: A symbol is a thing that stands for something else.
spanish_example_en: This symbol tells us that we cannot smoke in this area.

english_meaning: If someone or something is thin, they are not fat.

spanish_meaning: No grueso ni con mucha grasa.
spanish_example: El hombre era delgado porque comía poco.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone or something is thin, they are not fat.
spanish_example_en: The man was thin because he didn’t eat much.

english_meaning: People study accounting so they can deal with money and finance properly.

spanish_meaning: Estudio para llevar y controlar el dinero.
spanish_example: Estudia contabilidad en una empresa.
spanish_meaning_en: People study accounting so they can deal with money and finance properly.
spanish_example_en: He works for an accounting firm.

english_meaning: To appeal to someone is to be interesting or attractive.
english_example: Sleeping all day appeals to me, but I have to go to school.
spanish_meaning: Resultar interesante o atractivo para alguien.
spanish_example: Dormir todo el día me atrae, pero tengo que ir a la escuela.
spanish_meaning_en: To appeal to someone is to be interesting or attractive.
spanish_example_en: Sleeping all day appeals to me, but I have to go to school.

english_meaning: To assume something is to think that it is true, even with no proof.
english_example: I assume you are both familiar with this plan.
spanish_meaning: Creer que algo es cierto sin pruebas.
spanish_example: Supongo que ambos conocen bien este plan.
spanish_meaning_en: To assume something is to think that it is true, even with no proof.
spanish_example_en: I assume you are both familiar with this plan.

english_example: Can I borrow a pencil to use today? I’ll give it back to you tomorrow.
spanish_meaning: Tomar algo de alguien para luego devolverlo.

spanish_meaning: Lo más simple o fundamental.
spanish_example: Hoy aprendí algunas habilidades básicas de inglés en la escuela.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is basic, it is very simple or easy.
spanish_example_en: I learned some basic English skills in school today.

english_meaning: A bit is a small amount of something.
english_example: I ate a bit of chocolate before I went to bed.
spanish_meaning: Una pequeña cantidad.
spanish_example: Comí un poco de chocolate antes de dormir.
spanish_meaning_en: A bit is a small amount of something.
spanish_example_en: I ate a bit of chocolate before I went to bed.

english_meaning: To consider something means to think about it.
english_example: Pete didn’t like his job. He considered getting a new one.
spanish_meaning: Pensar seriamente sobre algo.
spanish_example: Pete no le gustó su trabajo y consideró buscar uno nuevo.
spanish_meaning_en: To consider something means to think about it.
spanish_example_en: Pete didn’t like his job. He considered getting a new one.

english_meaning: To destroy means to damage something so badly that it cannot be used.
english_example: The glass was destroyed.
spanish_meaning: Dañar algo hasta dejarlo inutilizable.
spanish_example: El vaso fue destruido.
spanish_meaning_en: To destroy means to damage something so badly that it cannot be used.
spanish_example_en: The glass was destroyed.

english_meaning: To entertain someone is to do something that they enjoy.
english_example: The clown entertained the kids at the party.
spanish_meaning: Divertir o hacer pasar un buen rato.
spanish_example: El payaso entretuvo a los niños en la fiesta.
spanish_meaning_en: To entertain someone is to do something that they enjoy.
spanish_example_en: The clown entertained the kids at the party.

english_meaning: If something is extra, it is more than what is needed.
english_example: The squirrel had extra nuts for the winter.
spanish_meaning: Cantidad superior a lo necesario.
spanish_example: La ardilla tenía nueces extra para el invierno.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is extra, it is more than what is needed.
spanish_example_en: The squirrel had extra nuts for the winter.

english_meaning: A goal is something you work toward.
english_example: Her goal was to become a doctor.
spanish_meaning: Meta o propósito al que se apunta.
spanish_example: Su objetivo era convertirse en médico.
spanish_meaning_en: A goal is something you work toward.
spanish_example_en: Her goal was to become a doctor.

english_meaning: To lie is to say or write something untrue to deceive someone.
english_example: Whenever Pinocchio lied to his father, his nose grew.
spanish_meaning: Decir una mentira.
spanish_example: Cada vez que Pinocchio mentía, su nariz crecía.
spanish_meaning_en: To lie is to say or write something untrue to deceive someone.
spanish_example_en: Whenever Pinocchio lied to his father, his nose grew.

english_meaning: Meat is food made of animals.
english_example: This piece of meat I’m eating tastes very good.
spanish_meaning: Alimento de origen animal.
spanish_example: Este trozo de carne que estoy comiendo sabe muy bien.
spanish_meaning_en: Meat is food made of animals.
spanish_example_en: This piece of meat I’m eating tastes very good.

english_meaning: An opinion is a thought about a person or a thing.
english_example: Meg told me her opinion of my story. She said it was not funny.
spanish_meaning: Juicio personal sobre algo.
spanish_example: Meg me dijo su opinión de mi historia y dijo que no era graciosa.
spanish_meaning_en: An opinion is a thought about a person or a thing.
spanish_example_en: Meg told me her opinion of my story. She said it was not funny.

english_meaning: If something is real, it actually exists.
english_example: Some people don’t believe in aliens from space, but I think they’re real.
spanish_meaning: Que existe de verdad.
spanish_example: Algunas personas no creen en extraterrestres, pero yo creo que son reales.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is real, it actually exists.
spanish_example_en: Some people don’t believe in aliens from space, but I think they’re real.

english_meaning: To reflect is when a surface sends back light, heat, sound or an image.
english_example: Her face was reflected in the smooth glass.
spanish_meaning: Devolver luz, sonido o imagen.
spanish_example: Su cara se reflejó en el vidrio liso.
spanish_meaning_en: To reflect is when a surface sends back light, heat, sound or an image.
spanish_example_en: Her face was reflected in the smooth glass.

english_meaning: To regard people is to think of them in a certain way.
english_example: The boy regarded the girl as a good friend.
spanish_meaning: Pensar de alguien de cierta manera.
spanish_example: El niño consideraba a la niña como una buena amiga.
spanish_meaning_en: To regard people is to think of them in a certain way.
spanish_example_en: The boy regarded the girl as a good friend.

english_meaning: To serve someone is to give them food or drinks.
english_example: He served us our drinks quickly.
spanish_meaning: Entregar comida o bebida a alguien.
spanish_example: Nos sirvió las bebidas rápidamente.
spanish_meaning_en: To serve someone is to give them food or drinks.
spanish_example_en: He served us our drinks quickly.

english_meaning: A vegetable is a plant used as food.
english_example: Carrots are my favorite vegetable.
spanish_meaning: Planta comestible no dulce usada para comer.
spanish_example: Las zanahorias son mi verdura favorita.
spanish_meaning_en: A vegetable is a plant used as food.
spanish_example_en: Carrots are my favorite vegetable.

english_meaning: A war is a big fight between two groups of people.
english_example: Many young men died in the war.
spanish_meaning: Conflicto armado entre grupos grandes.
spanish_example: Muchos jóvenes murieron en la guerra.
spanish_meaning_en: A war is a big fight between two groups of people.
spanish_example_en: Many young men died in the war.

english_meaning: If something is worth an amount of money, it costs that amount.
english_example: Our house is worth a lot of money.
spanish_meaning: Tener un precio o valor.
spanish_example: Nuestra casa vale mucho dinero.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is worth an amount of money, it costs that amount.
spanish_example_en: Our house is worth a lot of money.

english_meaning: To appear is to seem.
english_example: She appeared to be sad. She was crying.
spanish_meaning: Darse la impresión de ser algo.
spanish_example: Parecía estar triste; estaba llorando.
spanish_meaning_en: To appear is to seem.
spanish_example_en: She appeared to be sad. She was crying.

english_meaning: The base is the bottom of something.
english_example: The base of the table has three legs.
spanish_meaning: Parte inferior o fundamento.
spanish_example: La base de la mesa tiene tres patas.
spanish_meaning_en: The base is the bottom of something.
spanish_example_en: The base of the table has three legs.

english_meaning: The brain is the organ in your head that lets you think.
english_example: You must use your brain to solve the problem.
spanish_meaning: Órgano cerebral donde se piensa.
spanish_example: Debes usar el cerebro para resolver el problema.
spanish_meaning_en: The brain is the organ in your head that lets you think.
spanish_example_en: You must use your brain to solve the problem.

english_meaning: A career is a job that you do for a large part of your life.
english_example: He was in the hospitality business for most of his career.
spanish_meaning: Trayectoria profesional prolongada.
spanish_example: Estuvo en el sector hotelero durante la mayor parte de su carrera.
spanish_meaning_en: A career is a job that you do for a large part of your life.
spanish_example_en: He was in the hospitality business for most of his career.

english_meaning: A clerk is a type of worker. Clerks in a store help customers.
english_example: The clerk added up her bill for the groceries.
spanish_meaning: Empleado que atiende al público en una tienda/oficina.
spanish_example: El cajero sumó su cuenta para las compras de comestibles.
spanish_meaning_en: A clerk is a type of worker. Clerks in a store help customers.
spanish_example_en: The clerk added up her bill for the groceries.

english_meaning: Effort is hard work or an attempt to do something.
english_example: He always puts a lot of effort into his studies.
spanish_meaning: Energía física o mental puesta para lograr algo.
spanish_example: Él siempre pone mucho esfuerzo en sus estudios.
spanish_meaning_en: Effort is hard work or an attempt to do something.
spanish_example_en: He always puts a lot of effort into his studies.

english_meaning: To enter a place is to go into it.
english_example: Two guards greeted me as I entered the front door.
spanish_meaning: Pasar al interior de un lugar.
spanish_example: Dos guardias me saludaron cuando entré por la puerta principal.
spanish_meaning_en: To enter a place is to go into it.
spanish_example_en: Two guards greeted me as I entered the front door.

english_meaning: When something is excellent, it is very good.
english_example: I got an excellent score on my school test.
spanish_meaning: De muy buena calidad.
spanish_example: Saqué una puntuación excelente en mi examen escolar.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is excellent, it is very good.
spanish_example_en: I got an excellent score on my school test.

english_meaning: A hero is a brave person who does things to help others.
english_example: To children, the man in the blue and red costume was a real hero.
spanish_meaning: Persona valiente que ayuda a otros.
spanish_example: Para los niños, el hombre con traje azul y rojo era un héroe de verdad.
spanish_meaning_en: A hero is a brave person who does things to help others.
spanish_example_en: To children, the man in the blue and red costume was a real hero.

english_meaning: To hurry is to do something quickly.
english_example: I hurried home on my bike.
spanish_meaning: Hacer algo con prisa.
spanish_example: Me apresuré a casa en bicicleta.
spanish_meaning_en: To hurry is to do something quickly.
spanish_example_en: I hurried home on my bike.

english_meaning: To inform someone is to tell them about something.
english_example: I called and informed her about my idea.
spanish_meaning: Comunicar información a alguien.
spanish_example: Llamé e informé a ella sobre mi idea.
spanish_meaning_en: To inform someone is to tell them about something.
spanish_example_en: I called and informed her about my idea.

english_meaning: Later means after the present, expected, or usual time.
english_example: She missed the train, so she’ll arrive a little later than expected.
spanish_meaning: En un momento posterior.
spanish_example: Perdió el tren, así que llegará un poco más tarde de lo esperado.
spanish_meaning_en: Later means after the present, expected, or usual time.
spanish_example_en: She missed the train, so she’ll arrive a little later than expected.

english_meaning: To leave means to go away from someone or something.
english_example: He packed his bag and was ready to leave for home.
spanish_meaning: Salir o partir de un lugar.
spanish_example: Empacó su bolsa y estuvo listo para irse a casa.
spanish_meaning_en: To leave means to go away from someone or something.
spanish_example_en: He packed his bag and was ready to leave for home.

english_meaning: To locate something is to find it.
english_example: I could not locate my keys in the house.
spanish_meaning: Encontrar o situar algo.
spanish_example: No pude localizar mis llaves en la casa.
spanish_meaning_en: To locate something is to find it.
spanish_example_en: I could not locate my keys in the house.

english_meaning: A nurse is a person who helps sick people in the hospital.
english_example: A nurse helped me get better.
spanish_meaning: Profesional de salud que cuida pacientes.
spanish_example: Una enfermera me ayudó a recuperarme.
spanish_meaning_en: A nurse is a person who helps sick people in the hospital.
spanish_example_en: A nurse helped me get better.

english_meaning: An operation is when a doctor replaces or removes something in the body.
english_example: The operation on my arm was a success.
spanish_meaning: Intervención médica quirúrgica.
spanish_example: La operación de mi brazo fue un éxito.
spanish_meaning_en: An operation is when a doctor replaces or removes something in the body.
spanish_example_en: The operation on my arm was a success.

english_meaning: Pain is the feeling that you have when you are hurt.
english_example: His head was full of pain.
spanish_meaning: Sensación de molestia o sufrimiento físico.
spanish_example: Tenía dolor de cabeza.
spanish_meaning_en: Pain is the feeling that you have when you are hurt.
spanish_example_en: His head was full of pain.

english_meaning: To refuse something is to say “no” to it.
english_example: The dog refused to play with the cat.
spanish_meaning: Decir que no; no aceptar.
spanish_example: El perro rechazó jugar con el gato.
spanish_meaning_en: To refuse something is to say “no” to it.
spanish_example_en: The dog refused to play with the cat.

english_meaning: Though is used when one idea makes another seem surprising.
english_example: Though he was overweight, he liked to be active.
spanish_meaning: Conectores: aunque, a pesar de.
spanish_example: Aunque estaba con sobrepeso, le gustaba estar activo.
spanish_meaning_en: Though is used when one idea makes another seem surprising.
spanish_example_en: Though he was overweight, he liked to be active.

english_meaning: If something is various, there are many types of it.
english_example: She owned shoes of various styles.
spanish_meaning: Diversos o de varios tipos.
spanish_example: Tenía zapatos de varios estilos.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is various, there are many types of it.
spanish_example_en: She owned shoes of various styles.

english_meaning: Actual means real or true.
english_example: This is the actual sword that the king owned, not a fake one.
spanish_meaning: Que existe y no es falso.
spanish_example: Esta es la espada real que tuvo el rey, no una falsa.
spanish_meaning_en: Actual means real or true.
spanish_example_en: This is the actual sword that the king owned, not a fake one.

english_meaning: To amaze people is to surprise them very much.
english_example: The news in the paper amazed Jack.
spanish_meaning: Sorprender profundamente.
spanish_example: Las noticias del periódico sorprendieron a Jack.
spanish_meaning_en: To amaze people is to surprise them very much.
spanish_example_en: The news in the paper amazed Jack.

english_meaning: A charge is the price to pay for something.
english_example: The charge for the shirts was $15.
spanish_meaning: Precio que se paga por un producto o servicio.
spanish_example: La tarifa de las camisas fue de 15 dólares.
notes: note: aquí significa precio
spanish_meaning_en: A charge is the price to pay for something.
spanish_example_en: The charge for the shirts was $15.

english_meaning: To comfort someone means to make them feel better.
english_example: I wanted to comfort my friend after I heard the bad news.
spanish_meaning: Dar alivio o ánimo a alguien.
spanish_example: Quería consolar a mi amiga tras la mala noticia.
spanish_meaning_en: To comfort someone means to make them feel better.
spanish_example_en: I wanted to comfort my friend after I heard the bad news.

english_meaning: To contact people is to speak or write to them.
english_example: I contacted Sue about my party.
spanish_meaning: Ponerse en contacto con alguien.
spanish_example: Contacté a Sue para invitarla a mi fiesta.
spanish_meaning_en: To contact people is to speak or write to them.
spanish_example_en: I contacted Sue about my party.

english_meaning: A customer is a person who buys something at a store.
english_example: The customer put a few items in a bag.
spanish_meaning: Persona que compra productos o servicios.
spanish_example: El cliente guardó unos artículos en una bolsa.
spanish_meaning_en: A customer is a person who buys something at a store.
spanish_example_en: The customer put a few items in a bag.

english_meaning: To deliver something is to take it from one place to another.
english_example: The man delivered Chinese food to my house.
spanish_meaning: Llevar o hacer llegar algo a destino.
spanish_example: El hombre entregó comida china en mi casa.
spanish_meaning_en: To deliver something is to take it from one place to another.
spanish_example_en: The man delivered Chinese food to my house.

english_meaning: To earn means to get money for the work you do.
english_example: He earns his living as a chef in a great restaurant.
spanish_meaning: Obtener dinero a cambio de trabajo.
spanish_example: Gana su sustento como chef en un gran restaurante.
spanish_meaning_en: To earn means to get money for the work you do.
spanish_example_en: He earns his living as a chef in a great restaurant.

english_meaning: A gate is a type of door. Gates are usually made of metal or wood.
english_example: We want to put up a wooden gate around our house.
spanish_meaning: Acceso o cerco con estructura de apertura.
spanish_example: Queremos poner una puerta de madera alrededor de nuestra casa.
spanish_meaning_en: A gate is a type of door. Gates are usually made of metal or wood.
spanish_example_en: We want to put up a wooden gate around our house.

english_meaning: To include something means to have it as part of a group.
english_example: Does this meal include a soft drink?
spanish_meaning: Formar parte de un conjunto.
spanish_example: ¿Esta comida incluye una bebida refrescante?
spanish_meaning_en: To include something means to have it as part of a group.
spanish_example_en: Does this meal include a soft drink?

english_meaning: To manage something means to control or be in charge of it.
english_example: I had to manage the meeting myself.
spanish_meaning: Dirigir o administrar algo.
spanish_example: Tuve que gestionar la reunión yo solo.
spanish_meaning_en: To manage something means to control or be in charge of it.
spanish_example_en: I had to manage the meeting myself.

english_meaning: A mystery is something that is difficult to understand or explain.
english_example: The path on the map was a complete mystery to me.
spanish_meaning: Algo enigmático o difícil de comprender.
spanish_example: El camino en el mapa fue un misterio para mí.
spanish_meaning_en: A mystery is something that is difficult to understand or explain.
spanish_example_en: The path on the map was a complete mystery to me.

english_meaning: To occur means to happen.
english_example: When did the thunderstorm occur?
spanish_meaning: Suceder o pasar.
spanish_example: ¿Cuándo ocurrió la tormenta?
spanish_meaning_en: To occur means to happen.
spanish_example_en: When did the thunderstorm occur?

english_meaning: If A is the opposite of B, A is completely different from B.
english_example: The opposite of black is white.
spanish_meaning: Lo contrario.
spanish_example: Lo opuesto al negro es el blanco.
spanish_meaning_en: If A is the opposite of B, A is completely different from B.
spanish_example_en: The opposite of black is white.

english_meaning: A plate is a flat round thing that you put food on.

spanish_meaning: Un plato es un objeto plano y redondo en el que pones comida.
spanish_example: Puse mi plato sobre la mesa para poder ponerle comida.
spanish_meaning_en: A plate is a flat round thing that you put food on.
spanish_example_en: I put my plate down so I could put some food on it.

english_meaning: To receive something is to get it.
english_example: I received a present on my birthday.
spanish_meaning: Obtener algo.
spanish_example: Recibí un regalo en mi cumpleaños.
spanish_meaning_en: To receive something is to get it.
spanish_example_en: I received a present on my birthday.

english_meaning: A reward is something given in exchange for good behavior or work.
english_example: He was given a reward for his excellent performance.
spanish_meaning: Premio por un buen desempeño o conducta.
spanish_example: Le dieron una recompensa por su excelente trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: A reward is something given in exchange for good behavior or work.
spanish_example_en: He was given a reward for his excellent performance.

english_meaning: To set something is to put it somewhere.
english_example: Please set the dice down on the table.
spanish_meaning: Colocar o situar algo.
spanish_example: Por favor, pon los dados sobre la mesa.
spanish_meaning_en: To set something is to put it somewhere.
spanish_example_en: Please set the dice down on the table.

english_meaning: To steal is to take something that is not yours.
english_example: The men tried to steal money from the bank.
spanish_meaning: Tomar lo ajeno de forma ilegal.
spanish_example: Los hombres intentaron robar dinero del banco.
spanish_meaning_en: To steal is to take something that is not yours.
spanish_example_en: The men tried to steal money from the bank.

english_meaning: A thief is someone who quietly takes things that do not belong to them.
english_example: A thief broke into our home and took my mother’s jewelry.
spanish_meaning: Persona que roba.
spanish_example: Un ladrón entró en nuestra casa y se llevó las joyas de mi madre.
spanish_meaning_en: A thief is someone who quietly takes things that do not belong to them.
spanish_example_en: A thief broke into our home and took my mother’s jewelry.

english_meaning: To advance is to go forward.
english_example: He advanced across the bridge slowly.
spanish_meaning: Progresar o pasar hacia delante.
spanish_example: Avanzó lentamente por el puente.
spanish_meaning_en: To advance is to go forward.

english_meaning: An athlete is a person who plays sports.

spanish_meaning: Persona que practica deportes de forma competitiva.
spanish_example: Algunos atletas pueden jugar muchos deportes muy bien.
spanish_meaning_en: An athlete is a person who plays sports.
spanish_example_en: Some athletes can play many sports very well.

english_meaning: If something is average, it is at a normal level.

spanish_meaning: Normal o típico.
spanish_example: No soy rico ni pobre; soy promedio.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is average, it is at a normal level.
spanish_example_en: I’m not rich or poor; I’m average.

english_meaning: Your behavior is the way you act.

spanish_meaning: Manera de actuar o comportarse.
spanish_example: Su comportamiento fue bueno este semestre; no causaron problemas.
spanish_meaning_en: Your behavior is the way you act.

english_meaning: Behind means to be at the back of something.

spanish_meaning: En la parte posterior de algo.
spanish_example: La niña se escondía detrás de un árbol.
spanish_meaning_en: Behind means to be at the back of something.
spanish_example_en: The little girl was hiding behind a tree.

english_meaning: A course is a class in school.

spanish_meaning: Asignatura o materia.

spanish_meaning_en: A course is a class in school.

english_meaning: To lower something is to make it go down.

spanish_meaning: Hacer que algo disminuya o descienda.
spanish_example: El gráfico muestra cómo bajó su producción durante el año.
spanish_meaning_en: To lower something is to make it go down.
spanish_example_en: The chart shows how his production has lowered over the year.

english_meaning: To match is to be the same or similar.

english_meaning: A member is a person who is part of a group.

spanish_meaning: Persona que pertenece a un grupo.

spanish_meaning_en: A member is a person who is part of a group.
spanish_example_en: Julie is the newest member of our team.

english_meaning: If something is mental, it has to do with your mind.

spanish_meaning: Relacionado con la mente o la inteligencia.
spanish_example: Me hice una imagen mental de la habitación.

english_meaning: A passenger is a person who rides in a car, train, or airplane.

english_meaning: Your personality is what you are like and how you behave.

spanish_meaning_en: A pole is a long thin stick made of wood or metal that supports things.
