english_meaning: The desert is an area of land without many plants or water.
english_example: Not many plants grow in the desert.
spanish_meaning: Zona árida con poca agua y vegetación.
spanish_example: No crecen muchas plantas en el desierto.
spanish_meaning_en: The desert is an area of land without many plants or water.
spanish_example_en: Not many plants grow in the desert.

english_meaning: If something is effective, it works well.
english_example: Swimming is an effective way to stay healthy.
spanish_meaning: Que produce el efecto esperado.
spanish_example: Nadar es una forma efectiva de mantenerse saludable.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is effective, it works well.
spanish_example_en: Swimming is an effective way to stay healthy.

english_meaning: To examine something is to look at it carefully.
english_example: The doctor examined my eyes today.
spanish_meaning: Observar con atención para evaluar.
spanish_example: El médico examinó mis ojos hoy.
spanish_meaning_en: To examine something is to look at it carefully.
spanish_example_en: The doctor examined my eyes today.

english_meaning: If something is false, it is not correct.
english_example: If you think the answer is false, press the red button.
spanish_meaning: Que no es cierto o auténtico.
spanish_example: Si crees que la respuesta es falsa, pulsa el botón rojo.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is false, it is not correct.
spanish_example_en: If you think the answer is false, press the red button.

english_meaning: When you figure something out, you come to understand it and find an answer.
english_example: I couldn’t figure out what he wanted me to do.
spanish_meaning: Comprender y hallar una solución o respuesta.
spanish_example: No pude resolver qué quería que hiciera.
spanish_meaning_en: When you figure something out, you come to understand it and find an answer.
spanish_example_en: I couldn’t figure out what he wanted me to do.

english_meaning: A gift is something you give someone.
english_example: Dave received many gifts for Christmas.
spanish_meaning: Una cosa que se da a alguien.
spanish_example: Dave recibió muchos regalos por Navidad.
spanish_meaning_en: A gift is something you give someone.
spanish_example_en: Dave received many gifts for Christmas.

english_meaning: Hunger is the feeling that you get when you need to eat.
english_example: After playing all day long, he was filled with hunger.
spanish_meaning: La necesidad de comer.
spanish_example: Después de jugar todo el día, él tenía mucha hambre.
spanish_meaning_en: Hunger is the feeling that you get when you need to eat.
spanish_example_en: After playing all day long, he was filled with hunger.

english_meaning: To imagine something is to think of it in your mind.
english_example: Sally imagined herself winning lots of money.
spanish_meaning: Formar una imagen o idea en la mente.
spanish_example: Sally se imaginó ganando mucho dinero.
spanish_meaning_en: To imagine something is to think of it in your mind.
spanish_example_en: Sally imagined herself winning lots of money.

english_meaning: A journey is a long trip.
english_example: I went on a journey across the country with my parents.
spanish_meaning: Un desplazamiento largo.
spanish_example: Hice un viaje por todo el país con mis padres.
spanish_meaning_en: A journey is a long trip.
spanish_example_en: I went on a journey across the country with my parents.

english_meaning: A puzzle is something that is hard to understand.
english_example: The question was a puzzle to him.
spanish_meaning: Algo difícil de comprender.
spanish_example: La pregunta fue un enigma para él.
spanish_meaning_en: A puzzle is something that is hard to understand.
spanish_example_en: The question was a puzzle to him.

english_meaning: Quite means that something is a certain way completely or very much.
english_example: I think typing on a keyboard is quite easy.
spanish_meaning: Totalmente o en gran medida.
spanish_example: Creo que escribir en un teclado es bastante fácil.
spanish_meaning_en: Quite means that something is a certain way completely or very much.
spanish_example_en: I think typing on a keyboard is quite easy.

english_meaning: Rather is used when you want to do one thing but not the other.
english_example: I would rather have the red one than the blue one.
spanish_meaning: Elegir una cosa en lugar de otra.
spanish_example: Prefiero el rojo en lugar del azul.
spanish_meaning_en: Rather is used when you want to do one thing but not the other.
spanish_example_en: I would rather have the red one than the blue one.

english_meaning: If something is specific, it is precise or exact.
english_example: Please choose a specific place on the map.
spanish_meaning: Preciso o exacto.
spanish_example: Por favor elige un lugar específico en el mapa.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is specific, it is precise or exact.
spanish_example_en: Please choose a specific place on the map.

english_meaning: A spider is a small creature with eight legs.
english_example: Some adults have a fear of little creatures such as spiders.
spanish_meaning: Un pequeño animal de ocho patas.
spanish_example: Algunos adultos tienen miedo de criaturas como las arañas.
spanish_meaning_en: A spider is a small creature with eight legs.
spanish_example_en: Some adults have a fear of little creatures such as spiders.

english_meaning: A tour is a short trip in which you see many sights.
english_example: I took a tour of Asia and Europe.
spanish_meaning: Viaje corto para ver muchos lugares.
spanish_example: Hice una excursión por Asia y Europa.
spanish_meaning_en: A tour is a short trip in which you see many sights.
spanish_example_en: I took a tour of Asia and Europe.

english_meaning: A trip is a journey to a certain place.
english_example: Ken took a trip to the city yesterday.
spanish_meaning: Un desplazamiento a un lugar.
spanish_example: Ken hizo un viaje a la ciudad ayer.
spanish_meaning_en: A trip is a journey to a certain place.
spanish_example_en: Ken took a trip to the city yesterday.

english_meaning: A band is a group of people who play music.
english_example: My brother is in a rock band.
spanish_meaning: Grupo de personas que tocan música.
spanish_example: Mi hermano está en una banda de rock.
spanish_meaning_en: A band is a group of people who play music.
spanish_example_en: My brother is in a rock band.

english_meaning: Barely means by the smallest amount, almost not.
english_example: I barely had enough money to pay for my bus ticket.
spanish_meaning: De manera mínima; casi no.
spanish_example: Apenas tenía dinero suficiente para pagar mi boleto de autobús.
spanish_meaning_en: Barely means by the smallest amount, almost not.
spanish_example_en: I barely had enough money to pay for my bus ticket.

english_meaning: If something is boring, it is not fun.
english_example: I think the Internet is boring.
spanish_meaning: No divertido.
spanish_example: Creo que Internet es aburrido.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is boring, it is not fun.
spanish_example_en: I think the Internet is boring.

english_meaning: To cancel means to decide that an event or a request will not happen.
english_example: She canceled the rest of her plans because of the rain.
spanish_meaning: Decidir que algo no se hará.
spanish_example: Ella canceló el resto de sus planes por la lluvia.
spanish_meaning_en: To cancel means to decide that an event or a request will not happen.
spanish_example_en: She canceled the rest of her plans because of the rain.

english_meaning: A driveway is a short private road that leads to a person’s home.
english_example: The long driveway led us to their new house.
spanish_meaning: Un acceso privado corto hacia una casa.
spanish_example: El largo camino de entrada nos llevó hasta su casa.
spanish_meaning_en: A driveway is a short private road that leads to a person’s home.
spanish_example_en: The long driveway led us to their new house.

english_meaning: Garbage is waste material like unwanted or spoiled food, bottles, paper, etc.
english_example: The boy cleaned up the garbage around his house.
spanish_meaning: Residuos o desechos.
spanish_example: El niño limpió la basura alrededor de su casa.
spanish_meaning_en: Garbage is waste material like unwanted or spoiled food, bottles, paper, etc.
spanish_example_en: The boy cleaned up the garbage around his house.

english_meaning: An instrument is something designed to do a certain task like play music.
english_example: My favorite musical instrument is the piano.
spanish_meaning: Objeto diseñado para una tarea.
spanish_example: Mi instrumento musical favorito es el piano.
spanish_meaning_en: An instrument is something designed to do a certain task like play music.
spanish_example_en: My favorite musical instrument is the piano.

english_meaning: A list is a record of information printed with an item on each line.
english_example: My mom makes a list of groceries to buy.
spanish_meaning: Un registro de información.
spanish_example: Mi mamá hace una lista de provisiones para comprar.
spanish_meaning_en: A list is a record of information printed with an item on each line.
spanish_example_en: My mom makes a list of groceries to buy.

english_meaning: Magic is the power to do impossible things.
english_example: The magician used magic to pull a rabbit out of his hat.
spanish_meaning: Capacidad de hacer cosas imposibles.
spanish_example: El mago usó magia para sacar un conejo de su sombrero.
spanish_meaning_en: Magic is the power to do impossible things.
spanish_example_en: The magician used magic to pull a rabbit out of his hat.

english_meaning: A message is a set of words that you send to someone.
english_example: I left a message for you in the envelope.
spanish_meaning: Un texto corto que se envía a alguien.
spanish_example: Dejé un mensaje para ti en el sobre.
spanish_meaning_en: A message is a set of words that you send to someone.
spanish_example_en: I left a message for you in the envelope.

english_meaning: To notice something is to see it for the first time.
english_example: Did you notice the view?
spanish_meaning: Ver o percibir algo por primera vez.
spanish_example: ¿Notaste el paisaje?
spanish_meaning_en: To notice something is to see it for the first time.
spanish_example_en: Did you notice the view?

english_meaning: To own something means to have it. That thing belongs to you.
english_example: My grandfather owns that house.
spanish_meaning: Tener algo o que te pertenezca.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo posee esa casa.
spanish_meaning_en: To own something means to have it. That thing belongs to you.
spanish_example_en: My grandfather owns that house.

english_meaning: To predict something is to say that it will happen.
english_example: She predicted that I would get married next year.
spanish_meaning: Decir que algo va a suceder.
spanish_example: Ella predijo que me casaría el próximo año.
spanish_meaning_en: To predict something is to say that it will happen.
spanish_example_en: She predicted that I would get married next year.

english_meaning: A professor is a person who teaches in college.
english_example: Mike’s science professor knows a lot about physics.
spanish_meaning: Persona que enseña en la universidad.
spanish_example: El profesor de ciencias de Mike sabe mucho de física.
spanish_meaning_en: A professor is a person who teaches in college.
spanish_example_en: Mike’s science professor knows a lot about physics.

english_meaning: To rush is to go somewhere or do something very quickly.
english_example: Nancy rushed to finish her homework.
spanish_meaning: Ir o hacer algo muy rápido.
spanish_example: Nancy se apresuró para terminar su tarea.
spanish_meaning_en: To rush is to go somewhere or do something very quickly.
spanish_example_en: Nancy rushed to finish her homework.

english_meaning: A schedule is a plan that tells you when to do things.
english_example: What is your class schedule for today?
spanish_meaning: Plan que indica cuándo hacer cosas.
spanish_example: ¿Cuál es tu horario de clases para hoy?
spanish_meaning_en: A schedule is a plan that tells you when to do things.
spanish_example_en: What is your class schedule for today?

english_meaning: To share something is to give some of it to another person.
english_example: Jimmy shared his apple with me.
spanish_meaning: Dar una parte de algo a otra persona.
spanish_example: Jimmy compartió su manzana conmigo.
spanish_meaning_en: To share something is to give some of it to another person.
spanish_example_en: Jimmy shared his apple with me.

english_meaning: A stage is a place where actors or musicians act or sing.
english_example: A large screen was on the stage.
spanish_meaning: Lugar donde actúan actores o músicos.
spanish_example: Había una gran pantalla en el escenario.
spanish_meaning_en: A stage is a place where actors or musicians act or sing.
spanish_example_en: A large screen was on the stage.

english_meaning: A storm is very bad weather. There is a lot of rain or snow.
english_example: Did that storm wake you up last night?
spanish_meaning: Clima muy malo con lluvia o nieve.
spanish_example: ¿La tormenta te despertó anoche?
spanish_meaning_en: A storm is very bad weather. There is a lot of rain or snow.
spanish_example_en: Did that storm wake you up last night?

english_meaning: You use within to say that something is inside another thing.
english_example: Within the box, there was a pizza.
spanish_meaning: En el interior de algo.
spanish_example: Dentro de la caja había una pizza.
spanish_meaning_en: You use within to say that something is inside another thing.
spanish_example_en: Within the box, there was a pizza.

english_meaning: A burden is a serious or difficult responsibility.
english_example: Children who do not behave are a burden to their parents.
spanish_meaning: Responsabilidad seria o difícil.
spanish_example: Los niños que no se portan bien son una carga para sus padres.
spanish_meaning_en: A burden is a serious or difficult responsibility.
spanish_example_en: Children who do not behave are a burden to their parents.

english_meaning: To compromise is to agree to something that is not exactly what you want.
english_example: We both compromised about the game we decided to play.
spanish_meaning: Aceptar algo que no es exactamente lo que quieres.
spanish_example: Ambos hicimos concesiones sobre el juego que decidimos.
spanish_meaning_en: To compromise is to agree to something that is not exactly what you want.
spanish_example_en: We both compromised about the game we decided to play.

english_meaning: To craft something is to make it using skill.
english_example: She crafted the bookcase out of solid pine wood and then painted it.
spanish_meaning: Crear algo usando habilidad.
spanish_example: Ella fabricó una estantería de madera de pino y la pintó.
spanish_meaning_en: To craft something is to make it using skill.
spanish_example_en: She crafted the bookcase out of solid pine wood and then painted it.

english_meaning: When you dive into water, you go down head first into the water.
english_example: I like sky-diving from an airplane.
spanish_meaning: Entrar en el agua de golpe y de cabeza.
spanish_example: Me gusta zambullirme al agua desde un avión.
spanish_meaning_en: When you dive into water, you go down head first into the water.
spanish_example_en: I like sky-diving from an airplane.

english_meaning: When people or things are fragile, they are not strong and can be damaged easily.
english_example: The fragile glassware was carefully packed into boxes.
spanish_meaning: Que se daña con facilidad.
spanish_example: La vajilla frágil se empaquetó con cuidado.
spanish_meaning_en: When people or things are fragile, they are not strong and can be damaged easily.
spanish_example_en: The fragile glassware was carefully packed into boxes.

english_meaning: If you cut something into two parts that are the same size, you cut it half.
english_example: The machine can divide the book in half.
spanish_meaning: La parte que divide algo en dos.
spanish_example: La máquina puede dividir el libro en dos mitades.
spanish_meaning_en: If you cut something into two parts that are the same size, you cut it half.
spanish_example_en: The machine can divide the book in half.

english_meaning: Innocence is a lack of experience of difficult or complex things in life.
english_example: Everyone who met her found her innocence to be charming.
spanish_meaning: Falta de experiencia de cosas difíciles.
spanish_example: Todos encontraron su inocencia encantadora.
spanish_meaning_en: Innocence is a lack of experience of difficult or complex things in life.
spanish_example_en: Everyone who met her found her innocence to be charming.

english_meaning: When you lead something, you go first and it follows you.
english_example: I will lead you to the right place.
spanish_meaning: Ir primero para que otros te sigan.
spanish_example: Te guiaré hacia el lugar correcto.
spanish_meaning_en: When you lead something, you go first and it follows you.
spanish_example_en: I will lead you to the right place.

english_meaning: When you have a meeting, you are with other people.
english_example: We have lots of meetings at our school to plan what to do.
spanish_meaning: Encuentro con otras personas.
spanish_example: Tenemos muchas reuniones en la escuela para planear qué hacer.
spanish_meaning_en: When you have a meeting, you are with other people.
spanish_example_en: We have lots of meetings at our school to plan what to do.

english_meaning: To merge two things is to combine them into one whole thing.
english_example: The storm clouds merged into one large menacing cloud that filled the sky.
spanish_meaning: Unir dos cosas en una sola.
spanish_example: Las nubes de tormenta se fusionaron en una nube grande.
spanish_meaning_en: To merge two things is to combine them into one whole thing.
spanish_example_en: The storm clouds merged into one large menacing cloud that filled the sky.

english_meaning: When something is moderate, it is not too big or too small in size or amount.
english_example: It takes a moderate amount of patience to be around small children all day.
spanish_meaning: No demasiado intenso ni extremo.
spanish_example: Se necesita paciencia moderada para estar con niños todo el día.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is moderate, it is not too big or too small in size or amount.
spanish_example_en: It takes a moderate amount of patience to be around small children all day.

english_meaning: To overwhelm is to exist in such a large amount that someone cannot deal with it.
english_example: The amount of homework her teacher assigned has overwhelmed her.
spanish_meaning: Afectar en exceso a alguien.
spanish_example: La cantidad de tarea le abrumó.
spanish_meaning_en: To overwhelm is to exist in such a large amount that someone cannot deal with it.
spanish_example_en: The amount of homework her teacher assigned has overwhelmed her.

english_meaning: When you make a payment, you give someone some money for what you get.
english_example: I made the last payment on my car yesterday. Now I am really the owner.
spanish_meaning: Entregar dinero por algo.
spanish_example: Hice el último pago de mi coche ayer.
spanish_meaning_en: When you make a payment, you give someone some money for what you get.
spanish_example_en: I made the last payment on my car yesterday. Now I am really the owner.

english_meaning: A perception of a situation is a way of thinking about it or understanding it.
english_example: Since he couldn’t see, his perception of life was much different than mine.
spanish_meaning: Manera de entender una situación.
spanish_example: Como no podía ver, su percepción de la vida era distinta a la mía.
spanish_meaning_en: A perception of a situation is a way of thinking about it or understanding it.
spanish_example_en: Since he couldn’t see, his perception of life was much different than mine.

english_meaning: When you settle something, you reach an agreement.
english_example: We settled the argument by looking up the answer on the web.
spanish_meaning: Llegar a un acuerdo.
spanish_example: Arreglamos la discusión mirando la respuesta en la web.
spanish_meaning_en: When you settle something, you reach an agreement.
spanish_example_en: We settled the argument by looking up the answer on the web.

english_meaning: A shiver is a shaking movement the body makes when someone is cold or scared.
english_example: I got shivers on my way home because it was so cold.
spanish_meaning: Temblor del cuerpo por frío o miedo.
spanish_example: Tuve escalofríos al regresar a casa porque hacía frío.
spanish_meaning_en: A shiver is a shaking movement the body makes when someone is cold or scared.
spanish_example_en: I got shivers on my way home because it was so cold.

english_meaning: When someone is sociable, they are friendly.
english_example: Many of my good friends are sociable, but I am shy.
spanish_meaning: Amigable y de trato fácil.
spanish_example: Muchos de mis buenos amigos son sociables, pero yo soy tímido.
spanish_meaning_en: When someone is sociable, they are friendly.
spanish_example_en: Many of my good friends are sociable, but I am shy.

english_meaning: When you speed, you go very fast.
english_example: Speeding is the cause of most car accidents.
spanish_meaning: Ir muy rápido.
spanish_example: Conducir rápido es la causa de la mayoría de accidentes.
spanish_meaning_en: When you speed, you go very fast.
spanish_example_en: Speeding is the cause of most car accidents.

english_meaning: When someone is talkative, he or she talks a lot.
english_example: My aunt is very talkative whenever she is on the phone.
spanish_meaning: Que habla mucho.
spanish_example: Mi tía es muy habladora cuando está al teléfono.
spanish_meaning_en: When someone is talkative, he or she talks a lot.
spanish_example_en: My aunt is very talkative whenever she is on the phone.

english_meaning: When something is usual, it is what we expect.
english_example: It was just the usual people who came.
spanish_meaning: Lo que se espera o ocurre seguido.
spanish_example: Fue la gente habitual la que vino.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is usual, it is what we expect.
spanish_example_en: It was just the usual people who came.

english_meaning: If something is above, it is at a higher level than something else.
english_example: He straightened the sign that was above the crowd.
spanish_meaning: En un nivel más alto.
spanish_example: Arregló el cartel que estaba encima de la multitud.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is above, it is at a higher level than something else.
spanish_example_en: He straightened the sign that was above the crowd.

english_meaning: If something is ahead of something else, it is in front of it.
english_example: The blue car drove on ahead of us.
spanish_meaning: Por delante o más adelantado.
spanish_example: El coche azul iba delante de nosotros.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is ahead of something else, it is in front of it.
spanish_example_en: The blue car drove on ahead of us.

english_meaning: An amount is how much there is of something.
english_example: Can I use my card to pay for the entire amount?
spanish_meaning: La cantidad total de algo.
spanish_example: ¿Puedo pagar el importe total con mi tarjeta?
spanish_meaning_en: An amount is how much there is of something.
spanish_example_en: Can I use my card to pay for the entire amount?

english_meaning: A belief is a strong feeling that something is correct or true.
english_example: A preacher or priest should have a strong belief in God.
spanish_meaning: Sentimiento de que algo es verdadero.
spanish_example: Un predicador debe tener una fuerte creencia en Dios.
spanish_meaning_en: A belief is a strong feeling that something is correct or true.
spanish_example_en: A preacher or priest should have a strong belief in God.

english_meaning: The center of something is the middle of it.
english_example: The center of a dart board is the most important spot.
spanish_meaning: La parte media.
spanish_example: El centro de un tablero de dardos es el punto más importante.
spanish_meaning_en: The center of something is the middle of it.
spanish_example_en: The center of a dart board is the most important spot.

english_meaning: If something is common, it happens often or there is much of it.
english_example: It is common for snow to fall in the winter.
spanish_meaning: Que ocurre a menudo o hay mucho.
spanish_example: Es común que nieve en invierno.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is common, it happens often or there is much of it.
spanish_example_en: It is common for snow to fall in the winter.

english_meaning: To cost is to require payment.
english_example: These designer shoes cost more than the regular ones.
spanish_meaning: Requerir dinero.
spanish_example: Estos zapatos de diseñador cuestan más que los normales.
spanish_meaning_en: To cost is to require payment.
spanish_example_en: These designer shoes cost more than the regular ones.

english_meaning: To demonstrate something is to show how it is done.
english_example: She demonstrated her plan to her co-workers.
spanish_meaning: Mostrar cómo se hace algo.
spanish_example: Ella mostró su plan a sus compañeras de trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: To demonstrate something is to show how it is done.
spanish_example_en: She demonstrated her plan to her co-workers.

english_meaning: Different describes someone or something that is not the same as others.
english_example: Each of my sisters has a different hair style.
spanish_meaning: No igual a otro.
spanish_example: Cada una de mis hermanas tiene un peinado diferente.
spanish_meaning_en: Different describes someone or something that is not the same as others.
spanish_example_en: Each of my sisters has a different hair style.

english_meaning: Evidence is a fact or thing that you use to prove something.
english_example: He used the pictures as evidence that UFOs are real.
spanish_meaning: Prueba o hecho que demuestra algo.
spanish_example: Usó las fotos como evidencia de que los OVNIs son reales.
spanish_meaning_en: Evidence is a fact or thing that you use to prove something.
spanish_example_en: He used the pictures as evidence that UFOs are real.

english_meaning: Honesty means the quality of being truthful or honest.
english_example: A courtroom should be a place of honesty.
spanish_meaning: Cualidad de ser sincero.
spanish_example: Una corte debe ser un lugar de honestidad.
spanish_meaning_en: Honesty means the quality of being truthful or honest.
spanish_example_en: A courtroom should be a place of honesty.

english_meaning: An idiom is a phrase with a meaning different from its words.
english_example: The idiom “when pigs fly” means that something will never happen.
spanish_meaning: Expresión con significado distinto a su literal.
spanish_example: El modismo “cuando vuelan los cerdos” significa que algo nunca pasará.
spanish_meaning_en: An idiom is a phrase with a meaning different from its words.
spanish_example_en: The idiom “when pigs fly” means that something will never happen.

english_meaning: If something is independent, it is not controlled by something else.
english_example: She chose to live an independent life in the country.
spanish_meaning: Que no depende de otro.
spanish_example: Ella eligió vivir de forma independiente en el campo.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is independent, it is not controlled by something else.
spanish_example_en: She chose to live an independent life in the country.

english_meaning: Inside means the inner part, space or side of something.
english_example: The inside of the box was empty.
spanish_meaning: Parte o espacio interno.
spanish_example: El interior de la caja estaba vacío.

spanish_meaning: Convencer es lograr que alguien acepte una idea o haga algo.
spanish_example: Los niños convencieron a sus padres de comprarles regalos.
spanish_meaning_en: To persuade someone is to make that person agree to do something.
spanish_example_en: The children persuaded their parents to buy them gifts.

english_meaning: A route is the way you go from one place to another.
english_example: I saw many new houses along the route to the city.
spanish_meaning: La ruta es el camino o recorrido entre dos lugares.
spanish_example: Vi muchas casas nuevas a lo largo de la ruta hacia la ciudad.
spanish_meaning_en: A route is the way you go from one place to another.
spanish_example_en: I saw many new houses along the route to the city.

english_meaning: Ruins are old buildings that are not used anymore.
english_example: I visited some interesting ruins in Greece.
spanish_meaning: Las ruinas son restos antiguos de edificios en desuso.
spanish_example: Visitamos ruinas muy interesantes en Grecia.
spanish_meaning_en: Ruins are old buildings that are not used anymore.
spanish_example_en: I visited some interesting ruins in Greece.

english_meaning: A scholar is a person who studies something and knows a lot about it.
english_example: The scholar knew much about art history.
spanish_meaning: El erudito es una persona que estudia mucho y conoce profundamente un tema.
spanish_example: El erudito sabía mucho de historia del arte.
spanish_meaning_en: A scholar is a person who studies something and knows a lot about it.
spanish_example_en: The scholar knew much about art history.

english_meaning: A significant person or thing is important.
english_example: I read many significant novels as a literature major in university.
spanish_meaning: Significativo significa importante o de gran relevancia.
spanish_example: Leí muchas novelas significativas en la universidad.
spanish_meaning_en: A significant person or thing is important.
spanish_example_en: I read many significant novels as a literature major in university.

english_meaning: A volcano is a mountain with a hole on top where hot liquid comes out.
english_example: When the volcano erupted, smoke and heat filled the air.
spanish_meaning: Un volcán es una montaña con cráter que puede expulsar lava y gases.
spanish_example: Cuando el volcán hizo erupción, se llenó de humo y calor el aire.
spanish_meaning_en: A volcano is a mountain with a hole on top where hot liquid comes out.
spanish_example_en: When the volcano erupted, smoke and heat filled the air.

english_meaning: Broad means that something is wide, not narrow.
english_example: The river is very long and broad.
spanish_meaning: Amplio significa ancho o extenso.
spanish_example: El río es muy largo y amplio.
spanish_meaning_en: Broad means that something is wide, not narrow.
spanish_example_en: The river is very long and broad.

english_meaning: A bush is a woody plant that is smaller than a tree.
english_example: My dad and I planted some small bushes around the house.
spanish_meaning: Un arbusto es una planta leñosa más pequeña que un árbol.
spanish_example: Mi papá y yo plantamos algunos arbustos alrededor de la casa.
spanish_meaning_en: A bush is a woody plant that is smaller than a tree.
spanish_example_en: My dad and I planted some small bushes around the house.

english_meaning: A capable person or thing can do an action.
english_example: The Olympic athlete is capable of lifting a lot of weight.
spanish_meaning: Capaz significa con habilidad o aptitud para hacer algo.
spanish_example: El atleta olímpico es capaz de levantar mucho peso.
spanish_meaning_en: A capable person or thing can do an action.
spanish_example_en: The Olympic athlete is capable of lifting a lot of weight.

english_meaning: To cheat is to be dishonest in order to win or do well.
english_example: They cheated on the test by sharing answers.
spanish_meaning: Engañar es actuar de forma deshonesta para lograr ventaja.
spanish_example: Engañaron en el examen al compartir respuestas.
spanish_meaning_en: To cheat is to be dishonest in order to win or do well.
spanish_example_en: They cheated on the test by sharing answers.

english_meaning: To concentrate is to give one’s full attention to something.
english_example: I could not concentrate on my homework because the room was so loud.
spanish_meaning: Concentrarse es prestar atención plena a una tarea.
spanish_example: No pude concentrarme en la tarea porque la habitación estaba muy ruidosa.
spanish_meaning_en: To concentrate is to give one’s full attention to something.
spanish_example_en: I could not concentrate on my homework because the room was so loud.

english_meaning: To conclude is to arrive at a logical end by looking at evidence.
english_example: I saw crumbs on my dog’s face, so I concluded that he ate my cookie.
spanish_meaning: Concluir es llegar a una decisión final mediante razonamiento.
spanish_example: Vi migas en la cara de mi perro, así que concluí que se comió mi galleta.
spanish_meaning_en: To conclude is to arrive at a logical end by looking at evidence.
spanish_example_en: I saw crumbs on my dog’s face, so I concluded that he ate my cookie.

english_meaning: Confident people believe that they can do something without failing.
english_example: She was confident she could climb the mountain due to her training.
spanish_meaning: Seguro significa tener confianza para hacerlo.
spanish_example: Ella estaba segura de que podía subir la montaña.
spanish_meaning_en: Confident people believe that they can do something without failing.
spanish_example_en: She was confident she could climb the mountain due to her training.

english_meaning: Considerable means large in size, amount, or extent.
english_example: They paid a considerable amount of money for that car.
spanish_meaning: Algo considerable es bastante grande en cantidad o grado.
spanish_example: Pagaron una suma considerable por ese coche.
spanish_meaning_en: Considerable means large in size, amount, or extent.
spanish_example_en: They paid a considerable amount of money for that car.

english_meaning: To convey is to communicate or make ideas known.
english_example: That picture of a crying child conveys a feeling of sadness.
spanish_meaning: Transmitir es comunicar una idea, emoción o mensaje.
spanish_example: La foto de ese niño llorando transmite tristeza.
spanish_meaning_en: To convey is to communicate or make ideas known.
spanish_example_en: That picture of a crying child conveys a feeling of sadness.

english_meaning: A definite thing is certain or sure to be true.
english_example: There is a definite connection between hard work and success.
spanish_meaning: en contexto formal: que es seguro o cierto.
spanish_example: Existe una conexión definitiva entre esfuerzo y éxito.
spanish_meaning_en: A definite thing is certain or sure to be true.
spanish_example_en: There is a definite connection between hard work and success.

english_meaning: Delight is a feeling of being very happy with something.
english_example: He felt such delight after getting a promotion at work.
spanish_meaning: Placer es la sensación de gran alegría o gusto.
spanish_example: Sintió un gran placer al recibir un ascenso.
spanish_meaning_en: Delight is a feeling of being very happy with something.
spanish_example_en: He felt such delight after getting a promotion at work.

english_meaning: A destination is the place where someone or something is going.
english_example: The destination of this plane is Munich, Germany.
spanish_meaning: El destino es el lugar de llegada de un viaje.
spanish_example: El destino de este avión es Múnich, Alemania.
spanish_meaning_en: A destination is the place where someone or something is going.
spanish_example_en: The destination of this plane is Munich, Germany.

english_meaning: The edge is the furthest part or side of something.
english_example: He ran to the edge of the cliff.
spanish_meaning: El borde es el extremo de algo.
spanish_example: Corrió hasta el borde del acantilado.
spanish_meaning_en: The edge is the furthest part or side of something.
spanish_example_en: He ran to the edge of the cliff.

english_meaning: A set of instructions explains how to do something.
english_example: Just follow the instructions and you will be OK.
spanish_meaning: Las instrucciones son pasos para hacer algo correctamente.
spanish_example: Solo sigue las instrucciones y estarás bien.
spanish_meaning_en: A set of instructions explains how to do something.
spanish_example_en: Just follow the instructions and you will be OK.

english_meaning: A path is a way from one place to another that people can walk along.
english_example: We followed a path through the woods.
spanish_meaning: Un sendero es un camino para caminar.
spanish_example: Seguimos un sendero por el bosque.
spanish_meaning_en: A path is a way from one place to another that people can walk along.
spanish_example_en: We followed a path through the woods.

english_meaning: To resort to something is to depend on it in order to solve a problem.
english_example: I hope they don’t resort to violence to end the argument.
spanish_meaning: Recurrir a algo es usarlo como medida o salida.
spanish_example: Espero que no recurran a la violencia para terminar la discusión.
spanish_meaning_en: To resort to something is to depend on it in order to solve a problem.
spanish_example_en: I hope they don’t resort to violence to end the argument.

english_meaning: A shadow is the dark area that is made when something blocks light.
english_example: The man’s shadow was taller than he was.
spanish_meaning: La sombra es la zona oscura que aparece al bloquearse la luz.
spanish_example: La sombra del hombre era más alta que él.
spanish_meaning_en: A shadow is the dark area that is made when something blocks light.
spanish_example_en: The man’s shadow was taller than he was.

english_meaning: To succeed is to complete something as planned.
english_example: He will continue to work on the robot until he succeeds.
spanish_meaning: Triunfar es alcanzar el objetivo con éxito.
spanish_example: Él seguirá trabajando en el robot hasta que triunfe.
spanish_meaning_en: To succeed is to complete something as planned.
spanish_example_en: He will continue to work on the robot until he succeeds.

english_meaning: To suspect something is to believe that it might be true.
english_example: I suspect that those kids stole the money.
spanish_meaning: Sospechar es pensar que algo es posible, aunque no probado.
spanish_example: Sospecho que esos niños robaron el dinero.
spanish_meaning_en: To suspect something is to believe that it might be true.
spanish_example_en: I suspect that those kids stole the money.

english_meaning: A valley is a low area of land between two mountains or hills.
english_example: We looked at the valley below from the top of the mountain.
spanish_meaning: El valle es una zona baja entre montañas o colinas.
spanish_example: Desde lo alto vimos el valle de abajo.
spanish_meaning_en: A valley is a low area of land between two mountains or hills.
spanish_example_en: We looked at the valley below from the top of the mountain.

english_meaning: To be against something is to be touching it or opposed to it.
english_example: They both leaned against the wall.
spanish_meaning: en contra significa pegado a, o en oposición a.
spanish_example: Se apoyaron contra la pared.
spanish_meaning_en: To be against something is to be touching it or opposed to it.
spanish_example_en: They both leaned against the wall.

english_meaning: The beach is a sandy or rocky place by the ocean.

spanish_meaning: La playa es la franja de arena o roca junto al mar.
spanish_example: La niña construyó un castillo de arena en la playa.
spanish_meaning_en: The beach is a sandy or rocky place by the ocean.
spanish_example_en: The little girl built a sandcastle on the beach.

english_meaning: To damage something is to break it.

spanish_meaning: Dañar algo significa estropearlo o perjudicarlo.
spanish_example: El coche se dañó en el accidente.

english_meaning: To discover something is to find it for the first time.

spanish_meaning: Descubrir es encontrar o revelar algo por primera vez.
spanish_example: Descubrí información nueva en este libro.
spanish_meaning_en: To discover something is to find it for the first time.
spanish_example_en: I discovered some new information in this book.

english_meaning: An emotion is how you feel.

spanish_meaning: La emoción es un estado de sentimiento.
spanish_example: La rabia es una emoción muy común.

english_meaning: To fix something is to make it work.

spanish_example: Mi papá tiene muchas herramientas para reparar cosas rotas.
spanish_meaning_en: To fix something is to make it work.

english_meaning: To identify something is to find out what it is.
english_example: I used the file to identify his name.

spanish_example: Usé el expediente para identificar su nombre.
spanish_meaning_en: To identify something is to find out what it is.

english_meaning: An island is land in the middle of water.

spanish_example: Japón es un grupo de islas.

english_meaning: The ocean is all of the salt water that surrounds land.

spanish_example: El océano puede producir olas muy fuertes.
spanish_meaning_en: The ocean is all of the salt water that surrounds land.

english_meaning: Perhaps is used when you say that something could happen.

spanish_meaning_en: Perhaps is used when you say that something could happen.

english_meaning: If something is pleasant, you enjoy it.

english_meaning: A rock is a hard thing in the dirt.

english_meaning: To save something is to keep it from being hurt.

english_meaning: To smile is to show happiness with your mouth.

spanish_meaning_en: A taste is the flavor something makes in your mouth.

spanish_meaning_en: To throw something is to use your hand to make it go through the air.

spanish_meaning: La ola es un movimiento de agua en la superficie marina.

spanish_meaning_en: A council is a group of people who run a city or town.

english_meaning: To declare is to say something officially.

english_meaning: Extraordinary things are amazing.

spanish_example: El bombero que rescató a la niña fue extraordinario.

english_meaning: Fog is a thick cloud that is near the ground or water.

spanish_meaning: La niebla es una nube baja de humedad y poca visibilidad.

spanish_meaning_en: Fog is a thick cloud that is near the ground or water.

english_meaning: A funeral is a ceremony that takes place after a person dies.

english_meaning: Giant means very big.

spanish_meaning_en: An impression is the way of thinking about someone or something.

english_meaning: To resist something is to fight against it.

spanish_meaning_en: To resist something is to fight against it.

english_meaning: To reveal is to show something.

english_meaning: To rid is to make a place free from something or someone.

spanish_meaning: significa quitar o eliminar algo de un lugar.

spanish_example: La rosa tenía un tallo largo y delgado.
spanish_meaning_en: The stem of a plant is the stick that grows leaves or flowers.
spanish_example_en: The rose had a long thin stem.

english_meaning: A ton is a unit for measuring weight equal to 2000 pounds or 907.2 kilograms.
english_example: This dump truck can carry up to 25 tons.
spanish_meaning: Una tonelada es una unidad de peso de 2000 libras o 907.2 kilos.
spanish_example: Ese camión puede cargar hasta 25 toneladas.
spanish_meaning_en: A ton is a unit for measuring weight equal to 2000 pounds or 907.2 kilograms.
spanish_example_en: This dump truck can carry up to 25 tons.

english_meaning: Transport is the system or method of carrying passengers or goods from one place to another.
english_example: Cargo ships are used for the transport of manufactured goods.
spanish_meaning: El transporte es el método para mover personas o mercancías de un lugar a otro.
spanish_example: Los barcos de carga se usan para el transporte de bienes.
spanish_meaning_en: Transport is the system or method of carrying passengers or goods from one place to another.
spanish_example_en: Cargo ships are used for the transport of manufactured goods.

english_meaning: To align is to support a political group, country, or person that you agree with.
english_example: The crowd seemed to align themselves with the protestors.
spanish_meaning: Alinear significa apoyar un grupo o país con quien estás de acuerdo.
spanish_example: La multitud parecía alinearse con los manifestantes.
spanish_meaning_en: To align is to support a political group, country, or person that you agree with.
spanish_example_en: The crowd seemed to align themselves with the protestors.

english_meaning: An authority is someone who has the power to make decisions and enforce rules and laws.
english_example: The local police department is the authority whenever large crowds gather.
spanish_meaning: Una autoridad es alguien que puede tomar decisiones y hacer cumplir leyes.
spanish_example: La policía local es la autoridad cuando se reúne mucha gente.
spanish_meaning_en: An authority is someone who has the power to make decisions and enforce rules and laws.
spanish_example_en: The local police department is the authority whenever large crowds gather.

english_meaning: A barn is a large farm building that houses crops, equipment, and animals.
english_example: My grandparents keep hay in their red barn.
spanish_meaning: Un granero es un gran edificio de granja para guardar cultivos, equipos y animales.
spanish_example: Mis abuelos guardan heno en su granero rojo.
spanish_meaning_en: A barn is a large farm building that houses crops, equipment, and animals.
spanish_example_en: My grandparents keep hay in their red barn.

english_meaning: To betray is to be disloyal to someone who trusts you.
english_example: The four friends would never betray each other’s trust.
spanish_meaning: Traicionar es ser desleal con alguien que confía en ti.
spanish_example: Los cuatro amigos nunca traicionarían su confianza.
spanish_meaning_en: To betray is to be disloyal to someone who trusts you.
spanish_example_en: The four friends would never betray each other’s trust.

english_meaning: The Bible is the book of sacred writing in the Christian religion.
english_example: I read the Bible every night before I go to sleep.
spanish_meaning: La Biblia es el libro sagrado del cristianismo.
spanish_example: Leo la Biblia cada noche antes de dormir.
spanish_meaning_en: The Bible is the book of sacred writing in the Christian religion.
spanish_example_en: I read the Bible every night before I go to sleep.

english_meaning: A Catholic thing or person is one that is connected with the Roman Catholic Church.
english_example: Rosanne is Catholic, so she goes to church every Sunday.
spanish_meaning: Católico se relaciona con la Iglesia Católica Romana.
spanish_example: Rosanne es católica, así que va a misa cada domingo.
spanish_meaning_en: A Catholic thing or person is one that is connected with the Roman Catholic Church.
spanish_example_en: Rosanne is Catholic, so she goes to church every Sunday.

english_meaning: To cooperate is to work with someone to achieve something that you both want.
english_example: The members of the team agreed to cooperate in order to get the project done.
spanish_meaning: Cooperar es trabajar con alguien para lograr algo que ambos quieren.
spanish_example: Los miembros del equipo acordaron cooperar para terminar el proyecto.
spanish_meaning_en: To cooperate is to work with someone to achieve something that you both want.
spanish_example_en: The members of the team agreed to cooperate in order to get the project done.

english_meaning: To detain is to officially prevent someone from leaving a place.
english_example: The police had to detain the thief for shoplifting.
spanish_meaning: Detener es impedir oficialmente que alguien se vaya de un lugar.
spanish_example: La policía tuvo que detener al ladrón por robar en la tienda.
spanish_meaning_en: To detain is to officially prevent someone from leaving a place.
spanish_example_en: The police had to detain the thief for shoplifting.

english_meaning: An exit is something that is used as a way to get out of a place.
english_example: The green sign shows where the exit is located.
spanish_meaning: Una salida es una forma de salir de un lugar.
spanish_example: El letrero verde muestra dónde está la salida.
spanish_meaning_en: An exit is something that is used as a way to get out of a place.
spanish_example_en: The green sign shows where the exit is located.

english_meaning: An exceptional thing or person is one that is outstanding.
english_example: Holly is an exceptional student.
spanish_meaning: Algo o alguien excepcional es sobresaliente.
spanish_example: Holly es una estudiante excepcional.
spanish_meaning_en: An exceptional thing or person is one that is outstanding.
spanish_example_en: Holly is an exceptional student.

english_meaning: To flee is to leave somewhere very quickly in order to escape from danger.
english_example: When a predator approaches a deer, the deer will flee.
spanish_meaning: Huir es salir rápido para escapar de peligro.
spanish_example: Cuando un depredador se acerca a un ciervo, este huye.
spanish_meaning_en: To flee is to leave somewhere very quickly in order to escape from danger.
spanish_example_en: When a predator approaches a deer, the deer will flee.

english_meaning: A network is a group of people or organizations that are in some way connected to each other.
english_example: Lesley is part of a volunteer network that plants trees in the city.
spanish_meaning: Una red es un grupo de personas u organizaciones conectadas entre sí.
spanish_example: Lesley pertenece a una red de voluntarios que plantan árboles.
spanish_meaning_en: A network is a group of people or organizations that are in some way connected to each other.
spanish_example_en: Lesley is part of a volunteer network that plants trees in the city.

english_meaning: To outrage is to make someone feel very angry or shocked.
english_example: He was outraged when his proposal was turned down.
spanish_meaning: Indignar significa hacer que alguien se enfade o choque mucho.
spanish_example: Se indignó cuando rechazaron su propuesta.
spanish_meaning_en: To outrage is to make someone feel very angry or shocked.
spanish_example_en: He was outraged when his proposal was turned down.

english_meaning: A parish is the area for which a priest in some Christian churches is responsible.
english_example: Greg’s parish has grown over the past year.
spanish_meaning: Una parroquia es el área de responsabilidad de un sacerdote.
spanish_example: La parroquia de Greg ha crecido el último año.
spanish_meaning_en: A parish is the area for which a priest in some Christian churches is responsible.
spanish_example_en: Greg’s parish has grown over the past year.

english_meaning: A passage is a narrow space that people can move through.
english_example: Follow the passage down the stairs.
spanish_meaning: Un pasaje es un espacio estrecho por el que pasan personas.
spanish_example: Sigue el pasaje hasta las escaleras.
spanish_meaning_en: A passage is a narrow space that people can move through.
spanish_example_en: Follow the passage down the stairs.

english_meaning: A pastor is a minister in charge of a parish or church.
english_example: The pastor speaks to the members of the church.
spanish_meaning: Un pastor es un ministro a cargo de una iglesia o parroquia.
spanish_example: El pastor habla con los miembros de la iglesia.
spanish_meaning_en: A pastor is a minister in charge of a parish or church.
spanish_example_en: The pastor speaks to the members of the church.

english_meaning: A patrol is a group of people or vehicles that go through an area to make sure that it is free of trouble or danger.
english_example: The military patrol is responsible for keeping the area safe.
spanish_meaning: Una patrulla es un grupo de personas o vehículos que revisa una zona para mantenerla segura.
spanish_example: La patrulla militar se encarga de la seguridad del área.
spanish_meaning_en: A patrol is a group of people or vehicles that go through an area to make sure that it is free of trouble or danger.
spanish_example_en: The military patrol is responsible for keeping the area safe.

english_meaning: To raid is to attack a place in a short time in order to cause damage.
english_example: The soldiers are looking for the people who continue to raid the jungle villages.
spanish_meaning: Asaltar es atacar un lugar de golpe para causar daño.
spanish_example: Los soldados buscan a los que siguen asaltando las aldeas de la selva.
spanish_meaning_en: To raid is to attack a place in a short time in order to cause damage.
spanish_example_en: The soldiers are looking for the people who continue to raid the jungle villages.

english_meaning: A rail is a system of tracks on which trains travel.
english_example: I prefer traveling by rail because it’s more convenient than driving through traffic.
spanish_meaning: Los rieles son las vías por donde viajan los trenes.
spanish_example: Prefiero viajar por rieles porque evita el tráfico.
spanish_meaning_en: A rail is a system of tracks on which trains travel.
spanish_example_en: I prefer traveling by rail because it’s more convenient than driving through traffic.

english_meaning: A tunnel is an underground passage for cars, trains, etc. to go through.
english_example: The tunnel will lead you to the next city.
spanish_meaning: Un túnel es un paso subterráneo para autos, trenes, etc.
spanish_example: El túnel te llevará a la próxima ciudad.
spanish_meaning_en: A tunnel is an underground passage for cars, trains, etc. to go through.
spanish_example_en: The tunnel will lead you to the next city.

english_meaning: When something is comfortable, it makes you feel relaxed, confident, and not worried.
english_example: Will loves his comfortable new sofa.
spanish_meaning: Cómodo significa que algo te hace sentir relajado y seguro.
spanish_example: Will le encanta su nuevo sofá cómodo.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is comfortable, it makes you feel relaxed, confident, and not worried.
spanish_example_en: Will loves his comfortable new sofa.

english_meaning: Education is the process of teaching and learning, usually at a school, college, or university.
english_example: These students are receiving their education at a private school.
spanish_meaning: La educación es el proceso de enseñar y aprender, normalmente en escuela, colegio o universidad.
spanish_example: Estos estudiantes reciben su educación en una escuela privada.
spanish_meaning_en: Education is the process of teaching and learning, usually at a school, college, or university.
spanish_example_en: These students are receiving their education at a private school.

english_meaning: Encouragement is something that makes someone more determined, hopeful, or confident.
english_example: The mother gives constant encouragement to her son.
spanish_meaning: El apoyo es algo que hace que alguien esté más decidido, esperanzado o seguro.
spanish_example: La madre da apoyo constante a su hijo.
spanish_meaning_en: Encouragement is something that makes someone more determined, hopeful, or confident.
spanish_example_en: The mother gives constant encouragement to her son.

english_meaning: To endorse is to express formal support or approval for someone or something.
english_example: The committee will endorse their leader for the upcoming election.
spanish_meaning: Respaldar es expresar apoyo o aprobación oficial.
spanish_example: El comité respaldará a su líder en las próximas elecciones.
spanish_meaning_en: To endorse is to express formal support or approval for someone or something.
spanish_example_en: The committee will endorse their leader for the upcoming election.

english_meaning: When something is equal, it is the same in size, number, amount, or value as something else.
english_example: The apple and orange are equal in weight.
spanish_meaning: Igual significa el mismo tamaño, número, cantidad o valor.
spanish_example: La manzana y la naranja tienen igual peso.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is equal, it is the same in size, number, amount, or value as something else.
spanish_example_en: The apple and orange are equal in weight.

english_meaning: To face is to deal with something in a direct way.
english_example: Justin will face many challenges at work.
spanish_meaning: Enfrentar algo es tratarlo de forma directa.
spanish_example: Justin enfrentará muchos retos en el trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: To face is to deal with something in a direct way.
spanish_example_en: Justin will face many challenges at work.

english_meaning: A field is a subject that people study or an area of activity that they are involved in as part of their work.
english_example: Jane works in the healthcare field.
spanish_meaning: Un campo es una materia que la gente estudia o un área de trabajo.
spanish_example: Jane trabaja en el campo de salud.
spanish_meaning_en: A field is a subject that people study or an area of activity that they are involved in as part of their work.
spanish_example_en: Jane works in the healthcare field.

english_meaning: To foster is to help a skill, feeling, or idea develop over a period of time.
english_example: The afterschool program is designed to foster a sense of community at school.
spanish_meaning: Fomentar es ayudar a que una habilidad, sentimiento o idea se desarrolle.
spanish_example: El programa extraescolar está diseñado para fomentar el sentido de comunidad en la escuela.
spanish_meaning_en: To foster is to help a skill, feeling, or idea develop over a period of time.
spanish_example_en: The afterschool program is designed to foster a sense of community at school.

english_meaning: Guidance is help and advice that is given to someone about their work, education, or personal life.
english_example: Ms. Smith provided valuable guidance to all her students.
spanish_meaning: La orientación es ayuda y consejo sobre trabajo, estudios o vida personal.
spanish_example: La profesora Smith dio orientación valiosa a todos sus alumnos.
spanish_meaning_en: Guidance is help and advice that is given to someone about their work, education, or personal life.
spanish_example_en: Ms. Smith provided valuable guidance to all her students.

english_meaning: To hold is to have a particular degree, title, record, job, or position.
english_example: Damian will soon hold a degree in business administration.
spanish_meaning: Tener o mantener un título, puesto o récord es mantener la posición.
spanish_example: Damian pronto obtendrá un título en administración de empresas.
spanish_meaning_en: To hold is to have a particular degree, title, record, job, or position.
spanish_example_en: Damian will soon hold a degree in business administration.

english_meaning: Inclusion is the act of including someone or something in a larger group or set.
english_example: The school promotes the inclusion of parents in their students’ education.
spanish_meaning: Incluir a alguien en un grupo más grande es inclusión.
spanish_example: La escuela promueve la inclusión de los padres en la educación.
spanish_meaning_en: Inclusion is the act of including someone or something in a larger group or set.
spanish_example_en: The school promotes the inclusion of parents in their students’ education.

english_meaning: To inspire is to encourage someone by making them feel confident and eager to do something.

spanish_meaning: Inspirar es alentar a alguien haciéndole sentirse confiado.
spanish_example: Jim inspira a su hijo para que siga sus sueños.
spanish_meaning_en: To inspire is to encourage someone by making them feel confident and eager to do something.
spanish_example_en: Jim’s father likes to inspire his son by encouraging him to follow his dream.

english_meaning: Mere is used to emphasize how small or unimportant something or someone is.
english_example: Scott lost the race by a mere two seconds.
spanish_meaning: Mero se usa para enfatizar algo pequeño o poco importante.
spanish_example: Scott perdió la carrera por meros dos segundos.
spanish_meaning_en: Mere is used to emphasize how small or unimportant something or someone is.
spanish_example_en: Scott lost the race by a mere two seconds.

english_meaning: A position is a rank or role of someone in an organization or company.

spanish_meaning: Un puesto es un rango o función en una organización o empresa.

spanish_meaning_en: A position is a rank or role of someone in an organization or company.

english_meaning: Power is the ability to influence people or give them strong feelings.

spanish_meaning: Poder es la capacidad de influir en personas o provocar fuertes sentimientos.

spanish_meaning_en: Power is the ability to influence people or give them strong feelings.
spanish_example_en: The coach has the power to keep the players from playing if they don’t show up to practice.

english_meaning: Responsibility is a task or duty that you are expected or required to do.

spanish_meaning: La responsabilidad es una tarea o deber que se espera que hagas.

spanish_meaning_en: Responsibility is a task or duty that you are expected or required to do.
spanish_example_en: Parents have the responsibility to raise their children with love.

english_meaning: To soar is to increase quickly to a high level.

spanish_meaning: Elevarse significa subir rápidamente hasta una altura alta.

spanish_example_en: The temperature today will soar to a high of 100 degrees Fahrenheit.

english_meaning: A tool is a piece of equipment or a skill that is useful for doing your job.

spanish_meaning: Una herramienta es un equipo o una habilidad útil para tu trabajo.

spanish_meaning_en: A tool is a piece of equipment or a skill that is useful for doing your job.
spanish_example_en: Presentation skills are an important tool for communication.

english_meaning: When someone is vocal, he or she expresses a strong opinion publicly.
english_example: Shawn gets vocal when someone disagrees with his opinions.
spanish_meaning: Cuando alguien es vocal, expresa una opinión fuerte en público.
spanish_example: Shawn es vocal cuando alguien no está de acuerdo con sus ideas.

english_meaning: When something is academic, it relates to schools and education.

spanish_meaning_en: When something is academic, it relates to schools and education.
spanish_example_en: ˌækəˈdemɪk

english_meaning: To blur is to make something unclear or difficult to see or remember.

spanish_meaning: Desenfocar significa hacer algo difícil de ver o recordar con claridad.

spanish_meaning_en: To blur is to make something unclear or difficult to see or remember.

spanish_meaning: Un café es un pequeño restaurante donde puedes comprar bebidas y comidas sencillas.

english_meaning: A canvas is a thick piece of cloth that artists use to paint on.

spanish_meaning: Un lienzo es una tela gruesa que usan los artistas para pintar.
spanish_example: La artista pinta colores vivos en un lienzo.
spanish_meaning_en: A canvas is a thick piece of cloth that artists use to paint on.

spanish_example: Mi hermana disfruta ver películas en el cine.

spanish_meaning_en: When something is distinctive, it has a special quality, character, or appearance that is different and easy to recognize.
spanish_example_en: Olives have a distinctive flavor.

spanish_meaning: Algo clave es sumamente importante.

spanish_meaning_en: When something is key, it is extremely important.

spanish_meaning: Un movimiento es una serie de actividades organizadas en la que la gente trabaja junta.

spanish_example: Samuel hizo de la lectura del periódico una práctica diaria.
spanish_meaning_en: A practice is something that is done often or regularly.
