spanish_meaning_en: When something is traditional, it is based on old-fashioned ideas.
spanish_example_en: I prefer traditional furniture.

english_meaning: Unlike is used in place of “completely different from a particular person or thing.”
english_example: Unlike his older brother, Jim is short.
spanish_meaning: A diferencia de significa completamente distinto de alguien o algo.
spanish_example: A diferencia de su hermano mayor, Jim es bajo.
spanish_meaning_en: Unlike is used in place of “completely different from a particular person or thing.”
spanish_example_en: Unlike his older brother, Jim is short.

english_meaning: Width is the distance from one side of something to the other side.
english_example: The man is measuring the width of the box.
spanish_meaning: El ancho es la distancia entre un lado y el otro de algo.
spanish_example: El hombre mide el ancho de la caja.
spanish_meaning_en: Width is the distance from one side of something to the other side.
spanish_example_en: The man is measuring the width of the box.

english_meaning: An anthem is a formal or religious song.
english_example: Everyone sings the national anthem before the baseball game starts.
spanish_meaning: Un himno es una canción formal o religiosa.
spanish_example: Todos cantan el himno nacional antes de que empiece el partido.
spanish_meaning_en: An anthem is a formal or religious song.
spanish_example_en: Everyone sings the national anthem before the baseball game starts.

english_meaning: Beyond is used to say that one thing is more than another.
english_example: The job is beyond the man’s ability.
spanish_meaning: Más allá significa que algo sobrepasa otra cosa.
spanish_example: El trabajo está más allá de la capacidad de ese hombre.
spanish_meaning_en: Beyond is used to say that one thing is more than another.
spanish_example_en: The job is beyond the man’s ability.

english_meaning: Determination is what you have when you try to do something even when it is difficult.
english_example: Chris is the youngest competitor, but he has the most determination.
spanish_meaning: La determinación es insistencia al intentar algo aunque sea difícil.
spanish_example: Chris es el competidor más joven, pero tiene más determinación.
spanish_meaning_en: Determination is what you have when you try to do something even when it is difficult.
spanish_example_en: Chris is the youngest competitor, but he has the most determination.

english_meaning: Fighting is physical conflict between people or groups in a war, in the street, etc.
english_example: Heavy fighting continued for days.
spanish_meaning: El combate es el conflicto físico entre personas o grupos en guerra o en la calle.
spanish_example: El combate fue intenso durante días.
spanish_meaning_en: Fighting is physical conflict between people or groups in a war, in the street, etc.
spanish_example_en: Heavy fighting continued for days.

english_meaning: When something is joint, it is done by or involves two or more people or groups.
english_example: Mike and Joanna are joint owners of a coffee shop.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto significa hecho por dos o más personas o grupos.
spanish_example: Mike y Joanna son dueños conjuntos de una cafetería.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is joint, it is done by or involves two or more people or groups.
spanish_example_en: Mike and Joanna are joint owners of a coffee shop.

english_meaning: A landing is when you return to the ground or another surface after a flight or a boat ride.
english_example: The man is in charge of leading the landing in the warzone.
spanish_meaning: Aterrizaje es volver al suelo o a una superficie tras un vuelo o viaje en bote.
spanish_example: El hombre dirige el aterrizaje en la zona de guerra.
spanish_meaning_en: A landing is when you return to the ground or another surface after a flight or a boat ride.
spanish_example_en: The man is in charge of leading the landing in the warzone.

english_meaning: To mark is to celebrate an important event or time by doing something.
english_example: My grandparents are having a party to mark their 50th anniversary.
spanish_meaning: Marcar significa señalar o celebrar una fecha o momento importante.
spanish_example: Mis abuelos hicieron una fiesta para marcar su aniversario número 50.
spanish_meaning_en: To mark is to celebrate an important event or time by doing something.
spanish_example_en: My grandparents are having a party to mark their 50th anniversary.

english_meaning: To mobilize is to prepare an army to fight in a war.
english_example: Thousands of soldiers were ready to mobilize for the fight.
spanish_meaning: Móvilizar significa preparar al ejército para la guerra.
spanish_example: Miles de soldados estaban listos para móvilizarse para la lucha.
spanish_meaning_en: To mobilize is to prepare an army to fight in a war.
spanish_example_en: Thousands of soldiers were ready to mobilize for the fight.

english_meaning: A nation is a large area of land that is controlled by its own government.
english_example: India is a nation that is developing rapidly.
spanish_meaning: Una nación es una gran área de tierra con gobierno propio.
spanish_example: India es una nación que se desarrolla rápidamente.
spanish_meaning_en: A nation is a large area of land that is controlled by its own government.
spanish_example_en: India is a nation that is developing rapidly.

english_meaning: Originally is used in place of “in the beginning” or “when something first happened or began.”
english_example: This building was originally a bank.
spanish_meaning: Originalmente significa en los inicios, cuando algo comenzó.
spanish_example: Este edificio fue originalmente un banco.
spanish_meaning_en: Originally is used in place of “in the beginning” or “when something first happened or began.”
spanish_example_en: This building was originally a bank.

english_meaning: An outbreak is a sudden start or increase of fighting or disease.
english_example: The scientists prepared for an outbreak of a dangerous virus.
spanish_meaning: Un brote es un inicio repentino de lucha o enfermedad.
spanish_example: Los científicos se prepararon para un brote de un virus peligroso.
spanish_meaning_en: An outbreak is a sudden start or increase of fighting or disease.
spanish_example_en: The scientists prepared for an outbreak of a dangerous virus.

english_meaning: To pray is to speak to God in order to ask for help or to give thanks.
english_example: The girl likes to pray for world peace.
spanish_meaning: Rezar es hablar con Dios para pedir ayuda o dar gracias.
spanish_example: La niña gusta de rezar por la paz del mundo.
spanish_meaning_en: To pray is to speak to God in order to ask for help or to give thanks.
spanish_example_en: The girl likes to pray for world peace.

english_meaning: A prisoner is someone who is taken by force and kept somewhere.
english_example: He has been a prisoner for many years.
spanish_meaning: Un prisionero es alguien retenido por la fuerza en un lugar.
spanish_example: Ha sido prisionero durante muchos años.
spanish_meaning_en: A prisoner is someone who is taken by force and kept somewhere.
spanish_example_en: He has been a prisoner for many years.

english_meaning: To sacrifice is to give up something in order to get something you want or do something else for someone.
english_example: Karen has had to sacrifice a lot for her children.
spanish_meaning: Sacrificar es renunciar a algo para obtener algo o hacer algo por otro.
spanish_example: Karen ha tenido que sacrificar mucho por sus hijos.
spanish_meaning_en: To sacrifice is to give up something in order to get something you want or do something else for someone.
spanish_example_en: Karen has had to sacrifice a lot for her children.

english_meaning: Silence is the complete absence of sound or noise.
english_example: I can only sleep in complete silence.
spanish_meaning: Silencio es ausencia total de sonido.
spanish_example: Solo puedo dormir en silencio total.
spanish_meaning_en: Silence is the complete absence of sound or noise.
spanish_example_en: I can only sleep in complete silence.

english_meaning: Thus is used in place of “as a result of something that was just mentioned.”
english_example: Victor wants to lose weight; thus, he’ll go on a diet and start exercising.
spanish_meaning: Así se usa para decir “como resultado de lo mencionado”.
spanish_example: Victor quiere bajar de peso; así irá en dieta y empezará a ejercitarse.
spanish_meaning_en: Thus is used in place of “as a result of something that was just mentioned.”
spanish_example_en: Victor wants to lose weight; thus, he’ll go on a diet and start exercising.

english_meaning: A tribute is something that you say, do, or give in order to express respect for someone.
english_example: In some countries, people wear poppies as a tribute to veterans.
spanish_meaning: Un tributo es algo dicho, hecho o dado para mostrar respeto.
spanish_example: En algunos países, la gente lleva amapolas como tributo a los veteranos.
spanish_meaning_en: A tribute is something that you say, do, or give in order to express respect for someone.
spanish_example_en: In some countries, people wear poppies as a tribute to veterans.

english_meaning: A victory is a situation in which you win a battle, game, election, or dispute.
english_example: The man was thrilled with his victory.
spanish_meaning: La victoria es cuando ganas una batalla, juego, elección o disputa.
spanish_example: El hombre estaba feliz con su victoria.
spanish_meaning_en: A victory is a situation in which you win a battle, game, election, or dispute.
spanish_example_en: The man was thrilled with his victory.

english_meaning: To be wild is to be without control.
english_example: Dandelions grow wild in the cracks of the streets.
spanish_meaning: Salvaje significa sin control.
spanish_example: Las diente de león crecen salvajes en las grietas de las calles.
spanish_meaning_en: To be wild is to be without control.
spanish_example_en: Dandelions grow wild in the cracks of the streets.

english_meaning: To wound is to injure someone or something by cutting or breaking the skin.
english_example: The boy knew he might wound his knees if he didn’t wear pads on them.
spanish_meaning: Herir es causar daño cortando o rompiendo la piel.
spanish_example: El niño sabía que podría herir sus rodillas si no llevaba rodilleras.
spanish_meaning_en: To wound is to injure someone or something by cutting or breaking the skin.
spanish_example_en: The boy knew he might wound his knees if he didn’t wear pads on them.

english_meaning: To assimilate is to adopt the ways of a new culture and fully become a part of it.
english_example: When the family moves to the US they will assimilate into American culture.
spanish_meaning: Asimilar es adoptar las costumbres de una nueva cultura e integrarse.
spanish_example: Cuando la familia se mudó a EE. UU., asimiló la cultura americana.
spanish_meaning_en: To assimilate is to adopt the ways of a new culture and fully become a part of it.
spanish_example_en: When the family moves to the US they will assimilate into American culture.

english_meaning: An attempt is an act of trying to do something, especially something difficult.
english_example: This is Ben’s second attempt to climb Mt. Everest.
spanish_meaning: Un intento es un acto de tratar de hacer algo, sobre todo algo difícil.
spanish_example: Este es el segundo intento de Ben por escalar el Everest.
spanish_meaning_en: An attempt is an act of trying to do something, especially something difficult.
spanish_example_en: This is Ben’s second attempt to climb Mt. Everest.

english_meaning: A case is an example of a particular situation or of something happening.
english_example: A deposit of $1,000 is required, but in this case, we will accept half.
spanish_meaning: Un caso es un ejemplo de una situación particular.
spanish_example: Se requiere un depósito de $1,000, pero en este caso aceptaremos la mitad.
spanish_meaning_en: A case is an example of a particular situation or of something happening.
spanish_example_en: A deposit of $1,000 is required, but in this case, we will accept half.

english_meaning: To cease is to stop doing something or to stop something happening.
english_example: The rain will cease in the afternoon.
spanish_meaning: Cesar significa detenerse o hacer que algo deje de pasar.
spanish_example: La lluvia cesará por la tarde.
spanish_meaning_en: To cease is to stop doing something or to stop something happening.
spanish_example_en: The rain will cease in the afternoon.

english_meaning: Danger is the possibility that someone or something will be harmed, destroyed, or killed.
english_example: Smoking cigarettes is a danger to health.
spanish_meaning: Peligro es la posibilidad de daño, destrucción o muerte.
spanish_example: Fumar cigarrillos es un peligro para la salud.
spanish_meaning_en: Danger is the possibility that someone or something will be harmed, destroyed, or killed.
spanish_example_en: Smoking cigarettes is a danger to health.

english_meaning: If something is devastating, it causes great harm or damage to something or someone.
english_example: The tsunami had devastating effects on the seaside village.
spanish_meaning: Devastador significa que causa mucho daño o destrucción.
spanish_example: El tsunami tuvo efectos devastadores en el pueblo costero.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is devastating, it causes great harm or damage to something or someone.
spanish_example_en: The tsunami had devastating effects on the seaside village.

english_meaning: Diversity is the quality or state of having many different forms, types, ideas, etc.
english_example: My company puts an emphasis on the importance on having cultural diversity.
spanish_meaning: La diversidad es la variedad de formas, tipos o ideas.
spanish_example: Mi empresa enfatiza la importancia de la diversidad cultural.
spanish_meaning_en: Diversity is the quality or state of having many different forms, types, ideas, etc.
spanish_example_en: My company puts an emphasis on the importance on having cultural diversity.

english_meaning: An earthquake is a sudden shaking of a part of the Earth’s surface that often causes a lot of damage.
english_example: The earthquake caused the road to crack in multiple places.
spanish_meaning: Un terremoto es un fuerte temblor de la Tierra que causa daño.
spanish_example: El terremoto hizo que la carretera se rompiera en muchos lugares.
spanish_meaning_en: An earthquake is a sudden shaking of a part of the Earth’s surface that often causes a lot of damage.
spanish_example_en: The earthquake caused the road to crack in multiple places.

english_meaning: If something is endangered, it has become very rare and may eventually die out altogether.
english_example: The whooping crane is an endangered species.
spanish_meaning: Amenazado significa que se ha vuelto muy raro y puede desaparecer.
spanish_example: El grulla cornuda es una especie en peligro de extinción.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is endangered, it has become very rare and may eventually die out altogether.
spanish_example_en: The whooping crane is an endangered species.

english_meaning: Extinction is when a particular animal, plant, type of person, custom, skill, etc. stops existing.
english_example: The extinction of the dodo bird occurred in the 1600s.
spanish_meaning: La extinción es cuando un animal, planta, tipo de persona, costumbre o habilidad deja de existir.
spanish_example: La extinción del dodo ocurrió en el siglo XVII.
spanish_meaning_en: Extinction is when a particular animal, plant, type of person, custom, skill, etc. stops existing.
spanish_example_en: The extinction of the dodo bird occurred in the 1600s.

english_meaning: We use forever in place of “for all future time” or “for a very long time.”
english_example: Michelle and Tanya want to be best friends forever.
spanish_meaning: Usamos “para siempre” para decir “para todo el tiempo que queda” o “por muchísimo tiempo”.
spanish_example: Michelle y Tanya quieren ser mejores amigas para siempre.
spanish_meaning_en: We use forever in place of “for all future time” or “for a very long time.”
spanish_example_en: Michelle and Tanya want to be best friends forever.

english_meaning: We use generally in place of “in most cases” or “to most people.”
english_example: The suburbs are generally viewed as a good place to raise children.
spanish_meaning: Generalmente significa “en la mayoría de los casos” o “para la mayoría de la gente”.
spanish_example: En general, los suburbios se ven como un buen lugar para criar hijos.
spanish_meaning_en: We use generally in place of “in most cases” or “to most people.”
spanish_example_en: The suburbs are generally viewed as a good place to raise children.

english_meaning: Genocide is the deliberate murder of a whole group or race of people.
english_example: As a result of the genocide, many children have been orphaned.
spanish_meaning: El genocidio es el asesinato deliberado de todo un grupo o una etnia de personas.
spanish_example: A causa del genocidio, muchos niños quedaron huérfanos.
spanish_meaning_en: Genocide is the deliberate murder of a whole group or race of people.
spanish_example_en: As a result of the genocide, many children have been orphaned.

english_meaning: To hit is to affect someone or something in a harmful or dangerous way.
english_example: The ship was hit by a violent storm.
spanish_meaning: Golpear o afectar a alguien o algo de forma dañina o peligrosa.
spanish_example: El barco fue impactado por una tormenta violenta.
spanish_meaning_en: To hit is to affect someone or something in a harmful or dangerous way.
spanish_example_en: The ship was hit by a violent storm.

english_meaning: To lose is to have something that is important or necessary taken from you or destroyed.
english_example: The family never thought they’d lose everything to fire.
spanish_meaning: Perder significa que algo importante se te quita o se destruye.
spanish_example: La familia nunca pensó que perdería todo en el incendio.
spanish_meaning_en: To lose is to have something that is important or necessary taken from you or destroyed.
spanish_example_en: The family never thought they’d lose everything to fire.

english_meaning: If something is native, it refers to the place someone was born and raised.
english_example: Carlos is a hero in his native country of Brazil.
spanish_meaning: Nativo se refiere al lugar donde alguien nació y creció.
spanish_example: Carlos es un héroe en su país nativo, Brasil.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is native, it refers to the place someone was born and raised.
spanish_example_en: Carlos is a hero in his native country of Brazil.

english_meaning: A percent is an amount that is equal to one one-hundredth of something.
english_example: Liz needs to get 80 percent on her test to pass the course.
spanish_meaning: El porcentaje es una cantidad igual a una parte de cada cien.
spanish_example: Liz necesita sacar un 80 por ciento para aprobar la prueba.
spanish_meaning_en: A percent is an amount that is equal to one one-hundredth of something.
spanish_example_en: Liz needs to get 80 percent on her test to pass the course.

english_meaning: A reason is a statement or fact that explains why something is the way it is, or why someone does, thinks, or says something.
english_example: The reason Eric is late for work is that he forgot to set his alarm.
spanish_meaning: Una razón es un hecho o explicación de por qué algo sucede o de por qué alguien actúa.
spanish_example: La razón por la que Eric llega tarde es que se olvidó de poner la alarma.
spanish_meaning_en: A reason is a statement or fact that explains why something is the way it is, or why someone does, thinks, or says something.
spanish_example_en: The reason Eric is late for work is that he forgot to set his alarm.

english_meaning: A reference is the act of referring to something or someone.
english_example: My supervisor made a reference to the data I’d collected.
spanish_meaning: Una referencia es la acción de aludir a algo o alguien.
spanish_example: El supervisor hizo una referencia a los datos que había recopilado.
spanish_meaning_en: A reference is the act of referring to something or someone.
spanish_example_en: My supervisor made a reference to the data I’d collected.

english_meaning: If something is urban, it is related to towns and cities.
english_example: It is common to see tall buildings in urban areas.
spanish_meaning: Lo urbano se relaciona con las ciudades y los pueblos.
spanish_example: Es común ver edificios altos en zonas urbanas.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is urban, it is related to towns and cities.
spanish_example_en: It is common to see tall buildings in urban areas.

english_meaning: To burn is to use something as a source of energy.
english_example: Running is good exercise for people who want to burn fat and lose weight.
spanish_meaning: Quemar significa usar algo como fuente de energía.
spanish_example: Correr es un buen ejercicio para quemar grasa y bajar de peso.
spanish_meaning_en: To burn is to use something as a source of energy.
spanish_example_en: Running is good exercise for people who want to burn fat and lose weight.

english_meaning: To check is to ask someone whether something is correct, true, or allowed.
english_example: Peter wants to check with the teacher to see if he is doing the assignment right.
spanish_meaning: Comprobar significa preguntar o verificar si algo es correcto, verdadero o permitido.
spanish_example: Peter quiere comprobar con el profesor si hace bien la tarea.
spanish_meaning_en: To check is to ask someone whether something is correct, true, or allowed.
spanish_example_en: Peter wants to check with the teacher to see if he is doing the assignment right.

english_meaning: A coconut is a large brown fruit that has a hard shell and white flesh.
english_example: A coconut produces a healthy oil.
spanish_meaning: El coco es una fruta grande y marrón con cáscara dura y pulpa blanca.
spanish_example: Un coco produce un aceite saludable.
spanish_meaning_en: A coconut is a large brown fruit that has a hard shell and white flesh.
spanish_example_en: A coconut produces a healthy oil.

english_meaning: Epilepsy is a medical condition that affects the brain and can make someone become unconscious or unable to control their movement for a short time.
english_example: The doctor diagnosed the patient with epilepsy.
spanish_meaning: La epilepsia es una enfermedad del cerebro que puede causar desmayos o falta de control de movimiento por poco tiempo.
spanish_example: El médico diagnosticó al paciente con epilepsia.
spanish_meaning_en: Epilepsy is a medical condition that affects the brain and can make someone become unconscious or unable to control their movement for a short time.
spanish_example_en: The doctor diagnosed the patient with epilepsy.

english_meaning: Fat is an oily solid or liquid substance in food.
english_example: The fat contained in salmon, avocados, and nuts is healthy.
spanish_meaning: La grasa es una sustancia aceitosa sólida o líquida de los alimentos.
spanish_example: La grasa del salmón, aguacate y nueces es saludable.
spanish_meaning_en: Fat is an oily solid or liquid substance in food.
spanish_example_en: The fat contained in salmon, avocados, and nuts is healthy.

english_meaning: To gain is to gradually get more and more of a quality, feeling, etc.
english_example: Winning the award will help Steven gain confidence.
spanish_meaning: Ganar significa obtener de forma gradual más cantidad de una cualidad, emoción o cosa.
spanish_example: Ganar ese premio le ayudará a Steven a ganar confianza.
spanish_meaning_en: To gain is to gradually get more and more of a quality, feeling, etc.
spanish_example_en: Winning the award will help Steven gain confidence.

english_meaning: Glucose is a natural form of sugar that exists in plants and fruit.
english_example: People with diabetes need to check their glucose levels.
spanish_meaning: La glucosa es una forma natural de azúcar que hay en plantas y frutas.
spanish_example: Las personas con diabetes deben controlar sus niveles de glucosa.
spanish_meaning_en: Glucose is a natural form of sugar that exists in plants and fruit.
spanish_example_en: People with diabetes need to check their glucose levels.

english_meaning: When something is high, it is greater than usual in amount, number, or degree.
english_example: The thermometer shows a high body temperature.
spanish_meaning: Alto significa mayor de lo normal en cantidad, número o intensidad.
spanish_example: El termómetro muestra una temperatura corporal alta.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is high, it is greater than usual in amount, number, or degree.
spanish_example_en: The thermometer shows a high body temperature.

english_meaning: To keep is to stay in a particular state, condition, or position.
english_example: Brian needs to keep working in order to finish his project on time.
spanish_meaning: Mantener significa permanecer en un estado, condición o posición específica.
spanish_example: Brian debe mantenerse trabajando para terminar su proyecto a tiempo.
spanish_meaning_en: To keep is to stay in a particular state, condition, or position.
spanish_example_en: Brian needs to keep working in order to finish his project on time.

english_meaning: If something is known, it is something that people are aware of or have discovered.
english_example: Bob is known throughout the neighborhood as a burglars.
spanish_meaning: Conocido significa que algo o alguien es sabido por muchas personas.
spanish_example: Bob es conocido en todo el vecindario como el ladrón.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is known, it is something that people are aware of or have discovered.
spanish_example_en: Bob is known throughout the neighborhood as a burglars.

english_meaning: The liver is a large organ in the body that produces bile and cleans blood.
english_example: The professor showed us what the human liver looks like.
spanish_meaning: El hígado es un órgano grande que produce bilis y limpia la sangre.
spanish_example: El profesor nos mostró cómo se ve el hígado humano.
spanish_meaning_en: The liver is a large organ in the body that produces bile and cleans blood.
spanish_example_en: The professor showed us what the human liver looks like.

english_meaning: If something is low, it is smaller than usual in amount, number, or degree.
english_example: The temperature gets very low in the winter.
spanish_meaning: Bajo significa menor que lo normal en cantidad, número o grado.
spanish_example: La temperatura baja mucho en invierno.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is low, it is smaller than usual in amount, number, or degree.
spanish_example_en: The temperature gets very low in the winter.

english_meaning: Management is the act or process of controlling and dealing with something.
english_example: Practicing yoga is good for stress management.
spanish_meaning: La gestión es el acto de controlar y manejar algo.
spanish_example: Practicar yoga ayuda a la gestión del estrés.
spanish_meaning_en: Management is the act or process of controlling and dealing with something.
spanish_example_en: Practicing yoga is good for stress management.

english_meaning: When something is metabolic, it relates to the chemical process in which plants or animals use food and water to make energy.
english_example: Fish have a very high metabolic rate.
spanish_meaning: Metabólico se refiere al proceso químico con el que plantas y animales usan alimentos y agua para obtener energía.
spanish_example: Los peces tienen una tasa metabólica muy alta.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is metabolic, it relates to the chemical process in which plants or animals use food and water to make energy.
spanish_example_en: Fish have a very high metabolic rate.

english_meaning: Oil is a smooth, thick liquid made from plants or some animals, and is especially in cooking.
english_example: The chef put the oil in the pan and heated it up.
spanish_meaning: El aceite es un líquido espeso y viscoso de origen vegetal o animal, usado mucho en cocina.
spanish_example: El cocinero puso el aceite en la sartén y lo calentó.
spanish_meaning_en: Oil is a smooth, thick liquid made from plants or some animals, and is especially in cooking.
spanish_example_en: The chef put the oil in the pan and heated it up.

english_meaning: An olive is a small egg-shaped black or green fruit.
english_example: I prepared a bowl of olives for my guests.
spanish_meaning: La aceituna es una fruta pequeña, negra o verde, con forma ovalada.
spanish_example: Preparé un tazón de aceitunas para mis invitados.
spanish_meaning_en: An olive is a small egg-shaped black or green fruit.
spanish_example_en: I prepared a bowl of olives for my guests.

english_meaning: Performance is how well someone or something functions, works, etc.
english_example: Liam’s performance in the race was not his best.
spanish_meaning: El rendimiento es la manera en que alguien o algo funciona o trabaja.

spanish_meaning_en: A diploma is a certificate proving that someone has completed their studies.

spanish_meaning_en: Geology is the study of the Earth’s natural structures and how they change.

english_meaning: If someone is keen, they are intelligent.

spanish_meaning_en: The minimum is the smallest amount.

spanish_meaning: Algo radiactivo emite radiación o está relacionado con ella.
spanish_example: Los estados radiactivos pueden ser muy peligrosos para la salud.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is radioactive, then it lets out, or is related to, radiation.

spanish_meaning: La relatividad es un conjunto de ideas sobre el tiempo y el espacio creadas por Einstein.

spanish_meaning: La sociología estudia la sociedad humana, sus organizaciones y problemas.

english_meaning: If something is subsequent, then it comes after something else in time.

spanish_meaning_en: If something is subsequent, then it comes after something else in time.
spanish_example_en: The flood and the subsequent rescue of those caught in the flood were on TV.

spanish_meaning_en: If something is synthetic, then it is made to be like something natural.
spanish_example_en: Clothing made out of synthetic fabrics is very effective at keeping people warm.

spanish_meaning_en: If something is terminal, then it causes or results in death.
spanish_example_en: Since his condition was not terminal, he felt a great sense of relief.

spanish_meaning_en: When a bad thing is acute, it is very severe and intense.

english_meaning: A creation is something original that is made.

spanish_meaning_en: A proponent is a person who supports an idea or a plan.
spanish_example_en: He was a proponent of using environmentally-friendly products.

english_meaning: To refine something is to make it better by making changes.
english_example: The principal refined his method of controlling students over the years.
spanish_meaning: Refinar significa mejorar algo mediante cambios.
spanish_example: Con los años, el director fue refinando su forma de controlar a los estudiantes.
spanish_meaning_en: To refine something is to make it better by making changes.
spanish_example_en: The principal refined his method of controlling students over the years.

english_meaning: To restrict something is to limit it and prevent it from getting bigger.
english_example: The club restricted the amount of members.
spanish_meaning: Restringir significa limitar o impedir que algo crezca.
spanish_example: El club restringió el número de miembros.
spanish_meaning_en: To restrict something is to limit it and prevent it from getting bigger.
spanish_example_en: The club restricted the amount of members.

english_meaning: A team is a group of people who work closely together.
english_example: The All Blacks are my favorite sports team.
spanish_meaning: Un equipo es un grupo de personas que trabajan juntas.
spanish_example: Los All Blacks son mi equipo deportivo favorito.
spanish_meaning_en: A team is a group of people who work closely together.
spanish_example_en: The All Blacks are my favorite sports team.

english_meaning: When something is aggregate, it is made up of smaller amounts added together.
english_example: The company totaled its aggregate sales for the entire year.
spanish_meaning: Agregado significa formado por partes menores sumadas.
spanish_example: La empresa totalizó sus ventas agregadas de todo el año.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is aggregate, it is made up of smaller amounts added together.
spanish_example_en: The company totaled its aggregate sales for the entire year.

english_meaning: An antibiotic is a medical drug used to kill bacteria and treat infections.
english_example: The doctor gave me a shot of an antibiotic when I got the flu.
spanish_meaning: Un antibiótico es un medicamento médico para matar bacterias y tratar infecciones.
spanish_example: El médico me puso una inyección de antibiótico cuando tuve gripe.
spanish_meaning_en: An antibiotic is a medical drug used to kill bacteria and treat infections.
spanish_example_en: The doctor gave me a shot of an antibiotic when I got the flu.

english_meaning: A circuit is a piece of an electronic device that allows electricity to flow.
english_example: Be very careful not to shock yourself when fixing an electrical circuit.
spanish_meaning: Un circuito es una parte de un dispositivo eléctrico que permite que pase la electricidad.
spanish_example: Al reparar un circuito eléctrico, ten cuidado para no electrocutarte.
spanish_meaning_en: A circuit is a piece of an electronic device that allows electricity to flow.
spanish_example_en: Be very careful not to shock yourself when fixing an electrical circuit.

english_meaning: To complement something or someone is to make them better.
english_example: The wool scarf complemented her lovely eyes.
spanish_meaning: Complementar significa mejorar algo o alguien.
spanish_example: El pañuelo de lana complementó sus hermosos ojos.
spanish_meaning_en: To complement something or someone is to make them better.
spanish_example_en: The wool scarf complemented her lovely eyes.

english_meaning: To compress something means to press or squeeze it so that it takes up less space.
english_example: I compressed my clothes to fit into a single suitcase.
spanish_meaning: Comprimir significa presionar o apretar para que ocupe menos espacio.
spanish_example: Comprimí mi ropa para que cupiera en una sola maleta.
spanish_meaning_en: To compress something means to press or squeeze it so that it takes up less space.
spanish_example_en: I compressed my clothes to fit into a single suitcase.

english_meaning: A database is a collection of data that is stored in a computer.
english_example: The company has a database of all the names and accounts of their customers.
spanish_meaning: Una base de datos es un conjunto de datos guardados en un ordenador.
spanish_example: La empresa tiene una base de datos con todos los nombres y cuentas de sus clientes.
spanish_meaning_en: A database is a collection of data that is stored in a computer.
spanish_example_en: The company has a database of all the names and accounts of their customers.

english_meaning: An equivalent is an amount or value that is the same as another amount or value.
english_example: I worked the equivalent of sixty hours this week.
spanish_meaning: Un equivalente es una cantidad o valor igual a otro.
spanish_example: Trabajé el equivalente a sesenta horas está semana.
spanish_meaning_en: An equivalent is an amount or value that is the same as another amount or value.
spanish_example_en: I worked the equivalent of sixty hours this week.

english_meaning: When someone is immune to a disease, they cannot be affected by it.
english_example: Children usually get shots to make them immune to certain diseases.
spanish_meaning: Inmune significa que no se puede contraer una enfermedad.
spanish_example: Los niños se vacunan para hacerse inmunes a ciertas enfermedades.
spanish_meaning_en: When someone is immune to a disease, they cannot be affected by it.
spanish_example_en: Children usually get shots to make them immune to certain diseases.

english_meaning: Input is information that is put into a computer.
english_example: Type the input into the computer program.
spanish_meaning: La entrada es la información que se introduce en un ordenador.
spanish_example: Escribe la entrada en el programa informático.
spanish_meaning_en: Input is information that is put into a computer.
spanish_example_en: Type the input into the computer program.

english_meaning: When a relationship is intimate, the two things are very closely connected.
english_example: I only tell my secrets to my most intimate friends.
spanish_meaning: Íntimo significa muy cercano o estrechamente conectado.
spanish_example: Sólo comparto mis secretos con mis amigos más íntimos.
spanish_meaning_en: When a relationship is intimate, the two things are very closely connected.
spanish_example_en: I only tell my secrets to my most intimate friends.

english_meaning: A magnet is a piece of iron or other material which attracts iron toward it.
english_example: I used a magnet to pick up the nails that were scattered on the floor.
spanish_meaning: Un imán es un material que atrae hierro.
spanish_example: Usé un imán para recoger los clavos que estaban tirados en el suelo.
spanish_meaning_en: A magnet is a piece of iron or other material which attracts iron toward it.
spanish_example_en: I used a magnet to pick up the nails that were scattered on the floor.

english_meaning: A person’s metabolism is the way chemical processes in their body use energy.
english_example: If you exercise every day, your metabolism speeds up.
spanish_meaning: El metabolismo es el proceso químico por el que el cuerpo usa energía.
spanish_example: Si haces ejercicio todos los días, tu metabolismo se acelera.
spanish_meaning_en: A person’s metabolism is the way chemical processes in their body use energy.
spanish_example_en: If you exercise every day, your metabolism speeds up.

english_meaning: A microchip is a small device in a computer that holds information.
english_example: I can put more data on my computer if I buy a more powerful microchip.
spanish_meaning: Un microchip es un dispositivo pequeño que guarda información en un dispositivo tecnológico.
spanish_example: Puedo guardar más datos en mi ordenador si compro un microchip más potente.
spanish_meaning_en: A microchip is a small device in a computer that holds information.
spanish_example_en: I can put more data on my computer if I buy a more powerful microchip.

english_meaning: A phase is a stage in a process or the gradual development of something.
english_example: The first phase in the recycling project involves finding volunteers to help out.
spanish_meaning: Una fase es una etapa en el desarrollo de algo.
spanish_example: La primera fase del proyecto de reciclaje consiste en buscar voluntarios.
spanish_meaning_en: A phase is a stage in a process or the gradual development of something.
spanish_example_en: The first phase in the recycling project involves finding volunteers to help out.

english_meaning: To pinch means to take a piece of skin between one’s fingers and squeeze.
english_example: I pinched my nose so I couldn’t smell the odor from the garbage.
spanish_meaning: Pellizcar es tomar piel entre los dedos y apretar.
spanish_example: Me pellizqué la nariz para no oler el olor de la basura.
spanish_meaning_en: To pinch means to take a piece of skin between one’s fingers and squeeze.
spanish_example_en: I pinched my nose so I couldn’t smell the odor from the garbage.

english_meaning: When something is prevalent, it is common.
english_example: Growing a beard is more prevalent in some countries than in others.
spanish_meaning: Algo prevalente es algo común o frecuente.
spanish_example: En algunos países es más prevalente llevar barba que en otros.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is prevalent, it is common.
spanish_example_en: Growing a beard is more prevalent in some countries than in others.

english_meaning: When something is quantum, it relates to the behavior of atomic particles.
english_example: The physics student studied quantum mechanics.
spanish_meaning: Cuántico se relaciona con el comportamiento de partículas atómicas.
spanish_example: El alumno de física estudió mecánica cuántica.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is quantum, it relates to the behavior of atomic particles.
spanish_example_en: The physics student studied quantum mechanics.

english_meaning: A ratio is a relationship between two things expressed in numbers or amounts.
english_example: The boy-to-girl ratio is one to three.
spanish_meaning: La proporción es la relación entre dos cosas expresada en números.
spanish_example: La proporción de niños y niñas es de uno a tres.
spanish_meaning_en: A ratio is a relationship between two things expressed in numbers or amounts.
spanish_example_en: The boy-to-girl ratio is one to three.

english_meaning: A spiral is a shape which winds round and round as if around a cylinder.
english_example: A strand of DNA looks like two interlocking spirals.
spanish_meaning: Una espiral es una forma en espiral alrededor de un eje.
spanish_example: Una hebra de ADN parece dos espirales entrelazadas.
spanish_meaning_en: A spiral is a shape which winds round and round as if around a cylinder.
spanish_example_en: A strand of DNA looks like two interlocking spirals.

english_meaning: When something is viral, it is a disease or infection that is caused by a virus.
english_example: The girl was in bed for a week when she had a viral infection.
spanish_meaning: Una enfermedad viral está causada por un virus.
spanish_example: La chica estuvo en cama una semana por una infección viral.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is viral, it is a disease or infection that is caused by a virus.
spanish_example_en: The girl was in bed for a week when she had a viral infection.

english_meaning: An acid is a chemical that can burn or dissolve other substances.
english_example: In chemistry class, we mixed two acids together and watched the reaction.
spanish_meaning: Un ácido es una sustancia química que puede quemar o disolver otras sustancias.
spanish_example: En química mezclamos dos ácidos y observamos la reacción.
spanish_meaning_en: An acid is a chemical that can burn or dissolve other substances.
spanish_example_en: In chemistry class, we mixed two acids together and watched the reaction.

english_meaning: An administration is the group of people who manage a company or organization.
english_example: She hoped she could be promoted to a job in administration.
spanish_meaning: La administración es el grupo de personas que gestionan una empresa.
spanish_example: Ella esperaba ascender a un puesto en administración.
spanish_meaning_en: An administration is the group of people who manage a company or organization.
spanish_example_en: She hoped she could be promoted to a job in administration.

english_meaning: Administrative describes anything related to managing a company or organization.
english_example: I work as an administrative assistant to the owner of the company.
spanish_meaning: Administrativo se relaciona con la gestión de una empresa o organización.
spanish_example: Trabajo como asistente administrativo del dueño de la empresa.
spanish_meaning_en: Administrative describes anything related to managing a company or organization.
spanish_example_en: I work as an administrative assistant to the owner of the company.

english_meaning: Biotechnology is the use of living parts, such as cells, in industry and technology.
english_example: Researchers at the biotechnology company use bacteria to make medicine.
spanish_meaning: La biotecnología usa componentes vivos, como células, en la industria y tecnología.
spanish_example: Los investigadores de la compañía de biotecnología usan bacterias para fabricar medicina.
spanish_meaning_en: Biotechnology is the use of living parts, such as cells, in industry and technology.
spanish_example_en: Researchers at the biotechnology company use bacteria to make medicine.

english_meaning: Cholesterol is a substance in fat, tissues, and blood of all animals.
english_example: When people have too much cholesterol, they are at a high risk for heart problems.
spanish_meaning: El colesterol es una sustancia grasa presente en tejidos y sangre.
spanish_example: Al tener demasiado colesterol, aumenta el riesgo de problemas del corazón.
spanish_meaning_en: Cholesterol is a substance in fat, tissues, and blood of all animals.
spanish_example_en: When people have too much cholesterol, they are at a high risk for heart problems.

english_meaning: A coalition is a group of people or organizations working for a common purpose.
english_example: The companies formed a coalition to make trade less expensive.
spanish_meaning: Una coalición es un grupo que trabaja junto por un objetivo común.
spanish_example: Las empresas formaron una coalición para abaratar el comercio.
spanish_meaning_en: A coalition is a group of people or organizations working for a common purpose.
spanish_example_en: The companies formed a coalition to make trade less expensive.

english_meaning: When something is deceptive, it encourages one to believe something that is false.
english_example: The scary-looking man’s appearance is deceptive; he is actually very nice.
spanish_meaning: Engañoso significa inducir a creer algo falso.
spanish_example: La apariencia de ese hombre era engañosa; en realidad era muy amable.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is deceptive, it encourages one to believe something that is false.
spanish_example_en: The scary-looking man’s appearance is deceptive; he is actually very nice.

english_meaning: Diabetes is a medical condition in which a person’s body cannot control the level of sugar in their blood.
english_example: Overweight people are more likely to suffer from diabetes than slimmer ones.
spanish_meaning: La diabetes es una enfermedad en la que el cuerpo no controla bien el azúcar en la sangre.
spanish_example: Las personas con sobrepeso tienen más riesgo de sufrir diabetes.
spanish_meaning_en: Diabetes is a medical condition in which a person’s body cannot control the level of sugar in their blood.
spanish_example_en: Overweight people are more likely to suffer from diabetes than slimmer ones.

english_meaning: To eliminate something that is unwanted means to completely remove it.
english_example: Wearing a seatbelt eliminates some of the dangers of driving a car.
spanish_meaning: Eliminar significa quitar algo por completo.
spanish_example: Usar cinturón de seguridad elimina algunos riesgos al conducir.
spanish_meaning_en: To eliminate something that is unwanted means to completely remove it.
spanish_example_en: Wearing a seatbelt eliminates some of the dangers of driving a car.

english_meaning: Erosion is the destruction of rock or soil due to flowing water or weather.
english_example: Canyons are formed because rivers of fast-moving water caused erosion.
spanish_meaning: La erosión es la destrucción de rocas o suelo por agua o clima.
spanish_example: Los cañones se forman porque el agua rápida causa erosión.
spanish_meaning_en: Erosion is the destruction of rock or soil due to flowing water or weather.
spanish_example_en: Canyons are formed because rivers of fast-moving water caused erosion.

english_meaning: Ethics are moral beliefs or rules about right or wrong.
english_example: The act of stealing certainly doesn’t go against some people’s ethics.
spanish_meaning: La ética son las creencias o normas sobre lo correcto e incorrecto.
spanish_example: Para algunas personas, robar claramente va contra la ética.
spanish_meaning_en: Ethics are moral beliefs or rules about right or wrong.
spanish_example_en: The act of stealing certainly doesn’t go against some people’s ethics.

english_meaning: If something is explicit, it is very clear, open, and truthful.
english_example: The man gave a very explicit account of the car accident.
spanish_meaning: Explícito significa claro, abierto y directo.
spanish_example: El hombre dio un relato muy explícito del accidente de coche.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is explicit, it is very clear, open, and truthful.
spanish_example_en: The man gave a very explicit account of the car accident.

english_meaning: A framework is a set of rules or ideas that people use to solve problems.
english_example: His ideas fit into the framework of a successful business plan.
spanish_meaning: Un marco es un conjunto de reglas o ideas para resolver problemas.
spanish_example: Sus ideas encajaban en el marco de un plan de negocios exitoso.
spanish_meaning_en: A framework is a set of rules or ideas that people use to solve problems.
spanish_example_en: His ideas fit into the framework of a successful business plan.

english_meaning: To manufacture something means to make it in a factory.
english_example: My father’s company manufactures steel building materials.
spanish_meaning: Fabricar significa hacer algo en una fábrica.
spanish_example: La empresa de mi padre fábrica materiales de construcción de acero.
spanish_meaning_en: To manufacture something means to make it in a factory.
spanish_example_en: My father’s company manufactures steel building materials.

english_meaning: A mechanism is a part of a machine that performs a certain function.
english_example: I can’t open my car door because the locking mechanism is broke.
spanish_meaning: Un mecanismo es una parte de una máquina que realiza una función.
spanish_example: No puedo abrir la puerta del coche porque el mecanismo de cierre está roto.
spanish_meaning_en: A mechanism is a part of a machine that performs a certain function.
spanish_example_en: I can’t open my car door because the locking mechanism is broke.

english_meaning: To minimize means to reduce something to the lowest possible level.
english_example: I checked my homework twice to minimize errors I might have made.
spanish_meaning: Minimizar significa reducir algo al nivel más bajo posible.
spanish_example: Revisé la tarea dos veces para minimizar los errores.
spanish_meaning_en: To minimize means to reduce something to the lowest possible level.
spanish_example_en: I checked my homework twice to minimize errors I might have made.

english_meaning: Nectar is a sweet liquid produced by flowers that bees and other insects collect.
english_example: Bees use nectar to make their honey.
spanish_meaning: El néctar es un líquido dulce producido por flores.
spanish_example: Las abejas usan néctar para hacer miel.
spanish_meaning_en: Nectar is a sweet liquid produced by flowers that bees and other insects collect.
spanish_example_en: Bees use nectar to make their honey.

english_meaning: A notion is an idea or belief about something.
english_example: I had a notion that this route would get us to the beach.
spanish_meaning: Una noción es una idea o creencia sobre algo.
spanish_example: Tenía la noción de que este camino nos llevaría a la playa.
spanish_meaning_en: A notion is an idea or belief about something.
spanish_example_en: I had a notion that this route would get us to the beach.

english_meaning: A regime is a system of government or management.
english_example: He was a member of the old regime that had been overthrown in the election.
spanish_meaning: Un régimen es un sistema de gobierno o de manejo.
spanish_example: Fue miembro del antiguo régimen que fue derrocado en la elección.
spanish_meaning_en: A regime is a system of government or management.
spanish_example_en: He was a member of the old regime that had been overthrown in the election.

english_meaning: When something is straightforward, it is good because it is easy to understand.
english_example: The teacher’s grading system was straightforward and fair.
spanish_meaning: Sencillo o claro significa fácil de entender.
spanish_example: El sistema de calificación del profesor era sencillo y justo.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is straightforward, it is good because it is easy to understand.
spanish_example_en: The teacher’s grading system was straightforward and fair.

english_meaning: If someone is bankrupt, then they are unable to pay their debts.
english_example: The store had few customers and soon went bankrupt.
spanish_meaning: Quedarse en quiebra significa no poder pagar las deudas.
spanish_example: La tienda tuvo pocos clientes y pronto se fue a la quiebra.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is bankrupt, then they are unable to pay their debts.
spanish_example_en: The store had few customers and soon went bankrupt.

english_meaning: To conform to rules or laws is to obey them.
english_example: The new student had to conform to the school’s dress code.
spanish_meaning: Conformarse significa obedecer normas o reglas.
spanish_example: El nuevo estudiante tuvo que cumplir el código de vestimenta de la escuela.
spanish_meaning_en: To conform to rules or laws is to obey them.
spanish_example_en: The new student had to conform to the school’s dress code.

english_meaning: To employ someone means to give work to them.
english_example: The bookstore employed two full-time clerks.
spanish_meaning: Emplear significa dar trabajo a alguien.
spanish_example: La librería emplea a dos dependientes a tiempo completo.
spanish_meaning_en: To employ someone means to give work to them.
spanish_example_en: The bookstore employed two full-time clerks.

english_meaning: To expel someone means to force them to leave a place.
english_example: Since he would not follow the rules, the principal had to expel the student.
spanish_meaning: Expulsar significa obligar a alguien a salir de un lugar.
spanish_example: Como no seguía las reglas, el director tuvo que expulsar al estudiante.
spanish_meaning_en: To expel someone means to force them to leave a place.
spanish_example_en: Since he would not follow the rules, the principal had to expel the student.

english_meaning: An extension is a part added to something to give it more time or space.
english_example: My parents decided to add an extension to our house for the new baby.
spanish_meaning: Una extensión es una parte añadida para tener más espacio o tiempo.
spanish_example: Mis padres decidieron añadir una extensión a nuestra casa para el bebé nuevo.
spanish_meaning_en: An extension is a part added to something to give it more time or space.
spanish_example_en: My parents decided to add an extension to our house for the new baby.

english_meaning: If something is forthcoming, then it is about to happen in the future.
english_example: Some economists predicted that the forthcoming world economy would be severe.
spanish_meaning: Próximo significa inminente o que ocurrirá pronto.
spanish_example: Algunos economistas predijeron que la próxima economía mundial sería grave.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is forthcoming, then it is about to happen in the future.
spanish_example_en: Some economists predicted that the forthcoming world economy would be severe.

english_meaning: To furnish means to put furniture in a house or room.
english_example: Most homes are furnished with tables, chairs, and beds.
spanish_meaning: Amueblar significa poner muebles en una casa o habitación.
spanish_example: La mayoría de las casas están amuebladas con mesas, sillas y camas.
spanish_meaning_en: To furnish means to put furniture in a house or room.
spanish_example_en: Most homes are furnished with tables, chairs, and beds.

english_meaning: Hygiene is the conditions or methods needed for health and cleanliness.
english_example: People who brush their teeth at least twice a day are practicing good hygiene.
spanish_meaning: La higiene son los hábitos y condiciones para conservar la salud y la limpieza.
spanish_example: Quienes se cepillan los dientes al menos dos veces al día practican buena higiene.
spanish_meaning_en: Hygiene is the conditions or methods needed for health and cleanliness.
spanish_example_en: People who brush their teeth at least twice a day are practicing good hygiene.

english_meaning: If something is hygienic, then it is clean and unlikely to cause disease.
english_example: My sister works very hard to keep her entire home as hygienic as possible.
spanish_meaning: Higiénico significa limpio y que no provoca enfermedad.
spanish_example: Mi hermana se esfuerza mucho para que toda su casa sea lo más higiénica posible.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is hygienic, then it is clean and unlikely to cause disease.
spanish_example_en: My sister works very hard to keep her entire home as hygienic as possible.

english_meaning: A landlord is a man who rents property to a person.
english_example: The landlord collected everyone’s rent money on the first day of every month.
spanish_meaning: Alquilar significa ser el propietario que renta una propiedad.
spanish_example: El arrendador cobró la renta de todos el primer día de cada mes.
spanish_meaning_en: A landlord is a man who rents property to a person.
spanish_example_en: The landlord collected everyone’s rent money on the first day of every month.

english_meaning: To lease means to rent property, usually an apartment or land.
english_example: When the family first leased the apartment, the rent was very low.
spanish_meaning: Alquilar significa rentar una propiedad, normalmente un departamento o terreno.
spanish_example: Cuando la familia alquiló el apartamento, la renta era muy baja.
spanish_meaning_en: To lease means to rent property, usually an apartment or land.
spanish_example_en: When the family first leased the apartment, the rent was very low.

english_meaning: If something is mandatory, then it is required by law.
english_example: It’s mandatory that everyone be at least 16 to drive a car in the US.
spanish_meaning: Algo obligatorio es requerido por ley.
spanish_example: Es obligatorio que todos tengan al menos 16 años para conducir en EE. UU.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is mandatory, then it is required by law.
spanish_example_en: It’s mandatory that everyone be at least 16 to drive a car in the US.

english_meaning: To mend something means to fix it when it is broken or damaged.
english_example: Mother mended the rip in my pants with a piece of cloth.
spanish_meaning: Reparar significa arreglar algo roto o dañado.
spanish_example: Mi madre reparó la rotura de mis pantalones con un pedazo de tela.
spanish_meaning_en: To mend something means to fix it when it is broken or damaged.
spanish_example_en: Mother mended the rip in my pants with a piece of cloth.

english_meaning: A mortgage is a loan for property, especially a home or a business.
english_example: When they bought their new home, the married couple had to sign a mortgage.
spanish_meaning: La hipoteca es un préstamo para una propiedad, especialmente una casa o un negocio.
spanish_example: Cuando compraron su casa, la pareja firmó una hipoteca.
spanish_meaning_en: A mortgage is a loan for property, especially a home or a business.
spanish_example_en: When they bought their new home, the married couple had to sign a mortgage.

english_meaning: Personnel are employees in a business.
english_example: When business increased, we had to hire more personnel.
spanish_meaning: El personal es el conjunto de empleados de un negocio.
spanish_example: Cuando el negocio creció, tuvimos que contratar más personal.
spanish_meaning_en: Personnel are employees in a business.
spanish_example_en: When business increased, we had to hire more personnel.

english_meaning: Plumbing is the system of pipes used in a home to supply water.
english_example: When the plumbing stopped working, no one was allowed to use the toilets.
spanish_meaning: La fontanería es el sistema de tuberías de una casa para suministrar agua.
spanish_example: Cuando la fontanería dejó de funcionar, nadie podía usar los baños.
spanish_meaning_en: Plumbing is the system of pipes used in a home to supply water.

spanish_example_en: A red octagon is used to signify a place to stop.

english_meaning: A strap is a thin long piece of fabric used to fasten, carry, or hold something.

spanish_meaning_en: A strap is a thin long piece of fabric used to fasten, carry, or hold something.
spanish_example_en: She put the strap of her purse over her shoulder and walked out the door.

english_meaning: Your style is the way you do things.

spanish_example: Su ropa estaba en el estilo más moderno.
spanish_meaning_en: Your style is the way you do things.
spanish_example_en: Her clothes were all in the latest style.

english_meaning: A tangle is something or many things twisted together.
english_example: The laces of his shoes were in such a tangle that he could not untie them.
spanish_meaning: Un enredo es algo o muchas cosas enredadas juntas.
spanish_example: Los cordones de sus zapatos estaban en un gran enredo.
spanish_meaning_en: A tangle is something or many things twisted together.
spanish_example_en: The laces of his shoes were in such a tangle that he could not untie them.

english_meaning: Vanity is excessive pride or love of one’s own appearance or things one has done.
english_example: Her vanity won’t allow her to pass a mirror without looking at herself.
spanish_meaning: La vanidad es orgullo excesivo por la propia apariencia o logros.
spanish_example: Su vanidad no la deja pasar ante un espejo sin mirarse.
spanish_meaning_en: Vanity is excessive pride or love of one’s own appearance or things one has done.
spanish_example_en: Her vanity won’t allow her to pass a mirror without looking at herself.

english_meaning: If something goes ashore, it goes from the water to the land.
english_example: After a long day of fishing, Glen pulled his boat ashore, so it wouldn’t float away.
spanish_meaning: A la orilla significa pasar del agua a tierra firme.
spanish_example: Tras un día de pesca, Glen llevó su bote a la orilla para que no se fuera.
spanish_meaning_en: If something goes ashore, it goes from the water to the land.
spanish_example_en: After a long day of fishing, Glen pulled his boat ashore, so it wouldn’t float away.

english_meaning: To contradict means to state the opposite of what someone else has said.
english_example: Ken was always fighting with his little sister because she kept contradicting him.
spanish_meaning: Contradecir significa decir lo contrario de lo que otra persona dijo.
spanish_example: Ken siempre peleaba con su hermana porque ella lo contradijo.
spanish_meaning_en: To contradict means to state the opposite of what someone else has said.
spanish_example_en: Ken was always fighting with his little sister because she kept contradicting him.

english_meaning: A counterpart is something that is very similar to something else in what it does.
english_example: Our manager will meet our rival company’s counterpart later today.
spanish_meaning: Un homólogo es algo muy similar a algo más en función.
spanish_example: Más tarde hoy se reunirá con el homólogo de la empresa rival.
spanish_meaning_en: A counterpart is something that is very similar to something else in what it does.
spanish_example_en: Our manager will meet our rival company’s counterpart later today.

english_meaning: When something or someone is devoid of a thing, they are missing it.
english_example: The movie was devoid of any violence, so it was a perfect movie for the family.
spanish_meaning: Cuando alguien o algo está carente de algo, le falta ese algo.
spanish_example: La película estaba carente de violencia, así que era ideal para la familia.
spanish_meaning_en: When something or someone is devoid of a thing, they are missing it.
spanish_example_en: The movie was devoid of any violence, so it was a perfect movie for the family.

english_meaning: To diverge is to become different or to follow a different direction.
english_example: The road diverged into two paths that led to our houses.
spanish_meaning: Divergir es alejarse de una dirección apropiada.
spanish_example: El camino se dividió en dos rutas que conducían a nuestras casas.
spanish_meaning_en: To diverge is to become different or to follow a different direction.
spanish_example_en: The road diverged into two paths that led to our houses.

english_meaning: An embryo is a human or animal that is still growing inside its mother.
english_example: Some doctors say that what a mother eats has a big effect on her embryo.
spanish_meaning: Un embrión es un ser humano o animal que todavía crece en la madre.
spanish_example: Algunos médicos dicen que lo que come la madre afecta mucho a su embrión.
spanish_meaning_en: An embryo is a human or animal that is still growing inside its mother.
spanish_example_en: Some doctors say that what a mother eats has a big effect on her embryo.

english_meaning: When something finally happens, it happens at the end of a series of events.
english_example: We finally reached our destination.
spanish_meaning: Finalmente significa que algo ocurre al final de una serie de eventos.
spanish_example: Finalmente llegamos a nuestro destino.
spanish_meaning_en: When something finally happens, it happens at the end of a series of events.
spanish_example_en: We finally reached our destination.

english_meaning: A gazette is a newspaper.

spanish_meaning: Una gaceta es un periódico.
spanish_example: Alice quiere escribir para una gaceta cuando sea mayor.
spanish_meaning_en: A gazette is a newspaper.
spanish_example_en: Alice wants to write for a gazette when she’s older.

english_meaning: If something is homogeneous, it is made up of things which are all the same.
english_example: All of the houses on Victor’s block were boring and homogenous.
spanish_meaning: Homogéneo significa que está hecho de cosas todas iguales.
spanish_example: Todas las casas de la calle de Victor eran aburridas y homogéneas.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is homogeneous, it is made up of things which are all the same.
spanish_example_en: All of the houses on Victor’s block were boring and homogenous.

english_meaning: An individual is a single member of a group.

spanish_meaning: Un individuo es un miembro de un grupo.
spanish_example: Cada individuo es responsable de sí mismo.
spanish_meaning_en: An individual is a single member of a group.
spanish_example_en: Each individual is responsible for themselves.

english_meaning: A major event is a very important event.

spanish_meaning: Algo mayor es muy importante o grande.
spanish_example: Hubo un cambio mayor en la política gubernamental.
spanish_meaning_en: A major event is a very important event.
spanish_example_en: There had been a major shift in government policy.

english_meaning: To obstruct something means to get in its way.
english_example: The car broke down on the road and obstructed traffic for hours.
spanish_meaning: Obstruir algo significa estorbar su camino.
spanish_example: El coche se descompuso y obstruyó el tráfico durante horas.
spanish_meaning_en: To obstruct something means to get in its way.
spanish_example_en: The car broke down on the road and obstructed traffic for hours.

english_meaning: To plunge means to move down into something very quickly.

spanish_meaning: Sumergirse es moverse rápidamente hacia abajo o dentro de algo.
spanish_example: El agua de la piscina estaba muy fría, pero el niño se sumergió de todas formas.
spanish_meaning_en: To plunge means to move down into something very quickly.
spanish_example_en: The water in the pool was very cold, but the boy plunged in anyway.

english_meaning: To prolong means to make something last for a longer time.

spanish_meaning: Prolongar significa hacer que algo dure más tiempo.

spanish_meaning_en: To prolong means to make something last for a longer time.

english_meaning: To publicize is to make something get a lot of attention.

spanish_meaning: Publicitar es hacer que algo reciba mucha atención.
spanish_example: La empresa publicitó las vacantes de trabajo en el periódico.
spanish_meaning_en: To publicize is to make something get a lot of attention.
spanish_example_en: The company publicized the job positions in the newspaper.

english_meaning: A surplus is an extra amount of something.

spanish_meaning: Un excedente es una cantidad extra de algo.
spanish_example: La tienda vendió sus productos excedentes con descuento.
spanish_meaning_en: A surplus is an extra amount of something.
spanish_example_en: The store sold their surplus items on sale.

english_meaning: When something survives, it manages to continue to live in spite of difficult circumstances.

spanish_meaning: Sobrevivir significa continuar viviendo pese a dificultades.
spanish_example: Ella sobrevivió a un infarto.
spanish_meaning_en: When something survives, it manages to continue to live in spite of difficult circumstances.
spanish_example_en: She survived a heart attack.

english_meaning: To theorize means to develop ideas about something.

spanish_meaning: Teorizar significa desarrollar ideas sobre algo.
spanish_example: Disfrutaron teorizar sobre cómo funcionaban las cosas.
spanish_meaning_en: To theorize means to develop ideas about something.

spanish_meaning: Verificar significa averiguar si algo es cierto.
spanish_example: Julio llamó al cine para verificar que la función empezaba a las nueve.
spanish_meaning_en: To verify means to find out if something is true.
spanish_example_en: Julian called the movie theater to verify that the movie started at nine.

english_meaning: If something or someone is vigorous, they use a lot of energy.

spanish_meaning: Vigoroso significa usar mucha energía.

spanish_example_en: Agatha couldn’t play the piano anymore because of her arthritis.

spanish_example_en: Dick gave a credible reason for being late and didn’t get in any trouble.

spanish_meaning: Algo viable puede hacer lo que se propone.
