spanish_meaning_en: When something is viable, it is capable of doing what it is intended to do.
spanish_example_en: Her method of solving the math problem seemed viable.

english_meaning: When something is voluntary, it is done by choice but is not required.
english_example: Many people attended the voluntary boat safety class.
spanish_meaning: Voluntario significa hecho por elección y no obligatorio.
spanish_example: Mucha gente asistió a la clase de seguridad marítima voluntaria.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is voluntary, it is done by choice but is not required.
spanish_example_en: Many people attended the voluntary boat safety class.

english_meaning: An airway is the passage by which air reaches a person’s lungs.
english_example: Since he was sick, mucus would occasionally get stuck in his airway.
spanish_meaning: La vía aérea es el pasaje por el que entra el aire a los pulmones.
spanish_example: Como estaba enfermo, la mucosidad se quedaba atrapada en su vía aérea.
spanish_meaning_en: An airway is the passage by which air reaches a person’s lungs.
spanish_example_en: Since he was sick, mucus would occasionally get stuck in his airway.

english_meaning: The Almighty is a name for a god in a religion.
english_example: Before eating, they thanked the Almighty for their food.
spanish_meaning: El Todopoderoso es un nombre para Dios en una religión.
spanish_example: Antes de comer, agradecieron al Todopoderoso por su comida.
spanish_meaning_en: The Almighty is a name for a god in a religion.
spanish_example_en: Before eating, they thanked the Almighty for their food.

english_meaning: An advent is the arrival or beginning of an important person, thing, or event.
english_example: During the advent of radio, very few programs actually played music.
spanish_meaning: Advenimiento es la llegada o comienzo de un evento importante.
spanish_example: Durante el advenimiento de la radio, pocos programas tocaban música.
spanish_meaning_en: An advent is the arrival or beginning of an important person, thing, or event.
spanish_example_en: During the advent of radio, very few programs actually played music.

english_meaning: A cycle is a series of events that go in a circle from the end back to the beginning again.
english_example: They were caught in a cycle of poverty, lack of education, and unemployment.
spanish_meaning: Un ciclo es una serie de eventos que vuelven del final al principio.
spanish_example: Quedaron atrapados en un ciclo de pobreza, falta de educación y desempleo.
spanish_meaning_en: A cycle is a series of events that go in a circle from the end back to the beginning again.
spanish_example_en: They were caught in a cycle of poverty, lack of education, and unemployment.

english_meaning: If something is definitive, then it is the most official.
english_example: This library has the definitive collection of books on ancient Egypt.
spanish_meaning: Definitivo significa lo más oficial o concluyente.
spanish_example: Esta biblioteca tiene la colección definitiva de libros sobre el antiguo Egipto.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is definitive, then it is the most official.
spanish_example_en: This library has the definitive collection of books on ancient Egypt.

english_meaning: To equate one thing with another is to compare them and consider them very similar.
english_example: She equated the man’s messy appearance with a lack of responsibility.
spanish_meaning: Equiparar una cosa con otra es compararlas y considerarlas muy parecidas.
spanish_example: Ella equiparó la apariencia desordenada del hombre con falta de responsabilidad.
spanish_meaning_en: To equate one thing with another is to compare them and consider them very similar.
spanish_example_en: She equated the man’s messy appearance with a lack of responsibility.

english_meaning: To found something on an idea or principal is to base it on that idea.
english_example: This is founded on the belief that all people are created equal.
spanish_meaning: Basarse en una idea o principio significa apoyarse en esa idea.
spanish_example: Esto se basa en la creencia de que todos los seres humanos son iguales.
spanish_meaning_en: To found something on an idea or principal is to base it on that idea.
spanish_example_en: This is founded on the belief that all people are created equal.

english_meaning: An ideology is a system of belief.
english_example: Within the teachers’ ideology, there was a belief in the value of parent participation.
spanish_meaning: Una ideología es un sistema de creencias.
spanish_example: Dentro de la ideología de los maestros había fe en la participación de los padres.
spanish_meaning_en: An ideology is a system of belief.
spanish_example_en: Within the teachers’ ideology, there was a belief in the value of parent participation.

english_meaning: To inhale means to take air or a smell into the lungs.
english_example: The camper inhaled the cool and fresh mountain air and felt relaxed.
spanish_meaning: Inhalar significa tomar aire u olor hacia los pulmones.
spanish_example: El campista inhaló el aire fresco de la montaña y se relajó.
spanish_meaning_en: To inhale means to take air or a smell into the lungs.
spanish_example_en: The camper inhaled the cool and fresh mountain air and felt relaxed.

english_meaning: To invoke something is to mention it to support an argument or ask for help.
english_example: Before going into battle, the soldier invoked the name of his god for protection.
spanish_meaning: Invocar significa mencionar o llamar en apoyo de algo.
spanish_example: Antes de la batalla, el soldado invocó el nombre de su dios para pedir protección.
spanish_meaning_en: To invoke something is to mention it to support an argument or ask for help.
spanish_example_en: Before going into battle, the soldier invoked the name of his god for protection.

english_meaning: If something is lunar, then it is related to the moon.
english_example: During a lunar eclipse, Earth’s shadow darkens the moon’s surface.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con la luna.
spanish_example: Un eclipse lunar oscurece la superficie de la luna.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is lunar, then it is related to the moon.
spanish_example_en: During a lunar eclipse, Earth’s shadow darkens the moon’s surface.

english_meaning: A novelty is something that is new, original, or strange.
english_example: Ice cream was a novelty for him since he rarely had any.
spanish_meaning: Una novedad es algo nuevo, original o extraño.
spanish_example: El helado era una novedad para él, porque raramente tenía alguno.
spanish_meaning_en: A novelty is something that is new, original, or strange.
spanish_example_en: Ice cream was a novelty for him since he rarely had any.

english_meaning: If something is outmoded, then it is no longer in fashion or use.
english_example: Black and white television sets are outmoded.
spanish_meaning: Anticuado significa que ya no está de moda o no se usa.
spanish_example: Los televisores en blanco y negro son anticuados.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is outmoded, then it is no longer in fashion or use.
spanish_example_en: Black and white television sets are outmoded.

english_meaning: To personalize something means to design it to meet a person’s unique needs.
english_example: He personalized the woman’s exercises to give her a better workout.
spanish_meaning: Personalizar algo es adaptarlo a las necesidades de una persona.
spanish_example: Ella personalizó los ejercicios de la mujer para darle un mejor entrenamiento.
spanish_meaning_en: To personalize something means to design it to meet a person’s unique needs.
spanish_example_en: He personalized the woman’s exercises to give her a better workout.

english_meaning: If something is psychiatric, then it is related to mental illness or its treatment.
english_example: The doctor said the patient might have a psychiatric problem.
spanish_meaning: Psiquiátrico se relaciona con las enfermedades mentales o su tratamiento.
spanish_example: El médico dijo que el paciente podía tener un problema psiquiátrico.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is psychiatric, then it is related to mental illness or its treatment.
spanish_example_en: The doctor said the patient might have a psychiatric problem.

english_meaning: Psychiatry is the study and treatment of mental illness.
english_example: Studying psychiatry has taught me a lot about mental illness.
spanish_meaning: La psiquiatría es el estudio y tratamiento de las enfermedades mentales.
spanish_example: Estudiar psiquiatría me enseñó mucho sobre la salud mental.
spanish_meaning_en: Psychiatry is the study and treatment of mental illness.
spanish_example_en: Studying psychiatry has taught me a lot about mental illness.

english_meaning: A reflex is an unconscious action in which a body part responds to an event.
english_example: There was a reflex when the doctor hit his knee with a rubber hammer.
spanish_meaning: Un reflejo es una respuesta automática del cuerpo.
spanish_example: Al golpearle la rodilla con un martillo, tuvo un reflejo inmediato.
spanish_meaning_en: A reflex is an unconscious action in which a body part responds to an event.
spanish_example_en: There was a reflex when the doctor hit his knee with a rubber hammer.

english_meaning: A seizure is a sudden condition in which the body loses control and shakes.
english_example: Bright flashing lights can cause seizures in children.
spanish_meaning: Una convulsión es una pérdida súbita de control del cuerpo con temblores.
spanish_example: Las luces intensas y parpadeantes pueden causar convulsiones en niños.
spanish_meaning_en: A seizure is a sudden condition in which the body loses control and shakes.
spanish_example_en: Bright flashing lights can cause seizures in children.

english_meaning: A session is a meeting at a regular time in order to discuss and solve an issue.
english_example: Every three months, the managers met in a session to discuss regional sales.
spanish_meaning: Una sesión es una reunión periódica para tratar y resolver un problema.
spanish_example: Cada tres meses, los gerentes se reunían en sesión para hablar de ventas.
spanish_meaning_en: A session is a meeting at a regular time in order to discuss and solve an issue.
spanish_example_en: Every three months, the managers met in a session to discuss regional sales.

english_meaning: A utensil is a common tool or container.
english_example: It seemed that a fork was the wrong utensil for trying to eat the soup.
spanish_meaning: Un utensilio es una herramienta o recipiente de uso común.
spanish_example: Un tenedor no era el utensilio adecuado para comer sopa.
spanish_meaning_en: A utensil is a common tool or container.
spanish_example_en: It seemed that a fork was the wrong utensil for trying to eat the soup.

english_meaning: To allocate something means to put it aside for a certain purpose or person.
english_example: The government allocated $100 million to aid the disaster relief effort.
spanish_meaning: Asignar algo significa reservarlo para un fin o persona.
spanish_example: El gobierno asignó 100 millones de dólares a la ayuda por el desastre.
spanish_meaning_en: To allocate something means to put it aside for a certain purpose or person.
spanish_example_en: The government allocated $100 million to aid the disaster relief effort.

english_meaning: When food is appetizing, it looks and smells very good.
english_example: The appetizing plate of cookies was gone in half an hour.
spanish_meaning: Algo apetitoso se ve y huele muy bien.
spanish_example: El plato de galletas apetitoso desapareció en media hora.
spanish_meaning_en: When food is appetizing, it looks and smells very good.
spanish_example_en: The appetizing plate of cookies was gone in half an hour.

english_meaning: When you assign work, you give that work to particular people.
english_example: I was assigned the job of making sure everyone had plenty to drink.
spanish_meaning: Asignar trabajo es dar tareas a personas específicas.
spanish_example: Me asignaron el trabajo de asegurar suficiente agua para todos.
spanish_meaning_en: When you assign work, you give that work to particular people.
spanish_example_en: I was assigned the job of making sure everyone had plenty to drink.

english_meaning: A cavity is a hole or space in something.
english_example: There was a small cavity in the wall of the cave, where an animal lived.
spanish_meaning: Una cavidad es un agujero o espacio en algo.
spanish_example: Había una pequeña cavidad en la pared de la cueva donde vivía un animal.
spanish_meaning_en: A cavity is a hole or space in something.
spanish_example_en: There was a small cavity in the wall of the cave, where an animal lived.

english_meaning: If something moves clockwise, it moves in a circle in the same direction as a clock.
english_example: Turn the screw clockwise to tighten it.
spanish: en el sentido de las agujas del reloj
spanish_meaning: Moverse en sentido de las agujas del reloj significa girar como una aguja de reloj.
spanish_example: Gira el tornillo en sentido de las agujas del reloj para apretarlo.
spanish_meaning_en: If something moves clockwise, it moves in a circle in the same direction as a clock.
spanish_example_en: Turn the screw clockwise to tighten it.

english_meaning: When circles or rings are concentric, they have the same center.
english_example: The target was a series of concentric circles.
spanish_meaning: Dos círculos concéntricos tienen el mismo centro.
spanish_example: El blanco era una serie de círculos concéntricos.
spanish_meaning_en: When circles or rings are concentric, they have the same center.
spanish_example_en: The target was a series of concentric circles.

english_meaning: Courtesy is the excellence of manners or social conduct.
english_example: Jenna always behaves with great courtesy when people visit her home.
spanish_meaning: La cortesía es buena educación y trato amable.
spanish_example: Jenna siempre muestra mucha cortesía cuando llega gente a su casa.
spanish_meaning_en: Courtesy is the excellence of manners or social conduct.
spanish_example_en: Jenna always behaves with great courtesy when people visit her home.

english_meaning: When food is crisp, it is hard or has a hard surface in a way that is pleasant.
english_example: My favorite snack is a bag of crisp, delicious potato chips.
spanish_meaning: Algo crujiente tiene superficie dura de forma agradable.
spanish_example: Mi merienda favorita es una bolsa de papas crujientes y deliciosas.
spanish_meaning_en: When food is crisp, it is hard or has a hard surface in a way that is pleasant.
spanish_example_en: My favorite snack is a bag of crisp, delicious potato chips.

english_meaning: Discord is disagreement or fighting.
english_example: There was much discord between the experts on the talk show.
spanish_meaning: La discordia es desacuerdo o disputa.
spanish_example: Hubo mucha discordia entre los expertos en el programa de televisión.
spanish_meaning_en: Discord is disagreement or fighting.
spanish_example_en: There was much discord between the experts on the talk show.

english_meaning: To generate something means to cause it to develop or begin.
english_example: The mayor promised to generate new jobs and programs to help the poor.
spanish_meaning: Generar algo significa hacer que algo aparezca o empiece.
spanish_example: El alcalde prometió generar nuevos empleos y programas para ayudar a los pobres.
spanish_meaning_en: To generate something means to cause it to develop or begin.
spanish_example_en: The mayor promised to generate new jobs and programs to help the poor.

english_meaning: An item is a single separate piece.
english_example: There were some items of clothing found at the crime scene.
spanish_meaning: Un ítem es una pieza única y separada.
spanish_example: Encontraron algunos ítems de ropa en la escena del crimen.
spanish_meaning_en: An item is a single separate piece.
spanish_example_en: There were some items of clothing found at the crime scene.

english_meaning: An interchange of ideas between people is a discussion of each person’s idea.
english_example: There was an interchange of ideas between the groups.
spanish_meaning: El intercambio es una discusión de ideas entre personas.
spanish_example: Hubo un intercambio de ideas entre los grupos.
spanish_meaning_en: An interchange of ideas between people is a discussion of each person’s idea.
spanish_example_en: There was an interchange of ideas between the groups.

english_meaning: When something is multicultural, it relates to many different cultures.
english_example: Everyone was welcome to attend the multicultural celebration.
spanish_meaning: Lo multicultural se relaciona con muchas culturas.
spanish_example: Todo el mundo fue bienvenido al evento multicultural.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is multicultural, it relates to many different cultures.
spanish_example_en: Everyone was welcome to attend the multicultural celebration.

english_meaning: An omission is something that has been left out or not done.
english_example: Mike was upset because of the omission of his name during the ceremony.
spanish_meaning: La omisión es algo que se dejó fuera o no se hizo.
spanish_example: Mike se molestó por la omisión de su nombre durante la ceremonia.
spanish_meaning_en: An omission is something that has been left out or not done.
spanish_example_en: Mike was upset because of the omission of his name during the ceremony.

english_meaning: To oversee something means to make sure that it is being done properly.
english_example: His job was to oversee the progress of the construction project.
spanish_meaning: Supervisar algo es asegurarse de que se haga bien.
spanish_example: Su trabajo era supervisar el progreso del proyecto de construcción.
spanish_meaning_en: To oversee something means to make sure that it is being done properly.
spanish_example_en: His job was to oversee the progress of the construction project.

english_meaning: To pierce something means to make a hole in it using a sharp object.
english_example: The arrow pierced the target in the very center.
spanish_meaning: Perforar significa hacer un agujero con un objeto punzante.
spanish_example: La flecha perforó el blanco justo en el centro.
spanish_meaning_en: To pierce something means to make a hole in it using a sharp object.
spanish_example_en: The arrow pierced the target in the very center.

english_meaning: When you select something, you carefully choose it.
english_example: They had to select textbooks for each class.
spanish_meaning: Seleccionar significa elegir con cuidado.
spanish_example: Tuvieron que seleccionar libros de texto para cada clase.
spanish_meaning_en: When you select something, you carefully choose it.
spanish_example_en: They had to select textbooks for each class.

english_meaning: The structure involves the shape and organization of the parts of a building or group.
english_example: There are big differences in family structure in different cultures.
spanish_meaning: La estructura es la forma y organización de las partes de algo.
spanish_example: Existen grandes diferencias en la estructura familiar entre culturas.
spanish_meaning_en: The structure involves the shape and organization of the parts of a building or group.
spanish_example_en: There are big differences in family structure in different cultures.

english_meaning: Visual relates to seeing.
english_example: The visual displays were very attractive.
spanish_meaning: Algo visual se relaciona con la vista.
spanish_example: Las imágenes visuales eran muy atractivas.
spanish_meaning_en: Visual relates to seeing.
spanish_example_en: The visual displays were very attractive.

english_meaning: When something is wavy, it is not straight but has a series of curves.
english_example: The child drew wavy lines all over the piece of paper.
spanish_meaning: Algo ondulado no es recto y tiene curvas.
spanish_example: El niño dibujó líneas onduladas por toda la hoja.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is wavy, it is not straight but has a series of curves.
spanish_example_en: The child drew wavy lines all over the piece of paper.

english_meaning: Conversion is the act of changing something into a different state or form.
english_example: The city discussed the conversion of the parking lot into a skateboard park.
spanish_meaning: La conversión es el cambio de un estado o forma a otro.
spanish_example: El barrio discutió la conversión del aparcamiento en parque de skate.
spanish_meaning_en: Conversion is the act of changing something into a different state or form.
spanish_example_en: The city discussed the conversion of the parking lot into a skateboard park.

english_meaning: To cram things means to put them into a place that can barely contain them.
english_example: He crammed all of his shirts into the top drawer of the dresser.
spanish_meaning: Amontonar cosas significa ponerlas en un lugar que apenas las contiene.
spanish_example: Metió todas sus camisas en el cajón superior.
spanish_meaning_en: To cram things means to put them into a place that can barely contain them.
spanish_example_en: He crammed all of his shirts into the top drawer of the dresser.

english_meaning: To defer means to arrange for an action to happen at a later time.
english_example: The girl was very sleepy, so she chose to defer her bath until morning.
spanish_meaning: Aplazar significa dejar una acción para más tarde.
spanish_example: La niña estaba muy cansada y decidió aplazar su baño hasta la mañana.
spanish_meaning_en: To defer means to arrange for an action to happen at a later time.
spanish_example_en: The girl was very sleepy, so she chose to defer her bath until morning.

english_meaning: To export products means to sell them to other countries.
english_example: The United States exports many cereals and grains.
spanish_meaning: Exportar productos es venderlos en otros países.
spanish_example: Estados Unidos exporta muchos cereales y granos.
spanish_meaning_en: To export products means to sell them to other countries.
spanish_example_en: The United States exports many cereals and grains.

english_meaning: Fumes are unhealthy smoke and gases that are made by fires or chemicals.
english_example: The engine put unpleasant, black fumes into the air.
spanish_meaning: Los vapores son humos y gases dañinos producidos por fuego o químicos.
spanish_example: El motor emitió vapores negros desagradables al aire.
spanish_meaning_en: Fumes are unhealthy smoke and gases that are made by fires or chemicals.
spanish_example_en: The engine put unpleasant, black fumes into the air.

english_meaning: When something is habitual, it is a behavior that a person usually does or has.
english_example: The man was a habitual liar who was incapable of being honest with anyone.
spanish_meaning: Una conducta habitual es algo que alguien suele hacer.
spanish_example: El hombre era un mentiroso habitual e incapaz de ser honesto.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is habitual, it is a behavior that a person usually does or has.
spanish_example_en: The man was a habitual liar who was incapable of being honest with anyone.

english_meaning: To justify something means to show or prove that it is necessary.
english_example: The government tried to justify its decision to bring the country into a war.
spanish_meaning: Justificar algo es mostrar o probar que es necesario.
spanish_example: El gobierno trató de justificar su decisión de llevar al país a la guerra.
spanish_meaning_en: To justify something means to show or prove that it is necessary.
spanish_example_en: The government tried to justify its decision to bring the country into a war.

english_meaning: We use notwithstanding when we want to show that, although something has been done, it is not enough. A similar term is “in spite of”.
english_example: Notwithstanding your objections, we have decided to move forward with the project.
spanish_meaning: Usamos a pesar de para indicar que algo ocurre aunque otra cosa ya se haya hecho.
spanish_example: A pesar de tus objeciones, decidimos avanzar con el proyecto.
spanish_meaning_en: We use notwithstanding when we want to show that, although something has been done, it is not enough. A similar term is “in spite of”.
spanish_example_en: Notwithstanding your objections, we have decided to move forward with the project.

english_meaning: Output is the amount of something that a person or thing produces.
english_example: The boss hired more workers to increase the factory’s output of products.
spanish_meaning: La producción es la cantidad que una persona o cosa genera.
spanish_example: El jefe contrató más trabajadores para aumentar la producción de la fábrica.
spanish_meaning_en: Output is the amount of something that a person or thing produces.
spanish_example_en: The boss hired more workers to increase the factory’s output of products.

english_meaning: Overpopulation is the state of having too many people in an area.
english_example: The organization is concerned with the overpopulation of the world.
spanish_meaning: La sobrepoblación es la situación de tener demasiada gente en un área.
spanish_example: La organización está preocupada por la sobrepoblación mundial.
spanish_meaning_en: Overpopulation is the state of having too many people in an area.
spanish_example_en: The organization is concerned with the overpopulation of the world.

english_meaning: A patent is a right to be the only person allowed to make or sell a new product.
english_example: He quickly established a patent for his brilliant invention.
spanish_meaning: Una patente es el derecho exclusivo para fabricar o vender un producto nuevo.
spanish_example: Rápidamente registró una patente para su brillante invención.
spanish_meaning_en: A patent is a right to be the only person allowed to make or sell a new product.
spanish_example_en: He quickly established a patent for his brilliant invention.

english_meaning: To penalize someone means to punish him or her.
english_example: The team was penalized when they broke the rules.
spanish_meaning: Penalizar a alguien significa castigarlo.
spanish_example: El equipo fue penalizado por romper las reglas.
spanish_meaning_en: To penalize someone means to punish him or her.
spanish_example_en: The team was penalized when they broke the rules.

english_meaning: Petroleum is a liquid natural resource from which many fuels are made.
english_example: The petroleum at that factory is used to make gasoline.
spanish_meaning: El petróleo es un recurso natural líquido del que se hacen muchos combustibles.
spanish_example: El petróleo de esa fábrica se usa para producir gasolina.
spanish_meaning_en: Petroleum is a liquid natural resource from which many fuels are made.
spanish_example_en: The petroleum at that factory is used to make gasoline.

english_meaning: A region is a large area of land usually based on some common feature.
english_example: This is the wheat-growing region of the country.
spanish_meaning: Una región es una gran zona de tierra con rasgos comunes.
spanish_example: Esta es la región cerealera del país.
spanish_meaning_en: A region is a large area of land usually based on some common feature.
spanish_example_en: This is the wheat-growing region of the country.

english_meaning: A scrap of something is a small amount of it.
english_example: Chris liked to use many scraps of paper when thinking up ideas.
spanish_meaning: Un scrap es una pequeña cantidad de algo.
spanish_example: A Chris le gustaba usar muchos recortes de papel para pensar ideas.
spanish_meaning_en: A scrap of something is a small amount of it.
spanish_example_en: Chris liked to use many scraps of paper when thinking up ideas.

english_meaning: A sector is a part of a country’s economy in a specific type of industry.
english_example: We learned about just a few of the factories within the manufacturing sector.
spanish_meaning: Un sector es una parte de la economía de un país dedicada a una industria.
spanish_example: Aprendimos sobre algunas fábricas del sector manufacturero.
spanish_meaning_en: A sector is a part of a country’s economy in a specific type of industry.
spanish_example_en: We learned about just a few of the factories within the manufacturing sector.

english_meaning: To subscribe to something is to agree with it.
english_example: She subscribed to the view that musical education should be kept in schools.
spanish_meaning: Suscribirse a algo es estar de acuerdo con ello.
spanish_example: Ella se suscribió a la idea de mantener la educación musical en las escuelas.
spanish_meaning_en: To subscribe to something is to agree with it.
spanish_example_en: She subscribed to the view that musical education should be kept in schools.

english_meaning: To subsist means to have the food, water, and money needed to stay alive.
english_example: Some people are forced to subsist on only a few dollars a month.

spanish_meaning: Dañar o arruinar algo es arruinarlo.
spanish_example: Los adolescentes destrozaron la casa sin motivo.
spanish_meaning_en: To wreck something means to destroy or ruin it.
spanish_example_en: The teenagers wrecked the house for no reason at all.

english_meaning: To admonish someone is to tell them you disapprove of their behavior.
english_example: The teacher admonished Mark because he was chewing gum in class.
spanish_meaning: Amonestar a alguien es reprenderlo por su conducta.
spanish_example: El maestro amonestó a Mark porque mascaba chicle en clase.
spanish_meaning_en: To admonish someone is to tell them you disapprove of their behavior.
spanish_example_en: The teacher admonished Mark because he was chewing gum in class.

english_meaning: If something is audible, then it is able to be heard.
english_example: The sound of the drums was audible from miles away.
spanish_meaning: Algo audible puede oírse.
spanish_example: El sonido de los tambores se oía desde varios kilómetros.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is audible, then it is able to be heard.
spanish_example_en: The sound of the drums was audible from miles away.

english_meaning: If things or people are awesome, they are impressive or frightening.
english_example: The huge military plane was an awesome sight.
spanish_meaning: Algo impresionante es asombroso o impactante.
spanish_example: El enorme avión militar era una vista impresionante.
spanish_meaning_en: If things or people are awesome, they are impressive or frightening.
spanish_example_en: The huge military plane was an awesome sight.

english_meaning: To beware means to be careful of something or someone that is dangerous.
english_example: You should beware of driving fast on wet roads.
spanish_meaning: Tener cuidado de alguien es prevenir un peligro.
spanish_example: Debes cuidarte de conducir rápido en carreteras mojadas.
spanish_meaning_en: To beware means to be careful of something or someone that is dangerous.
spanish_example_en: You should beware of driving fast on wet roads.

english_meaning: To brag means to talk of one’s abilities or achievements in a proud way.
english_example: He had strong muscles and bragged about them to the entire class.
spanish_meaning: Presumir es hablar con orgullo de las propias habilidades o logros.
spanish_example: Él presumió de sus músculos ante toda la clase.
spanish_meaning_en: To brag means to talk of one’s abilities or achievements in a proud way.
spanish_example_en: He had strong muscles and bragged about them to the entire class.

english_meaning: If someone is conscious of something, then they are aware of it.
english_example: The new student was conscious of the other students staring at her.
spanish_meaning: Ser consciente de algo significa darse cuenta.
spanish_example: La nueva estudiante era consciente de que todos la estaban mirando.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is conscious of something, then they are aware of it.
spanish_example_en: The new student was conscious of the other students staring at her.

english_meaning: To disagree with someone means to have a different opinion from them.
english_example: The lawyers disagreed about the best way to settle the case.
spanish_meaning: Disentir significa tener una opinión diferente.
spanish_example: Los abogados disentían sobre la mejor forma de resolver el caso.
spanish_meaning_en: To disagree with someone means to have a different opinion from them.
spanish_example_en: The lawyers disagreed about the best way to settle the case.

english_meaning: To echo means that a sound repeats itself because it bounced off an object.
english_example: The child yelled over the canyon, and the sound echoed off the wall.
spanish_meaning: Un eco es la repetición de un sonido al rebotar.
spanish_example: El niño gritó en el cañón y su voz hizo eco en la pared.
spanish_meaning_en: To echo means that a sound repeats itself because it bounced off an object.
spanish_example_en: The child yelled over the canyon, and the sound echoed off the wall.

english_meaning: If something is eventual, it will happen at the end of a series of events.
english_example: The constant training and planning led the team to an eventual victory.
spanish_meaning: Algo eventual sucede al final de una cadena de hechos.
spanish_example: El entrenamiento y la planificación llevaron al equipo a una victoria final.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is eventual, it will happen at the end of a series of events.
spanish_example_en: The constant training and planning led the team to an eventual victory.

english_meaning: A hint is information that suggests something will happen or is true.
english_example: I quietly passed on a hint to my sister about the test.
spanish_meaning: Una sugerencia es una pista de que algo es probable o verdadero.
spanish_example: Le di una sugerencia a mi hermana sobre el examen.
spanish_meaning_en: A hint is information that suggests something will happen or is true.
spanish_example_en: I quietly passed on a hint to my sister about the test.

english_meaning: An idiot is a person who is not smart or who has done something silly.
english_example: Because he got lost in the forest, the man felt like an idiot.
spanish_meaning: Un idiota es una persona poco inteligente o que hace algo tonto.
spanish_example: Como se perdió en el bosque, el hombre se sintió un idiota.
spanish_meaning_en: An idiot is a person who is not smart or who has done something silly.
spanish_example_en: Because he got lost in the forest, the man felt like an idiot.

english_meaning: If something is immense, it is very large.
english_example: An immense amount of money was needed to buy such a large boat.
spanish_meaning: Algo inmenso es de un tamaño muy grande.
spanish_example: Se necesitó una cantidad inmensa de dinero para comprar ese barco.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is immense, it is very large.
spanish_example_en: An immense amount of money was needed to buy such a large boat.

english_meaning: If something is indirect, then it is not the easiest or straightest way.
english_example: He chose to take the most indirect route to the coast.
spanish_meaning: Indirecto significa no ser el camino más directo.
spanish_example: Eligió la ruta más indirecta hasta la costa.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is indirect, then it is not the easiest or straightest way.
spanish_example_en: He chose to take the most indirect route to the coast.

english_meaning: An option is a choice between two or more things.
english_example: The children were given the option of three houses to pick from.
spanish_meaning: Una opción es elegir entre dos o más cosas.
spanish_example: A los niños les dieron la opción de elegir entre tres casas.
spanish_meaning_en: An option is a choice between two or more things.
spanish_example_en: The children were given the option of three houses to pick from.

english_meaning: A pastime is an activity that you do often for fun.
english_example: In the US, baseball is considered a national pastime.
spanish_meaning: Un pasatiempo es una actividad recreativa frecuente.
spanish_example: En Estados Unidos, el béisbol se considera un pasatiempo nacional.
spanish_meaning_en: A pastime is an activity that you do often for fun.
spanish_example_en: In the US, baseball is considered a national pastime.

english_meaning: If something is perfect, then it is without any mistakes.
english_example: She got all the questions right, so her score was perfect.
spanish_meaning: Perfecto significa sin errores.
spanish_example: Sacó todas las respuestas bien, así que su puntuación fue perfecta.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is perfect, then it is without any mistakes.
spanish_example_en: She got all the questions right, so her score was perfect.

english_meaning: To pinpoint something means to locate it exactly.
english_example: The navigation system in my car is able to pinpoint my exact location.
spanish_meaning: Señalar con precisión significa ubicar algo exactamente.
spanish_example: El navegador de mi coche puede señalar mi ubicación exacta.
spanish_meaning_en: To pinpoint something means to locate it exactly.
spanish_example_en: The navigation system in my car is able to pinpoint my exact location.

english_meaning: To switch means to change something to something else.
english_example: Mom switched the TV station from the news to her favorite show.
spanish_meaning: Cambiar significa pasar de una cosa a otra.
spanish_example: La madre cambió el canal de las noticias a su programa favorito.
spanish_meaning_en: To switch means to change something to something else.
spanish_example_en: Mom switched the TV station from the news to her favorite show.

english_meaning: If something is thorough, it is complete or done carefully.
english_example: Tina did a thorough job of cleaning the stains out of the carpet.
spanish_meaning: Exhaustivo significa completo y cuidadosamente hecho.
spanish_example: Tina hizo un trabajo exhaustivo limpiando las manchas de la alfombra.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is thorough, it is complete or done carefully.
spanish_example_en: Tina did a thorough job of cleaning the stains out of the carpet.

english_meaning: To torment someone means to cause them to suffer on purpose.
english_example: She tormented her little brother by taking his favorite toy.
spanish_meaning: Atormentar a alguien es hacerle sufrir a propósito.
spanish_example: Ella atormentaba a su hermano pequeño quitándole su juguete favorito.
spanish_meaning_en: To torment someone means to cause them to suffer on purpose.
spanish_example_en: She tormented her little brother by taking his favorite toy.

english_meaning: If something is abrupt, it is sudden or unexpected.
english_example: When the dancer fell down on her back, the ballet came to an abrupt stop.
spanish_meaning: Repentino significa súbito e inesperado.
spanish_example: Cuando la bailarina cayó hacia atrás, el ballet se detuvo bruscamente.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is abrupt, it is sudden or unexpected.
spanish_example_en: When the dancer fell down on her back, the ballet came to an abrupt stop.

english_meaning: Agony is severe pain or suffering.
english_example: The boy yelled in agony when the puppy bit him.
spanish_meaning: La agonía es dolor o sufrimiento intenso.
spanish_example: El niño gritó de agonía cuando el perro pequeño le mordió.
spanish_meaning_en: Agony is severe pain or suffering.
spanish_example_en: The boy yelled in agony when the puppy bit him.

english_meaning: An assassin is someone who murders an important person.
english_example: An assassin killed Martin Luther King on April 4, 1968.
spanish_meaning: Un asesino es quien mata a una persona importante.
spanish_example: Un asesino mató a Martin Luther King el 4 de abril de 1968.
spanish_meaning_en: An assassin is someone who murders an important person.
spanish_example_en: An assassin killed Martin Luther King on April 4, 1968.

english_meaning: A beard is hair that grows on a man’s chin and cheeks.
english_example: The farmer had a long beard, as white as snow.
spanish_meaning: La barba es el vello del mentón y las mejillas de un hombre.
spanish_example: El granjero tenía una barba larga, blanca como la nieve.
spanish_meaning_en: A beard is hair that grows on a man’s chin and cheeks.
spanish_example_en: The farmer had a long beard, as white as snow.

english_meaning: A beast is a large and dangerous animal.
english_example: Tanzania is home to many famous beasts, such as lions, leopards, and hyenas.
spanish_meaning: Una bestia es un animal grande y peligroso.
spanish_example: Tanzania alberga muchas bestias famosas como leones y leopardos.
spanish_meaning_en: A beast is a large and dangerous animal.
spanish_example_en: Tanzania is home to many famous beasts, such as lions, leopards, and hyenas.

english_meaning: A chapel is a building where people go to pray and worship God.
english_example: We were married in a small wedding chapel in Las Vegas.
spanish_meaning: La capilla es un lugar para rezar y adorar a Dios.
spanish_example: Nos casamos en una pequeña capilla en Las Vegas.
spanish_meaning_en: A chapel is a building where people go to pray and worship God.
spanish_example_en: We were married in a small wedding chapel in Las Vegas.

english_meaning: A commentator is a person who gives opinions or describes something, often on TV or the radio.
english_example: According to many commentators, this winter has been one of the coldest in the last century.
spanish_meaning: Un comentarista da opiniones o describe algo, a menudo por TV o radio.
spanish_example: Según muchos comentaristas, este invierno fue de los más fríos del último siglo.
spanish_meaning_en: A commentator is a person who gives opinions or describes something, often on TV or the radio.
spanish_example_en: According to many commentators, this winter has been one of the coldest in the last century.

english_meaning: To con someone is to trick that person into doing something or giving up money.
english_example: It was unfair of the fortune teller to con me out of 500 dollars.
spanish_meaning: Engañar a alguien es hacerle una trampa para que haga algo o entregue dinero.
spanish_example: Fue injusto que el adivino me engañara y me quitara 500 dólares.
spanish_meaning_en: To con someone is to trick that person into doing something or giving up money.
spanish_example_en: It was unfair of the fortune teller to con me out of 500 dollars.

english_meaning: A consensus is a general agreement among a group of people.
english_example: The consensus among the children was that ghosts exist.
spanish_meaning: El consenso es un acuerdo general entre un grupo.
spanish_example: El consenso entre los niños fue que los fantasmas existen.
spanish_meaning_en: A consensus is a general agreement among a group of people.
spanish_example_en: The consensus among the children was that ghosts exist.

english_meaning: A conspiracy is a secret plan that two or more people make together to do something harmful or illegal.
english_example: The businessman thought there was a conspiracy among his coworkers to get him fired.
spanish_meaning: Una conspiración es un plan secreto de varias personas para hacer daño o algo ilegal.
spanish_example: El empresario pensó que había una conspiración entre sus colegas para despedirlo.
spanish_meaning_en: A conspiracy is a secret plan that two or more people make together to do something harmful or illegal.
spanish_example_en: The businessman thought there was a conspiracy among his coworkers to get him fired.

english_meaning: A coup is an uprising in which people try to overthrow the government.
english_example: At the end of the 18th century, Napoleon Bonaparte staged a coup to overthrow a group that had control of France.
spanish_meaning: Un golpe de Estado es la rebelión para derrocar al gobierno.
spanish_example: Al final del siglo XVIII, Napoleón dio un golpe para tomar el poder en Francia.
spanish_meaning_en: A coup is an uprising in which people try to overthrow the government.
spanish_example_en: At the end of the 18th century, Napoleon Bonaparte staged a coup to overthrow a group that had control of France.

english_meaning: To be cynical is to be unwilling to believe that people have good intentions.
english_example: The man became cynical after being lied to many times.
spanish_meaning: Una persona cínica duda de las buenas intenciones de otros.
spanish_example: El hombre se volvió cínico tras ser engañado muchas veces.
spanish_meaning_en: To be cynical is to be unwilling to believe that people have good intentions.
spanish_example_en: The man became cynical after being lied to many times.

english_meaning: A dome is a curved roof of a building.
english_example: I looked up at the dome to admire its beauty.
spanish_meaning: Una cúpula es una cubierta curva de un edificio.
spanish_example: Observé la cúpula para admirar su belleza.
spanish_meaning_en: A dome is a curved roof of a building.
spanish_example_en: I looked up at the dome to admire its beauty.

english_meaning: To exclaim something is to say it loudly, suddenly, and excitedly.
english_example: The singer interrupted the applause to exclaim his thanks after his performance.
spanish_meaning: Exclamar es decir algo de manera súbita y enérgica.
spanish_example: El cantante interrumpió el aplauso para exclamar su agradecimiento.
spanish_meaning_en: To exclaim something is to say it loudly, suddenly, and excitedly.
spanish_example_en: The singer interrupted the applause to exclaim his thanks after his performance.

english_meaning: A forum is an open public place in which meetings are held.
english_example: The scientist shared her research at a special forum in the city.
spanish_meaning: Un foro es un espacio público para debates y reuniones.
spanish_example: La científica compartió su investigación en un foro especial de la ciudad.
spanish_meaning_en: A forum is an open public place in which meetings are held.
spanish_example_en: The scientist shared her research at a special forum in the city.

english_meaning: Glamour is a quality relating to riches, wealth, and beauty.
english_example: The young woman showed of her glamour during the photo shoot.
spanish_meaning: El glamour es una cualidad ligada a belleza, lujo y atractivo.
spanish_example: La joven mostró su glamour durante la sesión de fotos.
spanish_meaning_en: Glamour is a quality relating to riches, wealth, and beauty.
spanish_example_en: The young woman showed of her glamour during the photo shoot.

english_meaning: To harass someone is to bother or attack them repeatedly.
english_example: The young girl would often harass her baby brother.
spanish_meaning: Hostigar a alguien es molestarlo o atacarlo repetidamente.
spanish_example: La niña pequeña molestaba con frecuencia a su hermano menor.
spanish_meaning_en: To harass someone is to bother or attack them repeatedly.
spanish_example_en: The young girl would often harass her baby brother.

english_meaning: If you illuminate something, you shine light on or brighten it.
english_example: In order to illuminate the stage, the crew turned on the spotlight.
spanish_meaning: Iluminar algo es darle luz o hacerlo más claro.
spanish_example: Para iluminar el escenario, el equipo encendió el foco.
spanish_meaning_en: If you illuminate something, you shine light on or brighten it.
spanish_example_en: In order to illuminate the stage, the crew turned on the spotlight.

english_meaning: To inflame something is to provoke or intensify it.
english_example: The tax increase will infame the public’s frustration with the government.
spanish_meaning: Inflamar algo es provocar o intensificarlo.
spanish_example: El aumento de impuestos inflama la frustración del público.
spanish_meaning_en: To inflame something is to provoke or intensify it.
spanish_example_en: The tax increase will infame the public’s frustration with the government.

english_meaning: A melody is a series of musical notes that form the main part of a song.
english_example: I had the melody of the song stuck in my head all day.
spanish_meaning: La melodía es una secuencia de notas que forma la parte principal de una canción.
spanish_example: Tuve la melodía de la canción pegada en la cabeza todo el día.
spanish_meaning_en: A melody is a series of musical notes that form the main part of a song.
spanish_example_en: I had the melody of the song stuck in my head all day.

english_meaning: If you do something awhile, you do it for a short time.
english_example: Wait here awhile, and I’ll bring some tea.
spanish_meaning: Hacer algo un rato significa hacerlo durante un corto tiempo.
spanish_example: Espera aquí un rato y te traeré té.
spanish_meaning_en: If you do something awhile, you do it for a short time.
spanish_example_en: Wait here awhile, and I’ll bring some tea.

english_meaning: Cyberspace is the imaginary place on the Internet where activities occur.
english_example: I didn’t receive your email. It probably got lost in cyberspace.
spanish_meaning: El ciberespacio es el espacio virtual de internet.
spanish_example: No recibí tu correo; seguramente se perdió en el ciberespacio.
spanish_meaning_en: Cyberspace is the imaginary place on the Internet where activities occur.
spanish_example_en: I didn’t receive your email. It probably got lost in cyberspace.

english_meaning: To edit means to correct a piece of writing so that it is suitable to be published.
english_example: She asked her brother to edit her paper before she submitted it to her teacher.
spanish_meaning: Editar significa corregir un texto para que esté listo para publicarse.
spanish_example: Pidió a su hermano que editara su ensayo antes de entregarlo.
spanish_meaning_en: To edit means to correct a piece of writing so that it is suitable to be published.
spanish_example_en: She asked her brother to edit her paper before she submitted it to her teacher.

english_meaning: An essay is a short piece of writing on a certain subject.
english_example: She had to write a two-page essay for her English class.
spanish_meaning: Un ensayo es un texto breve sobre un tema.
spanish_example: Ella tuvo que escribir un ensayo de dos páginas para su clase de inglés.
spanish_meaning_en: An essay is a short piece of writing on a certain subject.
spanish_example_en: She had to write a two-page essay for her English class.

english_meaning: To evaluate something means to study it, so a decision can be made.
english_example: Jo and Ken evaluated the plan and agreed that it would work.
spanish_meaning: Evaluar algo significa analizarlo para decidir algo.
spanish_example: Jo y Ken evaluaron el plan y acordaron que funcionaba.
spanish_meaning_en: To evaluate something means to study it, so a decision can be made.
spanish_example_en: Jo and Ken evaluated the plan and agreed that it would work.

english_meaning: To faint means to go unconscious and fall down.

spanish_meaning: Desmayarse significa perder el conocimiento y caer.
spanish_example: Carol se desmayó porque llevaba más de un día sin comer.
spanish_meaning_en: To faint means to go unconscious and fall down.
spanish_example_en: Carol fainted because she hadn’t eaten in over a day.

english_meaning: If something is global, it happens all around the world.

spanish_meaning: Algo global ocurre en todo el mundo.
spanish_example: La contaminación es un problema global.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is global, it happens all around the world.

english_meaning: A gymnasium is a building with equipment that you can use to get exercise.
english_example: When I go to the gymnasium, I use the treadmill for twenty minutes.
spanish_meaning: Un gimnasio es un edificio con equipos para hacer ejercicio.
spanish_example: Cuando voy al gimnasio, uso la cinta por veinte minutos.
spanish_meaning_en: A gymnasium is a building with equipment that you can use to get exercise.
spanish_example_en: When I go to the gymnasium, I use the treadmill for twenty minutes.

english_meaning: To highlight something means to mark it with a color so that it is easy to see.

spanish_meaning: Destacar algo significa resaltarlo para que se vea.
spanish_example: Subrayé las palabras más difíciles de mi lista de vocabulario.
spanish_meaning_en: To highlight something means to mark it with a color so that it is easy to see.
spanish_example_en: I read my vocabulary list and highlighted the most difficult words.

english_meaning: If someone is ignorant about something, they have no knowledge about it.

spanish_meaning: Ignorante es quien no sabe mucho sobre un tema.
spanish_example: Sé algo ignorante de sus teorías, ¿puedes explicármelas?
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is ignorant about something, they have no knowledge about it.
spanish_example_en: I’m a bit ignorant about his theories. Can you explain them to me?

english_meaning: An index is a list of words at the end of a book that gives information.
english_example: If you look in the index, you’ll find the right page number.
spanish_meaning: Un índice es una lista de palabras en un libro con su ubicación.
spanish_example: Si buscas en el índice, encontrarás el número de página correcto.
spanish_meaning_en: An index is a list of words at the end of a book that gives information.
spanish_example_en: If you look in the index, you’ll find the right page number.

english_meaning: A lecture is a long, educational speech.
english_example: His lecture on world hunger was very informative.
spanish_meaning: Una conferencia es un discurso largo y educativo.
spanish_example: Su conferencia sobre hambre mundial fue muy informativa.
spanish_meaning_en: A lecture is a long, educational speech.
spanish_example_en: His lecture on world hunger was very informative.

english_meaning: A moral is a message at the end of a story that teaches you something.
english_example: At the end of the story, the mother explained the moral to her children.
spanish_meaning: La moraleja o mensaje moral es lo que enseña una historia.
spanish_example: Al final del cuento, la madre explicó la moral a sus hijos.
spanish_meaning_en: A moral is a message at the end of a story that teaches you something.
spanish_example_en: At the end of the story, the mother explained the moral to her children.

english_meaning: To operate means to work or function.
english_example: I’m sorry, but the trains are not operating today.
spanish_meaning: Operar significa funcionar o trabajar.

spanish_meaning_en: To operate means to work or function.

english_meaning: If something is private, it is only used by one person or group.

spanish_meaning: Privado significa que pertenece o se usa solo por una persona o grupo.
spanish_example: No mires mi diario, es privado.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is private, it is only used by one person or group.

english_meaning: When something is recent, it happened a short time ago.

spanish_meaning: Reciente significa que ocurrió hace poco.
spanish_example: No sabía qué había causado su comportamiento reciente.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is recent, it happened a short time ago.
spanish_example_en: I did not know what had caused his recent behavior.

english_meaning: A resolution is a personal decision.

spanish_meaning: Una resolución es una decisión o propósito personal.

spanish_meaning_en: A resolution is a personal decision.

english_meaning: A semester is a portion of a school year.

spanish_meaning: Un semestre es una parte del año escolar.
spanish_example: Jack está en su segundo semestre de universidad.
spanish_meaning_en: A semester is a portion of a school year.

english_meaning: If something is typewritten, it is written on a computer or typewriter.

spanish_meaning: Mecanografiado significa escrito con máquina de escribir o computadora.

spanish_meaning_en: If something is typewritten, it is written on a computer or typewriter.

english_meaning: When something is weird, it is very strange.

spanish_meaning: Raro significa muy extraño.
spanish_example: El papá de mi mejor amigo es un poco raro.

english_meaning: To accustom oneself to something is to get used to it.

spanish_meaning: Acostumbrarse a algo significa habituarse.
spanish_example: Me cuesta acostumbrarme a los husos horarios nuevos cuando viajo.
spanish_meaning_en: To accustom oneself to something is to get used to it.
spanish_example_en: I find it difficult to accustom myself to new time zones when I travel around the world.

spanish_meaning: El alfa es el miembro más dominante o de mayor rango del grupo.
spanish_example: El león más grande era el macho alfa de la manada.
spanish_meaning_en: The alpha member of a group is the most dominant or has the highest rank.
spanish_example_en: The largest lion was the alpha male of the pack.

english_meaning: To arouse means to awaken interest or attention in someone.
english_example: The politician’s words were meant to arouse the protestors’ anger.
spanish_meaning: Provocar significa despertar interés o atención.
spanish_example: Las palabras del político buscaban provocar la rabia de los manifestantes.
spanish_meaning_en: To arouse means to awaken interest or attention in someone.
spanish_example_en: The politician’s words were meant to arouse the protestors’ anger.

english_meaning: If someone is articulate, he or she has the ability to express ideas clearly and effectively.
english_example: Alfred’s friends loved talking to him because he was so articulate and intelligent.
spanish_meaning: Una persona articulada expresa sus ideas con claridad.
spanish_example: A Alfred le encantaba hablar porque era muy articulado.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is articulate, he or she has the ability to express ideas clearly and effectively.
spanish_example_en: Alfred’s friends loved talking to him because he was so articulate and intelligent.

english_meaning: To buzz means to show excitement about something.
english_example: Seoul buzzes from dawn till dusk.

spanish_example: Se siente mucho revuelo desde el amanecer en Seúl.
spanish_meaning_en: To buzz means to show excitement about something.
spanish_example_en: Seoul buzzes from dawn till dusk.

english_meaning: To clash means to fight or argue over something.

spanish_meaning: Chocar significa pelear o discutir.
spanish_example: Las hermanas a menudo chocan por la forma correcta de hacer las cosas.
spanish_meaning_en: To clash means to fight or argue over something.
spanish_example_en: The sisters often clash over the right way to do things.

english_meaning: Classification means putting things into categories or groups that have things in common.
english_example: According to scientific classification, a duck-billed platypus is an egg-laying mammal.
spanish_meaning: Clasificar significa agrupar cosas por categorías.
spanish_example: Según la clasificación científica, el ornitorrinco es un mamífero que pone huevos.
spanish_meaning_en: Classification means putting things into categories or groups that have things in common.
spanish_example_en: According to scientific classification, a duck-billed platypus is an egg-laying mammal.

english_meaning: To cling to something means to hold onto it tightly.

spanish_meaning: Aferrarse a algo es sujetarlo con fuerza.
spanish_example: Una perezosa madre se aferra fuertemente a su cría en los árboles.
spanish_meaning_en: To cling to something means to hold onto it tightly.
spanish_example_en: A mother sloth will cling tightly to her baby in the treetops.

english_meaning: Compliance is the act of following a rule or doing what you are supposed to do.
english_example: The bratty child never shows compliance with her mother’s rules.
spanish_meaning: Cumplimiento es seguir una regla o hacer lo que corresponde.
spanish_example: El niño malcriado nunca muestra cumplimiento con las reglas de su madre.
spanish_meaning_en: Compliance is the act of following a rule or doing what you are supposed to do.
spanish_example_en: The bratty child never shows compliance with her mother’s rules.

english_meaning: To comprehend something means to understand it.
english_example: The player could not comprehend why he lost the game.
spanish_meaning: Comprender algo es entenderlo.
spanish_example: El jugador no pudo comprender por qué perdió el partido.
spanish_meaning_en: To comprehend something means to understand it.
spanish_example_en: The player could not comprehend why he lost the game.

english_meaning: To congregate means to gather in one place.
english_example: Seeing a group of birds congregate in the sky is an awesome sight.
spanish_meaning: Congregarse significa reunirse en un mismo lugar.
spanish_example: Ver un grupo de aves congregarse en el cielo es una vista increíble.
spanish_meaning_en: To congregate means to gather in one place.
spanish_example_en: Seeing a group of birds congregate in the sky is an awesome sight.

english_meaning: Custody is the right to take care of someone or something.
english_example: The prisoners were put in the custody of the jailers.
spanish_meaning: La custodia es el derecho de cuidar a alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Los prisioneros quedaron bajo la custodia de los guardias.
spanish_meaning_en: Custody is the right to take care of someone or something.
spanish_example_en: The prisoners were put in the custody of the jailers.

english_meaning: To disperse means to scatter everywhere.
english_example: I watched the people disperse in all directions to avoid the charging bull.
spanish_meaning: Dispersar significa esparcirse en todas direcciones.
spanish_example: Vi cómo la gente se dispersaba para evitar al toro.
spanish_meaning_en: To disperse means to scatter everywhere.
spanish_example_en: I watched the people disperse in all directions to avoid the charging bull.

english_meaning: To embody means to symbolize or represent something.

spanish_meaning: Encarnar significa representar o simbolizar algo.
spanish_example: Se decía que Teresa de Calcuta encarnaba la bondad y la amabilidad.
spanish_meaning_en: To embody means to symbolize or represent something.
spanish_example_en: Mother Teresa was said to embody goodness and kindness.

english_meaning: Empirical means involving scientific proof and evidence.
english_example: Scientists must always find empirical evidence in their work.
spanish_meaning: Empírico significa basado en pruebas científicas.
spanish_example: Los científicos siempre deben buscar evidencia empírica en su trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: Empirical means involving scientific proof and evidence.
spanish_example_en: Scientists must always find empirical evidence in their work.

english_meaning: Exclusion is the act of keeping someone out of a group.
english_example: People gathered together to protest the exclusion of immigrants from our country.
spanish_meaning: La exclusión es dejar a alguien fuera de un grupo.
spanish_example: La gente se reunió para protestar la exclusión de inmigrantes del país.
spanish_meaning_en: Exclusion is the act of keeping someone out of a group.
spanish_example_en: People gathered together to protest the exclusion of immigrants from our country.

english_meaning: To flock means to gather in one place.
english_example: Groups of birds will flock to the river in the spring.
spanish_meaning: Reunirse en grupo es juntarse en un mismo lugar.
spanish_example: En primavera, bandadas de aves se reunían en el río.
spanish_meaning_en: To flock means to gather in one place.
spanish_example_en: Groups of birds will flock to the river in the spring.

english_meaning: When animals graze, they feed on plants.

spanish_meaning: Pastar es comer plantas, como hacen las ovejas.

spanish_meaning_en: When animals graze, they feed on plants.

english_meaning: If someone is intelligent, they are very smart.

spanish_meaning_en: If someone is intelligent, they are very smart.
spanish_example_en: The intelligent student graduated from high school with the highest marks in his class.

english_meaning: A jungle is a type of forest in a warm, rainy tropical area, where trees and plants grow very close together.
english_example: Many kinds of animals, including birds, reptiles, and even elephants live in a jungle.
spanish_meaning: La selva es un bosque tropical lluvioso con árboles y plantas muy juntos.

spanish_meaning_en: A jungle is a type of forest in a warm, rainy tropical area, where trees and plants grow very close together.
spanish_example_en: Many kinds of animals, including birds, reptiles, and even elephants live in a jungle.

english_meaning: An account with a bank is an arrangement to keep one’s money there.

spanish_example: Después de pagar el coche nuevo, mi cuenta bancaria estaba casi vacía.
spanish_meaning_en: An account with a bank is an arrangement to keep one’s money there.
spanish_example_en: After I paid for the new car, my bank account was nearly empty.

english_meaning: An architect is a person who designs buildings.

spanish_meaning: El arquitecto diseña edificios.
spanish_example: Los planos de la nueva casa fueron dibujados por un arquitecto famoso.
spanish_meaning_en: An architect is a person who designs buildings.
spanish_example_en: The plans for building the new home were drawn by a famous architect.

english_meaning: To conceal something means to hide it.

spanish_meaning_en: To conceal something means to hide it.
spanish_example_en: You should conceal your money so no one can take it from you.

spanish_meaning_en: If someone commits a crime, he or she does something bad that can be punished by law.
spanish_example_en: Police quickly arrived at the scene of the crime.

english_meaning: A deed is a certificate that proves that someone owns something.

spanish_meaning_en: A deed is a certificate that proves that someone owns something.

english_meaning: Gratitude is a feeling of being thankful.

spanish_meaning: El hábitat es el hogar natural de animales o plantas.

spanish_meaning_en: A habitat is the natural home of animals or plants.

english_meaning: To intervene means to help stop a problem between two people or groups.

spanish_meaning_en: A landmark is an object that helps people find or remember a location.
spanish_example_en: The tall tree was used as a landmark for people to find the road to the inn.
