spanish_meaning_en: To assist someone is to help them.
spanish_example_en: Andrew assisted me with my homework.

english_meaning: A breeze is a soft wind.
english_example: The breeze caused the leaves to fall off the tree.
spanish_meaning: La brisa es un viento suave.
spanish_example: La brisa hizo caer las hojas del árbol.
spanish_meaning_en: A breeze is a soft wind.
spanish_example_en: The breeze caused the leaves to fall off the tree.

english_meaning: To defy someone is to work against them or refuse to do what they say.
english_example: The students got into trouble for defying their teacher’s rules.
spanish_meaning: Desafiar significa negarse a obedecer.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes se metieron en problemas por desafiar las reglas.
spanish_meaning_en: To defy someone is to work against them or refuse to do what they say.
spanish_example_en: The students got into trouble for defying their teacher’s rules.

english_meaning: To display something is to show it, especially by putting it in a certain place.
english_example: The museum displayed many wonderful paintings.
spanish_meaning: Mostrar significa exponer o poner a la vista.
spanish_example: El museo mostró muchas pinturas maravillosas.
spanish_meaning_en: To display something is to show it, especially by putting it in a certain place.
spanish_example_en: The museum displayed many wonderful paintings.

english_meaning: If something or someone is efficient, they do not waste energy.
english_example: My car is very efficient. I rarely have to buy gas.
spanish_meaning: Algo eficiente consigue resultados sin perder energía.
spanish_example: Mi coche es muy eficiente.
spanish_meaning_en: If something or someone is efficient, they do not waste energy.
spanish_example_en: My car is very efficient. I rarely have to buy gas.

english_meaning: If someone is feeble, they are small or weak.
english_example: The boy was too feeble to carry the object very far.
spanish_meaning: Debil significa pequeño o con poca fuerza.
spanish_example: El niño era demasiado debil para llevar ese objeto.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is feeble, they are small or weak.
spanish_example_en: The boy was too feeble to carry the object very far.

english_meaning: To forgive someone is to stop being angry with them.
english_example: Sandra forgave Peter after he said he was sorry.
spanish_meaning: Perdonar es dejar de estar enojado con alguien.
spanish_example: Sandra perdono a Peter después de que pidio perdon.
spanish_meaning_en: To forgive someone is to stop being angry with them.
spanish_example_en: Sandra forgave Peter after he said he was sorry.

english_meaning: If someone is lively, they have a lot of energy.
english_example: Jennifer is very lively: she’s always running and playing.
spanish_meaning: Vivaz significa lleno de energía.
spanish_example: Jennifer es muy vivaz.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is lively, they have a lot of energy.
spanish_example_en: Jennifer is very lively: she’s always running and playing.

english_meaning: If something is majestic, it is large and impressive.
english_example: The rich people lived in a big, majestic house.
spanish_meaning: Majestuoso significa grande e impresionante.
spanish_example: Vivian tuvo una casa majestuosa.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is majestic, it is large and impressive.
spanish_example_en: The rich people lived in a big, majestic house.

english_meaning: You use nor to connect two negative ideas.
english_example: I eat neither apples nor oranges. I don’t like either one.
spanish_meaning: Ni une dos ideas negativas.
spanish_example: No como manzanas ni naranjas.
spanish_meaning_en: You use nor to connect two negative ideas.
spanish_example_en: I eat neither apples nor oranges. I don’t like either one.

english_meaning: If someone is outraged, they are very angry.
english_example: My father was outraged when he saw that I had crashed his car.
spanish_meaning: Indignado significa sentirse muy enojado y ofendido.
spanish_example: Mi padre se indignó cuando vio que habían chocado su coche.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is outraged, they are very angry.
spanish_example_en: My father was outraged when he saw that I had crashed his car.

english_meaning: If someone is pessimistic, they believe that the worst will happen.
english_example: John is pessimistic. He always thinks something bad will happen.
spanish_meaning: Pesimista significa esperar que pase lo peor.
spanish_example: John es pesimista y siempre piensa que pasa algo malo.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is pessimistic, they believe that the worst will happen.
spanish_example_en: John is pessimistic. He always thinks something bad will happen.

english_meaning: The relationship between two or more people is the way in which they behave toward each other.
english_example: The teacher was proud of her good relationship with the students.
spanish_meaning: Una relación es la forma de trato entre personas.
spanish_example: La maestra estaba orgullosa de su buena relación con los estudiantes.
spanish_meaning_en: The relationship between two or more people is the way in which they behave toward each other.
spanish_example_en: The teacher was proud of her good relationship with the students.

english_meaning: To slap someone means to hit them with the palm of the hand.
english_example: Out of anger, Helen slapped Eunice on the face.
spanish_meaning: Abofetear es golpear con la mano abierta.
spanish_example: Helen abofeteo a Eunice en la cara.
spanish_meaning_en: To slap someone means to hit them with the palm of the hand.
spanish_example_en: Out of anger, Helen slapped Eunice on the face.

english_meaning: To smash something is to break it into many small pieces.
english_example: Jacob smashed the window with a rock.
spanish_meaning: Derribar o romper en pedazos.
spanish_example: Jacob rompio la ventana con una roca.
spanish_meaning_en: To smash something is to break it into many small pieces.
spanish_example_en: Jacob smashed the window with a rock.

english_meaning: Someone who is successful achieves what they intended to achieve.
english_example: My father is a successful businessman.
spanish_meaning: Exitoso significa lograr lo que se quería.
spanish_example: Mi padre es un hombre de negocios exitoso.
spanish_meaning_en: Someone who is successful achieves what they intended to achieve.
spanish_example_en: My father is a successful businessman.

english_meaning: A wage is the money that a person gets for doing a job.
english_example: The wages I receive from my job are really great!
spanish_meaning: El salario es dinero por el trabajo.
spanish_example: Las ganancias que recibo son muy grandes.
spanish_meaning_en: A wage is the money that a person gets for doing a job.
spanish_example_en: The wages I receive from my job are really great!

english_meaning: You use whereas to show how two things are different.
english_example: My sister loves horror movies, whereas I prefer comedies.
spanish_meaning: Mientras que conecta dos ideas contrastantes.
spanish_example: Mi hermana ama peliculas de terror, mientras que yo prefiero comedias.
spanish_meaning_en: You use whereas to show how two things are different.
spanish_example_en: My sister loves horror movies, whereas I prefer comedies.

english_meaning: When something is animate, it has life.
english_example: Rocks and machines are not animate things.
spanish_meaning: Algo animado tiene vida.
spanish_example: Las rocas y las máquinas no son seres animados.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is animate, it has life.
spanish_example_en: Rocks and machines are not animate things.

english_meaning: To classify things is to put them into groups based on their type.
english_example: The biologist classified the plant as a completely new species.
spanish_meaning: Clasificar significa separar por tipo.
spanish_example: El biólogo clasifico la planta como especie nueva.
spanish_meaning_en: To classify things is to put them into groups based on their type.
spanish_example_en: The biologist classified the plant as a completely new species.

english_meaning: To concede is to admit against your wish that something is true.
english_example: The student conceded that he had cheated on the test.
spanish_meaning: Conceder significa aceptar o admitir.
spanish_example: El estudiante concedió que había hecho trampa.
spanish_meaning_en: To concede is to admit against your wish that something is true.
spanish_example_en: The student conceded that he had cheated on the test.

english_meaning: A concept is an idea about something.
english_example: I learned some concepts about molecules before working in the science lab.
spanish_meaning: Un concepto es una idea.
spanish_example: Aprendi conceptos sobre moleculas.
spanish_meaning_en: A concept is an idea about something.
spanish_example_en: I learned some concepts about molecules before working in the science lab.

english_meaning: To construct something means to make or build it.
english_example: The men used wood and metal to construct a house.
spanish_meaning: Construir significa fabricar o levantar.
spanish_example: Los hombres usaron madera y metal para construir una casa.
spanish_meaning_en: To construct something means to make or build it.
spanish_example_en: The men used wood and metal to construct a house.

english_meaning: A decade is a period of ten years.
english_example: She celebrated her three decades of work with the company.
spanish_meaning: Una decada es un período de diez años.
spanish_example: Ella celebro sus tres decadas de trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: A decade is a period of ten years.
spanish_example_en: She celebrated her three decades of work with the company.

english_meaning: A diagram is a simple drawing that explains what something is or how it works.
english_example: By following the diagram, I was able to put the desk together.
spanish_meaning: Un diagrama es un dibujo explicativo.
spanish_example: Siguiendo el diagrama pude armar el escritorio.
spanish_meaning_en: A diagram is a simple drawing that explains what something is or how it works.
spanish_example_en: By following the diagram, I was able to put the desk together.

english_meaning: A ferry is a boat that carries passengers over short distances.
english_example: The ferry took the people across the lake.
spanish_meaning: Un ferry es un barco que cruza personas y coches por el agua.
spanish_example: El ferry cruzó el lago.
spanish_meaning_en: A ferry is a boat that carries passengers over short distances.
spanish_example_en: The ferry took the people across the lake.

english_meaning: If something is handy, it is useful.
english_example: An eraser is handy if you make a lot of mistakes.
spanish_meaning: Útil significa práctico.
spanish_example: Un borrador es útil.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is handy, it is useful.
spanish_example_en: An eraser is handy if you make a lot of mistakes.

english_meaning: To isolate is to separate one person or thing from a group.
english_example: The teacher isolated the bad child from the class before talking with her.
spanish_meaning: Aislar significa separar de un grupo.
spanish_example: La maestra aislo a la niña antes de hablar con ella.
spanish_meaning_en: To isolate is to separate one person or thing from a group.
spanish_example_en: The teacher isolated the bad child from the class before talking with her.

english_meaning: A longing is a strong feeling of wanting.
english_example: Since he skipped breakfast, he had a longing for food all morning.
spanish_meaning: Anhelo o deseo intenso.
spanish_example: Al saltar desayuno, tuvo un fuerte deseo de comida.
spanish_meaning_en: A longing is a strong feeling of wanting.
spanish_example_en: Since he skipped breakfast, he had a longing for food all morning.

english_meaning: If something is numerous, there are many of those things.
english_example: It was hard to drive fast since there were numerous holes in the road.
spanish_meaning: Numeroso significa muy grande en cantidad.
spanish_example: Había numerosos hoyos en el camino.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is numerous, there are many of those things.
spanish_example_en: It was hard to drive fast since there were numerous holes in the road.

english_meaning: A particle is a very small piece of something.
english_example: The bottles of wine were covered in a layer of dust particles.
spanish_meaning: Una partícula es un pedazo muy pequeño.
spanish_example: Había una capa de partículas de polvo.
spanish_meaning_en: A particle is a very small piece of something.
spanish_example_en: The bottles of wine were covered in a layer of dust particles.

english_meaning: A plea is a request that is urgent or emotional.
english_example: The poor, hungry man made a plea for food.
spanish_meaning: Una peticion/petición es un pedido emocional.
spanish_example: El hombre hambriento hizo una peticion de comida.
spanish_meaning_en: A plea is a request that is urgent or emotional.
spanish_example_en: The poor, hungry man made a plea for food.

english_meaning: To refrain from something is to avoid doing it.
english_example: The doctor asked Mary to refrain from eating fast food as part of her diet.
spanish_meaning: Abstenerse es evitar hacerlo.
spanish_example: La doctora pidio que se abstuviera de comida rápida.
spanish_meaning_en: To refrain from something is to avoid doing it.
spanish_example_en: The doctor asked Mary to refrain from eating fast food as part of her diet.

english_meaning: A review of something is a formal inspection of it by people in authority.
english_example: The government ordered a careful review of the economic situation.
spanish_meaning: Una revisión es una inspección formal.
spanish_example: El gobierno ordenó una revisión de la economía.
spanish_meaning_en: A review of something is a formal inspection of it by people in authority.
spanish_example_en: The government ordered a careful review of the economic situation.

english_meaning: If someone is sophisticated, they know many things about the world.
english_example: Jake is one of the most sophisticated people I’ve ever met.
spanish_meaning: Sophisticado significa refinado o con experiencia.
spanish_example: Jake es una persona muy sofisticada.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is sophisticated, they know many things about the world.
spanish_example_en: Jake is one of the most sophisticated people I’ve ever met.

english_meaning: To surrender something is to give it up.
english_example: The thief surrendered the money to the police when he was caught.
spanish_meaning: Rendirse significa entregar o darse por vencido.
spanish_example: El ladrón se rindo y entrego el dinero.
spanish_meaning_en: To surrender something is to give it up.
spanish_example_en: The thief surrendered the money to the police when he was caught.

english_meaning: If something is upright, it is standing up straight.
english_example: Meerkats can’t walk like humans, but they can stand upright.
spanish_meaning: Erguido significa de pie y derecho.
spanish_example: Las mearcas no caminan como humanos, pero pueden ponerse erguidas.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is upright, it is standing up straight.
spanish_example_en: Meerkats can’t walk like humans, but they can stand upright.

english_meaning: If something is worthwhile, it is important or useful.
english_example: On his visit to Canada, he realized studying English was worthwhile.
spanish_meaning: Algo valioso es importante o útil.
spanish_example: Estudiar inglés fue totalmente valioso.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is worthwhile, it is important or useful.
spanish_example_en: On his visit to Canada, he realized studying English was worthwhile.

english_meaning: An alliance is a group of people who work together.
english_example: The junior executives formed an alliance with each other.
spanish_meaning: Una alianza es un grupo que trabaja junto.
spanish_example: Los ejecutivos formaron una alianza.
spanish_meaning_en: An alliance is a group of people who work together.
spanish_example_en: The junior executives formed an alliance with each other.

english_meaning: Applause is the noise made when people clap their hands to show approval.
english_example: At the end of his speech, everyone burst into loud applause.
spanish_meaning: El aplauso es el ruido que hace la gente al aplaudir para mostrar aprobación.
spanish_example: Al final de su discurso, todos estallaron en fuertes aplausos.
spanish_meaning_en: Applause is the noise made when people clap their hands to show approval.
spanish_example_en: At the end of his speech, everyone burst into loud applause.

english_meaning: If you are armed, you are carrying a weapon.
english_example: The robber was armed with a gun.
spanish_meaning: Armar significa portar un arma.
spanish_example: El ladrón estaba armado con una pistola.
spanish_meaning_en: If you are armed, you are carrying a weapon.
spanish_example_en: The robber was armed with a gun.

english_meaning: If something is authoritative, it uses the best information available.
english_example: This book is an authoritative source on ancient Egypt’s Queen Nefertiti.
spanish_meaning: Autoritativo significa de autoridad o fiable.
spanish_example: Este libro es una fuente autoritaria.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is authoritative, it uses the best information available.
spanish_example_en: This book is an authoritative source on ancient Egypt’s Queen Nefertiti.

english_meaning: Bravery is brave behavior.
english_example: The books commended the general’s bravery.
spanish_meaning: La valentía es un comportamiento valiente.
spanish_example: Los libros elogiaron la valentía del general.
spanish_meaning_en: Bravery is brave behavior.
spanish_example_en: The books commended the general’s bravery.

english_meaning: A ceremony is an event that happens on special occasions.
english_example: Tom and Amy’s marriage ceremony is in June.
spanish_meaning: Una ceremonia es un evento formal.
spanish_example: La ceremonia de matrimonio fue en junio.
spanish_meaning_en: A ceremony is an event that happens on special occasions.
spanish_example_en: Tom and Amy’s marriage ceremony is in June.

english_meaning: Culture is the music, art, and writings of a certain place or group of people.
english_example: Ancient Mexican culture is renowned for its architecture.
spanish_meaning: La cultura es el arte y las costumbres de un pueblo.
spanish_example: La cultura mexicana es reconocida.
spanish_meaning_en: Culture is the music, art, and writings of a certain place or group of people.
spanish_example_en: Ancient Mexican culture is renowned for its architecture.

english_meaning: A defense is something that is done to protect another thing.
english_example: The air force works in defense of its country.
spanish_meaning: La defensa es protección.
spanish_example: La fuerza aérea lucha en defensa de su país.
spanish_meaning_en: A defense is something that is done to protect another thing.
spanish_example_en: The air force works in defense of its country.

english_meaning: A detail is a small piece of information.
english_example: Jane told Ben all the details of her trip to China.
spanish_meaning: Un detalle es una pieza de informacion.
spanish_example: Jane conto todos los detalles de su viaje.
spanish_meaning_en: A detail is a small piece of information.
spanish_example_en: Jane told Ben all the details of her trip to China.

english_meaning: When a group of things is diverse, it is made up of a wide variety of things.
english_example: Big cities have diverse populations with people of different races and ages.
spanish_meaning: Diverso significa variado.
spanish_example: La ciudad tiene una poblacion diversa.
spanish_meaning_en: When a group of things is diverse, it is made up of a wide variety of things.
spanish_example_en: Big cities have diverse populations with people of different races and ages.

english_meaning: To enchant someone is to make them feel very interested or happy.
english_example: The beautiful woman enchanted everybody in the room.
spanish_meaning: Encantar significa agradar mucho.
spanish_example: La mujer encanto a todos.
spanish_meaning_en: To enchant someone is to make them feel very interested or happy.
spanish_example_en: The beautiful woman enchanted everybody in the room.

english_meaning: To equip someone is to give them the things needed to do something.
english_example: Steve was equipped with the tools needed to do the job.
spanish_meaning: Equipar significa dar lo necesario.
spanish_example: Steve estaba equipado para el trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: To equip someone is to give them the things needed to do something.
spanish_example_en: Steve was equipped with the tools needed to do the job.

english_meaning: An exception is someone or something not conforming to a rule.
english_example: Most students thought the test was hard, but Tim was the exception.
spanish_meaning: Una excepción es lo que no sigue una regla.
spanish_example: Todos pensaron que era difícil, pero Tim fue una excepción.
spanish_meaning_en: An exception is someone or something not conforming to a rule.
spanish_example_en: Most students thought the test was hard, but Tim was the exception.

english_meaning: A genre is a type of literature, art, or music characterized by its style.
english_example: She is considered a master in the comedy genre.
spanish_meaning: Un género es un tipo de arte o música.
spanish_example: Ella es experta en el género de comedia.
spanish_meaning_en: A genre is a type of literature, art, or music characterized by its style.
spanish_example_en: She is considered a master in the comedy genre.

english_meaning: An impact is the effect someone or something has.
english_example: My grandmother had a great impact on my life.
spanish_meaning: Impacto es el efecto.
spanish_example: Mi abuela tuvo un gran impacto en mi vida.
spanish_meaning_en: An impact is the effect someone or something has.
spanish_example_en: My grandmother had a great impact on my life.

english_meaning: To lure someone is to convince them to do something by using a trick.
english_example: The store lures people in with big signs that say “Sale!”
spanish_meaning: Atraer es atraer con algun truco.
spanish_example: El cartel de "Oferta" atrae a las personas.
spanish_meaning_en: To lure someone is to convince them to do something by using a trick.
spanish_example_en: The store lures people in with big signs that say “Sale!”

english_meaning: An obstacle is an object or a problem that stops you from doing something.
english_example: Climbing over the giant rock was the biggest obstacle for the hikers.
spanish_meaning: Un obstaculo es algo que impide.
spanish_example: La roca gigante fue un gran obstaculo.
spanish_meaning_en: An obstacle is an object or a problem that stops you from doing something.
spanish_example_en: Climbing over the giant rock was the biggest obstacle for the hikers.

english_meaning: A shelter is a place to go that is safe from danger or bad weather.
english_example: The frog took shelter from the storm in a nearby hold.
spanish_meaning: Un refugio es un lugar seguro.
spanish_example: La rana se refugio durante la tormenta.
spanish_meaning_en: A shelter is a place to go that is safe from danger or bad weather.
spanish_example_en: The frog took shelter from the storm in a nearby hold.

english_meaning: To supply something means to give people what they need or want.
english_example: The water heater supplied the house with warm water.
spanish_meaning: Suministrar significa dar a alguien lo que necesita.
spanish_example: El calefactor suministró agua caliente a la casa.
spanish_meaning_en: To supply something means to give people what they need or want.
spanish_example_en: The water heater supplied the house with warm water.

english_meaning: If people are vain, they are only concerned with how they look.
english_example: Rebecca is so vain that she looks at herself in every mirror.
spanish_meaning: Vanidoso significa preocuparce por la apariencia.
spanish_example: Rebecca es muy vanidosa.
spanish_meaning_en: If people are vain, they are only concerned with how they look.
spanish_example_en: Rebecca is so vain that she looks at herself in every mirror.

english_meaning: An alternative is something that you can choose instead of your first choice.
english_example: Her first plan to make extra money seemed weak, so she chose an alternative.
spanish_meaning: Alternativa significa otra opcion.
spanish_example: Eligio una alternativa.
spanish_meaning_en: An alternative is something that you can choose instead of your first choice.
spanish_example_en: Her first plan to make extra money seemed weak, so she chose an alternative.

english_meaning: An avenue is a road, often a large one with buildings on each side.
english_example: Drive down this avenue and then turn left to get to the park.
spanish_meaning: Avenida es una via ancha urbana.
spanish_example: Baja por está avenida y gira a la izquierda.
spanish_meaning_en: An avenue is a road, often a large one with buildings on each side.
spanish_example_en: Drive down this avenue and then turn left to get to the park.

english_meaning: The belly is the stomach of a person or animal.
english_example: His belly was full because he had eaten a lot of food.
spanish_meaning: El vientre es la zona del abdomen.
spanish_example: Su vientre estaba lleno porque había comido demasiado.
spanish_meaning_en: The belly is the stomach of a person or animal.
spanish_example_en: His belly was full because he had eaten a lot of food.

english_meaning: A bid to do something is an attempt to do it.
english_example: He made a bid to become the university’s next president.
spanish_meaning: Una puja es oferta o apuesta para algo.
spanish_example: Hizo una puja para ser presidente de la universidad.
spanish_meaning_en: A bid to do something is an attempt to do it.
spanish_example_en: He made a bid to become the university’s next president.

english_meaning: To blow means to move air or move something through the air, as in the wind.
english_example: The wind is blowing very hard today. We should stay inside.
spanish_meaning: Soplar significa mover aire.
spanish_example: El viento soplaba muy fuerte.
spanish_meaning_en: To blow means to move air or move something through the air, as in the wind.
spanish_example_en: The wind is blowing very hard today. We should stay inside.

english_meaning: A conflict is a fight between different people or groups.
english_example: The two nations had a conflict over which one could use the water in the river.
spanish_meaning: Conflicto es pelea entre grupos.
spanish_example: Las dos naciones tuvieron un conflicto.
spanish_meaning_en: A conflict is a fight between different people or groups.
spanish_example_en: The two nations had a conflict over which one could use the water in the river.

english_meaning: A continent is one of the seven large areas of land on the Earth.
english_example: Asia is the largest continent.
spanish_meaning: Un continente es una gran región de tierra.
spanish_example: Asia es el continente más grande.
spanish_meaning_en: A continent is one of the seven large areas of land on the Earth.
spanish_example_en: Asia is the largest continent.

english_meaning: A current is a steady and constant flow of air or water in a river or ocean.
english_example: The ocean currents took the ship far off into the sea.
spanish_meaning: Una corriente es flujo continuo de agua o aire.
spanish_example: Las corrientes marinas desviaron el barco.
spanish_meaning_en: A current is a steady and constant flow of air or water in a river or ocean.
spanish_example_en: The ocean currents took the ship far off into the sea.

english_meaning: Disrespect is rudeness or behavior that shows a lack of respect.
english_example: He showed disrespect by arguing with his boss during a meeting.
spanish_meaning: La falta de respeto es rudeza o comportamiento que muestra poco respeto.
spanish_example: Mostró falta de respeto al discutir con su jefe durante una reunión.
spanish_meaning_en: Disrespect is rudeness or behavior that shows a lack of respect.
spanish_example_en: He showed disrespect by arguing with his boss during a meeting.

english_meaning: Enthusiasm is a very strong good feeling about something.
english_example: The crowd showed their enthusiasm for the soccer team by cheering loudly.
spanish_meaning: El entusiasmo es una alegría o interés muy fuerte por algo.
spanish_example: El público mostró entusiasmo por el equipo de fútbol animándolo con fuerza.

spanish_meaning_en: If someone is esteemed, many people like or respect them.
spanish_example_en: An esteemed scientist is coming to the university to talk about his discoveries.

english_meaning: If something is ethical, it is the right thing to do.
english_example: Many people believe that it is ethical to help others in need.
spanish_meaning: Ético significa moralmente correcto.
spanish_example: Muchos piensan que es ético ayudar a quienes necesitan apoyo.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is ethical, it is the right thing to do.
spanish_example_en: Many people believe that it is ethical to help others in need.

english_meaning: If plants or animals are extinct, there are none left.
english_example: There used to be dinosaurs all over the world, but now they are extinct.
spanish_meaning: Extinto significa que ya no existe.
spanish_example: Los dinosaurios eran comunes y ahora están extintos.
spanish_meaning_en: If plants or animals are extinct, there are none left.
spanish_example_en: There used to be dinosaurs all over the world, but now they are extinct.

english_meaning: If a person or plant is hardy, it is strong and can live through difficult conditions.
english_example: The farmer is a hardy man and doesn’t mind working outside.
spanish_meaning: Resistente significa fuerte y capaz de soportar dificultades.
spanish_example: El granjero es un hombre resistente y no le importa trabajar fuera.
spanish_meaning_en: If a person or plant is hardy, it is strong and can live through difficult conditions.
spanish_example_en: The farmer is a hardy man and doesn’t mind working outside.

english_meaning: An institute is an organization that is interested in research or teaching.
english_example: I am going to a lecture about Ancient Rome at the Historical Institute.
spanish_meaning: Un instituto es una organización dedicada a la enseñanza o investigación.
spanish_example: Voy a una conferencia en el Instituto Histórico sobre Roma antigua.
spanish_meaning_en: An institute is an organization that is interested in research or teaching.
spanish_example_en: I am going to a lecture about Ancient Rome at the Historical Institute.

english_meaning: Jealousy is a feeling of wanting something that somebody else has.
english_example: She felt a lot of jealousy when she saw her friend with a new car.
spanish_meaning: Los celos son el deseo de tener lo que otra persona posee.
spanish_example: Sintió celos al ver a su amiga con un coche nuevo.
spanish_meaning_en: Jealousy is a feeling of wanting something that somebody else has.
spanish_example_en: She felt a lot of jealousy when she saw her friend with a new car.

english_meaning: To migrate means to move from one place to another.
english_example: Many birds migrate to warmer countries in the winter.
spanish_meaning: Migrar significa mudarse de un lugar a otro.
spanish_example: Muchos pájaros migran a lugares más cálidos en invierno.
spanish_meaning_en: To migrate means to move from one place to another.
spanish_example_en: Many birds migrate to warmer countries in the winter.

english_meaning: To nurture something means to care for it as it grows or develops.
english_example: Robert nurtured his plants, and that is why they grow so well.
spanish_meaning: Criar algo es cuidarlo mientras crece y se desarrolla.
spanish_example: Robert crió sus plantas, y por eso crecieron tan bien.
spanish_meaning_en: To nurture something means to care for it as it grows or develops.
spanish_example_en: Robert nurtured his plants, and that is why they grow so well.

english_meaning: If something is overhead, it is located above you.
english_example: As we sat on the top of the hill, a plane flew overhead.
spanish_meaning: Sobre algo significa encima de ti.
spanish_example: Nos sentamos en la colina y un avión voló sobre nuestras cabezas.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is overhead, it is located above you.
spanish_example_en: As we sat on the top of the hill, a plane flew overhead.

english_meaning: A principle is a belief about the correct way to behave.
english_example: To maintain principles, it’s vital to watch, listen, and speak carefully.
spanish_meaning: Un principio es una creencia sobre cómo actuar.
spanish_example: Mantener los principios exige escuchar, mirar y hablar con cuidado.
spanish_meaning_en: A principle is a belief about the correct way to behave.
spanish_example_en: To maintain principles, it’s vital to watch, listen, and speak carefully.

english_meaning: If a place is rural, it is in the countryside instead of the city.
english_example: I want to live in a small house in a rural area.
spanish_meaning: Un lugar rural está en el campo, no en la ciudad.
spanish_example: Quiero vivir en una casa pequeña en una zona rural.
spanish_meaning_en: If a place is rural, it is in the countryside instead of the city.
spanish_example_en: I want to live in a small house in a rural area.

english_meaning: If a place is secluded, it is far away from any other place.
english_example: There was a secluded bench in the park.
spanish_meaning: Aislado significa lejos de otros sitios.
spanish_example: Había un banco aislado en el parque.
spanish_meaning_en: If a place is secluded, it is far away from any other place.
spanish_example_en: There was a secluded bench in the park.

english_meaning: A species is a type of plant or animal.
english_example: There are 21 different species of butterfly in this forest.
spanish_meaning: Una especie es un tipo de planta o animal.
spanish_example: Hay 21 especies de mariposa en este bosque.
spanish_meaning_en: A species is a type of plant or animal.
spanish_example_en: There are 21 different species of butterfly in this forest.

english_meaning: A swamp is a very wet area of land.
english_example: There are lots of wild animals living in the swamp.
spanish_meaning: Un pantano es un lugar de tierra muy húmedo.
spanish_example: Muchos animales viven en el pantano.
spanish_meaning_en: A swamp is a very wet area of land.
spanish_example_en: There are lots of wild animals living in the swamp.

english_meaning: To traverse means to move or travel through an area.
english_example: The explorer traversed the desert alone on a camel.
spanish_meaning: Atravesar significa pasar por o viajar a través de un lugar.
spanish_example: El explorador atravesó el desierto sobre un camello.
spanish_meaning_en: To traverse means to move or travel through an area.
spanish_example_en: The explorer traversed the desert alone on a camel.

english_meaning: Zoology is a subject in which people study animals.
english_example: Helen wants to study zoology because she has always liked animals.
spanish_meaning: La zoología es la ciencia que estudia animales.
spanish_example: Helen quiere estudiar zoología porque siempre le gustaron los animales.
spanish_meaning_en: Zoology is a subject in which people study animals.
spanish_example_en: Helen wants to study zoology because she has always liked animals.

english_meaning: If someone acts accordingly, they act in a way that is suitable.
english_example: He feels like he did a good job and his boss should pay him accordingly.
spanish_meaning: Actuar en consecuencia significa actuar de manera apropiada.
spanish_example: Hizo un buen trabajo y el jefe debería pagarle en consecuencia.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone acts accordingly, they act in a way that is suitable.
spanish_example_en: He feels like he did a good job and his boss should pay him accordingly.

english_meaning: An anchor is a heavy object dropped from a boat to make it stay in one place.
english_example: When the ship reached its destination, the crew dropped the anchor.
spanish_meaning: Una ancla es un peso de metal o piedra que mantiene un barco en su sitio.
spanish_example: Al llegar al puerto, la tripulación arrojó el ancla.
spanish_meaning_en: An anchor is a heavy object dropped from a boat to make it stay in one place.
spanish_example_en: When the ship reached its destination, the crew dropped the anchor.

english_meaning: When you cause something, you make it happen.
english_example: Tiredness caused the accident.
spanish_meaning: Causar algo significa hacerlo suceder.
spanish_example: El cansancio causó el accidente.
spanish_meaning_en: When you cause something, you make it happen.
spanish_example_en: Tiredness caused the accident.

english_meaning: Context is the situations that form the background of an event.
english_example: They studied the context of the battle before giving their presentation.
spanish_meaning: El contexto es el entorno de una situación o evento.
spanish_example: Estudiaron el contexto de la batalla antes de la presentación.
spanish_meaning_en: Context is the situations that form the background of an event.
spanish_example_en: They studied the context of the battle before giving their presentation.

english_meaning: To designate someone or something means to give them a particular description.
english_example: The famous lighthouse was designated as a historical monument.
spanish_meaning: Designar significa nombrar o señalar para un rol.
spanish_example: El faro famoso fue designado como monumento histórico.
spanish_meaning_en: To designate someone or something means to give them a particular description.
spanish_example_en: The famous lighthouse was designated as a historical monument.

english_meaning: To distort something means to lie about it, or pull or twist out of shape.
english_example: The man’s lawyer distorted the facts so that he would be set free.
spanish_meaning: Distorsionar algo es cambiarlo, torciéndolo o alterando la verdad.
spanish_example: El abogado distorsionó los hechos para que quedara libre.
spanish_meaning_en: To distort something means to lie about it, or pull or twist out of shape.
spanish_example_en: The man’s lawyer distorted the facts so that he would be set free.

english_meaning: A dock is an enclosed area where ships go to be loaded, unloaded, and repaired.
english_example: The huge ship pulled into the dock, and the crew unloaded the cargo.
spanish_meaning: Un muelle es el lugar donde atracan y cargan los barcos.
spanish_example: El gran barco entró en el muelle y descargaron la carga.
spanish_meaning_en: A dock is an enclosed area where ships go to be loaded, unloaded, and repaired.
spanish_example_en: The huge ship pulled into the dock, and the crew unloaded the cargo.

english_meaning: If you have a lot of energy, you have plenty of strength and can do lots of things.
english_example: If you lack energy, try eating better food.
spanish_meaning: La energía es la fuerza o capacidad para hacer muchas cosas.
spanish_example: Si no tienes energía, come mejor.
spanish_meaning_en: If you have a lot of energy, you have plenty of strength and can do lots of things.
spanish_example_en: If you lack energy, try eating better food.

english_meaning: If something is frequent, then it happens or is done often.
english_example: While Dad was sick, the doctor made frequent visits to his house.
spanish_meaning: Frecuente significa que sucede muchas veces.
spanish_example: Mientras papá estaba enfermo, el médico hizo visitas frecuentes.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is frequent, then it happens or is done often.
spanish_example_en: While Dad was sick, the doctor made frequent visits to his house.

english_meaning: The gears are the part of a motor that controls the speed.
english_example: My brother has a car with four gears.
spanish_meaning: Los engranajes son las partes de una máquina que controlan la velocidad.
spanish_example: Mi hermano tiene un coche con cuatro engranajes.
spanish_meaning_en: The gears are the part of a motor that controls the speed.
spanish_example_en: My brother has a car with four gears.

english_meaning: When something is genuine, it is true or real.
english_example: After the painting was determined to be genuine, it sold for a million dollars.
spanish_meaning: Genuino significa verdadero o auténtico.
spanish_example: Tras comprobar que el cuadro era genuino, se vendió por un millón.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is genuine, it is true or real.
spanish_example_en: After the painting was determined to be genuine, it sold for a million dollars.

english_meaning: Grease is an oily substance put on moving parts so they work smoothly, or oil or fat in cooking.
english_example: When I was done working on the car, I had grease all over my hands.
spanish_meaning: La grasa es una sustancia aceitosa usada en piezas móviles o en cocina.
spanish_example: Al terminar el coche, tenía grasa en las manos.
spanish_meaning_en: Grease is an oily substance put on moving parts so they work smoothly, or oil or fat in cooking.
spanish_example_en: When I was done working on the car, I had grease all over my hands.

english_meaning: Knowledge is what you know.
english_example: He has trouble putting his knowledge into practice.
spanish_meaning: El conocimiento es lo que sabes.
spanish_example: Le cuesta aplicar su conocimiento en la práctica.
spanish_meaning_en: Knowledge is what you know.
spanish_example_en: He has trouble putting his knowledge into practice.

english_meaning: When you omit something, you leave it out or do not do it.
english_example: I omitted some important information: he is not coming.
spanish_meaning: Omitir algo significa dejarlo fuera o no hacerlo.
spanish_example: Omití información importante: él no viene.
spanish_meaning_en: When you omit something, you leave it out or do not do it.
spanish_example_en: I omitted some important information: he is not coming.

english_meaning: To offset means to use one thing to cancel out the effect of another thing.
english_example: Increased wages are offset by higher prices for goods.
spanish_meaning: Compensar es usar algo para contrarrestar otra cosa.
spanish_example: El aumento de salarios se compensa con precios más altos.
spanish_meaning_en: To offset means to use one thing to cancel out the effect of another thing.
spanish_example_en: Increased wages are offset by higher prices for goods.

english_meaning: To overlap something means to cover a piece of it.
english_example: The gift on top overlaps the other gift on the bottom.
spanish_meaning: Superponer algo es cubrir una parte con otra.
spanish_example: El regalo de arriba se superpone al de abajo.
spanish_meaning_en: To overlap something means to cover a piece of it.
spanish_example_en: The gift on top overlaps the other gift on the bottom.

english_meaning: When something is secondhand, it has been owned by someone else.
english_example: Her secondhand jeans were a bit faded in the front.
spanish_meaning: De segunda mano significa que lo usó otra persona.
spanish_example: Sus vaqueras de segunda mano estaban un poco gastadas.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is secondhand, it has been owned by someone else.
spanish_example_en: Her secondhand jeans were a bit faded in the front.

english_meaning: When you have skill at doing a job, you are good at doing it.
english_example: She worked with great skill and confidence.
spanish_meaning: La habilidad es la destreza para hacer bien un trabajo.
spanish_example: Trabajó con gran habilidad y seguridad.
spanish_meaning_en: When you have skill at doing a job, you are good at doing it.
spanish_example_en: She worked with great skill and confidence.

english_meaning: A slot is a narrow opening in a machine or container.
english_example: To operate the machine, put your coins into the slot.
spanish_meaning: Una ranura es una hendidura para meter monedas o piezas.
spanish_example: Para usar la máquina, mete las monedas en la ranura.
spanish_meaning_en: A slot is a narrow opening in a machine or container.
spanish_example_en: To operate the machine, put your coins into the slot.

english_meaning: A tactic is a careful plan to achieve something.
english_example: Sam thought of a good tactic to attract more business.
spanish_meaning: Una táctica es un plan ordenado para lograr algo.
spanish_example: Sam pensó en una buena táctica para atraer más clientes.
spanish_meaning_en: A tactic is a careful plan to achieve something.
spanish_example_en: Sam thought of a good tactic to attract more business.

english_meaning: If something is beneficial, it is good for you.
english_example: Drinking milk every day is beneficial for your bones.
spanish_meaning: Algo beneficioso es bueno para la salud o para la vida.
spanish_example: Beber leche todos los días es beneficioso para los huesos.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is beneficial, it is good for you.
spanish_example_en: Drinking milk every day is beneficial for your bones.

english_meaning: A birthplace is a place where a person is born or where something started.
english_example: China is the birthplace of chopsticks.
spanish_meaning: El lugar de nacimiento es donde una persona nace.
spanish_example: China es el lugar de nacimiento de los palillos.
spanish_meaning_en: A birthplace is a place where a person is born or where something started.
spanish_example_en: China is the birthplace of chopsticks.

english_meaning: The capacity of something is the amount of things that can be put in it.
english_example: The parking lot has reached its full capacity.
spanish_meaning: La capacidad es la cantidad que un espacio puede contener.
spanish_example: El estacionamiento llegó a su máxima capacidad.
spanish_meaning_en: The capacity of something is the amount of things that can be put in it.
spanish_example_en: The parking lot has reached its full capacity.

english_meaning: If something is comparative, it is being judged based on something else.
english_example: The money that John has is comparative to that of most other adults.
spanish_meaning: Comparativo significa que se compara con otra cosa.
spanish_example: El dinero de John es comparativo al de la mayoría de adultos.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is comparative, it is being judged based on something else.
spanish_example_en: The money that John has is comparative to that of most other adults.

english_meaning: If something is comprehensive, it has all the details about something else.
english_example: The teacher gave us a comprehensive review for the exam.
spanish_meaning: Completo significa que incluye muchos detalles.
spanish_example: El profesor nos dio una revisión completa para el examen.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is comprehensive, it has all the details about something else.
spanish_example_en: The teacher gave us a comprehensive review for the exam.

english_meaning: To conserve something is to protect it from being ruined or used completely.
english_example: The group worked to conserve the beauty of Europe’s national parks.
spanish_meaning: Conservar algo es protegerlo de perderse o deteriorarse.
spanish_example: El grupo trabajó para conservar la belleza de los parques nacionales.
spanish_meaning_en: To conserve something is to protect it from being ruined or used completely.
spanish_example_en: The group worked to conserve the beauty of Europe’s national parks.

english_meaning: If something is crucial, it is extremely important to another thing.
english_example: Clean air is crucial to the survival of humans, plants, and animals.
spanish_meaning: Algo crucial es extremadamente importante para otra cosa.
spanish_example: El aire limpio es crucial para la vida.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is crucial, it is extremely important to another thing.
spanish_example_en: Clean air is crucial to the survival of humans, plants, and animals.

english_meaning: Cumulative describes an increase by adding one after another.
english_example: The cumulative snowfall in the area is fifty centimeters per year.
spanish_meaning: Cumulativo significa que se suma poco a poco.
spanish_example: La nieve cumulativa en la zona llega a cincuenta centímetros al año.
spanish_meaning_en: Cumulative describes an increase by adding one after another.
spanish_example_en: The cumulative snowfall in the area is fifty centimeters per year.

english_meaning: To deposit something is to put it into a place or another thing.
english_example: I deposited the money into my bank account.
spanish_meaning: Depositar algo es colocarlo en un lugar, como una cuenta o caja.
spanish_example: Deposité el dinero en mi cuenta bancaria.
spanish_meaning_en: To deposit something is to put it into a place or another thing.
spanish_example_en: I deposited the money into my bank account.

english_meaning: To distribute something is to give it to a number of people.
english_example: The teacher distributed crayons and markers to her students.
spanish_meaning: Distribuir significa dar o repartir algo entre varias personas.
spanish_example: La maestra distribuyo crayones y rotulos a sus estudiantes.
spanish_meaning_en: To distribute something is to give it to a number of people.
spanish_example_en: The teacher distributed crayons and markers to her students.

english_meaning: The equator is an imaginary line that splits the Earth into north and south.
english_example: The equator crosses the northern part of South America.
spanish_meaning: El ecuador es una linea imaginaria que divide la Tierra entre norte y sur.
spanish_example: El ecuador atraviesa la parte norte de America del Sur.
spanish_meaning_en: The equator is an imaginary line that splits the Earth into north and south.
spanish_example_en: The equator crosses the northern part of South America.

english_meaning: Exotic describes something unusual because it is from far away.
english_example: Rebecca tried many exotic foods on her trip to Africa.
spanish_meaning: Exotico describe algo inusual porque viene de un lugar lejano.
spanish_example: Rebecca probó muchas comidas exóticas en su viaje a Africa.
spanish_meaning_en: Exotic describes something unusual because it is from far away.
spanish_example_en: Rebecca tried many exotic foods on her trip to Africa.

english_meaning: If something is federal, it relates to the government of a country.
english_example: Sometimes federal laws are different from state laws.
spanish_meaning: Algo federal está relacionado con el gobierno de un país.
spanish_example: A veces las leyes federales son diferentes de las estatales.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is federal, it relates to the government of a country.
spanish_example_en: Sometimes federal laws are different from state laws.

english_meaning: A formation is the way that something is made.
english_example: The formation of ice happens when water freezes.
spanish_meaning: La formacion es la manera en que algo se forma.
spanish_example: La formacion del hielo ocurre cuando el agua se congela.
spanish_meaning_en: A formation is the way that something is made.
spanish_example_en: The formation of ice happens when water freezes.

english_meaning: The frequency of something is the number of times that it happens.
english_example: The frequency of rainstorms is very high, especially during the spring.
spanish_meaning: La frecuencia es el numero de veces que algo sucede.
spanish_example: La frecuencia de tormentas de lluvia es muy alta en primavera.
spanish_meaning_en: The frequency of something is the number of times that it happens.
spanish_example_en: The frequency of rainstorms is very high, especially during the spring.

english_meaning: An objective is a goal or plan that someone has.
english_example: My objective this week is to finish my homework by 7:30 every night.
spanish_meaning: Un objetivo es una meta o plan.
spanish_example: Mi objetivo está semana es terminar mi tarea todos los días a las 7:30 de la noche.
spanish_meaning_en: An objective is a goal or plan that someone has.
spanish_example_en: My objective this week is to finish my homework by 7:30 every night.

english_meaning: Oxygen is a gas that all living things need to breathe.
english_example: She thinks there is more oxygen in the air in the country than in the city.
spanish_meaning: El oxígeno es un gas que los seres vivos necesitan para respirar.
spanish_example: En el campo suele haber más oxígeno en el aire que en la ciudad.
spanish_meaning_en: Oxygen is a gas that all living things need to breathe.
spanish_example_en: She thinks there is more oxygen in the air in the country than in the city.

english_meaning: A rainforest is a forest that is in a place where it rains very often.
english_example: The rainforest is home to many animals.
spanish_meaning: La selva tropical es un bosque muy lluvioso.
spanish_example: La selva tropical es hogar de muchos animales.
spanish_meaning_en: A rainforest is a forest that is in a place where it rains very often.
spanish_example_en: The rainforest is home to many animals.

english_meaning: A strategy is a plan for how to do something.
english_example: The team came up with a strategy to win the game.
spanish_meaning: Una estrategia es un plan para lograr algo.
spanish_example: El equipo invento una estrategia para ganar el juego.
spanish_meaning_en: A strategy is a plan for how to do something.
spanish_example_en: The team came up with a strategy to win the game.

english_meaning: If an area is wooded, it is covered with trees.
english_example: Jim and Ben decided to go hiking in the wooded area by the river.
spanish_meaning: Un área arbolada está cubierta de árboles.
spanish_example: Jim y Ben decidieron hacer senderismo en la zona arbolada junto al río.
spanish_meaning_en: If an area is wooded, it is covered with trees.
spanish_example_en: Jim and Ben decided to go hiking in the wooded area by the river.

english_meaning: Avail is help or benefit.
english_example: His studying was to no avail because he failed the test.
spanish_meaning: El provecho o la ayuda que algo proporciona.
spanish_example: Su estudio fue de poco provecho porque no aprobo el examen.
spanish_meaning_en: Avail is help or benefit.
spanish_example_en: His studying was to no avail because he failed the test.

english_meaning: To define means to clearly state, show, or explain what something is.
english_example: People define success in many different ways.
spanish_meaning: Definir es expresar con claridad que cosa es algo.
spanish_example: Las personas definen el exito de muchas maneras distintas.
spanish_meaning_en: To define means to clearly state, show, or explain what something is.
spanish_example_en: People define success in many different ways.

english_meaning: To dread is to be afraid of something that could, or is going to, happen.
english_example: I dread the possibility that I will not get into college.
spanish_meaning: Tener temor o miedo de que algo pueda pasar.
spanish_example: Temo la posibilidad de que no entre a la universidad.
spanish_meaning_en: To dread is to be afraid of something that could, or is going to, happen.
spanish_example_en: I dread the possibility that I will not get into college.

english_meaning: To expand is to become bigger in size.
english_example: A balloon will expand as you blow air into it.
spanish_meaning: Expandir significa hacerse más grande.
spanish_example: Un globo se expande cuando soplas aire dentro.
spanish_meaning_en: To expand is to become bigger in size.
spanish_example_en: A balloon will expand as you blow air into it.

english_meaning: If something is fundamental, it is a basic part of something.
english_example: The fundamental rules of basketball are easy.
spanish_meaning: Algo fundamental es una parte básica e importante.
spanish_example: Las reglas fundamentales del baloncesto son fáciles.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is fundamental, it is a basic part of something.
spanish_example_en: The fundamental rules of basketball are easy.

english_meaning: If something is horrifying, it is frightening and very unpleasant.
english_example: There was a horrifying car accident today.
spanish_meaning: Algo horripilante es aterrador y muy desagradable.
spanish_example: Hubo un accidente de auto horripilante hoy.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is horrifying, it is frightening and very unpleasant.
spanish_example_en: There was a horrifying car accident today.

english_meaning: If someone is incredulous about something, they do not believe that it is true.
english_example: She was incredulous that monkeys could ever drive a car.
spanish_meaning: Es incredulo quien no cree que algo sea verdad.
spanish_example: Ella estaba incredula de que los monos pudieran conducir un auto.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is incredulous about something, they do not believe that it is true.
spanish_example_en: She was incredulous that monkeys could ever drive a car.

english_meaning: To linger is to last for a long time.
english_example: The smell of fresh cookies lingered in the bakery.
spanish_meaning: Perdurar significa durar mucho tiempo.
spanish_example: El olor a galletas recién hechas perduro en la panaderia.
spanish_meaning_en: To linger is to last for a long time.
spanish_example_en: The smell of fresh cookies lingered in the bakery.

english_meaning: An organism is a living thing, especially a very small one.
english_example: We studied the organism under the microscope.
spanish_meaning: Un organismo es un ser vivo, especialmente uno pequeño.
spanish_example: Estudiamos el organismo bajo el microscopio.
spanish_meaning_en: An organism is a living thing, especially a very small one.
spanish_example_en: We studied the organism under the microscope.

english_meaning: To paraphrase is to make someone else’s writing or speech shorter.
english_example: The students were asked to paraphrase the story they had just heard.
spanish_meaning: Parafrasear es decir lo mismo con menos palabras.
spanish_example: Alumnos tuvieron que parafrasear la historia que acababan de oir.
spanish_meaning_en: To paraphrase is to make someone else’s writing or speech shorter.
spanish_example_en: The students were asked to paraphrase the story they had just heard.

english_meaning: A plague is a serious disease that quickly spreads to many people.
english_example: A plague in Europe killed millions of people.
spanish_meaning: Una peste es una enfermedad grave que se propaga rápidamente.
spanish_example: Una peste en Europa mató a millones de personas.
spanish_meaning_en: A plague is a serious disease that quickly spreads to many people.
spanish_example_en: A plague in Europe killed millions of people.

english_meaning: If something happens presently, it is happening right now.
english_example: Presently, our profits are good, but by next year we can do even better.
spanish_meaning: Actualmente significa en este momento.
spanish_example: Actualmente nuestras ganancias son buenas, pero el próximo año podemos hacerlo aún mejor.
notes: ambiguo entre "actualmente" y "proximamente" en otros contextos
spanish_meaning_en: If something happens presently, it is happening right now.
spanish_example_en: Presently, our profits are good, but by next year we can do even better.

english_meaning: If something is random, it happens without any pattern or reason.
english_example: Young children often ask random questions.
spanish_meaning: Cosas aleatorias suceden sin patrón ni razón aparente.
spanish_example: Los niños pequeños hacen preguntas aleatorias con frecuencia.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is random, it happens without any pattern or reason.
spanish_example_en: Young children often ask random questions.

english_meaning: A riot is a crowd that reacts to bad news by violently breaking laws.
english_example: A riot broke out after the candidate lost the election.
spanish_meaning: Un disturbio es una multitud que responde con violencia y desorden.
spanish_example: Después de la derrota electoral, estalló un disturbio.
spanish_meaning_en: A riot is a crowd that reacts to bad news by violently breaking laws.
spanish_example_en: A riot broke out after the candidate lost the election.

english_meaning: To scribble is to write something quickly without caring about how it looks.
english_example: I scribbled a rough diagram of our plan and gave it to him.
spanish_meaning: Garabatear es escribir rápido sin cuidar la letra.
spanish_example: Yo garabateé un dibujo rápido de nuestro plan y se lo di.
spanish_meaning_en: To scribble is to write something quickly without caring about how it looks.
spanish_example_en: I scribbled a rough diagram of our plan and gave it to him.

english_meaning: A shrine is a religious building built to honor a person, event, or god.
english_example: He prayed at the shrine for an hour.
spanish_meaning: Un santuario es un edificio religioso de devocion.
spanish_example: Oró durante una hora en el santuario.
spanish_meaning_en: A shrine is a religious building built to honor a person, event, or god.
spanish_example_en: He prayed at the shrine for an hour.

english_meaning: Solitude is the state of being totally alone.
english_example: John lives a life of solitude because he doesn’t get along well with people.
spanish_meaning: La soledad es estar completamente solo.
spanish_example: John vive una vida de soledad porque no se lleva bien con la gente.
spanish_meaning_en: Solitude is the state of being totally alone.
spanish_example_en: John lives a life of solitude because he doesn’t get along well with people.

english_meaning: If something is stark, then it is severe or clear in appearance or outline.
english_example: There is a stark contrast between their test scores.
spanish_meaning: Algo extremo/severo es muy marcado.
spanish_example: Hay un contraste extremo entre sus puntajes.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is stark, then it is severe or clear in appearance or outline.
spanish_example_en: There is a stark contrast between their test scores.

english_meaning: To summon a person is to ask them to come to you.
english_example: We summoned the doctor as soon as we noticed she was sick.
spanish_meaning: Convocar significa llamar a alguien para que venga.
spanish_example: Convocamos al doctor apenas notamos que estaba enferma.
spanish_meaning_en: To summon a person is to ask them to come to you.
spanish_example_en: We summoned the doctor as soon as we noticed she was sick.

english_meaning: To worsen is to get worse.
english_example: The weather suddenly worsened, and we had to stay inside.
spanish_meaning: Empeorar es volverse peor.
spanish_example: El clima empeoro de repente y tuvimos que quedarnos adentro.
spanish_meaning_en: To worsen is to get worse.
spanish_example_en: The weather suddenly worsened, and we had to stay inside.

english_meaning: An automobile is a car.
english_example: The first automobiles were very different from the ones that exist today.
spanish_meaning: Un automóvil es un coche.
spanish_example: Los primeros automóviles eran muy diferentes a los de hoy.
spanish_meaning_en: An automobile is a car.
spanish_example_en: The first automobiles were very different from the ones that exist today.

english_meaning: A candidate is a person who is competing to win something such as a job.
english_example: Alice is the best candidate for the job.
spanish_meaning: Un candidato compite por ganar algo, como un empleo.
spanish_example: Alice es la mejor candidata para el puesto.
spanish_meaning_en: A candidate is a person who is competing to win something such as a job.
spanish_example_en: Alice is the best candidate for the job.

english_meaning: If something is confidential, it must be kept secret.
english_example: The information from the meeting is confidential.
spanish_meaning: Confidencial significa que debe mantenerse en secreto.
spanish_example: La informacion de la reunion es confidencial.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is confidential, it must be kept secret.
spanish_example_en: The information from the meeting is confidential.

english_meaning: If something is corporate, it is related to a large business.
english_example: Tom enjoys working in the corporate world.
spanish_meaning: Cosas corporativas pertenecen a una gran empresa.
spanish_example: A Tom le gusta trabajar en el mundo corporativo.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is corporate, it is related to a large business.
spanish_example_en: Tom enjoys working in the corporate world.

english_meaning: To enhance something is to make it better.
english_example: Amy’s blue shirt really enhances the color of her eyes.
spanish_meaning: Mejorar significa hacer algo más bueno.
spanish_example: La camisa azul de Amy mejora mucho el color de sus ojos.
spanish_meaning_en: To enhance something is to make it better.
spanish_example_en: Amy’s blue shirt really enhances the color of her eyes.

english_meaning: An era is a period of time that has something special about it.
english_example: During the medieval era, knights wore protective armor.
spanish_meaning: Una era es un período histórico con características propias.
spanish_example: En la era medieval, los caballeros usaban armadura.
spanish_meaning_en: An era is a period of time that has something special about it.
spanish_example_en: During the medieval era, knights wore protective armor.

english_meaning: A guideline is a rule about how to do something.
english_example: Before they began the project, the teacher gave them some guidelines.
spanish_meaning: Una guia es una regla o norma para actuar.
spanish_example: Antes de iniciar el proyecto, el profesor dio algunas directrices.
spanish_meaning_en: A guideline is a rule about how to do something.
spanish_example_en: Before they began the project, the teacher gave them some guidelines.

english_meaning: To incorporate is to add something to another thing.
english_example: I decided to incorporate a new ingredient into my cake recipe.
spanish_meaning: Incorporar es agregar una cosa a otra.
spanish_example: Decidi incorporar un nuevo ingrediente en mi receta de pastel.
spanish_meaning_en: To incorporate is to add something to another thing.
spanish_example_en: I decided to incorporate a new ingredient into my cake recipe.

english_meaning: To interact is to talk to or do something with another person.
english_example: The kids began to interact when the adults left the room.
spanish_meaning: Interactuar es comunicarte o actuar con otra persona.
spanish_example: Los niños comenzaron a interactuar cuando los adultos se fueron.
spanish_meaning_en: To interact is to talk to or do something with another person.
spanish_example_en: The kids began to interact when the adults left the room.

english_meaning: An interval is the time between two things happening.
english_example: Tony rested for brief intervals while he worked in the yard.
spanish_meaning: El intervalo es el tiempo entre dos momentos.
spanish_example: Tony descanso en intervalos cortos mientras trabajaba en el patio.
spanish_meaning_en: An interval is the time between two things happening.
spanish_example_en: Tony rested for brief intervals while he worked in the yard.

english_meaning: If something is mobile, it can be moved easily.
english_example: Mobile phones are convenient because you can use them anywhere.
spanish_meaning: Móvil significa que puede trasladarse o estar disponible.
spanish_example: Los celulares móviles son prácticos porque se pueden usar en cualquier lugar.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is mobile, it can be moved easily.
spanish_example_en: Mobile phones are convenient because you can use them anywhere.

english_meaning: To modify something is to change it a little bit.
english_example: I modified my outfit by adding a belt to it.
spanish_meaning: Modificar es cambiar algo levemente.
spanish_example: Modifique mi atuendo al agregarle un cinturón.
spanish_meaning_en: To modify something is to change it a little bit.
spanish_example_en: I modified my outfit by adding a belt to it.

english_meaning: If two things are parallel, they are the same distance away from each other (along their entire length).
english_example: There are two yellow parallel lines dividing both sides of traffic.
spanish_meaning: Paralelo significa que corre al mismo nivel o distancia.
spanish_example: En la carretera hay dos lineas paralelas amarillas en ambos sentidos.
spanish_meaning_en: If two things are parallel, they are the same distance away from each other (along their entire length).
spanish_example_en: There are two yellow parallel lines dividing both sides of traffic.

english_meaning: A phenomenon is something that can be seen as it is happening.
english_example: I thought the lights were a strange phenomenon, but they were just shooting stars.
spanish_meaning: Un fenomeno es algo observable que ocurre.
spanish_example: Pensé que las luces eran un fenomeno raro, pero eran simples estrellas fugaces.
spanish_meaning_en: A phenomenon is something that can be seen as it is happening.
spanish_example_en: I thought the lights were a strange phenomenon, but they were just shooting stars.

english_meaning: To pollute means to make air, water, or land dirty, unclean, or foul.
english_example: The careless factory polluted the river with chemicals.
spanish_meaning: Contaminar es ensuciar aire, agua o tierra.
spanish_example: La fábrica descuidada contaminó el río con químicos.
spanish_meaning_en: To pollute means to make air, water, or land dirty, unclean, or foul.
spanish_example_en: The careless factory polluted the river with chemicals.

english_meaning: To ridicule is to make fun of something in a mean way.
english_example: The other students ridicule Peter’s foreign accent.
spanish_meaning: Ridiculizar es burlarse de forma cruel.
spanish_example: Los otros alumnos ridiculizaron el acento extranjero de Peter.
spanish_meaning_en: To ridicule is to make fun of something in a mean way.
spanish_example_en: The other students ridicule Peter’s foreign accent.

english_meaning: If something is solar, it is related to the sun.
english_example: Using solar energy is good for the environment.
spanish_meaning: Algo solar se relaciona con el sol.
spanish_example: Usar energía solar es bueno para el medio ambiente.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is solar, it is related to the sun.
spanish_example_en: Using solar energy is good for the environment.

english_meaning: A territory is a piece of land that belongs to a country but isn’t a state.
english_example: Gibraltar is a territory of Great Britain.
spanish_meaning: Territorio es una zona de tierra que pertenece a un país.
spanish_example: Gibraltar es un territorio del Reino Unido.
spanish_meaning_en: A territory is a piece of land that belongs to a country but isn’t a state.
spanish_example_en: Gibraltar is a territory of Great Britain.

english_meaning: A tournament is a competition, usually with many people participating.
english_example: My dad is playing in a golf tournament tomorrow.
spanish_meaning: Un torneo es una competencia con muchos participantes.
spanish_example: Mi padre jugará en un torneo de golf mañana.
spanish_meaning_en: A tournament is a competition, usually with many people participating.
spanish_example_en: My dad is playing in a golf tournament tomorrow.

english_meaning: Transportation is any type of vehicle that can carry people or things.
english_example: I don’t have a car, so my normal transportation is the train.
spanish_meaning: El transporte es cualquier medio para llevar personas o cosas.
spanish_example: Como no tengo auto, mi transporte normal es el tren.
spanish_meaning_en: Transportation is any type of vehicle that can carry people or things.
spanish_example_en: I don’t have a car, so my normal transportation is the train.

english_meaning: A bill is a statement of money owed for goods or for a service.
english_example: I have so many bills that I do not know how to pay them all.
spanish_meaning: Una factura o cuenta es dinero que se debe pagar.
spanish_example: Tengo tantas facturas que no se pagar todas.
spanish_meaning_en: A bill is a statement of money owed for goods or for a service.
spanish_example_en: I have so many bills that I do not know how to pay them all.

english_meaning: A boundary is the line where one area of land stops and another begins.
english_example: This fence shows the boundary between our yard and yours.
spanish_meaning: La frontera es la linea que separa dos zonas.
spanish_example: La cerca muestra la frontera entre nuestro patio y el tuyo.
spanish_meaning_en: A boundary is the line where one area of land stops and another begins.
spanish_example_en: This fence shows the boundary between our yard and yours.

english_meaning: Chaos is a situation that is confusing and not ordered.
english_example: His presentation was in chaos. I couldn’t understand what he meant.
spanish_meaning: El caos es una situación confusa y desordenada.
spanish_example: Su exposición estaba en un caos y no pude entenderlo.
spanish_meaning_en: Chaos is a situation that is confusing and not ordered.
spanish_example_en: His presentation was in chaos. I couldn’t understand what he meant.

english_meaning: If a person is consistent, they always have the same behavior or attitude.
english_example: Sara comes in every day and is our most consistent worker.
spanish_meaning: Consistente significa constante y sin cambios.
spanish_example: Sara llega todos los días y es nuestra trabajadora más consistente.
spanish_meaning_en: If a person is consistent, they always have the same behavior or attitude.
spanish_example_en: Sara comes in every day and is our most consistent worker.

english_meaning: A cyclone is a large storm with heavy rain and winds that spin in a circle.
english_example: Hundreds of homes were damaged by the cyclone.
spanish_meaning: Un ciclón es una tormenta muy fuerte con lluvia y vientos circulares.
spanish_example: Cientos de hogares se dañaron por el ciclón.
spanish_meaning_en: A cyclone is a large storm with heavy rain and winds that spin in a circle.
spanish_example_en: Hundreds of homes were damaged by the cyclone.

english_meaning: If someone or something is doomed, they are going to fail or be destroyed.
english_example: Since I spent all my money, my date with Jane is doomed.
spanish_meaning: Condenado significa destinado al fracaso o destruccion.
spanish_example: Mi cita con Jane está condenada desde que gaste todo mi dinero.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone or something is doomed, they are going to fail or be destroyed.
spanish_example_en: Since I spent all my money, my date with Jane is doomed.

english_meaning: An heir is a person who receives the money or property of someone who dies.
english_example: The princess was the heir to the king and queen’s throne.
spanish_meaning: Un heredero recibe bienes o propiedades de alguien fallecido.
spanish_example: La princesa era la heredera del trono del rey y la reina.
spanish_meaning_en: An heir is a person who receives the money or property of someone who dies.
spanish_example_en: The princess was the heir to the king and queen’s throne.

english_meaning: If something is martial, it is related to fighting or war.
english_example: Karate is a martial art that began many years ago in Japan.
spanish_meaning: Marcial se relaciona con la guerra o la lucha.
spanish_example: El karate es un arte marcial que comenzo hace muchos años en Japon.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is martial, it is related to fighting or war.
spanish_example_en: Karate is a martial art that began many years ago in Japan.

english_meaning: If food is organic, it is grown without adding chemicals to it.
english_example: The organic carrots are more expensive, but they’re better for you.
spanish_meaning: orgánico indica cultivado sin químicos.
spanish_example: Las zanahorias orgánicas son más caras pero son mejores para la salud.
spanish_meaning_en: If food is organic, it is grown without adding chemicals to it.
spanish_example_en: The organic carrots are more expensive, but they’re better for you.

english_meaning: Poultry is a bird, such as a chicken, that is used for meat and eggs.
english_example: He raises poultry and sells their meat for extra money.
spanish_meaning: Las aves de corral son aves criadas para carne y huevos.
spanish_example: Cria aves de corral y vende su carne para ganar dinero extra.
spanish_meaning_en: Poultry is a bird, such as a chicken, that is used for meat and eggs.
spanish_example_en: He raises poultry and sells their meat for extra money.

english_meaning: To scramble is to move somewhere quickly and desperately.
english_example: The hikers scrambled down the side of the hill.
spanish_meaning: Apresurarse es moverse rápido y con urgencia.
spanish_example: Los excursionistas se apresuraron cuesta abajo de la colina.
spanish_meaning_en: To scramble is to move somewhere quickly and desperately.
spanish_example_en: The hikers scrambled down the side of the hill.

english_meaning: A sergeant is a soldier or police officer of middle rank.
english_example: He was promoted to sergeant after a year in the army.
spanish_meaning: Un sargento es un soldado o policia de rango medio.
spanish_example: Fue ascendido a sargento después de un año en el ejercito.
spanish_meaning_en: A sergeant is a soldier or police officer of middle rank.
spanish_example_en: He was promoted to sergeant after a year in the army.

english_meaning: If you describe something as sheer, it is complete and total.
english_example: I was impressed by her sheer dedication to jogging.
spanish_meaning: Absoluto significa completo o total.
spanish_example: Quedé impresionado por su dedicación absoluta al trote.
spanish_meaning_en: If you describe something as sheer, it is complete and total.
spanish_example_en: I was impressed by her sheer dedication to jogging.

english_meaning: A stance is an attitude about an issue that someone states clearly.
english_example: My stance is that using oil and gas is bad for the environment.
spanish_meaning: La postura es una posición o actitud clara ante un tema.
spanish_example: Mi postura es que usar petróleo y gas es malo para el ambiente.
spanish_meaning_en: A stance is an attitude about an issue that someone states clearly.
spanish_example_en: My stance is that using oil and gas is bad for the environment.

english_meaning: A telegraph is a method of sending electric messages on wires.
english_example: In the 1900s, the telegraph was the fastest way to send a message.
spanish_meaning: El telégrafo es un sistema de mensajes por cable.
spanish_example: A comienzos del siglo XX, el telégrafo era la forma más rápida de enviar mensajes.
spanish_meaning_en: A telegraph is a method of sending electric messages on wires.
spanish_example_en: In the 1900s, the telegraph was the fastest way to send a message.

english_meaning: Textile is cloth that has been woven or knitted.
english_example: The blue textile was going to be used to make blouses.
spanish_meaning: Lo textil es tela fabricada por tejido o tejido de punto.
spanish_example: La tela azul se iba a usar para hacer blusas.
spanish_meaning_en: Textile is cloth that has been woven or knitted.
spanish_example_en: The blue textile was going to be used to make blouses.

english_meaning: A tornado is a tube-shaped formation of air that spins very quickly.
english_example: During a tornado, the safest place to be is underground.
spanish_meaning: Un tornado es una tormenta circular muy intensa.
spanish_example: Durante un tornado, el lugar más seguro es estar bajo tierra.
spanish_meaning_en: A tornado is a tube-shaped formation of air that spins very quickly.
spanish_example_en: During a tornado, the safest place to be is underground.

english_meaning: A typhoon is a large tropical storm that moves in circles.
english_example: Thousands of people lost electricity after a typhoon hit Australia.
