spanish_meaning: Un tifón es una gran tormenta tropical que gira en espiral.
spanish_example: Miles de personas se quedaron sin electricidad tras el tifón en Australia.
spanish_meaning_en: A typhoon is a large tropical storm that moves in circles.
spanish_example_en: Thousands of people lost electricity after a typhoon hit Australia.

english_meaning: To wail is to show sadness by crying loudly.
english_example: The baby wailed because it was hungry.
spanish_meaning: Llorar a gritos expresa tristeza intensa.
spanish_example: El bebé lloraba a gritos porque tenía hambre.
spanish_meaning_en: To wail is to show sadness by crying loudly.
spanish_example_en: The baby wailed because it was hungry.

english_meaning: A wardrobe is the collection of all of a person’s clothing.
english_example: She bought some new clothes to expand her wardrobe.
spanish_meaning: El guardarropa es el conjunto de ropa de una persona.
spanish_example: Compro ropa nueva para ampliar su guardarropa.
spanish_meaning_en: A wardrobe is the collection of all of a person’s clothing.
spanish_example_en: She bought some new clothes to expand her wardrobe.

english_meaning: Something that has an adverse effect can be harmful, dangerous, or unfavorable.
english_example: I worry that the tornado will have an adverse effect on the farm.
spanish_meaning: Adverso es negativo o perjudicial.
spanish_example: Me preocupa que el tornado tenga un efecto adverso en la granja.
spanish_meaning_en: Something that has an adverse effect can be harmful, dangerous, or unfavorable.
spanish_example_en: I worry that the tornado will have an adverse effect on the farm.

english_meaning: An alternate option is a different option.
english_example: Taking the bus and driving to work are alternate ways to travel.
spanish_meaning: Una opción alternativa es una opción diferente.
spanish_example: Tomar el autobús y conducir al trabajo son formas alternativas de viajar.
spanish_meaning_en: An alternate option is a different option.
spanish_example_en: Taking the bus and driving to work are alternate ways to travel.

english_meaning: Materials that are biodegradable break down naturally into substances that do not harm the environment.
english_example: I use biodegradable compost to feed my garden.
spanish_meaning: Los materiales biodegradables se descomponen de forma natural en sustancias que no dañan el medio ambiente.
spanish_example: Utilizo abono biodegradable para alimentar mi jardín.
spanish_meaning_en: Materials that are biodegradable break down naturally into substances that do not harm the environment.
spanish_example_en: I use biodegradable compost to feed my garden.

english_meaning: When something is boxed, it is inside a package.
english_example: Justine brought a boxed gift to the birthday party.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo está encajonado, está dentro de un paquete.
spanish_example: Justine trajo una caja de regalo a la fiesta de cumpleaños.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is boxed, it is inside a package.
spanish_example_en: Justine brought a boxed gift to the birthday party.

english_meaning: If you choke on something, it stops you from breathing.
english_example: The gum Malinda swallowed made her choke.
spanish_meaning: Si te ahogas con algo, te impide respirar.
spanish_example: El chicle que Malinda tragó la hizo ahogarse.
spanish_meaning_en: If you choke on something, it stops you from breathing.
spanish_example_en: The gum Malinda swallowed made her choke.

english_meaning: When something is convenient, it saves you time or effort.
english_example: Walking through the park is a convenient way to exercise on the way to work.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es conveniente, le ahorra tiempo o esfuerzo.
spanish_example: Caminar por el parque es una forma cómoda de hacer ejercicio de camino al trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is convenient, it saves you time or effort.
spanish_example_en: Walking through the park is a convenient way to exercise on the way to work.

english_meaning: To discard something is to throw it away.
english_example: After repairing the window, discard any broken glass.
spanish_meaning: Desechar algo es tirarlo.
spanish_example: Después de reparar la ventana, deseche los vidrios rotos.
spanish_meaning_en: To discard something is to throw it away.
spanish_example_en: After repairing the window, discard any broken glass.

english_meaning: A dolphin is a large sea mammal that breathes air.
english_example: Intelligent dolphins have learned to communicate with humans.
spanish_meaning: Un delfín es un gran mamífero marino que respira aire.
spanish_example: Los delfines inteligentes han aprendido a comunicarse con los humanos.
spanish_meaning_en: A dolphin is a large sea mammal that breathes air.
spanish_example_en: Intelligent dolphins have learned to communicate with humans.

english_meaning: Ecologically means that something is done in a way that concerns living organisms and the environment.
english_example: We planted our garden according to ecologically correct practices.
spanish_meaning: Ecológicamente significa que algo se hace de una manera que concierne a los organismos vivos y al medio ambiente.
spanish_example: Plantamos nuestro jardín según prácticas ecológicamente correctas.
spanish_meaning_en: Ecologically means that something is done in a way that concerns living organisms and the environment.
spanish_example_en: We planted our garden according to ecologically correct practices.

english_meaning: Something that is fatal results in someone’s death.
english_example: Being shot by the attacker proved fatal to the victim.
spanish_meaning: Algo que es fatal resulta en la muerte de alguien.
spanish_example: El disparo del atacante resultó fatal para la víctima.
spanish_meaning_en: Something that is fatal results in someone’s death.
spanish_example_en: Being shot by the attacker proved fatal to the victim.

english_meaning: The number of times something happens is the incidence of the event.
english_example: We need to increase the incidence of success in school.
spanish_meaning: El número de veces que algo sucede es la incidencia del evento.
spanish_example: Necesitamos aumentar la incidencia del éxito en la escuela.
spanish_meaning_en: The number of times something happens is the incidence of the event.
spanish_example_en: We need to increase the incidence of success in school.

english_meaning: Municipal means that something belongs to a city or local government.
english_example: The municipal parking lot downtown can fit one hundred cars.
spanish_meaning: Algo municipal pertenece a una ciudad o gobierno local.
spanish_example: En el aparcamiento municipal del centro caben cien coches.
spanish_meaning_en: Municipal means that something belongs to a city or local government.
spanish_example_en: The municipal parking lot downtown can fit one hundred cars.

english_meaning: Overuse occurs when something is utilized too many times.
english_example: Rochelle’s overuse of the exercise bike caused the gears to break.
spanish_meaning: El uso excesivo ocurre cuando algo se utiliza demasiadas veces.
spanish_example: El uso excesivo de la bicicleta estática por parte de Rochelle provocó que los engranajes se rompieran.
spanish_meaning_en: Overuse occurs when something is utilized too many times.
spanish_example_en: Rochelle’s overuse of the exercise bike caused the gears to break.

english_meaning: A pond is a freshwater body that is smaller than a lake.
english_example: I learned to swim at the pond behind the recreation center.
spanish_meaning: Un estanque es una masa de agua dulce de menor tamaño que un lago.
spanish_example: Aprendí a nadar en el estanque detrás del centro recreativo.
spanish_meaning_en: A pond is a freshwater body that is smaller than a lake.
spanish_example_en: I learned to swim at the pond behind the recreation center.

english_meaning: When you recycle an object, you use its parts to make something else.
english_example: We recycle our newspapers so that they can be made into packing boxes.
spanish_meaning: Cuando reciclas un objeto, usas sus partes para hacer otra cosa.
spanish_example: Reciclamos nuestros periódicos para poder convertirlos en cajas de embalaje.
spanish_meaning_en: When you recycle an object, you use its parts to make something else.
spanish_example_en: We recycle our newspapers so that they can be made into packing boxes.

english_meaning: An object that is reusable can be utilized over and over again.
english_example: Saburo keeps his empty jelly jars because they are reusable for storing sewing supplies.
spanish_meaning: Un objeto reutilizable se puede utilizar una y otra vez.
spanish_example: Saburo guarda sus frascos de gelatina vacíos porque son reutilizables para guardar materiales de costura.
spanish_meaning_en: An object that is reusable can be utilized over and over again.
spanish_example_en: Saburo keeps his empty jelly jars because they are reusable for storing sewing supplies.

english_meaning: When something is stuffed, it is pushed into a small space.
english_example: The newspapers were all stuffed into the drawer.
spanish_meaning: Cuando se rellena algo, se empuja hacia un espacio pequeño.
spanish_example: Todos los periódicos estaban metidos en el cajón.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is stuffed, it is pushed into a small space.
spanish_example_en: The newspapers were all stuffed into the drawer.

english_meaning: A tragic event causes sadness because it might involve death or suffering.
english_example: The entire community attended the memorial service for the victims of the tragic fire.
spanish_meaning: Un acontecimiento trágico causa tristeza porque puede implicar muerte o sufrimiento.
spanish_example: Toda la comunidad asistió al servicio en memoria de las víctimas del trágico incendio.
spanish_meaning_en: A tragic event causes sadness because it might involve death or suffering.
spanish_example_en: The entire community attended the memorial service for the victims of the tragic fire.

english_meaning: To utilize something is to use it for a specific purpose.
english_example: The team can utilize the lab equipment to complete the experiment.
spanish_meaning: Utilizar algo es utilizarlo para un propósito específico.
spanish_example: El equipo puede utilizar el equipo de laboratorio para completar el experimento.
spanish_meaning_en: To utilize something is to use it for a specific purpose.
spanish_example_en: The team can utilize the lab equipment to complete the experiment.

english_meaning: A whale is a very large mammal that lives in the ocean.
english_example: We were surprised when a whale surfaced next to our boat.
spanish_meaning: Una ballena es un mamífero muy grande que vive en el océano.
spanish_example: Nos sorprendimos cuando una ballena apareció junto a nuestro barco.
spanish_meaning_en: A whale is a very large mammal that lives in the ocean.
spanish_example_en: We were surprised when a whale surfaced next to our boat.

english_meaning: An amateur is a person who does something for fun and isn’t paid for it.
english_example: The amateur took pictures just as well as the person who worked for money.
spanish_meaning: Un aficionado es una persona que hace algo por diversión y no le pagan por ello.
spanish_example: El aficionado tomó fotografías tan bien como la persona que trabajaba por dinero.
spanish_meaning_en: An amateur is a person who does something for fun and isn’t paid for it.
spanish_example_en: The amateur took pictures just as well as the person who worked for money.

english_meaning: If something is ambiguous, it is not entirely clear.
english_example: It’s ambiguous as to whether a newborn baby looks like a girl or a boy.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es ambiguo es que no está del todo claro.
spanish_example: Es ambiguo si un bebé recién nacido parece una niña o un niño.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is ambiguous, it is not entirely clear.
spanish_example_en: It’s ambiguous as to whether a newborn baby looks like a girl or a boy.

english_meaning: If someone is anonymous, no one knows who he or she is.
english_example: An anonymous donor gave a thousand dollars to the museum.
spanish_meaning: Si alguien es anónimo, nadie sabe quién es.
spanish_example: Un donante anónimo donó mil dólares al museo.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is anonymous, no one knows who he or she is.
spanish_example_en: An anonymous donor gave a thousand dollars to the museum.

english_meaning: To attain something is to succeed at something or to get something you want.
english_example: If you want to attain a healthy body, you must exercise every day.
spanish_meaning: Lograr algo es tener éxito en algo o conseguir algo que deseas.
spanish_example: Si quieres conseguir un cuerpo sano, debes hacer ejercicio todos los días.
spanish_meaning_en: To attain something is to succeed at something or to get something you want.
spanish_example_en: If you want to attain a healthy body, you must exercise every day.

english_meaning: Autonomy is another word for freedom or independence.
english_example: In the 1800s, the people of India fought for autonomy from Britain.
spanish_meaning: Autonomía es otra palabra para libertad o independencia.
spanish_example: En el siglo XIX, el pueblo de la India luchó por su autonomía de Gran Bretaña.
spanish_meaning_en: Autonomy is another word for freedom or independence.
spanish_example_en: In the 1800s, the people of India fought for autonomy from Britain.

english_meaning: A concession is something that one person gives up to another.
english_example: China gave Hong Kong to Britain as a concession after the war.
spanish_meaning: Una concesión es algo que una persona le da a otra.
spanish_example: China entregó Hong Kong a Gran Bretaña como concesión después de la guerra.
spanish_meaning_en: A concession is something that one person gives up to another.
spanish_example_en: China gave Hong Kong to Britain as a concession after the war.

english_meaning: Decay is the result of something slowly being broken down or destroyed naturally.
english_example: The decay in the old building was obvious.
spanish_meaning: La descomposición es el resultado de que algo se descompone lentamente o se destruye de forma natural.
spanish_example: El deterioro del antiguo edificio era evidente.
spanish_meaning_en: Decay is the result of something slowly being broken down or destroyed naturally.
spanish_example_en: The decay in the old building was obvious.

english_meaning: To dwell somewhere means to live there.
english_example: Before he was a successful writer, Mark Twain dwelled in the city of Hannibal, Missouri.
spanish_meaning: Habitar en algún lugar significa vivir allí.
spanish_example: Antes de convertirse en un escritor de éxito, Mark Twain vivió en la ciudad de Hannibal, Missouri.
spanish_meaning_en: To dwell somewhere means to live there.
spanish_example_en: Before he was a successful writer, Mark Twain dwelled in the city of Hannibal, Missouri.

english_meaning: To enlighten someone is to teach them about something.
english_example: Greek philosophers wanted to enlighten the people of Athens with their ideas.
spanish_meaning: Iluminar a alguien es enseñarle sobre algo.
spanish_example: Los filósofos griegos querían iluminar al pueblo de Atenas con sus ideas.
spanish_meaning_en: To enlighten someone is to teach them about something.
spanish_example_en: Greek philosophers wanted to enlighten the people of Athens with their ideas.

english_meaning: To enrich means to make someone rich or increase their wealth.
english_example: Taxes on people’s income can be used to enrich the government.
spanish_meaning: Enriquecer significa hacer rico a alguien o aumentar su riqueza.
spanish_example: Los impuestos sobre los ingresos de las personas pueden utilizarse para enriquecer al gobierno.
spanish_meaning_en: To enrich means to make someone rich or increase their wealth.
spanish_example_en: Taxes on people’s income can be used to enrich the government.

english_meaning: To flourish means to do very well and be in an excellent condition.
english_example: The Roman Empire flourished in Europe two thousand years ago.
spanish_meaning: Florecer significa hacerlo muy bien y estar en excelentes condiciones.
spanish_example: El Imperio Romano floreció en Europa hace dos mil años.
spanish_meaning_en: To flourish means to do very well and be in an excellent condition.
spanish_example_en: The Roman Empire flourished in Europe two thousand years ago.

english_meaning: Geometry is the study of shapes and how to measure them.
english_example: If you want to be able to calculate the area of a circle, you must study geometry.
spanish_meaning: La geometría es el estudio de las formas y cómo medirlas.
spanish_example: Si quieres poder calcular el área de un círculo, debes estudiar geometría.
spanish_meaning_en: Geometry is the study of shapes and how to measure them.
spanish_example_en: If you want to be able to calculate the area of a circle, you must study geometry.

english_meaning: To gleam means to sparkle and shine.
english_example: The waves of the ocean would gleam every night at sunset.
spanish_meaning: Brillar significa brillar y brillar.
spanish_example: Las olas del océano brillarían cada noche al atardecer.
spanish_meaning_en: To gleam means to sparkle and shine.
spanish_example_en: The waves of the ocean would gleam every night at sunset.

english_meaning: Greed is wanting to have more of something than you need or should have.
english_example: The story of King Midas is a story of greed.
spanish_meaning: La codicia es querer tener más de algo de lo que necesitas o deberías tener.
spanish_example: La historia del rey Midas es una historia de avaricia.
spanish_meaning_en: Greed is wanting to have more of something than you need or should have.
spanish_example_en: The story of King Midas is a story of greed.

english_meaning: Harmony is a feeling that everything is peaceful, balanced, and in agreement.
english_example: The United Nations is struggling to bring peace and harmony to the world.
spanish_meaning: La armonía es una sensación de que todo está en paz, en equilibrio y de acuerdo.
spanish_example: Las Naciones Unidas están luchando por traer paz y armonía al mundo.
spanish_meaning_en: Harmony is a feeling that everything is peaceful, balanced, and in agreement.
spanish_example_en: The United Nations is struggling to bring peace and harmony to the world.

english_meaning: Indigenous means that something or someone exists naturally in an environment or area.
english_example: The Pueblo people were an indigenous tribe in Arizona who lived in adobe homes.
spanish_meaning: Indígena significa que algo o alguien existe naturalmente en un entorno o área.
spanish_example: El pueblo pueblo era una tribu indígena de Arizona que vivía en casas de adobe.
spanish_meaning_en: Indigenous means that something or someone exists naturally in an environment or area.
spanish_example_en: The Pueblo people were an indigenous tribe in Arizona who lived in adobe homes.

english_meaning: Jurisdiction means the power or right to make judgments about the law and how it is upheld.
english_example: We did not have jurisdiction to bring the criminal to trial.
spanish_meaning: Jurisdicción significa el poder o derecho de emitir juicios sobre la ley y cómo se cumple.
spanish_example: No teníamos competencia para llevar al criminal a juicio.
spanish_meaning_en: Jurisdiction means the power or right to make judgments about the law and how it is upheld.
spanish_example_en: We did not have jurisdiction to bring the criminal to trial.

english_meaning: A parade is a series of things or people that come or are shown one after another.
english_example: This parade of elephants is part of an open-air exhibit to honor these majestic animals.
spanish_meaning: Un desfile es una serie de cosas o personas que vienen o se muestran una tras otra.
spanish_example: Este desfile de elefantes es parte de una exhibición al aire libre en honor a estos majestuosos animales.
spanish_meaning_en: A parade is a series of things or people that come or are shown one after another.
spanish_example_en: This parade of elephants is part of an open-air exhibit to honor these majestic animals.

english_meaning: A statue is a three-dimensional work of art, usually made of clay, marble, or metal.
english_example: The Venus de Milo is a famous ancient Greek statue.
spanish_meaning: Una estatua es una obra de arte tridimensional, generalmente hecha de arcilla, mármol o metal.
spanish_example: La Venus de Milo es una famosa estatua griega antigua.
spanish_meaning_en: A statue is a three-dimensional work of art, usually made of clay, marble, or metal.
spanish_example_en: The Venus de Milo is a famous ancient Greek statue.

english_meaning: A virgin is someone who has never had sex.
english_example: The virgin goddess of the moon was known as Diana.
spanish_meaning: Una virgen es alguien que nunca ha tenido relaciones sexuales.
spanish_example: La diosa virgen de la luna era conocida como Diana.
spanish_meaning_en: A virgin is someone who has never had sex.
spanish_example_en: The virgin goddess of the moon was known as Diana.

english_meaning: If something is abnormal, it is different from normal or average.
english_example: I felt abnormal this morning, like I was sick.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es anormal, es diferente de lo normal o promedio.
spanish_example: Esta mañana me sentí anormal, como si estuviera enferma.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is abnormal, it is different from normal or average.
spanish_example_en: I felt abnormal this morning, like I was sick.

english_meaning: If someone or something is absent, they are missing or not in the place they are expected to be.
english_example: I was absent from school yesterday because I went on a trip with my father.
spanish_meaning: Si alguien o algo falta, falta o no está en el lugar que se espera que esté.
spanish_example: Ayer estuve ausente de la escuela porque me fui de viaje con mi padre.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone or something is absent, they are missing or not in the place they are expected to be.
spanish_example_en: I was absent from school yesterday because I went on a trip with my father.

english_meaning: When something is adjacent, it is next to or adjoining something else.
english_example: My apartment is adjacent to a lovely park and playground.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo está adyacente, está al lado o contiguo a otra cosa.
spanish_example: Mi apartamento está junto a un hermoso parque y área de juegos para niños.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is adjacent, it is next to or adjoining something else.
spanish_example_en: My apartment is adjacent to a lovely park and playground.

english_meaning: Aluminum is a chemical element that is a light silver-coloured metal.
english_example: I used the aluminum foil to wrap the food.
spanish_meaning: El aluminio es un elemento químico que es un metal de color plateado claro.
spanish_example: Utilicé papel de aluminio para envolver la comida.
spanish_meaning_en: Aluminum is a chemical element that is a light silver-coloured metal.
spanish_example_en: I used the aluminum foil to wrap the food.

english_meaning: If something is applicable to a person or thing, it is relevant to them.
english_example: I discovered that my old password was no longer applicable to the website.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es aplicable a una persona o cosa, es relevante para ella.
spanish_example: Descubrí que mi antigua contraseña ya no era aplicable al sitio web.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is applicable to a person or thing, it is relevant to them.
spanish_example_en: I discovered that my old password was no longer applicable to the website.

english_meaning: If something is artificial, it was not made naturally but mimics something natural.
english_example: The artificial Christmas tree was made of plastic.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es artificial, no se hizo de forma natural sino que imita algo natural.
spanish_example: El árbol de Navidad artificial estaba hecho de plástico.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is artificial, it was not made naturally but mimics something natural.
spanish_example_en: The artificial Christmas tree was made of plastic.

english_meaning: A bicycle is a two-wheeled vehicle powered by pedaling.
english_example: I rode my bicycle down the mountain road.
spanish_meaning: Una bicicleta es un vehículo de dos ruedas que se mueve pedaleando.
spanish_example: Bajé mi bicicleta por la carretera de montaña.
spanish_meaning_en: A bicycle is a two-wheeled vehicle powered by pedaling.
spanish_example_en: I rode my bicycle down the mountain road.

english_meaning: To broker is to arrange or negotiate the details of something for others.
english_example: The lawyer will broker our agreement.
spanish_meaning: Intermediar es arreglar o negociar los detalles de algo para otros.
spanish_example: El abogado negociará nuestro acuerdo.
spanish_meaning_en: To broker is to arrange or negotiate the details of something for others.
spanish_example_en: The lawyer will broker our agreement.

english_meaning: A bureaucracy is a group of people who work together to help manage a large business or run a country.
english_example: The members of the bureaucracy were flooded by too many petitions.
spanish_meaning: Una burocracia es un grupo de personas que trabajan juntas para ayudar a administrar una gran empresa o administrar un país.
spanish_example: Los miembros de la burocracia se vieron inundados por demasiadas peticiones.
spanish_meaning_en: A bureaucracy is a group of people who work together to help manage a large business or run a country.
spanish_example_en: The members of the bureaucracy were flooded by too many petitions.

english_meaning: To configure something means to set it up and arrange it.
english_example: The engineer helped to configure my new computer.
spanish_meaning: Configurar algo significa configurarlo y arreglarlo.
spanish_example: El ingeniero me ayudó a configurar mi nueva computadora.
spanish_meaning_en: To configure something means to set it up and arrange it.
spanish_example_en: The engineer helped to configure my new computer.

english_meaning: To consolidate means to join or bring together into one thing.
english_example: When we moved in together, we had to consolidate our belongings.
spanish_meaning: Consolidar significa unir o reunir en una sola cosa.
spanish_example: Cuando nos mudamos juntos, tuvimos que consolidar nuestras pertenencias.
spanish_meaning_en: To consolidate means to join or bring together into one thing.
spanish_example_en: When we moved in together, we had to consolidate our belongings.

english_meaning: If something is done for your convenience, it allows you to do something easily or without any trouble.
english_example: I love the convenience of having a swimming pool in my back yard.
spanish_meaning: Si se hace algo para su conveniencia, le permitirá hacerlo fácilmente o sin ningún problema.
spanish_example: Me encanta la comodidad de tener una piscina en mi patio trasero.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is done for your convenience, it allows you to do something easily or without any trouble.
spanish_example_en: I love the convenience of having a swimming pool in my back yard.

english_meaning: To deduct means to subtract something.
english_example: I had to deduct my expenses from my checkbook.
spanish_meaning: Deducir significa restar algo.
spanish_example: Tuve que deducir mis gastos de mi chequera.
spanish_meaning_en: To deduct means to subtract something.
spanish_example_en: I had to deduct my expenses from my checkbook.

english_meaning: To deem means to consider something.
english_example: I deemed the ice cream to be very delicious.
spanish_meaning: Considerar significa considerar algo.
spanish_example: El helado me pareció muy delicioso.
spanish_meaning_en: To deem means to consider something.
spanish_example_en: I deemed the ice cream to be very delicious.

english_meaning: An entrepreneur is someone who starts a new business or organization in order to make money.
english_example: The entrepreneur made a success out of his new business.
spanish_meaning: Un emprendedor es alguien que inicia un nuevo negocio u organización para ganar dinero.
spanish_example: El empresario logró el éxito con su nuevo negocio.
spanish_meaning_en: An entrepreneur is someone who starts a new business or organization in order to make money.
spanish_example_en: The entrepreneur made a success out of his new business.

english_meaning: If something is evenly spread or spaced, it is divided equally into amounts, numbers, or values.
english_example: Sprinkle the sugar evenly all over the cookies.
spanish_meaning: Si algo está distribuido o espaciado uniformemente, se divide igualmente en cantidades, números o valores.
spanish_example: Espolvorea el azúcar uniformemente por todas las galletas.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is evenly spread or spaced, it is divided equally into amounts, numbers, or values.
spanish_example_en: Sprinkle the sugar evenly all over the cookies.

english_meaning: When something is fiscal, it is related to money or finances, especially that of a government or business.
english_example: Did the company show fiscal growth this year?
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es fiscal, está relacionado con el dinero o las finanzas, especialmente la de un gobierno o empresa.
spanish_example: ¿Mostró la empresa crecimiento fiscal este año?
spanish_meaning_en: When something is fiscal, it is related to money or finances, especially that of a government or business.
spanish_example_en: Did the company show fiscal growth this year?

english_meaning: A franchise is the right to sell another company’s products or services in a particular area.
english_example: The fast food restaurant has a franchise near my home.
spanish_meaning: Una franquicia es el derecho a vender los productos o servicios de otra empresa en un área determinada.
spanish_example: El restaurante de comida rápida tiene una franquicia cerca de mi casa.
spanish_meaning_en: A franchise is the right to sell another company’s products or services in a particular area.
spanish_example_en: The fast food restaurant has a franchise near my home.

english_meaning: If something is ideological, it is based on a system of beliefs or ideals, especially those that relate to a government or economy.
english_example: The citizens of England had ideological differenes about its government.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es ideológico, se basa en un sistema de creencias o ideales, especialmente aquellos que se relacionan con un gobierno o una economía.
spanish_example: Los ciudadanos de Inglaterra tenían diferencias ideológicas sobre su gobierno.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is ideological, it is based on a system of beliefs or ideals, especially those that relate to a government or economy.
spanish_example_en: The citizens of England had ideological differenes about its government.

english_meaning: A robot is a machine that can do the work of a person and operates automatically or is controlled by a computer.
english_example: I bought a robot that can clean the floor on its own.
spanish_meaning: Un robot es una máquina que puede hacer el trabajo de una persona y funciona automáticamente o está controlada por una computadora.
spanish_example: Compré un robot que puede limpiar el suelo por sí solo.
spanish_meaning_en: A robot is a machine that can do the work of a person and operates automatically or is controlled by a computer.
spanish_example_en: I bought a robot that can clean the floor on its own.

english_meaning: An abbey is a house, or group of houses, where monks or nuns live.
english_example: When the monk returned to the abbey, he went immediately to his bedroom.
spanish_meaning: Una abadía es una casa, o un grupo de casas, donde viven monjes o monjas.
spanish_example: Cuando el monje regresó a la abadía, se dirigió inmediatamente a su dormitorio.
spanish_meaning_en: An abbey is a house, or group of houses, where monks or nuns live.
spanish_example_en: When the monk returned to the abbey, he went immediately to his bedroom.

english_meaning: If something is abundant, then it is available in large quantities.
english_example: Cakes, cookies, and candy were so abundant that the child was very happy.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es abundante, entonces está disponible en grandes cantidades.
spanish_example: Había tantos pasteles, galletas y dulces que el niño estaba muy feliz.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is abundant, then it is available in large quantities.
spanish_example_en: Cakes, cookies, and candy were so abundant that the child was very happy.

english_meaning: To adjoin something means to be next to or attached to something else.
english_example: She can listen to her brother’s conversations because her room adjoins his.
spanish_meaning: Adjuntar algo significa estar al lado o unido a otra cosa.
spanish_example: Puede escuchar las conversaciones de su hermano porque su habitación está contigua a la de él.
spanish_meaning_en: To adjoin something means to be next to or attached to something else.
spanish_example_en: She can listen to her brother’s conversations because her room adjoins his.

english_meaning: If something is ample, then it is enough or more than enough.
english_example: There was an ample supply of oats to feed the horses.
spanish_meaning: Si algo sobra, entonces es suficiente o más que suficiente.
spanish_example: Había una gran provisión de avena para alimentar a los caballos.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is ample, then it is enough or more than enough.
spanish_example_en: There was an ample supply of oats to feed the horses.

english_meaning: If a place is arid, then it is hot and dry and gets very little or no rain.
english_example: Not many plants grow in the arid desert.
spanish_meaning: Si un lugar es árido, entonces es cálido y seco y llueve muy poco o nada.
spanish_example: No crecen muchas plantas en el árido desierto.
spanish_meaning_en: If a place is arid, then it is hot and dry and gets very little or no rain.
spanish_example_en: Not many plants grow in the arid desert.

english_meaning: A cathedral is an important, and often large and beautifully-built, church.
english_example: The large cathedral is full of people on Sunday mornings.
spanish_meaning: Una catedral es una iglesia importante y, a menudo, grande y hermosa.
spanish_example: La gran catedral está llena de gente los domingos por la mañana.
spanish_meaning_en: A cathedral is an important, and often large and beautifully-built, church.
spanish_example_en: The large cathedral is full of people on Sunday mornings.

english_meaning: A crisis is a difficult time when things are going to either get worse or better.
english_example: The crisis was over and things returned to normal.
spanish_meaning: Una crisis es un momento difícil en el que las cosas van a empeorar o mejorar.
spanish_example: La crisis terminó y todo volvió a la normalidad.
spanish_meaning_en: A crisis is a difficult time when things are going to either get worse or better.
spanish_example_en: The crisis was over and things returned to normal.

english_meaning: To deprive someone of something means to not let them have it.
english_example: Because the child was bad, she was deprived of her dessert after dinner.
spanish_meaning: Privar a alguien de algo significa no dejarle tenerlo.
spanish_example: Como la niña estaba mala, la privaron del postre después de la cena.
spanish_meaning_en: To deprive someone of something means to not let them have it.
spanish_example_en: Because the child was bad, she was deprived of her dessert after dinner.

english_meaning: A drought is a long period of time in which little or no rain falls.
english_example: After three months of drought, the vegetation and trees started dying.
spanish_meaning: Una sequía es un largo período de tiempo en el que llueve poco o nada.
spanish_example: Después de tres meses de sequía, la vegetación y los árboles empezaron a morir.
spanish_meaning_en: A drought is a long period of time in which little or no rain falls.
spanish_example_en: After three months of drought, the vegetation and trees started dying.

english_meaning: If someone is eligible, then they are permitted to do or have something.
english_example: Only people who bought tickets were eligible to win a prize.

spanish_example: Sólo las personas que compraron entradas podían ganar un premio.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is eligible, then they are permitted to do or have something.
spanish_example_en: Only people who bought tickets were eligible to win a prize.

english_meaning: To fast means to go without food or drink for a period of time.
english_example: In her religion, they fast for five days and then have a big feast.

spanish_meaning_en: To fast means to go without food or drink for a period of time.
spanish_example_en: In her religion, they fast for five days and then have a big feast.

english_meaning: To grumble means to complain.

spanish_meaning: Refunfuñar significa quejarse.
spanish_example: Se quejó de tener que trabajar hasta tarde el viernes.
spanish_meaning_en: To grumble means to complain.
spanish_example_en: He grumbled about having to work late on Friday.

english_meaning: If someone goes inland, they travel into the center of a country or land.

english_meaning: If something happens nonetheless, then it occurs despite some other thing.

english_meaning: An enterprise is a company or business.
english_example: My father owns an advertising enterprise.
spanish_meaning: Una empresa es una organización o negocio que realiza actividad comercial.
spanish_example: Mi padre es dueño de una empresa de publicidad.
spanish_meaning_en: An enterprise is a company or business.
spanish_example_en: My father owns an advertising enterprise.

english_meaning: To finance someone or something means to provide money for them.
english_example: The government financed the scientist’s experiments with new weapons.
spanish_meaning: Financiar a alguien o algo significa proporcionarle dinero.
spanish_example: El gobierno financió los experimentos del científico con nuevas armas.
spanish_meaning_en: To finance someone or something means to provide money for them.
spanish_example_en: The government financed the scientist’s experiments with new weapons.

english_meaning: When someone is indifferent toward something, they have a lack of interest in it.
english_example: Lisa is indifferent toward school. She doesn’t care what her final grades are.
spanish_meaning: Cuando alguien es indiferente hacia algo, tiene falta de interés en ello.
spanish_example: Lisa es indiferente hacia la escuela. A ella no le importa cuáles sean sus calificaciones finales.
spanish_meaning_en: When someone is indifferent toward something, they have a lack of interest in it.
spanish_example_en: Lisa is indifferent toward school. She doesn’t care what her final grades are.

english_meaning: To irrigate means to supply water to land so that crops can grow.
english_example: In dry climates, it is important to irrigate fields of crops.
spanish_meaning: Regar significa suministrar agua a la tierra para que puedan crecer los cultivos.
spanish_example: En climas secos, es importante regar los campos de cultivo.
spanish_meaning_en: To irrigate means to supply water to land so that crops can grow.
spanish_example_en: In dry climates, it is important to irrigate fields of crops.

english_meaning: To maximize something is to make it as great as possible in amount, size, or importance.
english_example: You should exercise regularly to maximize a healthy lifestyle.
spanish_meaning: Maximizar algo es hacerlo lo más grande posible en cantidad, tamaño o importancia.
spanish_example: Debe hacer ejercicio con regularidad para maximizar un estilo de vida saludable.
spanish_meaning_en: To maximize something is to make it as great as possible in amount, size, or importance.
spanish_example_en: You should exercise regularly to maximize a healthy lifestyle.

english_meaning: When something is monetary, it relates to money.
english_example: A strong monetary policy is important for a country to be successful.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es monetario, se relaciona con el dinero.
spanish_example: Una política monetaria fuerte es importante para que un país tenga éxito.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is monetary, it relates to money.
spanish_example_en: A strong monetary policy is important for a country to be successful.

english_meaning: A precaution is an action that is meant to stop something bad from happening.
english_example: As a precaution, you should put on a heavy coat before going out in cold weather.
spanish_meaning: Una precaución es una acción destinada a evitar que suceda algo malo.
spanish_example: Como precaución, conviene ponerse un abrigo grueso antes de salir cuando hace frío.
spanish_meaning_en: A precaution is an action that is meant to stop something bad from happening.
spanish_example_en: As a precaution, you should put on a heavy coat before going out in cold weather.

english_meaning: Preliminary describes something that happens before a more important event.
english_example: The runners must do well in the preliminary races to qualify for the final race.
spanish_meaning: Preliminar describe algo que sucede antes de un evento más importante.
spanish_example: Los corredores deben tener un buen desempeño en las carreras preliminares para clasificarse para la carrera final.
spanish_meaning_en: Preliminary describes something that happens before a more important event.
spanish_example_en: The runners must do well in the preliminary races to qualify for the final race.

english_meaning: To saturate something means to completely soak it with a liquid.
english_example: The sponge was saturated with soapy water and dripped all over the floor.
spanish_meaning: Saturar algo significa empaparlo completamente con un líquido.
spanish_example: La esponja se saturó con agua y jabón y goteó por todo el suelo.
spanish_meaning_en: To saturate something means to completely soak it with a liquid.
spanish_example_en: The sponge was saturated with soapy water and dripped all over the floor.

english_meaning: The simplicity of something is the fact that it is easy to do or understand.
english_example: We were able to find the house thanks to the simplicity of the directions.
spanish_meaning: La simplicidad de algo es el hecho de que sea fácil de hacer o entender.
spanish_example: Pudimos encontrar la casa gracias a la sencillez de las indicaciones.
spanish_meaning_en: The simplicity of something is the fact that it is easy to do or understand.
spanish_example_en: We were able to find the house thanks to the simplicity of the directions.

english_meaning: To sow seeds means to plant them in the ground.
english_example: He always sows his garden seeds in the springtime.
spanish_meaning: Sembrar semillas significa plantarlas en la tierra.
spanish_example: Siempre siembra las semillas de su jardín en primavera.
spanish_meaning_en: To sow seeds means to plant them in the ground.
spanish_example_en: He always sows his garden seeds in the springtime.

english_meaning: A spade is a tool used for digging.
english_example: The gardener used her spade to make a hole for the new plant.
spanish_meaning: Una pala es una herramienta que se utiliza para cavar.
spanish_example: La jardinera usó su pala para hacer un agujero para la nueva planta.
spanish_meaning_en: A spade is a tool used for digging.
spanish_example_en: The gardener used her spade to make a hole for the new plant.

english_meaning: A tomato is a round red fruit, but usually eaten as a vegetable. It’s often used in salads or pasta sauces.
english_example: I like tomato sandwiches.
spanish_meaning: El tomate es un fruto rojo y redondo, pero normalmente se come como verdura. Se suele utilizar en ensaladas o salsas para pasta.
spanish_example: Me gustan los sándwiches de tomate.
spanish_meaning_en: A tomato is a round red fruit, but usually eaten as a vegetable. It’s often used in salads or pasta sauces.
spanish_example_en: I like tomato sandwiches.

english_meaning: When something is upcoming, that means it will happen in the near future.
english_example: The kids were worried about their upcoming exam.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo está por llegar, significa que sucederá en un futuro cercano.
spanish_example: Los niños estaban preocupados por su próximo examen.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is upcoming, that means it will happen in the near future.
spanish_example_en: The kids were worried about their upcoming exam.

english_meaning: Anthropology is the study of people, society, and culture.
english_example: In anthropology class, I learned about simple tools that ancient cultures used.
spanish_meaning: La antropología es el estudio de las personas, la sociedad y la cultura.
spanish_example: En la clase de antropología, aprendí sobre herramientas simples que usaban las culturas antiguas.
spanish_meaning_en: Anthropology is the study of people, society, and culture.
spanish_example_en: In anthropology class, I learned about simple tools that ancient cultures used.

english_meaning: To applaud means to clap in order to show approval.
english_example: Everyone cheered and applauded Manny’s efforts.
spanish_meaning: Aplaudir significa aplaudir para mostrar aprobación.
spanish_example: Todos vitorearon y aplaudieron los esfuerzos de Manny.
spanish_meaning_en: To applaud means to clap in order to show approval.
spanish_example_en: Everyone cheered and applauded Manny’s efforts.

english_meaning: To appoint someone to a job means to give the job to them.
english_example: Two students were appointed to help the scientists with their research.
spanish_meaning: Designar a alguien para un trabajo significa darle el trabajo.
spanish_example: Se designó a dos estudiantes para ayudar a los científicos con su investigación.
spanish_meaning_en: To appoint someone to a job means to give the job to them.
spanish_example_en: Two students were appointed to help the scientists with their research.

english_meaning: When things are compatible, they work well or exist together successfully.
english_example: Jan and Fred are too different. They will never be compatible.
spanish_meaning: Cuando las cosas son compatibles, funcionan bien o coexisten exitosamente.
spanish_example: Jan y Fred son demasiado diferentes. Nunca serán compatibles.
spanish_meaning_en: When things are compatible, they work well or exist together successfully.
spanish_example_en: Jan and Fred are too different. They will never be compatible.

english_meaning: Competence is the ability to do something well or effectively.
english_example: The job was easy because the group had enough competence to do it well.
spanish_meaning: La competencia es la capacidad de hacer algo bien o eficazmente.
spanish_example: El trabajo fue fácil porque el grupo tenía la competencia suficiente para hacerlo bien.
spanish_meaning_en: Competence is the ability to do something well or effectively.
spanish_example_en: The job was easy because the group had enough competence to do it well.

english_meaning: To confer with someone means to discuss something with them to make a decision.
english_example: I will have to confer with my wife before I can purchase a new car.
spanish_meaning: Consultar con alguien significa discutir algo con él para tomar una decisión.
spanish_example: Tendré que consultar con mi esposa antes de poder comprar un auto nuevo.
spanish_meaning_en: To confer with someone means to discuss something with them to make a decision.
spanish_example_en: I will have to confer with my wife before I can purchase a new car.

english_meaning: When things are consecutive, they happen one after another without interruption.
english_example: The king ruled for ten consecutive years.
spanish_meaning: Cuando las cosas son consecutivas, suceden una tras otra sin interrupción.
spanish_example: El rey gobernó durante diez años consecutivos.
spanish_meaning_en: When things are consecutive, they happen one after another without interruption.
spanish_example_en: The king ruled for ten consecutive years.

english_meaning: When something is crude, it is not exact or detailed but can still be useful.
english_example: She drew crude hearts on the ground to show how much she loved him.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es tosco, no es exacto ni detallado, pero aun así puede resultar útil.
spanish_example: Dibujó toscos corazones en el suelo para mostrar cuánto lo amaba.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is crude, it is not exact or detailed but can still be useful.
spanish_example_en: She drew crude hearts on the ground to show how much she loved him.

english_meaning: A cube is a solid object with six square surfaces that are all the same size.
english_example: Please get me some ice cubes to put in my soda.
spanish_meaning: Un cubo es un objeto sólido con seis superficies cuadradas y todas del mismo tamaño.
spanish_example: Por favor, tráeme unos cubitos de hielo para poner en mi refresco.
spanish_meaning_en: A cube is a solid object with six square surfaces that are all the same size.
spanish_example_en: Please get me some ice cubes to put in my soda.

english_meaning: Feedback is comments to a person about how they are doing something.
english_example: I asked my boss for feedback on my work.
spanish_meaning: La retroalimentación son comentarios a una persona sobre cómo está haciendo algo.
spanish_example: Le pedí a mi jefe comentarios sobre mi trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: Feedback is comments to a person about how they are doing something.
spanish_example_en: I asked my boss for feedback on my work.

english_meaning: Ignorance of something is lack of knowledge about it.
english_example: When he failed the test, his ignorance of math was obvious.
spanish_meaning: El desconocimiento de algo es la falta de conocimiento sobre ello.
spanish_example: Cuando reprobó el examen, su ignorancia en matemáticas era obvia.
spanish_meaning_en: Ignorance of something is lack of knowledge about it.
spanish_example_en: When he failed the test, his ignorance of math was obvious.

english_meaning: When something is masculine, it is a quality or thing related to men.
english_example: American football is usually considered a masculine sport.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es masculino, es una cualidad o cosa relacionada con los hombres.
spanish_example: El fútbol americano suele considerarse un deporte masculino.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is masculine, it is a quality or thing related to men.
spanish_example_en: American football is usually considered a masculine sport.

english_meaning: A monument is a structure that is built to remind people of a person or event.
english_example: A large monument was built to honor the brave soldiers.
spanish_meaning: Un monumento es una estructura que se construye para recordar a la gente una persona o un evento.
spanish_example: Se construyó un gran monumento en honor a los valientes soldados.
spanish_meaning_en: A monument is a structure that is built to remind people of a person or event.
spanish_example_en: A large monument was built to honor the brave soldiers.

english_meaning: When someone is muscular, they are very fit and strong.
english_example: He exercised regularly so that his body could become muscular.
spanish_meaning: Cuando alguien es musculoso, está muy en forma y es fuerte.
spanish_example: Hacía ejercicio con regularidad para que su cuerpo se volviera musculoso.
spanish_meaning_en: When someone is muscular, they are very fit and strong.
spanish_example_en: He exercised regularly so that his body could become muscular.

english_meaning: A person’s posture is the manner in which they stand or sit.
english_example: Your back will feel better if you improve your posture.
spanish_meaning: La postura de una persona es la forma en que se para o se sienta.
spanish_example: Tu espalda se sentirá mejor si mejoras tu postura.
spanish_meaning_en: A person’s posture is the manner in which they stand or sit.
spanish_example_en: Your back will feel better if you improve your posture.

english_meaning: To situate something means to place or build it in a certain place.
english_example: The road was situated between the forest and the lake.
spanish_meaning: Situar algo significa colocarlo o construirlo en un lugar determinado.
spanish_example: El camino estaba situado entre el bosque y el lago.
spanish_meaning_en: To situate something means to place or build it in a certain place.
spanish_example_en: The road was situated between the forest and the lake.

english_meaning: To supervise something means to make sure that it is done correctly.
english_example: Allen supervised the construction workers to ensure everyone’s safety.
spanish_meaning: Supervisar algo significa asegurarse de que se haga correctamente.
spanish_example: Allen supervisó a los trabajadores de la construcción para garantizar la seguridad de todos.
spanish_meaning_en: To supervise something means to make sure that it is done correctly.
spanish_example_en: Allen supervised the construction workers to ensure everyone’s safety.

english_meaning: Symmetry is the state of having two halves that are exactly the same.
english_example: The artist made sure to use perfect symmetry when painting the butterfly.
spanish_meaning: La simetría es el estado de tener dos mitades que son exactamente iguales.
spanish_example: El artista se aseguró de utilizar una simetría perfecta al pintar la mariposa.
spanish_meaning_en: Symmetry is the state of having two halves that are exactly the same.
spanish_example_en: The artist made sure to use perfect symmetry when painting the butterfly.

english_meaning: A tattoo is a design that is drawn permanently on the skin with needles.
english_example: The surfer had tattoos on both his arms.
spanish_meaning: Un tatuaje es un diseño que se dibuja de forma permanente sobre la piel con agujas.
spanish_example: El surfista tenía tatuajes en ambos brazos.
spanish_meaning_en: A tattoo is a design that is drawn permanently on the skin with needles.
spanish_example_en: The surfer had tattoos on both his arms.

english_meaning: An undergraduate is a student at a college who is studying for a bachelor’s degree.
english_example: She was excited to finish high school and enroll as an undergraduate in the fall.
spanish_meaning: Una licenciatura es un estudiante de una universidad que está estudiando para obtener una licenciatura.
spanish_example: Estaba emocionada de terminar la escuela secundaria e inscribirse como estudiante universitaria en el otoño.
spanish_meaning_en: An undergraduate is a student at a college who is studying for a bachelor’s degree.
spanish_example_en: She was excited to finish high school and enroll as an undergraduate in the fall.

english_meaning: To cater to someone means to provide them with all the things they need or want.
english_example: Bill was too sick to get out of bed, so his nurse catered to his needs.
spanish_meaning: Atender a alguien significa proporcionarle todas las cosas que necesita o desea.
spanish_example: Bill estaba demasiado enfermo para levantarse de la cama, por lo que su enfermera atendió sus necesidades.
spanish_meaning_en: To cater to someone means to provide them with all the things they need or want.
spanish_example_en: Bill was too sick to get out of bed, so his nurse catered to his needs.

english_meaning: When someone is considerate, they pay attention to the needs of others.
english_example: The considerate boy gave his girlfriend a present to cheer her up when she was sad.
spanish_meaning: Cuando alguien es considerado, presta atención a las necesidades de los demás.
spanish_example: El considerado chico le dio un regalo a su novia para animarla cuando estaba triste.
spanish_meaning_en: When someone is considerate, they pay attention to the needs of others.
spanish_example_en: The considerate boy gave his girlfriend a present to cheer her up when she was sad.

english_meaning: The consumption of food or drink is the act of eating or drinking it.
english_example: These apples are too rotten for consumption.
spanish_meaning: El consumo de comida o bebida es el acto de comerla o beberla.
spanish_example: Estas manzanas están demasiado podridas para el consumo.
spanish_meaning_en: The consumption of food or drink is the act of eating or drinking it.
spanish_example_en: These apples are too rotten for consumption.

english_meaning: Criteria are factors on which a person judges or decides something.
english_example: Before she got the job, she had to meet all the necessary criteria.
spanish_meaning: Los criterios son factores sobre los cuales una persona juzga o decide algo.
spanish_example: Antes de conseguir el trabajo, tenía que cumplir todos los criterios necesarios.
spanish_meaning_en: Criteria are factors on which a person judges or decides something.
spanish_example_en: Before she got the job, she had to meet all the necessary criteria.

english_meaning: Crust is the tough outer part of a loaf of bread.
english_example: The little boy never ate the crust of his pizza.
spanish_meaning: La corteza es la parte exterior dura de una barra de pan.
spanish_example: El niño nunca se comió la masa de su pizza.
spanish_meaning_en: Crust is the tough outer part of a loaf of bread.
spanish_example_en: The little boy never ate the crust of his pizza.

english_meaning: To entitle someone means to give them the right to have or do something.
english_example: His golden ticket entitled him to sit in the front row at the concert.
spanish_meaning: Dar derecho a alguien significa darle el derecho de tener o hacer algo.
spanish_example: Su entrada dorada le permitía sentarse en primera fila del concierto.
spanish_meaning_en: To entitle someone means to give them the right to have or do something.
spanish_example_en: His golden ticket entitled him to sit in the front row at the concert.

english_meaning: To escort people means to safely accompany them to a place.
english_example: Her bodyguards escorted her to the movie theater.
spanish_meaning: Escoltar a las personas significa acompañarlas de forma segura a un lugar.
spanish_example: Sus guardaespaldas la acompañaron al cine.
spanish_meaning_en: To escort people means to safely accompany them to a place.
spanish_example_en: Her bodyguards escorted her to the movie theater.

english_meaning: When something is external, it is connected to an outer part.
english_example: It is warm inside my house, but the external temperature is freezing.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es externo, está conectado a una parte exterior.
spanish_example: Hace calor dentro de mi casa, pero la temperatura exterior es gélida.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is external, it is connected to an outer part.
spanish_example_en: It is warm inside my house, but the external temperature is freezing.

english_meaning: A facility is a building that exists for a particular purpose.
english_example: There are many educational facilities in big cities.
spanish_meaning: Una instalación es un edificio que existe para un propósito particular.
spanish_example: Hay muchas instalaciones educativas en las grandes ciudades.
spanish_meaning_en: A facility is a building that exists for a particular purpose.
spanish_example_en: There are many educational facilities in big cities.

english_meaning: A faculty is a mental or physical ability.
english_example: The boy’s mental faculties impressed all of his teachers.
spanish_meaning: Una facultad es una habilidad mental o física.
spanish_example: Las facultades mentales del niño impresionaron a todos sus profesores.
spanish_meaning_en: A faculty is a mental or physical ability.
spanish_example_en: The boy’s mental faculties impressed all of his teachers.

english_meaning: A heap of things is a large pile of them.
english_example: After the building was torn down, all that was left was a heap of bricks.
spanish_meaning: Un montón de cosas es un gran montón de ellas.
spanish_example: Después de que el edificio fue derribado, lo único que quedó fue un montón de ladrillos.
spanish_meaning_en: A heap of things is a large pile of them.
spanish_example_en: After the building was torn down, all that was left was a heap of bricks.

english_meaning: A hemisphere is one half of the Earth.
english_example: In the northern hemisphere, the weather is usually warmest in July and August.
spanish_meaning: Un hemisferio es la mitad de la Tierra.
spanish_example: En el hemisferio norte, el clima suele ser más cálido en julio y agosto.
spanish_meaning_en: A hemisphere is one half of the Earth.
spanish_example_en: In the northern hemisphere, the weather is usually warmest in July and August.

english_meaning: A hound is a type of dog that is often used for racing or hunting.
english_example: The men took their hounds with them when they went on the hunting trip.
spanish_meaning: Un sabueso es un tipo de perro que se utiliza a menudo para carreras o cazar.
spanish_example: Los hombres se llevaron a sus perros con ellos cuando fueron de caza.
spanish_meaning_en: A hound is a type of dog that is often used for racing or hunting.
spanish_example_en: The men took their hounds with them when they went on the hunting trip.

english_meaning: If something is impersonal, it is not friendly and makes people feel unimportant.
english_example: The boy felt scared on his first day at the big, impersonal high school.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es impersonal, no es amigable y hace que la gente se sienta sin importancia.
spanish_example: El niño se sintió asustado en su primer día en la gran e impersonal escuela secundaria.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is impersonal, it is not friendly and makes people feel unimportant.
spanish_example_en: The boy felt scared on his first day at the big, impersonal high school.

english_meaning: When you lick something, you pass your tongue over it.
english_example: He licked his ice cream before it melted.
spanish_meaning: Cuando lames algo, pasas la lengua por encima.
spanish_example: Lamió su helado antes de que se derritiera.
spanish_meaning_en: When you lick something, you pass your tongue over it.
spanish_example_en: He licked his ice cream before it melted.

english_meaning: An ornament is an attractive object that people display in their homes.
english_example: The woman kept some colorful ornaments on the shelves.
spanish_meaning: Un adorno es un objeto atractivo que la gente exhibe en sus hogares.
spanish_example: La mujer guardaba algunos adornos coloridos en los estantes.
spanish_meaning_en: An ornament is an attractive object that people display in their homes.
spanish_example_en: The woman kept some colorful ornaments on the shelves.

english_meaning: A pedestrian is a person who is walking on a street.
english_example: Drivers should be careful when pedestrians are walking around.
spanish_meaning: Un peatón es una persona que camina por una calle.
spanish_example: Los conductores deben tener cuidado cuando circulan peatones.
spanish_meaning_en: A pedestrian is a person who is walking on a street.
spanish_example_en: Drivers should be careful when pedestrians are walking around.

english_meaning: A sanctuary is a place where people in danger can go to be safe.
english_example: The church was made into a sanctuary for homeless people in the winter.
spanish_meaning: Un santuario es un lugar donde las personas en peligro pueden acudir para estar seguras.
spanish_example: La iglesia se convirtió en un santuario para personas sin hogar durante el invierno.
spanish_meaning_en: A sanctuary is a place where people in danger can go to be safe.
spanish_example_en: The church was made into a sanctuary for homeless people in the winter.

english_meaning: A spectator is someone who watches something, for example, a sports event.
english_example: There were thousands of spectators at the big game.
spanish_meaning: Un espectador es alguien que mira algo, por ejemplo, un evento deportivo.
spanish_example: Hubo miles de espectadores en el gran partido.
spanish_meaning_en: A spectator is someone who watches something, for example, a sports event.
spanish_example_en: There were thousands of spectators at the big game.

english_meaning: When you yell at someone, you shout at them.
english_example: Someone yelled out his name.
spanish_meaning: Cuando le gritas a alguien, le gritas.
spanish_example: Alguien gritó su nombre.
spanish_meaning_en: When you yell at someone, you shout at them.
spanish_example_en: Someone yelled out his name.

english_meaning: An accessory is a thing that is added to another thing to make it look better.
english_example: The store sold colorful accessories like bags, sunglasses, and makeup.
spanish_meaning: Un accesorio es algo que se añade a otra cosa para que luzca mejor.
spanish_example: La tienda vendía accesorios coloridos como bolsos, gafas de sol y maquillaje.
spanish_meaning_en: An accessory is a thing that is added to another thing to make it look better.
spanish_example_en: The store sold colorful accessories like bags, sunglasses, and makeup.

english_meaning: An acquisition is something that a person buys or gets in some way.
english_example: Marty was happy with his new acquisition: a very fast bicycle.
spanish_meaning: Una adquisición es algo que una persona compra o obtiene de alguna manera.
spanish_example: Marty estaba contento con su nueva adquisición: una bicicleta muy rápida.
spanish_meaning_en: An acquisition is something that a person buys or gets in some way.
spanish_example_en: Marty was happy with his new acquisition: a very fast bicycle.

english_meaning: Cardboard is a material made out of stiff paper. It is often used to make boxes.
english_example: We packed our things into cardboard boxes and moved to our new home.
spanish_meaning: El cartón es un material elaborado a partir de papel rígido. Se suele utilizar para fabricar cajas.
spanish_example: Empacamos nuestras cosas en cajas de cartón y nos mudamos a nuestro nuevo hogar.
spanish_meaning_en: Cardboard is a material made out of stiff paper. It is often used to make boxes.
spanish_example_en: We packed our things into cardboard boxes and moved to our new home.

english_meaning: A dilemma is a difficult situation in which a choice has to be made.
english_example: Choosing either the tastier or healthier drink proved to be quite a dilemma.
spanish_meaning: Un dilema es una situación difícil en la que hay que tomar una decisión.
