spanish_meaning: Un arroyo es un arroyo o río pequeño.
spanish_example: En las aguas poco profundas del arroyo sólo vivían peces pequeños.
spanish_meaning_en: A creek is a stream or small river.
spanish_example_en: Only small fish lived in the shallow waters of the creek.

english_meaning: A drill is a tool with a point that spins in order to make a hole.
english_example: The carpenter used the drill to make several holes in the wood.
spanish_meaning: Un taladro es una herramienta con una punta que gira para hacer un agujero.
spanish_example: El carpintero utilizó el taladro para hacer varios agujeros en la madera.
spanish_meaning_en: A drill is a tool with a point that spins in order to make a hole.
spanish_example_en: The carpenter used the drill to make several holes in the wood.

english_meaning: An executive is the top manager of a business.
english_example: After twenty years at the company, he finally became the executive.
spanish_meaning: Un ejecutivo es el máximo directivo de una empresa.
spanish_example: Después de veinte años en la empresa, finalmente se convirtió en ejecutivo.
spanish_meaning_en: An executive is the top manager of a business.
spanish_example_en: After twenty years at the company, he finally became the executive.

english_meaning: Fatigue is a feeling of extreme tiredness.
english_example: After three days with little sleep, she was feeling a lot of fatigue.
spanish_meaning: La fatiga es una sensación de cansancio extremo.
spanish_example: Después de tres días sin dormir, sentía mucho cansancio.
spanish_meaning_en: Fatigue is a feeling of extreme tiredness.
spanish_example_en: After three days with little sleep, she was feeling a lot of fatigue.

english_meaning: An incline is a sharp rise in something, especially a hill or mountain.
english_example: This mountain has one of the steepest inclines in the world.
spanish_meaning: Una pendiente es una subida brusca de algo, especialmente una colina o montaña.
spanish_example: Esta montaña tiene una de las pendientes más pronunciadas del mundo.
spanish_meaning_en: An incline is a sharp rise in something, especially a hill or mountain.
spanish_example_en: This mountain has one of the steepest inclines in the world.

english_meaning: If something is nasty, then it is not nice or pleasant.
english_example: The rotten apple left a nasty taste inside her mouth.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es desagradable, entonces no es agradable ni agradable.
spanish_example: La manzana podrida le dejó un sabor desagradable en la boca.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is nasty, then it is not nice or pleasant.
spanish_example_en: The rotten apple left a nasty taste inside her mouth.

english_meaning: To perceive something means to be aware of it.
english_example: He was talking loudly, so he did not perceive that the music had stopped.
spanish_meaning: Percibir algo significa ser consciente de ello.
spanish_example: Hablaba en voz alta, por lo que no percibió que la música había cesado.
spanish_meaning_en: To perceive something means to be aware of it.
spanish_example_en: He was talking loudly, so he did not perceive that the music had stopped.

english_meaning: A primate is a type of mammal that includes monkeys, apes, and humans.
english_example: Primates use their hands for such tasks as swinging from branches.
spanish_meaning: Un primate es un tipo de mamífero que incluye monos, simios y humanos.
spanish_example: Los primates usan sus manos para tareas como balancearse de las ramas.
spanish_meaning_en: A primate is a type of mammal that includes monkeys, apes, and humans.
spanish_example_en: Primates use their hands for such tasks as swinging from branches.

english_meaning: If something is primitive, then it is simple, basic, and not very developed.
english_example: The computers of the 1980s are primitive compared to those of today.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es primitivo, entonces es simple, básico y poco desarrollado.
spanish_example: Las computadoras de la década de 1980 son primitivas en comparación con las de hoy.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is primitive, then it is simple, basic, and not very developed.
spanish_example_en: The computers of the 1980s are primitive compared to those of today.

english_meaning: A stereotype is a general but often incorrect idea about a person or thing.
english_example: There’s a stereotype that pigs are dirty animals. But they are rather clean.
spanish_meaning: Un estereotipo es una idea general, pero a menudo incorrecta, sobre una persona o cosa.
spanish_example: Existe el estereotipo de que los cerdos son animales sucios. Pero están bastante limpios.
spanish_meaning_en: A stereotype is a general but often incorrect idea about a person or thing.
spanish_example_en: There’s a stereotype that pigs are dirty animals. But they are rather clean.

english_meaning: If something is sticky, then it is covered with a substance that things stick to.
english_example: Place the sticky part of the tape against the paper, so it will cling to the wall.
spanish_meaning: Si algo está pegajoso, entonces está cubierto con una sustancia a la que se adhieren las cosas.
spanish_example: Coloque la parte adhesiva de la cinta contra el papel para que se adhiera a la pared.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is sticky, then it is covered with a substance that things stick to.
spanish_example_en: Place the sticky part of the tape against the paper, so it will cling to the wall.

english_meaning: A termite is an insect that lives in groups and feeds on wood.
english_example: The wood we found was full of termites.
spanish_meaning: Una termita es un insecto que vive en grupos y se alimenta de madera.
spanish_example: La madera que encontramos estaba llena de termitas.
spanish_meaning_en: A termite is an insect that lives in groups and feeds on wood.
spanish_example_en: The wood we found was full of termites.

english_meaning: If we say that thereby something happens, we mean it is a result of something else.
english_example: He didn’t score a goal, thereby ending his chance at setting a record.
spanish_meaning: Si decimos que algo sucede, queremos decir que es el resultado de otra cosa.
spanish_example: No marcó ningún gol, lo que puso fin a sus posibilidades de establecer un récord.
spanish_meaning_en: If we say that thereby something happens, we mean it is a result of something else.
spanish_example_en: He didn’t score a goal, thereby ending his chance at setting a record.

english_meaning: A trail is a path for travelling by foot through a wild area.
english_example: A narrow trail cut through the field and over the hills.
spanish_meaning: Un sendero es un camino para recorrer a pie a través de un área salvaje.
spanish_example: Un sendero estrecho atravesaba el campo y pasaba por las colinas.
spanish_meaning_en: A trail is a path for travelling by foot through a wild area.
spanish_example_en: A narrow trail cut through the field and over the hills.

english_meaning: A twig is a short and thin branch from a tree or bush.
english_example: They started the fire with a handful of dry twigs.
spanish_meaning: Una ramita es una rama corta y delgada de un árbol o arbusto.
spanish_example: Encendieron el fuego con un puñado de ramitas secas.
spanish_meaning_en: A twig is a short and thin branch from a tree or bush.
spanish_example_en: They started the fire with a handful of dry twigs.

english_meaning: Welfare is the health and happiness of a person or group.
english_example: Having plenty of clean water is necessary for the welfare of people.
spanish_meaning: El bienestar es la salud y la felicidad de una persona o grupo.
spanish_example: Disponer de abundante agua potable es necesario para el bienestar de las personas.
spanish_meaning_en: Welfare is the health and happiness of a person or group.
spanish_example_en: Having plenty of clean water is necessary for the welfare of people.

english_meaning: If something is done on one’s behalf, it is done for that person by another.
english_example: The original speaker was sick, so his son gave the speech on his behalf.
spanish_meaning: Si algo se hace en nombre de uno, otro lo hace para esa persona.
spanish_example: El orador original estaba enfermo, por lo que su hijo pronunció el discurso en su nombre.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is done on one’s behalf, it is done for that person by another.
spanish_example_en: The original speaker was sick, so his son gave the speech on his behalf.

english_meaning: To flap means to move quickly up and down or from side to side.
english_example: The tiny bird flapped its wings and ate from the flowers.
spanish_meaning: Agitar significa moverse rápidamente hacia arriba y hacia abajo o de lado a lado.
spanish_example: El pajarillo agitó sus alas y comió de las flores.
spanish_meaning_en: To flap means to move quickly up and down or from side to side.
spanish_example_en: The tiny bird flapped its wings and ate from the flowers.

english_meaning: A glacier is a large piece of ice that moves very slowly.
english_example: The North Pole is covered by a huge glacier.
spanish_meaning: Un glaciar es un gran trozo de hielo que se mueve muy lentamente.
spanish_example: El Polo Norte está cubierto por un enorme glaciar.
spanish_meaning_en: A glacier is a large piece of ice that moves very slowly.
spanish_example_en: The North Pole is covered by a huge glacier.

english_meaning: The globe refers to the Earth.
english_example: Water covers most of the globe.
spanish_meaning: El globo se refiere a la Tierra.
spanish_example: El agua cubre la mayor parte del mundo.
spanish_meaning_en: The globe refers to the Earth.
spanish_example_en: Water covers most of the globe.

english_meaning: When something is horizontal, it is flat and level with the ground.
english_example: The Russian flag has three horizontal stripes of white, blue, and red.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo está horizontal, está plano y al nivel del suelo.
spanish_example: La bandera rusa tiene tres franjas horizontales de color blanco, azul y rojo.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is horizontal, it is flat and level with the ground.
spanish_example_en: The Russian flag has three horizontal stripes of white, blue, and red.

english_meaning: To hum means to make a low, continuous noise.
english_example: The man hummed his favorite song.
spanish_meaning: Tararear significa hacer un ruido bajo y continuo.
spanish_example: El hombre tarareaba su canción favorita.
spanish_meaning_en: To hum means to make a low, continuous noise.
spanish_example_en: The man hummed his favorite song.

english_meaning: An inventory is a supply of something.
english_example: Gwen was checking the inventory to make sure we had what we needed.
spanish_meaning: Un inventario es un suministro de algo.
spanish_example: Gwen estaba revisando el inventario para asegurarse de que tuviéramos lo que necesitábamos.
spanish_meaning_en: An inventory is a supply of something.
spanish_example_en: Gwen was checking the inventory to make sure we had what we needed.

english_meaning: If a thought or feeling is inward, it is not expressed or shown to others.
english_example: She had an inward feeling of guilt when she lied to her mother.
spanish_meaning: Si un pensamiento o sentimiento es interno, no se expresa ni se muestra a los demás.
spanish_example: Tenía un sentimiento interior de culpa cuando le mintió a su madre.
spanish_meaning_en: If a thought or feeling is inward, it is not expressed or shown to others.
spanish_example_en: She had an inward feeling of guilt when she lied to her mother.

english_meaning: A loaf of bread is bread shaped and baked in one piece.
english_example: Could you please buy a loaf of bread for sandwiches?
spanish_meaning: Una barra de pan tiene forma de pan y se hornea en una sola pieza.
spanish_example: ¿Podrías comprar una barra de pan para sándwiches?
spanish_meaning_en: A loaf of bread is bread shaped and baked in one piece.
spanish_example_en: Could you please buy a loaf of bread for sandwiches?

english_meaning: An oracle is a person who speaks with gods and gives advice about the future.
english_example: The king went to the oracle to ask if going to war was a good idea.
spanish_meaning: Un oráculo es una persona que habla con los dioses y les da consejos sobre el futuro.
spanish_example: El rey acudió al oráculo para preguntar si ir a la guerra era buena idea.
spanish_meaning_en: An oracle is a person who speaks with gods and gives advice about the future.
spanish_example_en: The king went to the oracle to ask if going to war was a good idea.

english_meaning: To orbit something means to move around it in a continuous, curving path.
english_example: The Moon orbits the Earth.
spanish_meaning: Orbitar algo significa moverse alrededor de él en una trayectoria curva y continua.
spanish_example: La Luna orbita la Tierra.
spanish_meaning_en: To orbit something means to move around it in a continuous, curving path.
spanish_example_en: The Moon orbits the Earth.

english_meaning: An overview is a general description of a situation.
english_example: My brother gave me an overview of the important parts of the book.
spanish_meaning: Una descripción general es una descripción general de una situación.
spanish_example: Mi hermano me dio una visión general de las partes importantes del libro.
spanish_meaning_en: An overview is a general description of a situation.
spanish_example_en: My brother gave me an overview of the important parts of the book.

english_meaning: A preview is an opportunity to see something before it is available to the public.
english_example: The band played us a preview of their new song.
spanish_meaning: Una vista previa es una oportunidad para ver algo antes de que esté disponible para el público.
spanish_example: La banda nos mostró un adelanto de su nueva canción.
spanish_meaning_en: A preview is an opportunity to see something before it is available to the public.
spanish_example_en: The band played us a preview of their new song.

english_meaning: If something is previous, then it happened earlier in time or order.
english_example: He turned back to the previous page to read the paragraph again.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es anterior, entonces sucedió antes en el tiempo o en el orden.
spanish_example: Volvió a la página anterior para leer el párrafo nuevamente.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is previous, then it happened earlier in time or order.
spanish_example_en: He turned back to the previous page to read the paragraph again.

english_meaning: To provide something means to supply it.
english_example: Each student was provided with a test and three sharp pencils.
spanish_meaning: Proporcionar algo significa suministrarlo.
spanish_example: A cada estudiante se le entregó una prueba y tres lápices afilados.
spanish_meaning_en: To provide something means to supply it.
spanish_example_en: Each student was provided with a test and three sharp pencils.

english_meaning: To recur means to happen more than once.
english_example: Burglaries seem to recur over and over in our neighborhood.
spanish_meaning: Recurrir significa suceder más de una vez.
spanish_example: Los robos parecen repetirse una y otra vez en nuestro vecindario.
spanish_meaning_en: To recur means to happen more than once.
spanish_example_en: Burglaries seem to recur over and over in our neighborhood.

english_meaning: When something is relevant, it is important to a certain person or situation.
english_example: The 30-year-old book about politics is still relevant to our society today.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es relevante, es importante para una determinada persona o situación.
spanish_example: El libro sobre política, publicado hace 30 años, sigue siendo relevante para nuestra sociedad actual.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is relevant, it is important to a certain person or situation.
spanish_example_en: The 30-year-old book about politics is still relevant to our society today.

english_meaning: A rite is a traditional ceremony carried out by a particular group or society.
english_example: Special masks are worn during the rite when a new baby is born.
spanish_meaning: Un rito es una ceremonia tradicional llevada a cabo por un grupo o sociedad en particular.
spanish_example: Se usan máscaras especiales durante el rito cuando nace un nuevo bebé.
spanish_meaning_en: A rite is a traditional ceremony carried out by a particular group or society.
spanish_example_en: Special masks are worn during the rite when a new baby is born.

english_meaning: To stall means to stop a process and continue it at a later time.
english_example: If you give the car a push, it won’t stall.
spanish_meaning: Detenerse significa detener un proceso y continuarlo más adelante.
spanish_example: Si le das un empujón al coche, no se detendrá.
spanish_meaning_en: To stall means to stop a process and continue it at a later time.
spanish_example_en: If you give the car a push, it won’t stall.

english_meaning: If something is supernatural, it is not real or explainable by laws of nature.
english_example: The dragon had supernatural powers, such as flying and breathing fire.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es sobrenatural, no es real ni explicable por las leyes de la naturaleza.
spanish_example: El dragón tenía poderes sobrenaturales, como volar y escupir fuego.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is supernatural, it is not real or explainable by laws of nature.
spanish_example_en: The dragon had supernatural powers, such as flying and breathing fire.

english_meaning: An aquarium is a place where fish and underwater animals are kept.
english_example: We took a trip to the aquarium and saw a scary shark.
spanish_meaning: Un acuario es un lugar donde se guardan peces y animales submarinos.
spanish_example: Hicimos un viaje al acuario y vimos un tiburón aterrador.
spanish_meaning_en: An aquarium is a place where fish and underwater animals are kept.
spanish_example_en: We took a trip to the aquarium and saw a scary shark.

english_meaning: If something is arbitrary, it is not based on any plan or system, so it seems random.
english_example: The classroom had many arbitrary rules that confused me.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es arbitrario, no se basa en ningún plan o sistema, por lo que parece aleatorio.
spanish_example: En el aula había muchas reglas arbitrarias que me confundían.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is arbitrary, it is not based on any plan or system, so it seems random.
spanish_example_en: The classroom had many arbitrary rules that confused me.

english_meaning: An autobiography is a true story of a person’s life written by that person.
english_example: I read an autobiography about my favorite entertainer.
spanish_meaning: Una autobiografía es una historia real de la vida de una persona escrita por esa persona.
spanish_example: Leí una autobiografía sobre mi artista favorito.
spanish_meaning_en: An autobiography is a true story of a person’s life written by that person.
spanish_example_en: I read an autobiography about my favorite entertainer.

english_meaning: A convention is behavior that is considered to be common or polite.
english_example: In the US, a popular convention is to shake hands when you meet someone.
spanish_meaning: Una convención es un comportamiento que se considera común o educado.
spanish_example: En Estados Unidos, una convención popular es estrechar la mano cuando conoces a alguien.
spanish_meaning_en: A convention is behavior that is considered to be common or polite.
spanish_example_en: In the US, a popular convention is to shake hands when you meet someone.

english_meaning: If someone is gracious, then they are kind and helpful to those who need it.
english_example: The operator was gracious enough to help me find the number.
spanish_meaning: Si alguien es amable, entonces es amable y servicial con quienes lo necesitan.
spanish_example: El operador tuvo la amabilidad de ayudarme a encontrar el número.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is gracious, then they are kind and helpful to those who need it.
spanish_example_en: The operator was gracious enough to help me find the number.

english_meaning: To improve something means to make it better.
english_example: He studied hard to improve his test scores from the previous year.
spanish_meaning: Mejorar algo significa hacerlo mejor.
spanish_example: Estudió mucho para mejorar los puntajes de sus exámenes del año anterior.
spanish_meaning_en: To improve something means to make it better.
spanish_example_en: He studied hard to improve his test scores from the previous year.

english_meaning: To insulate something means to protect it from heat, cold, or noise.
english_example: People can conserve energy by insulating their houses.
spanish_meaning: Aislar algo significa protegerlo del calor, el frío o el ruido.
spanish_example: La gente puede conservar energía aislando sus casas.
spanish_meaning_en: To insulate something means to protect it from heat, cold, or noise.
spanish_example_en: People can conserve energy by insulating their houses.

english_meaning: To intrigue means to cause an interest in something or someone.
english_example: Her mysterious past intrigued her new friend.
spanish_meaning: Intrigar significa provocar el interés por algo o alguien.
spanish_example: Su misterioso pasado intrigó a su nueva amiga.
spanish_meaning_en: To intrigue means to cause an interest in something or someone.
spanish_example_en: Her mysterious past intrigued her new friend.

english_meaning: Longevity is the ability to live for a long time.
english_example: Sea turtles have an amazing longevity.
spanish_meaning: La longevidad es la capacidad de vivir durante mucho tiempo.
spanish_example: Las tortugas marinas tienen una longevidad asombrosa.
spanish_meaning_en: Longevity is the ability to live for a long time.
spanish_example_en: Sea turtles have an amazing longevity.

english_meaning: To misplace something means to lose it.
english_example: I misplaced my wallet, and I didn’t find it until a week later.
spanish_meaning: Extraviar algo significa perderlo.
spanish_example: Perdí mi billetera y no la encontré hasta una semana después.
spanish_meaning_en: To misplace something means to lose it.
spanish_example_en: I misplaced my wallet, and I didn’t find it until a week later.

english_meaning: When children are naughty, they behave badly or do not do what they are told.
english_example: The boy had to go to his room because he was being naughty.
spanish_meaning: Cuando los niños son traviesos se portan mal o no hacen lo que les dicen.
spanish_example: El niño tuvo que irse a su habitación porque se estaba portando mal.
spanish_meaning_en: When children are naughty, they behave badly or do not do what they are told.
spanish_example_en: The boy had to go to his room because he was being naughty.

english_meaning: A norm is a way of behaving that is considered normal in a particular society.
english_example: Wearing a heavy coat all summer is not considered a norm.
spanish_meaning: Una norma es una forma de comportarse que se considera normal en una sociedad determinada.
spanish_example: Llevar un abrigo grueso durante todo el verano no se considera una norma.
spanish_meaning_en: A norm is a way of behaving that is considered normal in a particular society.
spanish_example_en: Wearing a heavy coat all summer is not considered a norm.

english_meaning: An orangutan is a large ape with red and brown hair and long arms.
english_example: Orangutans use their long arms to swing from trees.
spanish_meaning: Un orangután es un simio grande con pelo rojo y castaño y brazos largos.
spanish_example: Los orangutanes usan sus largos brazos para balancearse desde los árboles.
spanish_meaning_en: An orangutan is a large ape with red and brown hair and long arms.
spanish_example_en: Orangutans use their long arms to swing from trees.

english_meaning: To overload something means to put more things into it than it is meant to hold.
english_example: If you overload the truck, it might crash.
spanish_meaning: Sobrecargar algo significa ponerle más cosas de las que debe contener.
spanish_example: Si sobrecarga el camión, podría estrellarse.
spanish_meaning_en: To overload something means to put more things into it than it is meant to hold.
spanish_example_en: If you overload the truck, it might crash.

english_meaning: Philanthropy is the act of helping others without wanting anything in return.
english_example: The wealthy business owner is well known for his acts of philanthropy.
spanish_meaning: La filantropía es el acto de ayudar a los demás sin querer nada a cambio.
spanish_example: El rico empresario es bien conocido por sus actos de filantropía.
spanish_meaning_en: Philanthropy is the act of helping others without wanting anything in return.
spanish_example_en: The wealthy business owner is well known for his acts of philanthropy.

english_meaning: To probe into something means to ask questions to discover facts about it.
english_example: The bank probed into his financial history to see if he qualified for a loan.
spanish_meaning: Indagar en algo significa hacer preguntas para descubrir hechos sobre ello.
spanish_example: El banco investigó su historial financiero para ver si calificaba para un préstamo.
spanish_meaning_en: To probe into something means to ask questions to discover facts about it.
spanish_example_en: The bank probed into his financial history to see if he qualified for a loan.

english_meaning: A recipient of something is the person who receives it.
english_example: I was the recipient of four phone calls today.
spanish_meaning: Un destinatario de algo es la persona que lo recibe.
spanish_example: Hoy recibí cuatro llamadas telefónicas.
spanish_meaning_en: A recipient of something is the person who receives it.
spanish_example_en: I was the recipient of four phone calls today.

english_meaning: A reptile is a cold-blooded animal that lays eggs and has skin covered with scales.
english_example: Lizards are my favorite type of reptile.
spanish_meaning: Un reptil es un animal de sangre fría que pone huevos y tiene la piel cubierta de escamas.
spanish_example: Los lagartos son mi tipo de reptil favorito.
spanish_meaning_en: A reptile is a cold-blooded animal that lays eggs and has skin covered with scales.
spanish_example_en: Lizards are my favorite type of reptile.

english_meaning: To thrive means to do well and be successful, healthy, or strong.
english_example: He may be an old man, but he continues to thrive.
spanish_meaning: Prosperar significa hacerlo bien y tener éxito, estar sano o ser fuerte.
spanish_example: Puede que sea un anciano, pero sigue prosperando.
spanish_meaning_en: To thrive means to do well and be successful, healthy, or strong.
spanish_example_en: He may be an old man, but he continues to thrive.

english_meaning: When something is ultimate, it is the final result or aim of a long series of events.
english_example: By trying hard in school, I will reach my ultimate goal of becoming a doctor.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es definitivo, es el resultado final o el objetivo de una larga serie de acontecimientos.
spanish_example: Si me esforzo mucho en la escuela, alcanzaré mi objetivo final de convertirme en médico.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is ultimate, it is the final result or aim of a long series of events.
spanish_example_en: By trying hard in school, I will reach my ultimate goal of becoming a doctor.

english_meaning: If something is antique, it is very old and rare, and therefore valuable.
english_example: My grandmother’s antique rocking chair is worth a lot of money.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es antiguo, es muy antiguo y raro y, por tanto, valioso.
spanish_example: La mecedora antigua de mi abuela vale mucho dinero.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is antique, it is very old and rare, and therefore valuable.
spanish_example_en: My grandmother’s antique rocking chair is worth a lot of money.

english_meaning: An applicant is someone who writes a request to be considered for a job or prize.
english_example: Lots of applicants came into the store when the job position became available.
spanish_meaning: Un solicitante es alguien que escribe una solicitud para ser considerado para un trabajo o premio.
spanish_example: Muchos solicitantes llegaron a la tienda cuando el puesto de trabajo estuvo disponible.

spanish_meaning_en: When something is authentic, it is not false or a copy of the original.
spanish_example_en: We ate authentic Italian food on our vacation to Rome.

spanish_meaning_en: The chronology of a series of past events is when they happened.

spanish_meaning_en: A diplomat is a representative of a country who works with another country.

spanish_meaning_en: An epic is a long book, poem, or movie about a period of time or a great event.

spanish_meaning_en: An excerpt is a short piece of writing or music taken from a larger piece.
spanish_example_en: I didn’t listen to the entire symphony online, but I did play an excerpt.

spanish_meaning_en: A fossil is the hard remains of a prehistoric animal or plant.
spanish_example_en: The expert arranged the fossils to build the skeleton of the dinosaur.

english_meaning: To humiliate someone means to make them feel ashamed and embarrassed.

spanish_meaning_en: To humiliate someone means to make them feel ashamed and embarrassed.
spanish_example_en: I was humiliated when I tripped and fell down in front of the whole school.

english_meaning: When a poem is considered lyric, it expresses a lot of emotion.

spanish_meaning: Cuando un poema se considera lírico, expresa mucha emoción.

spanish_meaning_en: When a poem is considered lyric, it expresses a lot of emotion.
spanish_example_en: I enjoy reading and creating my own lyric poetry.

english_meaning: Majesty is supreme greatness or authority.

spanish_meaning_en: Majesty is supreme greatness or authority.
spanish_example_en: The majesty of the old castle amazed the tourists who came to see it.

english_meaning: The monarch of a country is someone who rules a country through hereditary right.

spanish_meaning_en: The monarch of a country is someone who rules a country through hereditary right.
spanish_example_en: The monarch lived in a beautiful palace with a grand gate.

english_meaning: To precede something means to come before it.

spanish_meaning_en: To precede something means to come before it.
spanish_example_en: The hurricane was preceded by a moment of still wind and clear sky.

english_meaning: When someone is punctual, they do something or arrive at the right time.

spanish_meaning: Cuando alguien es puntual, hace algo o llega en el momento adecuado.

spanish_meaning_en: When someone is punctual, they do something or arrive at the right time.
spanish_example_en: My mother hates being late. She is the most punctual person I know.

english_meaning: To recruit people means to select them to join or work for an organization.

spanish_meaning_en: To recruit people means to select them to join or work for an organization.
spanish_example_en: We successfully recruited someone to be the new manager.

english_meaning: A refund is money given back to a person when an item is returned to a store.

spanish_meaning: Un reembolso es dinero que se devuelve a una persona cuando se devuelve un artículo a una tienda.

spanish_meaning_en: A refund is money given back to a person when an item is returned to a store.

english_meaning: A register is an official list or record of people or things.

spanish_meaning_en: A register is an official list or record of people or things.
spanish_example_en: At a wedding, there is register for all of the guests to sign.

english_meaning: Renown is the quality of being well known due to having done good things.

spanish_meaning_en: Renown is the quality of being well known due to having done good things.

english_meaning: A tusk is a long, curved, pointed tooth of an elephant, boar, or walrus.
english_example: Sadly, some people hunt elephants and remove their tusks to sell them.
spanish_meaning: Un colmillo es un diente largo, curvo y puntiagudo de un elefante, jabalí o morsa.

spanish_meaning_en: A tusk is a long, curved, pointed tooth of an elephant, boar, or walrus.

english_meaning: To coexist with something means to exist with it in the same time and place.

spanish_meaning_en: To coexist with something means to exist with it in the same time and place.

spanish_meaning_en: To conceive something means to be able to imagine or believe it.

spanish_meaning_en: When something or someone is dubious, they are not considered honest.

spanish_example_en: Kelly’s ego made her think that she was some kind of a superhero.

english_meaning: Elastic is a rubber that stretches when it is pulled.

spanish_meaning: Si algo se hace bien, entonces se hace de la mejor manera o más completa.
spanish_example: El oponente más cercano estaba diez metros detrás. Ella ganó la carrera con creces.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is done soundly, then it is done in the best or most complete way.
spanish_example_en: The nearest opponent was ten meters behind. She won the race soundly.

english_meaning: A superintendent is a person who runs a certain department or building.
english_example: The superintendent decided to close the schools because of the weather.
spanish_meaning: Un superintendente es una persona que dirige un determinado departamento o edificio.
spanish_example: El superintendente decidió cerrar las escuelas debido al clima.
spanish_meaning_en: A superintendent is a person who runs a certain department or building.
spanish_example_en: The superintendent decided to close the schools because of the weather.

english_meaning: If something is congested, it is full or blocked.
english_example: Tom didn’t get home until after dark because the road was so congested.
spanish_meaning: Si algo está congestionado, está lleno o bloqueado.
spanish_example: Tom no llegó a casa hasta después del anochecer porque la carretera estaba muy congestionada.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is congested, it is full or blocked.
spanish_example_en: Tom didn’t get home until after dark because the road was so congested.

english_meaning: A courier is someone who takes and delivers mail or packages.
english_example: Before trains, most couriers used horses to travel.
spanish_meaning: Un mensajero es alguien que recibe y entrega correo o paquetes.
spanish_example: Antes de los trenes, la mayoría de los mensajeros utilizaban caballos para viajar.
spanish_meaning_en: A courier is someone who takes and delivers mail or packages.
spanish_example_en: Before trains, most couriers used horses to travel.

english_meaning: To deform something means to change it from its correct or original shape.
english_example: The computer program deformed the building’s picture into an unreal sight.
spanish_meaning: Deformar algo significa cambiarlo de su forma correcta u original.
spanish_example: El programa informático deformó la imagen del edificio hasta convertirla en una visión irreal.
spanish_meaning_en: To deform something means to change it from its correct or original shape.
spanish_example_en: The computer program deformed the building’s picture into an unreal sight.

english_meaning: Etiquette is the group of rules about how to be polite.
english_example: When in Asian countries, bowing is a form of etiquette.
spanish_meaning: La etiqueta es el grupo de reglas sobre cómo ser cortés.
spanish_example: En los países asiáticos, hacer una reverencia es una forma de etiqueta.
spanish_meaning_en: Etiquette is the group of rules about how to be polite.
spanish_example_en: When in Asian countries, bowing is a form of etiquette.

english_meaning: If something is exclusive, it is expensive and only for rich people.
english_example: The golf course was so exclusive that most people hadn’t even heard of it.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es exclusivo, es caro y sólo para gente rica.
spanish_example: El campo de golf era tan exclusivo que la mayoría de la gente ni siquiera había oído hablar de él.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is exclusive, it is expensive and only for rich people.
spanish_example_en: The golf course was so exclusive that most people hadn’t even heard of it.

english_meaning: Freight is a set of items carried on a train, boat, or airplane.
english_example: Trade ships only carried valuable freight like silk and spices.
spanish_meaning: El flete es un conjunto de artículos transportados en un tren, barco o avión.
spanish_example: Los barcos mercantes sólo transportaban mercancías valiosas como seda y especias.
spanish_meaning_en: Freight is a set of items carried on a train, boat, or airplane.
spanish_example_en: Trade ships only carried valuable freight like silk and spices.

english_meaning: A garment is a piece of clothing.
english_example: The businessman had all of his garments cleaned before the important meeting.
spanish_meaning: Una prenda es una prenda de vestir.
spanish_example: El empresario hizo limpiar todas sus prendas antes de la importante reunión.
spanish_meaning_en: A garment is a piece of clothing.
spanish_example_en: The businessman had all of his garments cleaned before the important meeting.

english_meaning: Insomnia is a condition in which a person has difficulty sleeping.
english_example: Nate’s insomnia prevented him from getting enough rest.
spanish_meaning: El insomnio es una condición en la que una persona tiene dificultad para dormir.
spanish_example: El insomnio de Nate le impidió descansar lo suficiente.
spanish_meaning_en: Insomnia is a condition in which a person has difficulty sleeping.
spanish_example_en: Nate’s insomnia prevented him from getting enough rest.

english_meaning: Intuitive is knowing about something without having support or proof.
english_example: Rhonda had an intuitive feeling that Shane wasn’t coming to school today.
spanish_meaning: Intuitivo es saber de algo sin tener sustento ni pruebas.
spanish_example: Rhonda tuvo la sensación intuitiva de que Shane no vendría a la escuela hoy.
spanish_meaning_en: Intuitive is knowing about something without having support or proof.
spanish_example_en: Rhonda had an intuitive feeling that Shane wasn’t coming to school today.

english_meaning: If something is liable to happen, it is very likely that it will happen.
english_example: During the summer months, hikers in the forest are liable to see deer and elk.
spanish_meaning: Si es posible que algo suceda, es muy probable que suceda.
spanish_example: Durante los meses de verano, los excursionistas en el bosque pueden ver ciervos y alces.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is liable to happen, it is very likely that it will happen.
spanish_example_en: During the summer months, hikers in the forest are liable to see deer and elk.

english_meaning: To obsess about something means to think about it all of the time.
english_example: After watching the Star Wars movies, Ike obsessed about becoming a Jedi.
spanish_meaning: Obsesionarse con algo significa pensar en ello todo el tiempo.
spanish_example: Después de ver las películas de Star Wars, Ike se obsesionó con convertirse en Jedi.
spanish_meaning_en: To obsess about something means to think about it all of the time.
spanish_example_en: After watching the Star Wars movies, Ike obsessed about becoming a Jedi.

english_meaning: When something is overboard, it is over the side of a boat and in the water.
english_example: Tom and Gary slipped on the wet floor and fell overboard.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo cae por la borda, está por la borda de un barco y en el agua.
spanish_example: Tom y Gary resbalaron en el suelo mojado y cayeron por la borda.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is overboard, it is over the side of a boat and in the water.
spanish_example_en: Tom and Gary slipped on the wet floor and fell overboard.

english_meaning: A premium is a payment that is higher than average.
english_example: Tony paid a premium for the gas because it made his car run better.
spanish_meaning: Una prima es un pago superior al promedio.
spanish_example: Tony pagó una prima por la gasolina porque hacía que su auto funcionara mejor.
spanish_meaning_en: A premium is a payment that is higher than average.
spanish_example_en: Tony paid a premium for the gas because it made his car run better.

english_meaning: A privilege is a special right given only to a certain person or group of people.
english_example: Only the best employee had the privilege of parking in that spot.
spanish_meaning: Un privilegio es un derecho especial otorgado únicamente a una determinada persona o grupo de personas.
spanish_example: Sólo el mejor empleado tenía el privilegio de aparcar en ese lugar.
spanish_meaning_en: A privilege is a special right given only to a certain person or group of people.
spanish_example_en: Only the best employee had the privilege of parking in that spot.

english_meaning: To propel something means to push or move it somewhere.
english_example: The strong wind propelled the leaf through the air and across the street.
spanish_meaning: Impulsar algo significa empujarlo o moverlo hacia algún lugar.
spanish_example: El fuerte viento impulsó la hoja por el aire y cruzó la calle.
spanish_meaning_en: To propel something means to push or move it somewhere.
spanish_example_en: The strong wind propelled the leaf through the air and across the street.

english_meaning: To socialize is to have a good time with people.
english_example: I like to socialize with my classmates after school.
spanish_meaning: Socializar es pasar un buen rato con la gente.
spanish_example: Me gusta socializar con mis compañeros de clase después de la escuela.
spanish_meaning_en: To socialize is to have a good time with people.
spanish_example_en: I like to socialize with my classmates after school.

english_meaning: To suppress something means to prevent it from happening.
english_example: She suppressed her urge to scream because she didn’t want to be noticed.
spanish_meaning: Suprimir algo significa impedir que suceda.
spanish_example: Reprimió las ganas de gritar porque no quería que la notaran.
spanish_meaning_en: To suppress something means to prevent it from happening.
spanish_example_en: She suppressed her urge to scream because she didn’t want to be noticed.

english_meaning: A tram is a vehicle like a streetcar that runs on electricity above ground.
english_example: I took the tram to Eighth Avenue.
spanish_meaning: Un tranvía es un vehículo parecido a un tranvía que funciona con electricidad en la superficie.
spanish_example: Tomé el tranvía hasta la Octava Avenida.
spanish_meaning_en: A tram is a vehicle like a streetcar that runs on electricity above ground.
spanish_example_en: I took the tram to Eighth Avenue.

english_meaning: To unsettle someone means to make them anxious or worried.
english_example: The dark clouds in the sky unsettled Beth.
spanish_meaning: Inquietar a alguien significa ponerlo ansioso o preocupado.
spanish_example: Las nubes oscuras en el cielo inquietaron a Beth.
spanish_meaning_en: To unsettle someone means to make them anxious or worried.
spanish_example_en: The dark clouds in the sky unsettled Beth.

english_meaning: To warp means to become bent into the wrong shape.
english_example: The woman put the clock above the fireplace, and the heat warped it.
spanish_meaning: Deformarse significa doblarse en una forma incorrecta.
spanish_example: La mujer puso el reloj encima de la chimenea y el calor lo deformó.
spanish_meaning_en: To warp means to become bent into the wrong shape.
spanish_example_en: The woman put the clock above the fireplace, and the heat warped it.

english_meaning: To accumulate something is to collect a lot of it over time.
english_example: The mail accumulated in their mailbox while they were on vacation.
spanish_meaning: Acumular algo es recolectar una gran cantidad a lo largo del tiempo.
spanish_example: El correo se acumuló en su buzón mientras estaban de vacaciones.
spanish_meaning_en: To accumulate something is to collect a lot of it over time.
spanish_example_en: The mail accumulated in their mailbox while they were on vacation.

english_meaning: When something is aerial, it relates to being in the air or flying.
english_example: The aerial photographer took pictures from the air balloon.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es aéreo se relaciona con estar en el aire o volar.
spanish_example: El fotógrafo aéreo tomó fotografías desde el globo.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is aerial, it relates to being in the air or flying.
spanish_example_en: The aerial photographer took pictures from the air balloon.

english_meaning: An apparatus is a device used for a particular purpose.
english_example: The campers had an apparatus that showed them their exact location.
spanish_meaning: Un aparato es un dispositivo utilizado para un propósito particular.
spanish_example: Los campistas disponían de un aparato que les mostraba su ubicación exacta.
spanish_meaning_en: An apparatus is a device used for a particular purpose.
spanish_example_en: The campers had an apparatus that showed them their exact location.

english_meaning: An avalanche is a large amount of snow, ice, and rock falling off a mountain.
english_example: The avalanche destroyed the mountain village.
spanish_meaning: Una avalancha es una gran cantidad de nieve, hielo y rocas que caen de una montaña.
spanish_example: La avalancha destruyó el pueblo de montaña.
spanish_meaning_en: An avalanche is a large amount of snow, ice, and rock falling off a mountain.
spanish_example_en: The avalanche destroyed the mountain village.

english_meaning: Consistency is the state of always behaving in the same way.
english_example: Her consistency in archery meant that our team had a chance to win.
spanish_meaning: La coherencia es el estado de comportarse siempre de la misma manera.
spanish_example: Su consistencia en el tiro con arco significó que nuestro equipo tuviera la oportunidad de ganar.
spanish_meaning_en: Consistency is the state of always behaving in the same way.
spanish_example_en: Her consistency in archery meant that our team had a chance to win.

english_meaning: To discharge someone is to allow them to leave from a place, usually a hospital.
english_example: I was discharged from the hospital after three days.
spanish_meaning: Dar el alta a alguien es permitirle salir de un lugar, normalmente un hospital.
spanish_example: Me dieron el alta del hospital después de tres días.
spanish_meaning_en: To discharge someone is to allow them to leave from a place, usually a hospital.
spanish_example_en: I was discharged from the hospital after three days.

english_meaning: An episode is something that happens as part of a series of events.
english_example: We watched the final exciting episode of the TV series.
spanish_meaning: Un episodio es algo que sucede como parte de una serie de eventos.
spanish_example: Vimos el emocionante episodio final de la serie de televisión.
spanish_meaning_en: An episode is something that happens as part of a series of events.
spanish_example_en: We watched the final exciting episode of the TV series.

english_meaning: When something is intact, it is complete and not damaged.
english_example: Despite being over 30 years old, my father’s model ship is still intact.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo está intacto, está completo y no dañado.
spanish_example: A pesar de tener más de 30 años, el modelo de barco de mi padre sigue intacto.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is intact, it is complete and not damaged.
spanish_example_en: Despite being over 30 years old, my father’s model ship is still intact.

english_meaning: When a person is mortal, they cannot live forever.
english_example: All people are mortal.
spanish_meaning: Cuando una persona es mortal, no puede vivir para siempre.
spanish_example: Todas las personas son mortales.
spanish_meaning_en: When a person is mortal, they cannot live forever.
spanish_example_en: All people are mortal.

english_meaning: An omen is a sign of what will happen in the future.
english_example: He thought that seeing a black cat in the street was an omen of bad luck.
spanish_meaning: Un presagio es una señal de lo que sucederá en el futuro.
spanish_example: Pensó que ver un gato negro en la calle era presagio de mala suerte.
spanish_meaning_en: An omen is a sign of what will happen in the future.
spanish_example_en: He thought that seeing a black cat in the street was an omen of bad luck.

english_meaning: When the sky is overcast, it is full of clouds and is not sunny.
english_example: The sky was overcast in the morning, but by noon it was bright again.
spanish_meaning: Cuando el cielo está nublado, está lleno de nubes y no hace sol.
spanish_example: Por la mañana el cielo estaba nublado, pero al mediodía volvió a brillar.
spanish_meaning_en: When the sky is overcast, it is full of clouds and is not sunny.
spanish_example_en: The sky was overcast in the morning, but by noon it was bright again.

english_meaning: When something is poignant, it causes a very strong feeling of sadness.
english_example: The girls cried at the end of the poignant movie.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es conmovedor, provoca un sentimiento de tristeza muy fuerte.
spanish_example: Las niñas lloraron al final de la conmovedora película.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is poignant, it causes a very strong feeling of sadness.
spanish_example_en: The girls cried at the end of the poignant movie.

english_meaning: A ranger is a person who protects forests or parks.
english_example: Peter wanted to be a ranger because he liked spending time outside.
spanish_meaning: Un guardabosques es una persona que protege bosques o parques.
spanish_example: Peter quería ser guardabosques porque le gustaba pasar tiempo al aire libre.
spanish_meaning_en: A ranger is a person who protects forests or parks.
spanish_example_en: Peter wanted to be a ranger because he liked spending time outside.

english_meaning: Rubble is piles of broken stone and wood created after a building is destroyed.
english_example: There was rubble all over the city after the earthquake.
spanish_meaning: Los escombros son montones de piedra rota y madera que se crean después de la destrucción de un edificio.
spanish_example: Después del terremoto quedaron escombros por toda la ciudad.
spanish_meaning_en: Rubble is piles of broken stone and wood created after a building is destroyed.
spanish_example_en: There was rubble all over the city after the earthquake.

english_meaning: To seclude someone means to keep them away from other people.
english_example: She was secluded on an island for over a year.
spanish_meaning: Aislar a alguien significa mantenerlo alejado de otras personas.
spanish_example: Estuvo recluida en una isla durante más de un año.
notes: Ambigua entre “aislarse” (s. pronominal) y “aislar” (transitivo).
spanish_meaning_en: To seclude someone means to keep them away from other people.
spanish_example_en: She was secluded on an island for over a year.

english_meaning: If something moves sideways, then it moves to or from the side.
english_example: Jim turned sideways in order to slow down and stop his snowboard.
spanish_meaning: Si algo se mueve hacia un lado, entonces se mueve hacia o desde un lado.
spanish_example: Jim se giró hacia un lado para reducir la velocidad y detener su tabla de snowboard.
spanish_meaning_en: If something moves sideways, then it moves to or from the side.
spanish_example_en: Jim turned sideways in order to slow down and stop his snowboard.

english_meaning: To sob is to cry loudly.
english_example: I sobbed when my youngest daughter got married.
spanish_meaning: Sollozar es llorar fuerte.
spanish_example: Lloré cuando mi hija menor se casó.
spanish_meaning_en: To sob is to cry loudly.
spanish_example_en: I sobbed when my youngest daughter got married.

english_meaning: When something or someone is sober, they are serious and calm.
english_example: After the funeral, everybody felt very sober.
spanish_meaning: En este contexto, sobrio significa serio y calmado.
spanish_example: Después del funeral, todos se sintieron muy sobrios.
notes: Source sense: serious and calm, not the alcohol sense.
spanish_meaning_en: In this context, sober means serious and calm.
spanish_example_en: After the funeral, everybody felt very sober.

english_meaning: A speck is a very small mark or amount.
english_example: A speck of blood appeared where the mosquito had bit him.
spanish_meaning: Una mota es una marca o cantidad muy pequeña.
spanish_example: Una mancha de sangre apareció en el lugar donde le había picado el mosquito.
spanish_meaning_en: A speck is a very small mark or amount.
spanish_example_en: A speck of blood appeared where the mosquito had bit him.

english_meaning: An upbringing is the way that someone is taught to behave by their parents.
english_example: He had a strict upbringing and was never allowed to watch television.
spanish_meaning: Una crianza es la forma en que sus padres le enseñan a alguien a comportarse.
spanish_example: Tuvo una educación estricta y nunca se le permitió ver televisión.
spanish_meaning_en: An upbringing is the way that someone is taught to behave by their parents.
spanish_example_en: He had a strict upbringing and was never allowed to watch television.

english_meaning: Bliss is a state of complete happiness.
english_example: Every time he sees his girlfriend, he feels a sense of bliss.
spanish_meaning: La dicha es un estado de completa felicidad.
spanish_example: Cada vez que ve a su novia, siente una sensación de felicidad.
spanish_meaning_en: Bliss is a state of complete happiness.
spanish_example_en: Every time he sees his girlfriend, he feels a sense of bliss.

english_meaning: A butler is the most important male servant in a wealthy house.
english_example: Whenever he needed something, he rang a bell, and the butler appeared.
spanish_meaning: Un mayordomo es el sirviente más importante de una casa rica.
spanish_example: Cada vez que necesitaba algo, tocaba un timbre y aparecía el mayordomo.
spanish_meaning_en: A butler is the most important male servant in a wealthy house.
spanish_example_en: Whenever he needed something, he rang a bell, and the butler appeared.

english_meaning: A cramp is a strong pain caused by a muscle after a lot of physical use.
english_example: After the marathon, Jenny got a terrible cramp in her calf muscle.
spanish_meaning: Un calambre es un dolor fuerte provocado por un músculo después de mucho uso físico.
spanish_example: Después del maratón, Jenny sufrió un terrible calambre en el músculo de la pantorrilla.
spanish_meaning_en: A cramp is a strong pain caused by a muscle after a lot of physical use.
spanish_example_en: After the marathon, Jenny got a terrible cramp in her calf muscle.

english_meaning: When you decorate a room, you make it more attractive by adding beautiful things to it.
english_example: Have you decorated the room for Christmas yet?
spanish_meaning: Cuando decoras una habitación, la haces más atractiva agregándole cosas hermosas.
spanish_example: ¿Ya has decorado la habitación para Navidad?
spanish_meaning_en: When you decorate a room, you make it more attractive by adding beautiful things to it.
spanish_example_en: Have you decorated the room for Christmas yet?

english_meaning: When a building is dilapidated, it is old and in bad condition.
english_example: Paint peeled off of the old dilapidated apartment building.
spanish_meaning: Cuando un edificio está ruinoso, es viejo y está en mal estado.
spanish_example: La pintura se desprendió del antiguo edificio de apartamentos en ruinas.
spanish_meaning_en: When a building is dilapidated, it is old and in bad condition.
spanish_example_en: Paint peeled off of the old dilapidated apartment building.

english_meaning: To evoke a memory or emotion means to make it occur.
english_example: The picture evoked memories of when she was a young girl.
spanish_meaning: Evocar un recuerdo o una emoción significa hacerlo ocurrir.
spanish_example: La imagen evocó recuerdos de cuando ella era una niña.
spanish_meaning_en: To evoke a memory or emotion means to make it occur.
spanish_example_en: The picture evoked memories of when she was a young girl.

english_meaning: A farewell is an instance of saying goodbye or a way to say it.
english_example: She got on the plane after we said our farewells.
spanish_meaning: Una despedida es una instancia de decir adiós o una manera de decirlo.
spanish_example: Ella subió al avión después de que nos despediéramos.
spanish_meaning_en: A farewell is an instance of saying goodbye or a way to say it.
spanish_example_en: She got on the plane after we said our farewells.

english_meaning: A faucet is a device that controls the flow of a liquid or gas.
english_example: Turn off the faucet when you are done brushing your teeth.
spanish_meaning: Un grifo es un dispositivo que controla el flujo de un líquido o gas.
spanish_example: Cierra el grifo cuando termines de cepillarte los dientes.
spanish_meaning_en: A faucet is a device that controls the flow of a liquid or gas.
spanish_example_en: Turn off the faucet when you are done brushing your teeth.

english_meaning: Filth is dirt or dirty things that disgust you.
english_example: There was tons of filth and trash on the shore of the river.
spanish_meaning: La inmundicia es suciedad o cosas sucias que te dan asco.
spanish_example: Había toneladas de suciedad y basura en la orilla del río.
spanish_meaning_en: Filth is dirt or dirty things that disgust you.
spanish_example_en: There was tons of filth and trash on the shore of the river.

english_meaning: A flaw is a mistake in something that causes it to be less effective or correct.
english_example: We discovered a major flaw in the metal chain.
spanish_meaning: Un defecto es un error en algo que hace que sea menos efectivo o correcto.
spanish_example: Descubrimos un defecto importante en la cadena de metal.
spanish_meaning_en: A flaw is a mistake in something that causes it to be less effective or correct.
spanish_example_en: We discovered a major flaw in the metal chain.

english_meaning: To grin means to smile broadly.
english_example: That joke makes me grin every time I hear it.
spanish_meaning: Sonreír significa sonreír ampliamente.
spanish_example: Ese chiste me hace sonreír cada vez que lo escucho.
spanish_meaning_en: To grin means to smile broadly.
spanish_example_en: That joke makes me grin every time I hear it.

english_meaning: Housekeeping is the maintenance of a house or an establishment like a hotel.
english_example: Housekeeping is not much fun, but it has to be done.
spanish_meaning: El servicio de limpieza es el mantenimiento de una casa o de un establecimiento como un hotel.
spanish_example: La limpieza no es muy divertida, pero hay que hacerla.
spanish_meaning_en: Housekeeping is the maintenance of a house or an establishment like a hotel.
spanish_example_en: Housekeeping is not much fun, but it has to be done.

english_meaning: A mound of something is a large pile of it.
english_example: There was a mound of clothes on the teenager’s messy floor.
spanish_meaning: Un montículo de algo es una gran pila de ello.
spanish_example: Había un montón de ropa en el desordenado suelo del adolescente.
spanish_meaning_en: A mound of something is a large pile of it.
spanish_example_en: There was a mound of clothes on the teenager’s messy floor.

english_meaning: When a body part is numb, it does not have any feeling.
english_example: After holding my hand under the icy water, my fingers were numb.
spanish_meaning: Cuando una parte del cuerpo está entumecida, no siente ninguna sensación.
spanish_example: Después de sostener mi mano bajo el agua helada, mis dedos estaban entumecidos.
spanish_meaning_en: When a body part is numb, it does not have any feeling.
spanish_example_en: After holding my hand under the icy water, my fingers were numb.

english_meaning: When people are reckless, they act in an unsafe way.
english_example: The reckless driver posed a threat to everyone else on the road.
spanish_meaning: Cuando las personas son imprudentes, actúan de manera insegura.
spanish_example: El conductor imprudente representó una amenaza para todos los demás en la carretera.
spanish_meaning_en: When people are reckless, they act in an unsafe way.
spanish_example_en: The reckless driver posed a threat to everyone else on the road.

english_meaning: Slate is a dark grey rock that can easily be split into layers.
english_example: The roof of the church is made of slate.
spanish_meaning: La pizarra es una roca de color gris oscuro que se puede dividir fácilmente en capas.
spanish_example: El tejado de la iglesia es de pizarra.
spanish_meaning_en: Slate is a dark grey rock that can easily be split into layers.
spanish_example_en: The roof of the church is made of slate.

english_meaning: A stool is a seat with legs but no support for a person’s arms and back.
english_example: I don’t find stools very comfortable to sit on.
spanish_meaning: Un taburete es un asiento con patas pero sin soporte para los brazos ni la espalda de una persona.
spanish_example: No encuentro que los taburetes sean muy cómodos para sentarme.
spanish_meaning_en: A stool is a seat with legs but no support for a person’s arms and back.
spanish_example_en: I don’t find stools very comfortable to sit on.

english_meaning: A testament to something shows that it exists or is true.
english_example: The beautiful performance was a testament to the singer’s natural talents.
spanish_meaning: Un testimonio de algo muestra que existe o es verdadero.
spanish_example: La hermosa actuación fue un testimonio de los talentos naturales de la cantante.
spanish_meaning_en: A testament to something shows that it exists or is true.
spanish_example_en: The beautiful performance was a testament to the singer’s natural talents.

english_meaning: Timber is wood that is used for building houses and making furniture.
english_example: Trees in this area are grown specifically to be used for timber.
spanish_meaning: La madera es la madera que se utiliza para construir casas y fabricar muebles.
spanish_example: Los árboles en esta área se cultivan específicamente para usarse como madera.
spanish_meaning_en: Timber is wood that is used for building houses and making furniture.
spanish_example_en: Trees in this area are grown specifically to be used for timber.

english_meaning: A valve is a device attached to a pipe and controls the flow of liquid or air.
english_example: The mechanic removed the dirt to clear the engine valve.
spanish_meaning: Una válvula es un dispositivo conectado a una tubería y controla el flujo de líquido o aire.
spanish_example: El mecánico quitó la suciedad para limpiar la válvula del motor.
spanish_meaning_en: A valve is a device attached to a pipe and controls the flow of liquid or air.
spanish_example_en: The mechanic removed the dirt to clear the engine valve.

english_meaning: Camouflage is something used to hide people and things.
english_example: The green and brown camouflage was best used for hiding in forests and jungles.
spanish_meaning: El camuflaje es algo que se utiliza para ocultar personas y cosas.
spanish_example: El camuflaje verde y marrón se utilizaba mejor para esconderse en bosques y selvas.
spanish_meaning_en: Camouflage is something used to hide people and things.
spanish_example_en: The green and brown camouflage was best used for hiding in forests and jungles.

english_meaning: When you confront someone, you meet them face to face to deal with a problem.
english_example: I confronted him as he left the meeting and told him I thought he was wrong.
spanish_meaning: Cuando confrontas a alguien, lo encuentras cara a cara para abordar un problema.
spanish_example: Lo confronté cuando salió de la reunión y le dije que pensaba que estaba equivocado.
spanish_meaning_en: When you confront someone, you meet them face to face to deal with a problem.
spanish_example_en: I confronted him as he left the meeting and told him I thought he was wrong.

english_meaning: To contemplate something means to think about it.
english_example: Mark took a moment to contemplate the math problem before solving it.
spanish_meaning: Contemplar algo significa pensar en ello.
spanish_example: Mark se tomó un momento para contemplar el problema matemático antes de resolverlo.
spanish_meaning_en: To contemplate something means to think about it.
spanish_example_en: Mark took a moment to contemplate the math problem before solving it.

english_meaning: To contend with something means to struggle to overcome it.
english_example: Stacy had to contend with a learning disability throughout high school.
spanish_meaning: Lidiar con algo significa luchar para superarlo.
spanish_example: Stacy tuvo que lidiar con una discapacidad de aprendizaje durante toda la escuela secundaria.
spanish_meaning_en: To contend with something means to struggle to overcome it.
spanish_example_en: Stacy had to contend with a learning disability throughout high school.

english_meaning: A cot is a small portable bed.
english_example: At the camp, the boys’ cabin was lined with cots.
spanish_meaning: Una cuna es una pequeña cama portátil.
spanish_example: En el campamento, la cabaña de los niños estaba llena de catres.
spanish_meaning_en: A cot is a small portable bed.
spanish_example_en: At the camp, the boys’ cabin was lined with cots.

english_meaning: To enlist means to join the military.
english_example: In their final year at school, the students were asked to enlist in the military.
spanish_meaning: Alistarse significa unirse al ejército.
spanish_example: En su último año de escuela, se pidió a los estudiantes que se alistaran en el ejército.
spanish_meaning_en: To enlist means to join the military.
spanish_example_en: In their final year at school, the students were asked to enlist in the military.

english_meaning: A frontier is a border between two regions or countries.
english_example: A fence was built along the frontier where the river curved.
spanish_meaning: Una frontera es una frontera entre dos regiones o países.
spanish_example: Se construyó una valla a lo largo de la frontera donde el río curvaba.
spanish_meaning_en: A frontier is a border between two regions or countries.
spanish_example_en: A fence was built along the frontier where the river curved.

english_meaning: A guerilla is a person who fights as part of an unofficial army.
english_example: Guerilla warfare involves a lot of hit-and-run fighting.
spanish_meaning: Una guerrilla es una persona que lucha como parte de un ejército no oficial.
spanish_example: La guerra de guerrillas implica muchos combates de atropello y fuga.
spanish_meaning_en: A guerilla is a person who fights as part of an unofficial army.
spanish_example_en: Guerilla warfare involves a lot of hit-and-run fighting.

english_meaning: A handbook is material that gives specific information or instructions.
english_example: If you look at the handbook, it will tell you which wires to connect to the TV.
spanish_meaning: Un manual es material que brinda información o instrucciones específicas.
spanish_example: Si consulta el manual, le indicará qué cables conectar al televisor.
spanish_meaning_en: A handbook is material that gives specific information or instructions.
spanish_example_en: If you look at the handbook, it will tell you which wires to connect to the TV.

english_meaning: If someone is hesitant, then they are not sure or are slow to act or speak.
english_example: Although he knew the answer, he was hesitant to say it because he might be wrong.
spanish_meaning: Si alguien duda, entonces no está seguro o tarda en actuar o hablar.
spanish_example: Aunque conocía la respuesta, dudaba en decirla porque podría estar equivocado.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is hesitant, then they are not sure or are slow to act or speak.
spanish_example_en: Although he knew the answer, he was hesitant to say it because he might be wrong.

english_meaning: If something is lush, then it is full of a variety of large, healthy plants.
english_example: The lush jungle was filled with plants, trees, and vines.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es exuberante, entonces está lleno de una variedad de plantas grandes y saludables.
spanish_example: La exuberante jungla estaba llena de plantas, árboles y enredaderas.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is lush, then it is full of a variety of large, healthy plants.
spanish_example_en: The lush jungle was filled with plants, trees, and vines.

english_meaning: An outfit is a set of clothes worn together, often for a certain job or event.
english_example: Kelly’s new outfit made her look so glamourous.
spanish_meaning: Un conjunto es un conjunto de prendas que se usan juntas, a menudo para un determinado trabajo o evento.
spanish_example: El nuevo atuendo de Kelly la hacía lucir muy glamorosa.
spanish_meaning_en: An outfit is a set of clothes worn together, often for a certain job or event.
spanish_example_en: Kelly’s new outfit made her look so glamourous.

english_meaning: A paw is an animal’s foot that has claws or soft pads.
english_example: The kitten cleaned its paws with its tongue.
spanish_meaning: Una pata es la pata de un animal que tiene garras o almohadillas suaves.
spanish_example: El gatito se limpió las patas con la lengua.
spanish_meaning_en: A paw is an animal’s foot that has claws or soft pads.
spanish_example_en: The kitten cleaned its paws with its tongue.

english_meaning: If something or someone is splendid, then they are very good.
english_example: From his head down to his shoes, his clothes looked splendid.
spanish_meaning: Si algo o alguien es espléndido, entonces es muy bueno.
spanish_example: Desde la cabeza hasta los zapatos, su ropa lucía espléndida.
spanish_meaning_en: If something or someone is splendid, then they are very good.
spanish_example_en: From his head down to his shoes, his clothes looked splendid.

english_meaning: To stray means to go in a wrong direction and become lost.
english_example: He found himself lost because he had strayed from the tour group.
spanish_meaning: Desviarse significa ir en una dirección equivocada y perderse.
spanish_example: Se encontró perdido porque se había desviado del grupo turístico.
spanish_meaning_en: To stray means to go in a wrong direction and become lost.
spanish_example_en: He found himself lost because he had strayed from the tour group.

english_meaning: If something is substantial, then it is of great importance, size, or value.
english_example: The bank said that he owed it a substantial amount of money.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es sustancial, entonces es de gran importancia, tamaño o valor.
spanish_example: El banco dijo que le debía una cantidad sustancial de dinero.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is substantial, then it is of great importance, size, or value.
spanish_example_en: The bank said that he owed it a substantial amount of money.

english_meaning: A torch is a stick with one end on fire that can be carried in order to give light.
english_example: He grabbed a piece of wood and stuck it in the fire in order to make a torch.
spanish_meaning: Una antorcha es un palo con un extremo encendido que se puede llevar para iluminar.
spanish_example: Agarró un trozo de madera y lo metió en el fuego para hacer una antorcha.
spanish_meaning_en: A torch is a stick with one end on fire that can be carried in order to give light.
spanish_example_en: He grabbed a piece of wood and stuck it in the fire in order to make a torch.

english_meaning: A tract is a large area of land.
english_example: On the other side of the mountains, there was a long tract of forest.
spanish_meaning: Un terreno es una gran extensión de terreno.
spanish_example: Al otro lado de las montañas, había una larga extensión de bosque.
spanish_meaning_en: A tract is a large area of land.
spanish_example_en: On the other side of the mountains, there was a long tract of forest.

english_meaning: A vigil is a period of watchful attention at night for a specific purpose.
english_example: He had a vigil in front of the tomb for three days.
spanish_meaning: Una vigilia es un período de atención vigilante durante la noche con un propósito específico.
spanish_example: Tuvo una vigilia delante del sepulcro durante tres días.
spanish_meaning_en: A vigil is a period of watchful attention at night for a specific purpose.
spanish_example_en: He had a vigil in front of the tomb for three days.

english_meaning: If someone is weary, then they are tired.

spanish_meaning: Si alguien está cansado, entonces está cansado.
spanish_example: Jane estaba cansada después de un largo día de trabajo.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is weary, then they are tired.
spanish_example_en: Jane was weary after a long day of work.

english_meaning: If something is amid something else, then it is in the middle of it.

spanish_meaning: Si algo está en medio de otra cosa, entonces está en medio de ella.
spanish_example: La abeja volaba afanosamente entre las flores del jardín.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is amid something else, then it is in the middle of it.
spanish_example_en: The bee was busily flying amid the flowers in the garden.

english_meaning: If something happens backstage, it occurs behind a theater’s stage.
english_example: After the show, the director went backstage and thanked the actors.
spanish_meaning: Si algo sucede detrás del escenario, ocurre detrás del escenario de un teatro.
spanish_example: Después del espectáculo, el director fue al backstage y agradeció a los actores.
spanish_meaning_en: If something happens backstage, it occurs behind a theater’s stage.
spanish_example_en: After the show, the director went backstage and thanked the actors.

english_meaning: A billionaire is someone who has at least one billion dollars.

spanish_meaning: Un multimillonario es alguien que tiene al menos mil millones de dólares.
spanish_example: La venta de sus inventos convirtió al inventor en multimillonario.
spanish_meaning_en: A billionaire is someone who has at least one billion dollars.
spanish_example_en: The sale of his inventions made the inventor a billionaire.

english_meaning: A brute is someone who behaves or looks like a violent animal.

spanish_meaning: Un bruto es alguien que se comporta o parece un animal violento.
spanish_example: Mi hermano mayor puede actuar como un bruto cuando no se sale con la suya.
spanish_meaning_en: A brute is someone who behaves or looks like a violent animal.
spanish_example_en: My older brother can act like a brute when he doesn’t get his way.

spanish_meaning: Chocar con algo significa golpearlo mientras se está en movimiento.

english_meaning: A culprit is someone who has committed a crime or other bad deed.

spanish_example: La policía todavía buscaba al culpable del robo.

spanish_meaning: Testificar significa dar testimonio como testigo.

spanish_meaning: Un tumulto es un ruido fuerte y confuso hecho por una gran multitud de personas.

spanish_meaning_en: A tumult is a loud and confused noise made by a large crowd of people.

english_meaning: To underestimate something or someone means to think less of them than they really are.

spanish_example_en: We lost the game because we underestimated the other team’s skill.

english_meaning: Uproar is loud noise caused by people who are very angry or upset.

spanish_meaning: El alboroto es un ruido fuerte causado por personas que están muy enojadas o molestas.

spanish_meaning_en: Uproar is loud noise caused by people who are very angry or upset.

english_meaning: To accelerate means to increase in speed.

spanish_meaning: Acelerar significa aumentar la velocidad.
