spanish_meaning_en: If something is linguistic, then it is concerned with language.
spanish_example_en: A linguistic way of studying culture focuses on words within that culture.

english_meaning: If someone is monolingual, then they speak only one language.
english_example: In today’s global economy, being monolingual limits your opportunities.
spanish_meaning: Si alguien es monolingüe, entonces habla un solo idioma.
spanish_example: En la economía global actual, ser monolingüe limita sus oportunidades.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is monolingual, then they speak only one language.
spanish_example_en: In today’s global economy, being monolingual limits your opportunities.

english_meaning: Nationality is an identity based on the nation from which you come.
english_example: His nationality is German, but he speaks French, Spanish, and Korean.
spanish_meaning: La nacionalidad es una identidad basada en la nación de la que provienes.
spanish_example: Su nacionalidad es alemana, pero habla francés, español y coreano.
spanish_meaning_en: Nationality is an identity based on the nation from which you come.
spanish_example_en: His nationality is German, but he speaks French, Spanish, and Korean.

english_meaning: A patriot is someone who loves, supports, and defends their country.
english_example: Every year, young patriots join their countries’ militaries.
spanish_meaning: Un patriota es alguien que ama, apoya y defiende a su país.
spanish_example: Cada año, jóvenes patriotas se unen a los ejércitos de sus países.
spanish_meaning_en: A patriot is someone who loves, supports, and defends their country.
spanish_example_en: Every year, young patriots join their countries’ militaries.

english_meaning: To prosecute someone means to take legal action against them.
english_example: They were prosecuted for fishing in the river without a permit.
spanish_meaning: Procesar a alguien significa emprender acciones legales contra él.
spanish_example: Fueron procesados ​​por pescar en el río sin permiso.
spanish_meaning_en: To prosecute someone means to take legal action against them.
spanish_example_en: They were prosecuted for fishing in the river without a permit.

english_meaning: If something is racial, then it is related to a race or races.
english_example: The differences between racial groups are physical characteristics and culture.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es racial, entonces está relacionado con una raza o razas.
spanish_example: Las diferencias entre grupos raciales son las características físicas y la cultura.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is racial, then it is related to a race or races.
spanish_example_en: The differences between racial groups are physical characteristics and culture.

english_meaning: If something is solemn, then it is serious and honest.
english_example: The professor preferred a solemn relationship between him and his students.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es solemne, entonces es serio y honesto.
spanish_example: El profesor prefería una relación solemne entre él y sus alumnos.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is solemn, then it is serious and honest.
spanish_example_en: The professor preferred a solemn relationship between him and his students.

english_meaning: Solidarity is a union formed from common responsibilities or interests.
english_example: All the citizens came together in a show of solidarity to create change.
spanish_meaning: La solidaridad es una unión formada a partir de responsabilidades o intereses comunes.
spanish_example: Todos los ciudadanos se unieron en una muestra de solidaridad para generar un cambio.
spanish_meaning_en: Solidarity is a union formed from common responsibilities or interests.
spanish_example_en: All the citizens came together in a show of solidarity to create change.

english_meaning: Tact is the ability to avoid offending people when dealing with problems.
english_example: Since both sides would not agree, it required someone with tact to make peace.
spanish_meaning: El tacto es la capacidad de evitar ofender a las personas cuando se enfrentan a problemas.
spanish_example: Como ambas partes no estaban de acuerdo, se necesitaba a alguien con tacto para hacer las paces.
spanish_meaning_en: Tact is the ability to avoid offending people when dealing with problems.
spanish_example_en: Since both sides would not agree, it required someone with tact to make peace.

english_meaning: To undermine someone means to betray them or weaken their efforts or authority.
english_example: The documents helped to undermine the workers’ trust in their bosses’ honesty.
spanish_meaning: Socavar a alguien significa traicionarlo o debilitar sus esfuerzos o su autoridad.
spanish_example: Los documentos ayudaron a socavar la confianza de los trabajadores en la honestidad de sus jefes.
spanish_meaning_en: To undermine someone means to betray them or weaken their efforts or authority.
spanish_example_en: The documents helped to undermine the workers’ trust in their bosses’ honesty.

english_meaning: To constrain something means to limit its development.
english_example: Jim cannot join us because he is constrained by previous plans.
spanish_meaning: Restringir algo significa limitar su desarrollo.
spanish_example: Jim no puede unirse a nosotros porque está limitado por planes anteriores.
spanish_meaning_en: To constrain something means to limit its development.
spanish_example_en: Jim cannot join us because he is constrained by previous plans.

english_meaning: A depot is a bus or train station.
english_example: He waited for his mother to arrive at the depot.
spanish_meaning: Una estación es una estación de autobús o de tren.
spanish_example: Esperó a que su madre llegara a la estación.
notes: Ambigüedad: “depot” también puede significar almacén.
spanish_meaning_en: A depot is a bus or train station.
spanish_example_en: He waited for his mother to arrive at the depot.

english_meaning: To emulate people means to imitate them because they are greatly admired.
english_example: As a small boy, he always tried to emulate his big brother.
spanish_meaning: Emular a las personas significa imitarlas porque son muy admiradas.
spanish_example: Desde pequeño siempre intentó emular a su hermano mayor.
spanish_meaning_en: To emulate people means to imitate them because they are greatly admired.
spanish_example_en: As a small boy, he always tried to emulate his big brother.

english_meaning: The forefinger is the finger between one’s thumb and middle finger.
english_example: He shouted, “There it is!” and pointed with his forefinger.
spanish_meaning: El dedo índice es el dedo entre el pulgar y el dedo medio.
spanish_example: Gritó: "¡Ahí está!". y señaló con el dedo índice.
spanish_meaning_en: The forefinger is the finger between one’s thumb and middle finger.
spanish_example_en: He shouted, “There it is!” and pointed with his forefinger.

english_meaning: The guts are all the organs inside a person or animal.
english_example: The doctor can tell you every process that happens in one’s guts.
spanish_meaning: Las tripas son todos los órganos del interior de una persona o animal.
spanish_example: El médico puede contarte cada proceso que ocurre en las entrañas.
spanish_meaning_en: The guts are all the organs inside a person or animal.
spanish_example_en: The doctor can tell you every process that happens in one’s guts.

english_meaning: When something is inherent, it is a natural part of something else.
english_example: Sweating is an inherent bodily function when exercising.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es inherente, es parte natural de otra cosa.
spanish_example: La sudoración es una función corporal inherente al ejercicio.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is inherent, it is a natural part of something else.
spanish_example_en: Sweating is an inherent bodily function when exercising.

english_meaning: To intimidate means to frighten others.
english_example: My dad intimidates my friends whenever they visit.
spanish_meaning: Intimidar significa asustar a los demás.
spanish_example: Mi papá intimida a mis amigos cada vez que vienen de visita.
spanish_meaning_en: To intimidate means to frighten others.
spanish_example_en: My dad intimidates my friends whenever they visit.

english_meaning: A janitor is a person who makes repairs and takes care of a building.
english_example: The school janitor cleaned up the messy cafeteria.
spanish_meaning: Un conserje es una persona que hace reparaciones y cuida un edificio.
spanish_example: El conserje de la escuela limpió la desordenada cafetería.
spanish_meaning_en: A janitor is a person who makes repairs and takes care of a building.
spanish_example_en: The school janitor cleaned up the messy cafeteria.

english_meaning: When something is moist, it is slightly wet.
english_example: The ground is still moist from the rain last night.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo está húmedo, está ligeramente mojado.
spanish_example: El suelo todavía está húmedo por la lluvia de anoche.
spanish_meaning_en: When something is moist, it is slightly wet.
spanish_example_en: The ground is still moist from the rain last night.

english_meaning: Nope is an informal way of saying “no.”
english_example: He asked if I had any money, and I had to say, “Nope.”
spanish_meaning: No es una forma informal de decir "no".
spanish_example: Me preguntó si tenía dinero y tuve que decir: "No".
spanish_meaning_en: Nope is an informal way of saying “no.”
spanish_example_en: He asked if I had any money, and I had to say, “Nope.”

english_meaning: To prod means to push someone or something with a finger or pointed object.
english_example: The bully prodded me in the chest with her finger.
spanish_meaning: Pinchar significa empujar a alguien o algo con un dedo u objeto puntiagudo.
spanish_example: La acosadora me empujó con el dedo en el pecho.
spanish_meaning_en: To prod means to push someone or something with a finger or pointed object.
spanish_example_en: The bully prodded me in the chest with her finger.

english_meaning: A ransom is a sum of money paid to a kidnapper to set the person free.
english_example: He kidnapped the prince and demanded $1 million as a ransom.
spanish_meaning: Un rescate es una suma de dinero pagada a un secuestrador para liberar a la persona.
spanish_example: Secuestró al príncipe y exigió un millón de dólares como rescate.
spanish_meaning_en: A ransom is a sum of money paid to a kidnapper to set the person free.
spanish_example_en: He kidnapped the prince and demanded $1 million as a ransom.

english_meaning: To restrain someone or something means to use physical strength to stop them.
english_example: Mike restrained Allen from reaching the door.
spanish_meaning: Restringir a alguien o algo significa usar la fuerza física para detenerlo.
spanish_example: Mike impidió que Allen llegara a la puerta.
spanish_meaning_en: To restrain someone or something means to use physical strength to stop them.
spanish_example_en: Mike restrained Allen from reaching the door.

english_meaning: Saliva is the watery liquid in people’s mouths that helps in digestion.
english_example: The baby could not keep the saliva from dripping out of its mouth.
spanish_meaning: La saliva es el líquido acuoso que se encuentra en la boca de las personas y que ayuda en la digestión.
spanish_example: El bebé no pudo evitar que la saliva le saliera de la boca.
spanish_meaning_en: Saliva is the watery liquid in people’s mouths that helps in digestion.
spanish_example_en: The baby could not keep the saliva from dripping out of its mouth.

english_meaning: To spit means to force liquid from one’s mouth.
english_example: He emerged from the pool and spit water from his mouth.
spanish_meaning: Escupir significa expulsar líquido de la boca.
spanish_example: Salió de la piscina y escupió agua por la boca.
spanish_meaning_en: To spit means to force liquid from one’s mouth.
spanish_example_en: He emerged from the pool and spit water from his mouth.

english_meaning: To sprint means to run very fast over a short distance.
english_example: The kids didn’t want to be late to class, so they sprinted to the bus stop.
spanish_meaning: Correr significa correr muy rápido en una distancia corta.
spanish_example: Los niños no querían llegar tarde a clase, así que corrieron hasta la parada del autobús.
spanish_meaning_en: To sprint means to run very fast over a short distance.
spanish_example_en: The kids didn’t want to be late to class, so they sprinted to the bus stop.

english_meaning: A stunt is something that is done in order to get attention or publicity.
english_example: The man jumped over the cars as a promotional stunt.
spanish_meaning: Un truco es algo que se hace para llamar la atención o la publicidad.
spanish_example: El hombre saltó sobre los coches como truco promocional.
spanish_meaning_en: A stunt is something that is done in order to get attention or publicity.
spanish_example_en: The man jumped over the cars as a promotional stunt.

english_meaning: When people are tolerant, they are respectful of others’ rights and beliefs.
english_example: The tolerant leader thought that everyone was equal regardless of race.
spanish_meaning: Cuando las personas son tolerantes, respetan los derechos y creencias de los demás.
spanish_example: El líder tolerante pensaba que todos eran iguales sin importar la raza.
spanish_meaning_en: When people are tolerant, they are respectful of others’ rights and beliefs.
spanish_example_en: The tolerant leader thought that everyone was equal regardless of race.

english_meaning: A vampire is a fictional monster that sleeps in a coffin and sucks people’s blood.
english_example: The vampire snuck up on the woman and bit her neck.
spanish_meaning: Un vampiro es un monstruo ficticio que duerme en un ataúd y chupa la sangre de la gente.
spanish_example: El vampiro se acercó sigilosamente a la mujer y le mordió el cuello.
spanish_meaning_en: A vampire is a fictional monster that sleeps in a coffin and sucks people’s blood.
spanish_example_en: The vampire snuck up on the woman and bit her neck.

english_meaning: To yawn means to open one’s mouth wide and breathe in air.
english_example: The child yawned and stretched her arms before going to bed for the night.
spanish_meaning: Bostezar significa abrir bien la boca y respirar aire.
spanish_example: La niña bostezó y estiró los brazos antes de acostarse.
spanish_meaning_en: To yawn means to open one’s mouth wide and breathe in air.
spanish_example_en: The child yawned and stretched her arms before going to bed for the night.

english_meaning: If something is adjoining, it is next to or joined with a building or room.
english_example: I couldn’t sleep because the people in the adjoining room were loud.
spanish_meaning: Si algo está contiguo, está al lado o unido a un edificio o habitación.
spanish_example: No podía dormir porque la gente en la habitación contigua hacía mucho ruido.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is adjoining, it is next to or joined with a building or room.
spanish_example_en: I couldn’t sleep because the people in the adjoining room were loud.

english_meaning: To allege something is to say that it is true without offering proof.
english_example: The little girl had alleged that her older brother hid her favorite doll.
spanish_meaning: Alegar algo es decir que es cierto sin ofrecer pruebas.
spanish_example: La pequeña había alegado que su hermano mayor escondió su muñeca favorita.
spanish_meaning_en: To allege something is to say that it is true without offering proof.
spanish_example_en: The little girl had alleged that her older brother hid her favorite doll.

english_meaning: An arch is a curved opening formed under a structure such as a bridge or doorway.
english_example: The arch of the bridge was not high enough for the tall boat to pass underneath.
spanish_meaning: Un arco es una abertura curva formada debajo de una estructura como un puente o una puerta.
spanish_example: El arco del puente no era lo suficientemente alto para que el barco pasara por debajo.
spanish_meaning_en: An arch is a curved opening formed under a structure such as a bridge or doorway.
spanish_example_en: The arch of the bridge was not high enough for the tall boat to pass underneath.

english_meaning: To assemble means to get together in one place.

spanish_meaning: Reunirse significa reunirse en un solo lugar.
spanish_example: Los padres se reunieron para discutir formas de mejorar la educación de sus hijos.
spanish_meaning_en: To assemble means to get together in one place.
spanish_example_en: The parents assembled to discuss ways to improve their children’s education.

english_meaning: A casualty is a person killed or injured in a war or an accident.
english_example: The only casualty in the car accident was a woman who broke her arm.
spanish_meaning: Una baja es una persona muerta o herida en una guerra o en un accidente.
spanish_example: La única víctima del accidente automovilístico fue una mujer que se rompió el brazo.
spanish_meaning_en: A casualty is a person killed or injured in a war or an accident.
spanish_example_en: The only casualty in the car accident was a woman who broke her arm.

english_meaning: To erect something means to build it.

spanish_meaning: Erigir algo significa construirlo.

spanish_meaning_en: To erect something means to build it.
spanish_example_en: The king erected two towers on the north and south sides of his castle.

english_meaning: If something is foul, then it is very unpleasant.

spanish_meaning: Si algo está mal, entonces es muy desagradable.
spanish_example: No dejaba que su perro bebiera del agua porque tenía mal olor.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is foul, then it is very unpleasant.
spanish_example_en: He wouldn’t let his dog drink from the water because it had a foul smell.

english_meaning: A hectare is a unit of measure equal to 10,000 square meters.

spanish_meaning: Una hectárea es una unidad de medida equivalente a 10.000 metros cuadrados.
spanish_example: Su granja familiar cubría muchas hectáreas.
spanish_meaning_en: A hectare is a unit of measure equal to 10,000 square meters.
spanish_example_en: His family farm covered many hectares.

english_meaning: To heighten an emotion means to increase the intensity of it.

spanish_meaning: Intensificar una emoción significa aumentar su intensidad.

spanish_meaning_en: To heighten an emotion means to increase the intensity of it.
spanish_example_en: The pleasant music heightened their enjoyment of the wonderful dinner.

english_meaning: Hospitality is friendly behavior and entertainment, shown to guests or strangers.

spanish_meaning: La hospitalidad es un comportamiento amistoso y de entretenimiento, mostrado hacia huéspedes o extraños.
spanish_example: Los viajeros quedaron sorprendidos por la hospitalidad brindada por el personal del hotel.
spanish_meaning_en: Hospitality is friendly behavior and entertainment, shown to guests or strangers.

spanish_meaning: Una mansión es una casa grande y cara.

spanish_example_en: The mansion had thirty bedrooms, two kitchens, and a pool.

english_meaning: To outnumber a group means to have a greater number than it.

spanish_meaning: Superar en número a un grupo significa tener un número mayor que él.
spanish_example: Las niñas superaban en número a los niños en la escuela por cuatro a uno.
spanish_meaning_en: To outnumber a group means to have a greater number than it.
spanish_example_en: The girls outnumbered the boys at the school by four to one.

english_meaning: If someone is overjoyed, then they are extremely happy.
english_example: He was overjoyed by the news of his promotion.
spanish_meaning: Si alguien está muy feliz, entonces es extremadamente feliz.
spanish_example: Estaba encantado con la noticia de su ascenso.
spanish_meaning_en: If someone is overjoyed, then they are extremely happy.
spanish_example_en: He was overjoyed by the news of his promotion.

english_meaning: A pasture is an area of land covered with grass for animals to use as food.

spanish_meaning: Un pasto es un área de tierra cubierta de pasto para que los animales lo utilicen como alimento.
spanish_example: Las ovejas fueron llevadas a un pasto donde había más pasto.
spanish_meaning_en: A pasture is an area of land covered with grass for animals to use as food.
spanish_example_en: The sheep were taken to a pasture where there was more grass.

english_meaning: A petition is a written request asking an authority to do something.

spanish_meaning: Una petición es una solicitud escrita que solicita a una autoridad que haga algo.
spanish_example: Todos los ciudadanos firmaron una petición pidiendo al alcalde que reparara las aceras.
spanish_meaning_en: A petition is a written request asking an authority to do something.
spanish_example_en: The citizens all signed a petition asking the mayor to repair the sidewalks.

english_meaning: To renovate a building means to repair it, or to build new structures on it.

spanish_meaning: Renovar un edificio significa repararlo o construir nuevas estructuras en él.
spanish_example: La antigua estación de bomberos fue rehabilitada para convertirla en un edificio de apartamentos.
spanish_meaning_en: To renovate a building means to repair it, or to build new structures on it.

english_meaning: To revise something means to change it, or update it to make it better.
english_example: When the editor discovered certain facts were wrong, he revised the book.
spanish_meaning: Revisar algo significa cambiarlo o actualizarlo para mejorarlo.
spanish_example: Cuando el editor descubrió que ciertos hechos estaban equivocados, revisó el libro.
spanish_meaning_en: To revise something means to change it, or update it to make it better.
spanish_example_en: When the editor discovered certain facts were wrong, he revised the book.

english_meaning: A slab is a large, thick, flat piece of stone, concrete, metal, or wood.

spanish_meaning: Una losa es una pieza grande, gruesa y plana de piedra, hormigón, metal o madera.

spanish_meaning_en: A slab is a large, thick, flat piece of stone, concrete, metal, or wood.
spanish_example_en: I looked at various slabs of stone to decorate my house.

english_meaning: A terrace is an open area that is connected to a house or an apartment.

spanish_meaning: Una terraza es un área abierta que está conectada a una casa o apartamento.
spanish_example: Por las tardes le gustaba sentarse en la terraza y consultar su correo electrónico.
spanish_meaning_en: A terrace is an open area that is connected to a house or an apartment.
spanish_example_en: In the afternoons, she liked to sit on the terrace and check her email.

english_meaning: Turf is a section of grass and the dirt in which it grows.

spanish_example: Después del partido, el césped parecía irregular.
spanish_meaning_en: Turf is a section of grass and the dirt in which it grows.
spanish_example_en: After the game, the turf looked ragged.

english_meaning: If something is analogous to another thing, then it is like it in certain ways.
english_example: The relationship with his teacher was analogous to that of a son and mother.
spanish_meaning: Si algo es análogo a otra cosa, entonces es similar en ciertos aspectos.
spanish_example: La relación con su maestra era análoga a la de un hijo y una madre.
spanish_meaning_en: If something is analogous to another thing, then it is like it in certain ways.
spanish_example_en: The relationship with his teacher was analogous to that of a son and mother.

english_example: He could see the ship on the horizon only if he used his binoculars.
spanish_meaning: Los binoculares son un dispositivo que se utiliza para ver cosas que están lejos.
spanish_example: Sólo podía ver el barco en el horizonte si usaba sus binoculares.
spanish_meaning_en: Binoculars are a device used for seeing things that are far away.
spanish_example_en: He could see the ship on the horizon only if he used his binoculars.

english_meaning: The bulk of something is its size.

spanish_meaning: La mayor parte de algo es su tamaño.
spanish_example: El gran elefante movía su mole con patas tan fuertes como troncos de árboles.
spanish_meaning_en: The bulk of something is its size.
spanish_example_en: The large elephant moved its bulk with legs as strong as tree trunks.

spanish_meaning: Si algo comprende otra cosa, consiste o se compone de ella.
spanish_example: El equipo de fútbol de nuestra escuela está compuesto principalmente por estudiantes del último año.
spanish_meaning_en: If something comprises something else, it consists of or is made up of it.
spanish_example_en: Our school’s football team is mostly comprised with seniors.

english_meaning: To depict something means to show or portray it, often using art.

spanish_example: El rostro de la estatua representaba la determinación y el coraje del general.
spanish_meaning_en: To depict something means to show or portray it, often using art.
spanish_example_en: The statue’s face depicted the general’s determination and courage.

english_meaning: If something is dual, then it is made up of two parts.

spanish_meaning: Si algo es dual, entonces se compone de dos partes.
spanish_example: La sala tenía una doble función. Era una sala de estar, pero de noche era un dormitorio.

spanish_meaning_en: Fahrenheit is a temperature scale where water freezes at 32° and boils at 212°.
spanish_example_en: When the temperature dropped to 32° Fahrenheit, it started snowing.

english_meaning: To fulfill something means to achieve or finish it.
english_example: The professor did not fulfill his promise not to miss a single class.
spanish_meaning: Cumplir algo significa lograrlo o terminarlo.
spanish_example: El profesor no cumplió su promesa de no faltar ni una sola clase.
spanish_meaning_en: To fulfill something means to achieve or finish it.
spanish_example_en: The professor did not fulfill his promise not to miss a single class.

english_meaning: A grove is a small group of trees.
english_example: All the trees in this grove are apple trees.
spanish_meaning: Una arboleda es un pequeño grupo de árboles.
spanish_example: Todos los árboles de esta arboleda son manzanos.
spanish_meaning_en: A grove is a small group of trees.
spanish_example_en: All the trees in this grove are apple trees.

english_meaning: Ore is the raw form of rock or material from which a valuable metal is taken.
english_example: The factory melted the ore and used it to make iron products.
spanish_meaning: El mineral es la forma bruta de roca o material del que se extrae un metal valioso.
spanish_example: La fábrica fundió el mineral y lo utilizó para fabricar productos de hierro.
spanish_meaning_en: Ore is the raw form of rock or material from which a valuable metal is taken.
spanish_example_en: The factory melted the ore and used it to make iron products.

english_meaning: The outback is the wild inland region of Australia where very few people live.
english_example: Many Australian farmers use the outback to raise cattle.
spanish_meaning: El interior es la región salvaje del interior de Australia donde vive muy poca gente.
spanish_example: Muchos agricultores australianos utilizan el interior para criar ganado.
notes: Ambigüedad: también se usa “interior australiano”.
spanish_meaning_en: The outback is the wild inland region of Australia where very few people live.
spanish_example_en: Many Australian farmers use the outback to raise cattle.

english_meaning: To outweigh something means to exceed it in value, amount, or importance.
english_example: Finding a warm place to sleep outweighed the need to find something to eat.
spanish_meaning: Superar algo significa excederlo en valor, cantidad o importancia.
spanish_example: Encontrar un lugar cálido para dormir pesaba más que la necesidad de encontrar algo para comer.
spanish_meaning_en: To outweigh something means to exceed it in value, amount, or importance.
spanish_example_en: Finding a warm place to sleep outweighed the need to find something to eat.

english_meaning: A paradox is a true statement or real event that seems illogical.
english_example: The paradox of her work was that the less she worked, the more she got done.
spanish_meaning: Una paradoja es una afirmación verdadera o un hecho real que parece ilógico.
spanish_example: La paradoja de su trabajo era que cuanto menos trabajaba, más hacía.
spanish_meaning_en: A paradox is a true statement or real event that seems illogical.
spanish_example_en: The paradox of her work was that the less she worked, the more she got done.

english_meaning: A pier is a structure that extends into a body of water.
english_example: If you want to catch bigger fish, then go to the far end of the pier.
spanish_meaning: Un muelle es una estructura que se extiende hacia un cuerpo de agua.
spanish_example: Si quieres pescar peces más grandes, ve al otro extremo del muelle.
spanish_meaning_en: A pier is a structure that extends into a body of water.
spanish_example_en: If you want to catch bigger fish, then go to the far end of the pier.

english_meaning: A shortcut is a route that is shorter than the main route.
english_example: We got to the house early because we took a shortcut through the forest.
spanish_meaning: Un atajo es una ruta más corta que la ruta principal.
spanish_example: Llegamos temprano a la casa porque tomamos un atajo por el bosque.
spanish_meaning_en: A shortcut is a route that is shorter than the main route.
spanish_example_en: We got to the house early because we took a shortcut through the forest.

english_meaning: A tariff is a tax or fee paid on certain imports or exports.
english_example: Our tariff on imported wool makes foreign wool more expensive.
spanish_meaning: Un arancel es un impuesto o tasa que se paga sobre determinadas importaciones o exportaciones.
spanish_example: Nuestro arancel sobre la lana importada encarece la lana extranjera.
spanish_meaning_en: A tariff is a tax or fee paid on certain imports or exports.
spanish_example_en: Our tariff on imported wool makes foreign wool more expensive.

english_meaning: A thermometer is a device that measures temperature.
english_example: The thermometer outside the window indicated that it was a hot day today.
spanish_meaning: Un termómetro es un dispositivo que mide la temperatura.
spanish_example: El termómetro fuera de la ventana indicó que hoy era un día caluroso.
spanish_meaning_en: A thermometer is a device that measures temperature.
spanish_example_en: The thermometer outside the window indicated that it was a hot day today.

english_meaning: To tilt something means to tip it into a sloping position.
english_example: She tilted her glass and almost spilled some of the wine inside.
spanish_meaning: Inclinar algo significa moverlo para que no quede recto o vertical.
spanish_example: Inclinó su copa y casi derramó parte del vino que contenía.
spanish_meaning_en: To tilt something means to tip it into a sloping position.
spanish_example_en: She tilted her glass and almost spilled some of the wine inside.

english_meaning: If a statement is vice versa, then its two main ideas are switched with one another.
english_example: Students learn from their teachers, and vice versa.
spanish_meaning: Si una afirmación es al revés, entonces sus dos ideas principales se intercambian entre sí.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes aprenden de sus profesores y viceversa.
spanish_meaning_en: If a statement is vice versa, then its two main ideas are switched with one another.
spanish_example_en: Students learn from their teachers, and vice versa.

english_meaning: The whereabouts of someone or something is the place where they are.
english_example: The police looked for the lost dog, but its whereabouts were still unknown.
spanish_meaning: El paradero de alguien o algo es el lugar donde se encuentra.
spanish_example: La policía buscó al perro perdido, pero aún se desconocía su paradero.
spanish_meaning_en: The whereabouts of someone or something is the place where they are.
spanish_example_en: The police looked for the lost dog, but its whereabouts were still unknown.
